era.zh: 公元220年：三国时代
era.en: 220 CE: Three Kingdoms Era

summary.zh: 公元220年，东汉正式终结，曹魏建立，蜀汉与孙吴政权相继形成，中国进入三国鼎立时期；与此同时，罗马帝国步入三世纪危机前夜，安息帝国仍统治伊朗与两河流域，贵霜、萨塔瓦哈那与诸区域王国并立，非洲、欧洲草原、东南亚、朝鲜、日本及美洲亦分布着众多王国、部族联盟与城市文明。
summary.en: In 220 CE, the Eastern Han formally ended and Cao Wei was established, while Shu Han and Eastern Wu were emerging, ushering China into the Three Kingdoms era; meanwhile, the Roman Empire stood at the onset of the Third Century Crisis, the Parthian Empire still ruled Iran and Mesopotamia, the Kushans, Satavahanas, and many regional kingdoms coexisted across Asia, and Africa, the European frontier, Southeast Asia, Korea, Japan, and the Americas were likewise populated by numerous kingdoms, tribal confederations, and urban civilizations.

governmentForm.zh: 中央集权的皇帝官僚帝国，承继东汉制度并加以重整
governmentForm.en: A centralized imperial bureaucratic empire inheriting and reorganizing Eastern Han institutions

socialStructure.zh: 社会以皇帝与宗室为顶层，其下为士族与官僚、地方豪强、平民农户、工匠商人，以及佃客、部曲与奴婢等依附人口。
socialStructure.en: Society was headed by the emperor and imperial clan, followed by gentry lineages and officials, regional magnates, common peasant households, artisans and merchants, and dependent populations such as retainers, estate followers, and slaves.

rulingClass.zh: 以曹氏宗室、谯沛集团旧臣、北方士族与受朝廷任用的经学官僚为统治核心
rulingClass.en: The ruling elite consisted of the Cao imperial clan, veteran followers from the Qiao-Pei group, northern gentry lineages, and classical scholar-officials employed by the court

succession.zh: 皇位原则上实行父死子继与嫡长继承，但仍受宫廷政治、宗室力量与重臣支持影响
succession.en: The throne was in principle inherited patrilineally, ideally by the legitimate eldest son, though court politics, imperial kinsmen, and support from senior ministers strongly affected succession

structure.zh: 魏初政权沿用汉制，以皇帝为最高权威，下设尚书台处理政务，中书与门下开始加强机要与诏令职能，三公与九卿保留为高层官僚架构的重要组成。
structure.en: The early Wei state largely continued Han institutions, with the emperor as supreme authority. The Department of State Affairs managed administration, while the Secretariat and Chancellery increasingly handled confidential policy and edicts; the Three Excellencies and Nine Ministers remained important elements of the upper bureaucracy.

function.zh: 总理日常政务、奏章审核、官员任命与中央行政执行
function.en: Managed routine administration, memorial review, appointments, and execution of central policy

function.zh: 起草诏令、参与机密决策并传达皇帝意志
function.en: Drafted edicts, participated in confidential decision-making, and conveyed the emperor’s will

function.zh: 审议诏令、谏议得失并充当皇帝近侧审核机构
function.en: Reviewed edicts, offered remonstrance, and served as a close advisory reviewing body for the emperor

function.zh: 监察京畿、弹劾不法官员并维护首都周边秩序
function.en: Supervised the metropolitan region, impeached corrupt officials, and maintained order around the capital

function.zh: 统筹军事名义事务、军政协调与将领任命建议
function.en: Oversaw formal military affairs, coordinated military administration, and advised on appointments of commanders

name.en: Office of the Minister over the Masses

function.zh: 掌户籍、地方治理协调与民政事务
function.en: Handled household registers, coordination of local administration, and civil governance

name.en: Office of the Minister of Works

function.zh: 负责工程、仓储、道路水利与部分屯田事务
function.en: Oversaw public works, granaries, roads, water control, and some agricultural colony affairs

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政基本沿州、郡、县三级体系运行。边地与新附地区常由重将兼领军政，重要州郡由朝廷直接任命刺史、太守与县令，以加强对北方与中原的控制。
localAdmin.en: Local administration functioned primarily through the provincial, commandery, and county hierarchy. Frontier and newly secured regions were often governed by powerful military officials with both civil and military authority, while key provinces and commanderies were staffed directly by court-appointed inspectors, governors, and magistrates.

legalSystem.zh: 法律制度以汉律为基础，并结合魏初新政进行修订，重视名例、户籍、军法与反叛罪，刑罚严峻但讲求成文法与官署审断程序。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on Han law but revised under the early Wei regime, emphasizing general statutes, household registration, military law, and treason. Punishments were severe, yet the state relied on written codes and formal bureaucratic adjudication.

taxationSystem.zh: 主要税收来自田租、户调、算赋、徭役折纳与官营收入。屯田制度为国家提供稳定粮赋，朝廷也通过盐铁、仓储与军需征发间接汲取资源。
taxationSystem.en: Major revenues came from land tax, household levies, poll and assessed taxes, commuted labor service, and state-managed income. The tuntian agricultural colony system supplied reliable grain revenue, while the court also drew resources indirectly through salt and iron controls, granary management, and military requisitions.

religion.zh: 以祖先祭祀、国家礼制、儒家礼仪和各类民间信仰为主，道教思想与方术活动活跃，佛教已传入北方但仍属有限传播阶段。
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor worship, state ritual, Confucian ceremonial practice, and various popular cults. Daoist ideas and occult techniques were active, while Buddhism had entered northern China but remained in an early and limited stage of spread.

philosophy.zh: 官方意识形态仍以儒学为正统，但法家式行政实践与名法观念深刻影响治国；同时清谈与玄学的思想土壤正在形成。
philosophy.en: Confucianism remained the official orthodoxy, but Legalist-style administrative practice and ideas of law and order strongly shaped governance; at the same time, the intellectual background for later qingtan and xuanxue discourse was beginning to form.

writingSystem.zh: 使用汉字，官方文书以隶书、章程化书写为主，楷书趋于成熟，碑刻、诏令、律令和经籍抄写广泛存在。
writingSystem.en: The civilization used Chinese characters. Official documents were mainly written in clerical script and standardized administrative hands, while regular script was maturing; inscriptions, edicts, legal texts, and manuscript copies of classics were widespread.

culturalAchievements.zh: 建安文学余风未绝，曹氏父子与文士集团推动诗赋发展；乐府传统延续，史学、经学整理和礼制重建也颇受重视。
culturalAchievements.en: The legacy of Jian’an literature remained strong, with the Cao family and associated literary circles advancing poetry and rhapsody. The yuefu tradition continued, and considerable attention was also given to historiography, classical scholarship, and restoration of ritual order.

languageFamily.zh: 汉藏语系中的古代汉语
languageFamily.en: Old Chinese within the Sino-Tibetan language family

gdpDescription.zh: 魏国在220年控制中原与华北最富庶农区之一，农业恢复速度快于竞争对手，人口、耕地、仓储与交通条件均优于蜀汉和孙吴，因此总经济规模为三国之首。
gdpDescription.en: In 220 CE, Wei controlled the Central Plain and much of North China, including some of the richest agricultural zones. Recovery in farming outpaced that of its rivals, and its population, cultivated land, granary network, and transport advantages exceeded those of Shu Han and Sun Wu, making it the largest economy among the Three Kingdoms.

mainIndustries.zh: 粟麦农业、屯田、养马、冶铁、制盐、纺织、漆器、兵器制造与官营仓储运输
mainIndustries.en: Millet and wheat agriculture, tuntian farming, horse breeding, ironworking, salt production, textiles, lacquerware, arms manufacture, and state granary transport

tradeGoods.zh: 粮食、丝织品、铁器、盐、马匹、漆器、铜器、皮革及少量输入的西域奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, silk textiles, iron goods, salt, horses, lacquerware, bronze items, leather, and limited imported luxuries from the Western Regions

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富以土地使用权、耕牛、农具、粮仓和家庭劳动力为主；豪族则拥有大庄园、部曲、佃客与更多金属器物、车马和丝帛储备。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth consisted mainly of land-use holdings, oxen, tools, grain stores, and family labor; elite lineages possessed large estates, retainers, tenant followers, and greater stocks of metal goods, vehicles, horses, and silk.

unit.en: shi of grain equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以农业为基础、国家强力干预的混合经济。国家通过户籍、田租、屯田、军需征发和关键资源控制主导经济命脉，市场交换在城市与交通节点仍然活跃。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on agriculture and strong state intervention. The state dominated core resources through household registration, land tax, tuntian colonies, military requisitioning, and control over key sectors, while market exchange remained active in cities and transport hubs.

source.en: State salt, iron, and workshop income
Transit taxes, market dues, and miscellaneous levies

treasuryDescription.zh: 魏国国库以粮储为核心，辅以钱币、丝帛、金属与战略物资。由于长期战争，储备虽较竞争者充足，但仍需不断维持粮道、仓廪与前线补给。
treasuryDescription.en: The Wei treasury was centered on grain reserves, supplemented by coin, silk, metal stocks, and strategic materials. Because of prolonged warfare, reserves were stronger than those of its rivals but still had to sustain supply lines, granaries, and frontier provisioning.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在恢复农业、扩大屯田、稳定户籍与压缩浪费，以确保对蜀、吴作战和对北方边境防御的持续供给。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized agricultural recovery, expansion of tuntian colonies, stabilization of household registers, and reduction of waste in order to sustain operations against Shu and Wu and maintain northern frontier defense.

description.zh: 军队主体，由长矛兵、刀盾兵、弩手与守城部队组成，负责中原驻防、攻城与边境作战。
description.en: The main body of the army, composed of spearmen, sword-and-shield troops, crossbowmen, and garrison infantry, responsible for Central Plain defense, sieges, and frontier warfare.

description.zh: 依托北方产马地与边郡传统建立的精锐机动力量，尤其适合对抗北方游牧与西北边境威胁。
description.en: An elite mobile force built on northern horse-producing regions and frontier traditions, especially suited to counter northern nomadic and northwestern frontier threats.

description.zh: 主要部署于黄河、淮河及南方前线，用于运输、渡河作战与对吴防御，但整体不如南方政权成熟。
description.en: Mainly deployed on the Yellow and Huai river systems and along the southern front for transport, river crossings, and defense against Wu, though overall less developed than southern naval forces.

description.zh: 驻守要塞、仓城和屯田区，兼具军屯生产、警戒巡防和地方镇压功能。
description.en: Stationed in fortresses, granary strongholds, and agricultural colonies, combining military farming, surveillance patrols, and local pacification duties.

technology.zh: 武器以铁制刀、矛、戟、甲胄与强弩为主，攻城技术、壁垒工事和军粮运输体系成熟；骑兵战术与大型编制指挥能力在三国中最为均衡。
technology.en: Weapons centered on iron swords, spears, halberds, armor, and powerful crossbows. Siegecraft, fortifications, and military supply systems were mature; cavalry tactics and command of large formations were among the most balanced in the Three Kingdoms.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自西南的蜀汉、东南的孙吴，以及北方与西北方向的鲜卑等草原势力与边境不稳因素。
threats.en: The principal threats came from Shu Han in the southwest, Sun Wu in the southeast, and from Xianbei and other steppe forces as well as instability along the northern and northwestern frontiers.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

description.zh: 重骑兵标志性武器，冲击力极强
description.en: Signature heavy cavalry weapon with tremendous charge impact

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于黄河中下游、河南、河北、关中东部与山东西部等北方核心区。经过东汉末年战乱，人口较西汉与东汉盛时明显下降，但在曹操、曹丕时期已呈恢复趋势。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated chiefly in the middle and lower Yellow River basin, Henan, Hebei, eastern Guanzhong, and western Shandong. After the devastation of the late Eastern Han, numbers were well below peak Western and Eastern Han levels, but under Cao Cao and Cao Pi they were recovering.

description.zh: 东汉首都，丝绸之路东端
description.en: Eastern Han capital, eastern terminus of the Silk Road

description.zh: 汉唐帝国首都，丝绸之路起点
description.en: Han-Tang capital, starting point of the Silk Road

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括皇室宗亲、士族官僚、地方豪强、编户齐民、佃农、工匠、商人、军户、部曲与奴婢。社会流动仍存在，但越来越受门第、军功与官僚网络影响。
socialClasses.en: Major social strata included the imperial clan, gentry officials, regional magnates, registered commoners, tenant farmers, artisans, merchants, military households, retainers, and slaves. Social mobility still existed, but was increasingly shaped by pedigree, military merit, and bureaucratic networks.

allies.zh: 220年时魏国并无稳定意义上的对等盟友，更多依赖册封、羁縻与边族互市关系来缓冲边疆压力。
allies.en: In 220 CE Wei had no stable equal ally in the formal sense, relying more on investiture, loose frontier arrangements, and exchange relations with border peoples to reduce pressure on its frontiers.

enemies.zh: 蜀汉与孙吴是直接战略对手；部分北方游牧与半游牧集团如鲜卑也构成潜在敌对力量。
enemies.en: Shu Han and Sun Wu were its direct strategic rivals; some northern nomadic and semi-nomadic groups such as the Xianbei also posed potential hostility.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交方针以“奉汉禅代、正统自居”为核心，对内巩固北方控制，对外优先压制蜀吴，同时以边疆安抚、册封和军事威慑维持西北与北方秩序。与更远方如西域、帕提亚及罗马的联系多为间接贸易与传闻层面的认知。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on claiming legitimate succession from the Han. Externally, Wei prioritized consolidating the north and containing Shu and Wu, while maintaining order in the northwest and north through appeasement, investiture, and military deterrence. Contacts with more distant powers such as the Western Regions, Parthia, and Rome were mostly indirect, mediated through trade and secondhand knowledge.

era.zh: 三国时代早期铁器国家文明
era.en: Early Three Kingdoms iron-age state civilization

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的强弩与军械制造、系统化屯田、仓储与漕运管理、筑城与攻城工程、较完善的户籍文书行政，以及趋于规范的楷隶书写体系。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included mature heavy crossbows and arms manufacture, systematic tuntian agriculture, granary and transport management, advanced fortification and siege engineering, relatively sophisticated household-registration administration, and increasingly standardized clerical and regular script writing.

strengths.zh: 魏国拥有三国中最大的人口基础、最广阔的核心农区、较强的官僚与军需体系，以及能够同时应对南北战线的综合实力。
strengths.en: Wei possessed the largest population base among the Three Kingdoms, the broadest core agricultural lands, stronger bureaucratic and logistical systems, and the overall capacity to handle both northern and southern fronts.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于政权新立、合法性仍需巩固，地方豪强与士族势力日增，长期战争消耗财政与民力，且南方水战能力相对不足。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in the newness of the regime and the need to consolidate legitimacy, the growing power of regional magnates and gentry lineages, the fiscal and demographic burden of prolonged warfare, and comparatively weaker naval capabilities in the south.

outlook.zh: 在220年前后，魏国前景总体强劲，短期内最有条件保持优势并主导天下局势；但若不能平衡皇权、宗室、重臣与士族关系，其内部权力结构未来可能成为比外敌更严重的隐患。
outlook.en: Around 220 CE, Wei’s outlook was broadly strong, and in the short term it was best positioned to retain superiority and dominate the balance of power. However, if it could not balance imperial authority, the imperial clan, senior ministers, and gentry interests, its internal power structure could become a more serious danger than foreign enemies.

description.zh: 曹丕于220年受禅称帝，控制华北与中原，成为三国中最强政权。
description.en: Cao Pi took the throne in 220 and controlled North China and the Central Plain, making Wei the strongest of the Three Kingdoms.

ideology.zh: 以法家治术与儒家名教相结合，通过九品中正制整合士族、强化中央集权、稳固曹氏皇权
ideology.en: Blending Legalist governance with Confucian moral hierarchy, consolidating centralized power through the Nine Ranks system to integrate the gentry and secure Cao dynastic rule

relationship.zh: 曹丕继承曹操基业后受禅称帝，建立曹魏。他通过九品中正制拉拢北方士族，同时以宗室权力制衡重臣，致力于将曹操时期的军阀集团转化为稳定的帝制国家。曹氏宗族是魏国权力架构的绝对核心。
relationship.en: After inheriting Cao Cao's legacy and receiving the Han abdication, Cao Pi established Cao Wei. He co-opted northern gentry through the Nine Ranks system while counterbalancing senior ministers with clan authority, striving to transform Cao Cao's warlord coalition into a stable imperial state. The Cao clan was the absolute center of Wei's power structure.

description.zh: 曹魏皇权的建立经历了从曹操的军阀霸业到曹丕的禅代帝制两个阶段。曹操一世以丞相名义挟天子令诸侯，奠定了曹氏在华北的绝对统治。曹丕于220年迫汉献帝禅位，正式建立魏朝，定都洛阳。为巩固政权，曹丕推行九品中正制，将选官权从地方太守收归中央，以品评人物的方式重新整合北方士族。同时，他削减宗王权力，防止诸曹诸夏侯宗亲形成地方割据，维持中央集权。曹氏集团的核心人物多出自谯沛旧部与汝颍士族，构成了魏国朝堂的执政骨干。
description.en: The establishment of Cao Wei imperial power went through two stages: Cao Cao's warlord hegemony and Cao Pi's formal dynastic founding through abdication. Cao Cao used the chancellorship to command the realm in the emperor's name, establishing absolute Cao dominance in North China. Cao Pi forced Emperor Xian's abdication in 220 and formally established the Wei dynasty with its capital at Luoyang. To consolidate the regime, Cao Pi implemented the Nine Ranks system, transferring official selection from local administrators to the center and reintegrating northern gentry through a system of character evaluation. He simultaneously reduced princely powers to prevent Cao and Xiahou clansmen from forming regional power bases, maintaining centralization. The Cao faction's core members came largely from the Qiao-Pei veteran network and the Ru-Ying gentry, forming the governing backbone of the Wei court.

role.zh: 开国皇帝，推行禅代与九品中正制
role.en: Founding emperor, implemented abdication transition and the Nine Ranks system

role.zh: 曹氏宗族军事核心，西线统帅
role.en: Military core of the Cao clan, commander of the western front

role.zh: 宗室军事骨干，负责东线对吴防御
role.en: Key clan military leader, responsible for the eastern front against Wu

leaderTitle.en: Vice President of the Imperial Secretariat

ideology.zh: 耐心积累军政权力，以士族利益代言者姿态结交北方名门，伺机取代曹氏皇权
ideology.en: Patient accumulation of military and political power, positioning as advocate for gentry interests to cultivate northern elite networks, awaiting the opportunity to supplant Cao dynastic rule

relationship.zh: 司马懿在曹操时代即被征辟入仕，但曹操深忌其「鹰视狼顾」之相。曹丕登基后对司马懿颇为信任倚重，但司马氏暗中编织的士族关系网络已为日后篡权埋下伏笔。司马家与曹氏之间的权力角逐将最终决定魏国命运。
relationship.en: Sima Yi was recruited into service during Cao Cao's era, though Cao Cao deeply distrusted his 'hawkish gaze and wolfish bearing.' After Cao Pi's enthronement, Sima Yi gained considerable trust and reliance, but the gentry network the Sima family was quietly weaving had already laid the groundwork for their eventual usurpation. The power struggle between the Sima and Cao houses would ultimately determine Wei's fate.

description.zh: 司马懿出身河内世族，学养深厚、城府极深，是曹魏政坛最具远见和耐心的政治家。曹操在世时即注意到司马懿的才干与野心，但碍于用人之需未加诛除。曹丕即位后，司马懿升任尚书仆射，参与核心决策。他在朝中广结士族、经营人脉，在军中通过参谋建言积累军事资本。司马懿的策略是长期渗透而非急于表态——在曹氏宗室势力强盛时保持低姿态，等待时机。事实证明，曹丕、曹叡先后早逝后，年幼嗣君无力驾驭大臣，司马懿终于在249年发动高平陵政变，一举夺取曹魏军政实权。其孙司马炎更在265年代魏建晋，完成了司马家族数十年隐忍布局的最终目标。
description.en: Sima Yi came from the prestigious Henei gentry, profoundly learned and deeply calculating—the most far-sighted and patient statesman on the Cao Wei political stage. Cao Cao recognized Sima Yi's talent and ambition but could not afford to eliminate him given the need for capable men. After Cao Pi's enthronement, Sima Yi rose to Vice President of the Imperial Secretariat and participated in core decision-making. He extensively cultivated gentry connections at court and accumulated military capital through advisory contributions. His strategy was long-term infiltration rather than premature assertion—maintaining a low profile while Cao clan power remained strong, waiting for the right moment. As history proved, after the successive early deaths of Cao Pi and Cao Rui left child heirs unable to control senior ministers, Sima Yi finally launched the Gaoping Tomb Incident in 249, seizing military-political power at a single stroke. His grandson Sima Yan completed the family's decades-long patient strategy by replacing Wei with Jin in 265.

title.en: Vice President of the Imperial Secretariat

role.zh: 司马氏家族领袖，暗中积蓄夺权资本
role.en: Head of the Sima clan, covertly accumulating the capital for seizure of power

role.zh: 司马懿长子，未来将继承并扩大司马氏权力
role.en: Sima Yi's eldest son, destined to inherit and expand Sima power

role.zh: 司马懿次子，日后掌控曹魏朝政
role.en: Sima Yi's second son, who would later dominate the Cao Wei court

ideology.zh: 致力于制度化建设与士族融合，以九品中正制为核心工具，推动曹魏从军事集团向文官帝国转型
ideology.en: Dedicated to institutional reform and gentry integration, using the Nine Ranks system as the central tool to transform Cao Wei from a military coalition into a civilian-bureaucratic empire

relationship.zh: 陈群等忠臣建制派是曹魏政权的制度设计师，与曹氏皇族保持忠诚合作。他们通过九品中正制等制度创新为魏国提供了稳定的选官与治理框架，是皇权与士族之间的关键纽带。
relationship.en: Loyal retainers like Chen Qun were the institutional architects of the Cao Wei regime, maintaining loyal cooperation with the Cao imperial house. Through innovations like the Nine Ranks system, they provided a stable framework for official selection and governance, serving as the crucial link between imperial power and the gentry.

description.zh: 以陈群为代表的忠臣建制派是曹魏政权成功从军阀割据过渡为帝国体制的关键力量。陈群出身颍川名族，是九品中正制的设计者与实际推行者。这套制度以中央任命的「中正官」品评各地人物才德，再据品级授官，既满足了士族参政的期望，又加强了中央对选官的控制。陈群、华歆、王朗等人构成了曹魏朝堂的文官核心，他们忠于曹氏皇权，但也代表着北方士族阶层的整体利益。他们的制度建设使魏国在三国之中行政效率最高，但九品中正制的长期运行也导致了「上品无寒门、下品无世族」的门阀垄断，最终为司马氏以士族代言人身份夺权提供了社会基础。
description.en: The loyal retainer faction represented by Chen Qun was the critical force enabling Cao Wei's successful transition from warlord regime to imperial institution. Chen Qun came from the renowned Yingchuan gentry and was the architect and implementer of the Nine Ranks system. This system used centrally appointed 'rectifiers' to evaluate local figures' talent and virtue, assigning official positions based on rank—satisfying gentry expectations for political participation while strengthening central control over appointments. Chen Qun, Hua Xin, Wang Lang, and others formed the civilian core of the Wei court, loyal to Cao imperial power but also representing the collective interests of the northern gentry class. Their institutional innovations gave Wei the highest administrative efficiency among the Three Kingdoms, but the long-term operation of the Nine Ranks system also led to aristocratic monopoly—'no poor men in the upper ranks, no great families in the lower'—ultimately providing the social foundation for the Sima family to seize power as self-proclaimed champions of the gentry.

role.zh: 九品中正制设计者，曹魏制度建设核心人物
role.en: Architect of the Nine Ranks system, core figure in Cao Wei institutional development

role.zh: 元老重臣，参与禅代大典并维护新朝秩序
role.en: Senior minister involved in the abdication ceremony and maintaining new dynastic order

role.zh: 经学名臣，负责礼制与法律建设
role.en: Classical scholar-official overseeing ritual and legal construction

governmentForm.zh: 君主制帝国，保留元首制外观但实际为高度军事化的专制皇权
governmentForm.en: Monarchical empire retaining the outward forms of the Principate but in practice a highly militarized autocracy

socialStructure.zh: 社会按法律身份与财富等级划分，包括元老阶层、骑士阶层、市民、自治城镇精英、军人、自由民与奴隶；各行省地方贵族逐渐融入帝国统治体系。
socialStructure.en: Society was divided by legal status and wealth into senators, equestrians, citizens, municipal elites, soldiers, freedmen, and slaves; provincial aristocracies were increasingly integrated into the imperial order.

rulingClass.zh: 皇帝、宫廷近臣、元老院贵族、骑士官僚、高级军官与行省总督
rulingClass.en: The emperor, palace intimates, senatorial aristocracy, equestrian administrators, senior military officers, and provincial governors

succession.zh: 无严格成文继承法，通常依靠皇帝指定、军队支持、王朝血统与元老院承认相结合
succession.en: There was no fixed written law of succession; succession usually depended on imperial designation, military backing, dynastic bloodline, and senatorial recognition

structure.zh: 中央政府以皇帝为核心，辅以宫廷官署、元老院、骑士阶层财政与行政机构，以及覆盖各行省的总督和城市自治体系。
structure.en: The central government was centered on the emperor, supported by palace bureaus, the Senate, equestrian fiscal and administrative offices, and a provincial and municipal system extending across the empire.

function.zh: 处理皇帝诏令、宫廷事务、请愿、机密通信与近卫安全
function.en: Handled imperial edicts, palace business, petitions, confidential correspondence, and guard security

function.zh: 管理皇室金库、税收征解、矿山、铸币与皇产
function.en: Managed the imperial treasury, tax collection, mines, minting, and imperial estates

function.zh: 负责地方司法、治安、税收监督与军政协调
function.en: Oversaw local justice, order, tax supervision, and civil-military coordination

function.zh: 处理礼仪性政务、部分行政咨询、公共工程与档案记录
function.en: Handled ceremonial state business, some administrative consultation, public works, and archival record keeping

function.zh: 保障罗马城及部分大城市的粮食、运输、仓储与市场供应
function.en: Ensured grain supply, transport, storage, and market provisioning for Rome and some major cities

localAdmin.zh: 帝国分为元老院行省与皇帝行省，地方依托行省总督、财务官、驻军指挥官及自治城镇议会治理；城市精英承担大量税务、治安与公共建设责任。
localAdmin.en: The empire was divided into senatorial and imperial provinces, governed locally through governors, fiscal procurators, military commanders, and self-governing city councils; municipal elites carried much of the burden of taxation, policing, and public construction.

legalSystem.zh: 以罗马法为核心，包含元老院决议、皇帝敕令、法学家解释和地方习惯法；220年时《安东尼努斯敕令》后大多数自由居民已获公民权，法律适用范围显著扩大。
legalSystem.en: Roman law formed the core of the legal system, including senatorial decrees, imperial rescripts, juristic interpretation, and local customary law; by 220, after the Constitutio Antoniniana, most free inhabitants had Roman citizenship, greatly expanding the scope of common legal application.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制包括土地税、人头税、关税、遗产税、矿业与皇产收入，以及为军队和交通系统提供的实物征发；行省税负因地区与法地位而异。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation included land tax, poll tax, customs duties, inheritance taxes, mining and estate revenues, and requisitions in kind for the army and transport system; provincial burdens varied by region and legal status.

religion.zh: 传统罗马多神信仰仍居主导，崇拜朱庇特、马尔斯、密涅瓦等神祇，并广泛吸收希腊、埃及、叙利亚和波斯诸神崇拜；皇帝崇拜在各行省仍具政治意义，基督教虽增长但尚属少数。
religion.en: Traditional Roman polytheism remained dominant, centered on deities such as Jupiter, Mars, and Minerva, while widely incorporating Greek, Egyptian, Syrian, and Persian cults; the imperial cult still had political importance in the provinces, and Christianity was growing but remained a minority faith.

philosophy.zh: 斯多亚主义、柏拉图主义、亚里士多德传统与修辞教育并存，法学、伦理学与希腊化哲学深刻影响帝国精英文化。
philosophy.en: Stoicism, Platonism, Aristotelian traditions, and rhetorical education coexisted, while jurisprudence, ethics, and Hellenistic philosophy deeply shaped elite imperial culture.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用拉丁字母，东部行省广泛使用希腊字母书写希腊语；官方文书、法律、铭文与商业账簿双语并行的情况常见。
writingSystem.en: The Latin alphabet was the principal writing system, while Greek script was widely used in the eastern provinces for Greek; bilingual usage in official documents, laws, inscriptions, and commercial accounts was common.

culturalAchievements.zh: 帝国在道路、桥梁、渡槽、浴场、圆形剧场、法学、军事工程与城市规划方面成就卓著；文学传统承继维吉尔、塔西佗等人，医学与地理知识也在希腊—罗马世界持续发展。
culturalAchievements.en: The empire excelled in roads, bridges, aqueducts, baths, amphitheaters, jurisprudence, military engineering, and urban planning; literary traditions inherited the legacy of Virgil and Tacitus, while medical and geographical knowledge continued to develop across the Greco-Roman world.

languageFamily.zh: 主要为印欧语系，西部以拉丁语为主，东部以希腊语为主要通用语，同时并存科普特语、阿拉姆语、柏柏尔语、凯尔特语等地方语言。
languageFamily.en: The empire was primarily Indo-European in linguistic character, with Latin dominant in the west and Greek the principal lingua franca in the east, alongside regional languages such as Coptic, Aramaic, Berber, and Celtic tongues.

gdpDescription.zh: 220年的罗马帝国拥有古典时代最庞大且最复杂的整合型经济之一，地中海航运、道路网络、货币体系与统一法律秩序使其市场联系远超同时代多数国家，但总体人均产出仍以农业社会水准为主。
gdpDescription.en: In 220, the Roman Empire possessed one of the largest and most complex integrated economies of the classical world; Mediterranean shipping, road networks, monetary circulation, and a unified legal order linked markets on a scale beyond most contemporaries, though per capita output still remained at an agrarian premodern level.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、橄榄油、葡萄酒、畜牧、陶器、纺织、采矿、冶金、石材、玻璃制造、建筑和军需生产
mainIndustries.en: Grain farming, olive oil, wine, pastoralism, pottery, textiles, mining, metallurgy, stoneworking, glassmaking, construction, and military supply production

tradeGoods.zh: 粮食、橄榄油、葡萄酒、陶器、金属制品、玻璃器、奴隶、莎草纸、香料、丝绸、宝石、象牙和奢侈纺织品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, olive oil, wine, pottery, metal goods, glassware, slaves, papyrus, spices, silk, gemstones, ivory, and luxury textiles

name.zh: 第纳尔与奥里乌斯体系

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距极大；少数元老与骑士家族拥有巨大庄园、现金和债权，大多数自由农、佃农与城市平民仅有有限土地、工具和牲畜，奴隶则通常不具独立财产。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal; a small number of senatorial and equestrian families held vast estates, liquid capital, and claims, while most free peasants, tenants, and urban commoners possessed only limited land, tools, and livestock, and slaves generally held no independent property.

economicSystem.zh: 以市场交换与庄园农业并存的税收型帝国经济；国家通过税收、军需采购、公共工程、铸币和粮食供应深度介入，但私人商业和地方市场仍是流通主体。
economicSystem.en: It was a tax-driven imperial economy combining market exchange with estate agriculture; the state intervened deeply through taxation, military procurement, public works, minting, and grain supply, but private trade and local markets remained the main engines of circulation.

source.zh: 遗产税、释放奴隶税及各类间接税
source.en: Inheritance tax, manumission tax, and miscellaneous indirect taxes

source.zh: 附庸贡献、罚没与特别收入

category.zh: 公共工程、道路与城市供应
category.en: Public works, roads, and urban supply

category.zh: 外交赠与、庆典与非常支出
category.en: Diplomatic gifts, ceremonies, and extraordinary expenses

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模可观但并不宽裕；卡拉卡拉提高军饷并依赖更重税负与币值调整，使财政表面稳定而内在压力上升。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was substantial but not abundant; Caracalla’s higher military pay and reliance on heavier taxation and currency adjustment made finances appear stable while underlying pressures increased.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策优先保障军队与皇权忠诚，通过扩大税基、加强皇室财政控制、没收财产和贬低银币成色来维持支出。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized the army and loyalty to the emperor, sustaining expenditure through a broader tax base, tighter imperial control of finances, confiscations, and debasement of the silver coinage.

description.zh: 由罗马公民构成的重装主力，驻守莱茵、多瑙、东方与不列颠边境，兼具野战与筑城能力。
description.en: The core heavy infantry force of Roman citizens, stationed on the Rhine, Danube, eastern front, and in Britain, capable of both field operations and fortification work.

description.zh: 来自行省的非军团部队，包括步兵、弓箭手、骑兵与特种边疆单位，是帝国机动与地方防务的重要支柱。
description.en: Provincial non-legionary units including infantry, archers, cavalry, and specialist frontier troops, forming a major pillar of imperial mobility and local defense.

description.zh: 负责皇帝安全、首都治安与关键政治军事任务，政治影响力远超人数比例。
description.en: Responsible for imperial security, order in the capital, and critical political-military tasks, with influence far exceeding their numbers.

description.zh: 驻于地中海、黑海、莱茵和尼罗河流域，负责运输、巡逻、护航与河海联合作战。
description.en: Stationed in the Mediterranean, Black Sea, Rhine, and Nile regions, responsible for transport, patrol, escort, and river-sea operations.

name.zh: 地方民兵与边防补充兵

description.zh: 由边境定居者、城市武装和临时征集者组成，用于补充守备与战时支援。
description.en: Composed of frontier settlers, civic militias, and temporary levies, used to reinforce garrisons and support wartime operations.

technology.zh: 军队装备包括锁子甲、鳞甲、铁盔、复合弓、投枪、长矛、攻城器械和完善的筑营技术；后勤依靠道路、仓储和河海运输网络，组织水平居当世前列。
technology.en: The army used mail, scale armor, iron helmets, composite bows, javelins, spears, siege engines, and highly developed camp-building methods; logistics relied on roads, depots, and river-sea transport networks, giving Rome one of the world’s most advanced military organizations.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自帕提亚边境战争、亚美尼亚与美索不达米亚局势、莱茵与多瑙河沿线的日耳曼人与萨尔马提亚人袭扰，以及内部军队拥立与宫廷政变风险。
threats.en: Major threats came from war on the Parthian frontier, instability in Armenia and Mesopotamia, raids by Germanic and Sarmatian peoples along the Rhine and Danube, and the internal danger of military usurpation and palace coups.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 可连发十矢的机械弩，守城利器
description.en: Mechanical crossbow firing ten bolts in succession, excellent for defense

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

description.zh: 单兵弩与床弩，中国军队核心远程武器
description.en: Infantry crossbow and mounted crossbow, core ranged weapon

populationDescription.zh: 人口约五千万，分布于从不列颠到埃及、从伊比利亚到叙利亚的广阔领土；人口最稠密地区为埃及、意大利、北非沿海、小亚细亚和叙利亚诸城邦带。
populationDescription.en: The population was about fifty million, spread across vast territories from Britain to Egypt and from Iberia to Syria; the densest regions included Egypt, Italy, the North African coast, Asia Minor, and the urban belts of Syria.

description.zh: 永恒之城，罗马帝国首都
description.en: Eternal City, capital of the Roman Empire

description.zh: 古代学术中心与地中海大港
description.en: Ancient center of learning and great Mediterranean port

description.zh: 古代叙利亚大都市，早期基督教中心
description.en: Great Syrian metropolis, early Christian center

description.zh: 北非强大贸易帝国的首都
description.en: Capital of a powerful North African trading empire

description.zh: 古代小亚细亚名城，阿尔忒弥斯神庙所在地
description.en: Famed city of Asia Minor, home of Temple of Artemis

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括皇室与元老贵族、骑士与市政精英、军官与军人、自由农与佃农、城市工匠与商人、自由民以及规模庞大的奴隶人口。
socialClasses.en: Social classes included the imperial household and senatorial nobility, equestrians and municipal elites, officers and soldiers, free peasants and tenants, urban artisans and merchants, freedmen, and a large slave population.

allies.zh: 形式上的盟友与附庸包括部分亚美尼亚派系、若干阿拉伯与东方边境客户国，以及受罗马保护的地方王侯与城市共同体。
allies.en: Formal allies and clients included certain Armenian factions, several Arab and eastern frontier client states, and local dynasts and civic communities under Roman protection.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对力量为帕提亚帝国；同时需防范日耳曼诸部、萨尔马提亚人与阿兰人，边境上对哈特拉、奥斯罗恩及其他缓冲政权的关系也时常紧张多变。
enemies.en: The principal hostile power was the Parthian Empire; Rome also had to guard against Germanic groups, Sarmatians, and Alans, while relations with Hatra, Osroene, and other buffer polities were often tense and fluid.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维护边疆纵深、控制附庸国、保障贸易路线和通过军事威慑维持霸权为核心；在东方采取直接干预与扶植缓冲政权并用的策略，在北方则以防线、驻军和惩罚性远征相结合。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on maintaining strategic frontiers, controlling client states, securing trade routes, and preserving hegemony through military deterrence; in the east Rome combined direct intervention with the support of buffer kingdoms, while in the north it relied on frontier lines, garrisons, and punitive expeditions.

era.zh: 古典晚期前夕的高罗马帝国技术阶段
era.en: High Imperial Roman technological stage on the eve of Late Antiquity

keyInnovations.zh: 拱券与混凝土建筑、渡槽与城市供水、标准化道路工程、先进攻城器械、玻璃制造、矿冶技术、法律文书制度和覆盖帝国的邮驿交通网络。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included arch and concrete construction, aqueducts and urban water systems, standardized road engineering, advanced siege machinery, glass production, mining and metallurgical methods, documentary legal administration, and an empire-wide courier and transport network.

strengths.zh: 帝国拥有广阔人口与税基、成熟法制、强大道路与海运网络、训练有素的职业军队以及高度城市化的精英治理体系，仍是地中海世界无可匹敌的超级强权。
strengths.en: The empire possessed a vast population and tax base, mature legal institutions, powerful road and maritime networks, a well-trained professional army, and an elite urban governing system, remaining the unrivaled superpower of the Mediterranean world.

weaknesses.zh: 财政日益依赖军费优先、币值贬损和重税；皇位继承缺乏稳定机制，军队政治化严重；地区差异、瘟疫余波和边疆多线压力正在侵蚀长期稳定。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included growing dependence on military-first spending, currency debasement, and heavy taxation; the imperial succession lacked a stable mechanism, the army was deeply politicized, and regional disparities, the aftereffects of plague, and multi-front frontier pressure were eroding long-term stability.

outlook.zh: 220年的罗马帝国表面仍然稳定且强盛，但已显露即将进入三世纪危机的结构性征兆；若皇权更替失序或东方、北方战线同时恶化，帝国将面临严重财政、军事与政治震荡。
outlook.en: In 220 the Roman Empire still appeared stable and powerful, but it already showed structural signs of the coming Third Century Crisis; if succession faltered or the eastern and northern fronts deteriorated at the same time, the empire would face severe fiscal, military, and political upheaval.

description.zh: 罗马帝国横跨地中海世界，在220年仍维持统一，但已接近三世纪危机。
description.en: The Roman Empire still spanned the Mediterranean in 220, remaining unified but nearing the Third Century Crisis.

ideology.zh: 塞维鲁王朝通过军事力量与王朝血统维系统治，近卫军的政治影响力持续膨胀，宫廷决策深受太后与近臣左右
ideology.en: The Severan dynasty maintained rule through military power and dynastic bloodline, with the Praetorian Guard's political influence ever expanding and court decisions heavily swayed by empress mothers and palace intimates

relationship.zh: 塞维鲁朝廷在卡拉卡拉（217年遇刺）之后陷入混乱，马克里努斯短暂篡位后，埃拉伽巴路斯（218-222年在位）借叙利亚军团复辟塞维鲁血统。宫廷政治高度依赖近卫军忠诚与皇太后尤利娅·梅萨的幕后操纵。
relationship.en: The Severan court fell into turmoil after Caracalla's assassination in 217. Following Macrinus's brief usurpation, Elagabalus (r. 218-222) restored the Severan line with Syrian legionary backing. Court politics depended heavily on Praetorian loyalty and the behind-the-scenes manipulation of empress grandmother Julia Maesa.

description.zh: 塞维鲁王朝由塞普蒂米乌斯·塞维鲁建立（193年），以军事力量为统治根基。该王朝通过提高军饷、扩大军团特权和削弱元老院权力来巩固皇权。到220年前后，王朝已进入末期——卡拉卡拉遇刺后经历了短暂的马克里努斯篡位，随后尤利娅·梅萨成功推举年仅14岁的外孙埃拉伽巴路斯登基。埃拉伽巴路斯引入叙利亚太阳神崇拜，其放荡行为严重激怒了罗马传统势力。亚历山大·塞维鲁在222年取而代之，试图恢复秩序，但军队对皇权的控制已成定局。塞维鲁王朝的衰亡预示着三世纪危机中军阀称帝、政权频繁更迭的混乱局面。
description.en: The Severan dynasty was founded by Septimius Severus in 193, with military power as its governing foundation. The dynasty consolidated imperial authority by raising military pay, expanding legionary privileges, and weakening the Senate. By around 220, the dynasty was in its twilight—after Caracalla's assassination and Macrinus's brief usurpation, Julia Maesa successfully enthroned her 14-year-old grandson Elagabalus. His introduction of Syrian sun worship and scandalous behavior gravely offended Roman traditionalists. Alexander Severus replaced him in 222 and attempted to restore order, but military control over the throne had become entrenched. The decline of the Severan dynasty foreshadowed the chaos of the Third Century Crisis, marked by soldier-emperors and rapid regime changes.

role.zh: 塞维鲁末期皇帝，引入叙利亚太阳神崇拜
role.en: Late Severan emperor who introduced Syrian sun worship

role.zh: 太后级幕后操纵者，维系塞维鲁血统延续
role.en: Matriarch and behind-the-scenes power broker maintaining Severan dynastic continuity

role.zh: 被培养为继任者，将于222年继位
role.en: Groomed as successor, to take the throne in 222

ideology.zh: 捍卫元老院传统权威，反对军人干政与东方宗教渗透，主张恢复元首制的文官治理秩序
ideology.en: Defending traditional senatorial authority, opposing military interference in politics and eastern religious infiltration, advocating restoration of civilian Principate governance

relationship.zh: 元老院贵族在塞维鲁王朝时期权力持续萎缩。塞普蒂米乌斯·塞维鲁公开将军队作为统治支柱，卡拉卡拉进一步边缘化元老院。元老贵族对埃拉伽巴路斯的宗教狂热与失德行为深感不满，暗中支持以亚历山大·塞维鲁取而代之。
relationship.en: Senatorial aristocratic power continued to shrink under the Severans. Septimius Severus openly made the army his governing pillar, and Caracalla further marginalized the Senate. The senatorial class deeply resented Elagabalus's religious fanaticism and scandalous behavior, covertly supporting the replacement with Alexander Severus.

description.zh: 罗马元老院贵族曾是共和时代的统治阶级，在帝制建立后虽然实权逐步萎缩，但仍保持着巨大的社会声望与经济影响力。到塞维鲁时代，元老院已基本沦为礼仪机构，重要军政决策均由皇帝及其近臣把持。然而，元老贵族仍然控制着帝国最大的私人地产，在行省治理中拥有不可忽视的行政经验，其文化与法律传统也是帝国政治合法性的重要组成部分。卡修斯·狄奥作为这一时期最重要的元老议员兼史学家，其著作深刻记录了元老阶层对军人皇帝崛起的忧虑与愤怒。元老院贵族虽然无法阻止军权对皇位的垄断，但他们在后续的三世纪危机中多次尝试通过扶植「元老皇帝」来恢复文官秩序。
description.en: The Roman senatorial aristocracy had been the ruling class of the Republic and, though their real power gradually diminished after the establishment of the Empire, they retained enormous social prestige and economic influence. By the Severan era, the Senate had become largely ceremonial, with major military and political decisions controlled by the emperor and his inner circle. Yet senators still controlled the empire's largest private estates, held irreplaceable administrative experience in provincial governance, and their cultural and legal traditions remained vital to imperial political legitimacy. Cassius Dio, the most important senator-historian of this period, profoundly documented the senatorial class's anxiety and anger over the rise of military emperors. Though unable to prevent the army's monopoly on the throne, senatorial aristocrats repeatedly attempted to restore civilian order by backing 'senatorial emperors' during the subsequent Third Century Crisis.

role.zh: 著名史学家，记录塞维鲁时代衰落
role.en: Celebrated historian documenting the Severan-era decline

role.zh: 杰出法学家，试图以法治约束军权
role.en: Eminent jurist who attempted to constrain military power through law

ideology.zh: 以军事实力为政治资本，边疆将领日益独立于中央，军功与士兵效忠成为皇位合法性的主要来源
ideology.en: Military power as political capital, frontier generals increasingly independent from the center, with military merit and soldier loyalty becoming the primary sources of imperial legitimacy

relationship.zh: 边疆军事将领是帝国防御体系的支柱，但也是皇位更迭的最大不稳定因素。塞维鲁王朝本身即由边疆将领夺权而建立，这一先例使得每位军团统帅都成为潜在的皇位觊觎者。
relationship.en: Frontier military commanders were the pillars of the imperial defense system but also the greatest destabilizing factor in succession. The Severan dynasty itself was established through frontier generals seizing power, setting a precedent that made every legion commander a potential aspirant to the throne.

description.zh: 罗马帝国的边疆军事将领在3世纪初已成为帝国最具实力的政治力量。莱茵河、多瑙河、幼发拉底河沿线的军团驻地既是防御据点，也是独立于罗马中央的权力中心。塞普蒂米乌斯·塞维鲁本人便是潘诺尼亚军团的指挥官，通过内战夺取皇位。他的成功向后来者证明了一条路径：只要掌握足够多的军团忠诚，任何边疆将领都可能成为皇帝。到220年前后，边疆军团的忠诚已从「效忠罗马帝国」转向「效忠本军团指挥官」。这种趋势在即将到来的三世纪危机（235-284年）中达到极端——半个世纪内出现了超过25位皇帝，其中大多数由军队拥立并被军队杀害。
description.en: By the early 3rd century, Rome's frontier military commanders had become the empire's most powerful political force. Garrison centers along the Rhine, Danube, and Euphrates were not only defensive posts but power centers independent of Rome. Septimius Severus himself was a Pannonian legion commander who seized the throne through civil war. His success demonstrated a path: any frontier general with sufficient legionary loyalty could become emperor. By around 220, legion loyalty had shifted from 'allegiance to the Roman Empire' toward 'allegiance to one's own commander.' This trend reached its extreme during the coming Third Century Crisis (235-284), when over 25 emperors appeared in half a century, most elevated and killed by their own armies.

name.zh: 马克西米努斯·色雷克斯

role.zh: 未来将成为首位「兵营皇帝」（235年），标志三世纪危机开始
role.en: Future first 'barracks emperor' (235), marking the start of the Third Century Crisis

role.zh: 首都军事力量最高指挥官，废立皇帝的关键角色
role.en: Supreme commander of capital military forces, key role in making and unmaking emperors

governmentForm.zh: 贵族封建化的君主帝国，中央王权与强大地方诸侯并存
governmentForm.en: A noble-feudal monarchic empire in which central royal authority coexisted with powerful regional magnates

socialStructure.zh: 社会以王室、七大贵族家族与地方诸侯为上层，其下为骑士贵族、祭司、城市居民、商人、农民与依附民。帝国地域广阔，伊朗高原、两河流域与附庸地区在法律和习俗上差异明显。
socialStructure.en: Society was headed by the royal house, the great noble clans, and regional lords, followed by lesser cavalry aristocrats, priests, urban residents, merchants, peasants, and dependent laborers. The empire was highly regionalized, with substantial legal and customary differences between the Iranian plateau, Mesopotamia, and vassal territories.

rulingClass.zh: 阿尔萨息王室、苏伦家族、卡伦家族等大贵族，以及地方总督和附庸王公
rulingClass.en: The Arsacid royal house, great noble families such as the Suren and Karen, and provincial governors and vassal kings

succession.zh: 王位通常由阿尔萨息王族内部继承，但须得到主要贵族与军事实力支持，常引发宗室内战与地方拥立
succession.en: The throne normally passed within the Arsacid house, but effective succession depended on acceptance by major nobles and military backing, often producing dynastic civil wars and regional claimants

structure.zh: 帝国政体较为分散。国王在宫廷与直属领地内拥有较强权力，但大量行省、边地和附庸王国由世袭贵族、地方总督或臣属国王治理。中央依靠宫廷官署、税吏、王室使者与军事统帅维持统合。
structure.en: The imperial system was decentralized. The king exercised strong authority in the court and crown domains, but many provinces, frontier regions, and vassal kingdoms were governed by hereditary nobles, provincial governors, or subordinate kings. The center relied on court offices, tax officials, royal envoys, and military commanders to preserve cohesion.

function.zh: 处理诏令、朝觐、外交文书与王室档案
function.en: Handled royal decrees, audiences, diplomatic correspondence, and palace records

function.zh: 征收土地税、商业税、关税及附庸贡赋
function.en: Collected land taxes, commercial levies, customs duties, and tribute from vassals

function.zh: 协调王室直属军、贵族征召与边防守备
function.en: Coordinated royal troops, noble levies, and frontier defense

function.zh: 联络地方总督、附庸国王与王室使者网络
function.en: Maintained communication with provincial governors, vassal kings, and the royal envoy network

function.zh: 处理贵族争端、契约、公法与王室仲裁
function.en: Managed aristocratic disputes, contracts, public law, and royal arbitration

function.zh: 管理军粮、牲畜供给、道路补给与储备仓
function.en: Managed military grain, animal supply, road provisioning, and reserve storehouses

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政高度不均。两河地区由城市、税区和总督辖区构成，伊朗高原则更多依赖世袭贵族庄园与地方领主。亚美尼亚、阿迪亚贝尼、哈特拉等边缘地区常保持半自治或附庸地位。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was highly uneven. Mesopotamia was organized through cities, tax districts, and gubernatorial jurisdictions, while the Iranian plateau relied more heavily on hereditary noble estates and local lords. Border regions such as Armenia, Adiabene, and Hatra often retained semi-autonomous or vassal status.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系为王命、贵族习惯法、地方城市法与宗教传统的混合体。缺乏统一成文帝国法典，案件常按身份、地区与族群处理，贵族享有较大司法特权。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined royal decree, aristocratic customary law, local urban law, and religious tradition. There was no single unified imperial legal code, and cases were often handled according to status, region, and ethnicity, with nobles enjoying broad judicial privilege.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依靠农业地租、灌溉区税收、商路关税、市场税、附庸贡赋和战时征发。中央对地方税源控制有限，许多收入被贵族和地方统治者截留。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue depended chiefly on agricultural land dues, taxes from irrigated districts, trade-route customs, market tolls, vassal tribute, and wartime requisitions. Central control over local revenue was limited, and much income was retained by nobles and regional rulers.

religion.zh: 帝国宗教多元。伊朗地区以早期琐罗亚斯德传统、地方伊朗神祇崇拜和王室祭仪为主；两河流域并存巴比伦传统、希腊化神庙、犹太教与早期基督教社群。
religion.en: The empire was religiously plural. Iranian regions were shaped by early Zoroastrian traditions, local Iranian cults, and royal ritual, while Mesopotamia also contained Babylonian traditions, Hellenistic temples, Jewish communities, and early Christian groups.

philosophy.zh: 思想文化并非以系统哲学著称，而是融合伊朗王权观、希腊化城市文化、占星术、祭司传统与实用政治伦理。
philosophy.en: Its intellectual life was not defined by a single formal philosophical school, but by a blend of Iranian kingship ideology, Hellenistic urban culture, astrology, priestly traditions, and pragmatic political ethics.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用帕提亚语书写的阿拉米字母体系，同时两河城市与钱币、铭文中广泛使用希腊文；行政与地方文书亦见阿拉米文及其他地方文字。
writingSystem.en: The empire primarily used Aramaic-derived scripts for Parthian, while Greek remained widespread in Mesopotamian cities, coinage, and inscriptions; administrative and local records also employed Aramaic and other regional scripts.

culturalAchievements.zh: 安息在东西文化交流中居关键地位，发展了独特的骑射贵族文化、前厅与拱券建筑传统、混合希腊—伊朗艺术风格，并推动丝路贸易中的奢侈品流通与外交中介。
culturalAchievements.en: Parthia played a crucial role in east-west cultural exchange, developing a distinctive mounted-archer aristocratic culture, iwans and vaulted architectural forms, a hybrid Greco-Iranian artistic style, and acting as a key intermediary in Silk Road luxury trade and diplomacy.

languageFamily.zh: 官方与上层语言以西伊朗语支的帕提亚语为主，城市精英常用希腊语；帝国内并存阿拉米语、巴比伦语残余、亚美尼亚语及其他地方语言。
languageFamily.en: The dominant elite language was Parthian, a Western Iranian language, while urban elites often used Greek; Aramaic, residual Babylonian traditions, Armenian, and other local languages also coexisted across the empire.

gdpDescription.zh: 安息经济总量在古代世界居于前列，依托两河灌溉农业、伊朗高原畜牧和欧亚贸易中转。但220年时内战、贵族割据与萨珊崛起已明显削弱中央对财富的动员能力。
gdpDescription.en: Parthia remained one of the larger economies of the ancient world, resting on Mesopotamian irrigation agriculture, Iranian plateau pastoralism, and Eurasian transit trade. By 220 CE, however, civil conflict, noble fragmentation, and the Sasanian rise had clearly weakened the center’s capacity to mobilize wealth.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、谷物与椰枣生产、畜牧养马、地毯与纺织、金属加工、陶器、驿站运输、商队贸易与关税经营
mainIndustries.en: Irrigation agriculture, grain and date production, livestock and horse breeding, carpets and textiles, metalworking, pottery, caravan transport, and customs-based trade

tradeGoods.zh: 马匹、羊毛、地毯、皮革、椰枣、谷物、沥青、珍珠、金属器、香料转运品、丝绸、宝石、玻璃器与罗马奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Horses, wool, carpets, leather, dates, grain, bitumen, pearls, metal goods, transshipped spices, silk, gemstones, glassware, and Roman luxury goods

name.zh: 德拉克马与四德拉克马

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异极大。贵族控制大片土地、牧群和税权；城市商人拥有现金与跨区域信用；普通农户以土地使用权、牲畜、粮食储备和家内器具为主要资产。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was extremely unequal. Nobles controlled large estates, herds, and fiscal rights; urban merchants held liquid wealth and interregional credit; ordinary farming households relied mainly on land-use rights, animals, grain stores, and domestic tools.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业地租和贵族庄园为基础、辅以城市商业和长途商队贸易的混合经济；中央干预有限，地方权力与私人网络在资源配置中作用很大。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on agrarian rents and noble estates, supplemented by urban commerce and long-distance caravan trade; central intervention was limited, and local power structures and private networks played major roles in resource allocation.

source.en: Mint income, fines, and extraordinary levies

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库仍具一定规模，但受连年内战、对贵族让利和地方截留影响，远不如帝国强盛时期稳定。宫廷可动用的现金与贵金属储备有限，紧急时需依赖临时征发与盟友支持。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury still retained significant resources, but repeated civil wars, concessions to nobles, and local retention of revenue made it far less stable than in stronger imperial periods. Liquid reserves and precious metal under direct court control were limited, and emergencies often required extraordinary levies and allied support.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持军队、收买贵族忠诚和保全商路收入为核心，偏重短期征发而非制度化改革，这在衰退期加剧了地方离心。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining the army, purchasing noble loyalty, and preserving trade-route income, relying more on short-term extraction than institutional reform, which intensified regional centrifugal forces during decline.

description.zh: 由王室与大贵族供给的重装骑兵，是安息军队的核心突击力量，擅长平原决战与快速机动。
description.en: Supplied by the crown and great nobles, these heavily armored cavalry formed the army’s principal shock force, effective in open-field battle and rapid maneuver.

description.zh: 以伊朗高原与草原边地骑手为主，使用复合弓、机动骚扰和包抄战术，是安息传统优势兵种。
description.en: Drawn largely from Iranian plateau and steppe-frontier riders, they used composite bows, mobility, harassment, and encirclement tactics, forming Parthia’s classic arm.

description.zh: 主要负责城防、驻屯、护送和围城支援，战斗质量不如骑兵，成分复杂，包括地方征发与佣兵。
description.en: These troops handled city defense, garrison duty, escort work, and siege support; they were generally less effective than the cavalry and included local levies and mercenaries.

description.zh: 来自附庸王国、阿拉伯边地和地方部族，适合边境作战与补充兵力，但忠诚度和持续性有限。
description.en: Raised from vassal kingdoms, Arab frontier groups, and local tribes, they were useful for frontier warfare and reinforcement but had limited reliability and endurance.

commanderInChief.zh: 阿尔达班四世，战时由王室亲族与大贵族统帅分军
commanderInChief.en: Artabanus IV, with field armies led by royal kinsmen and great nobles in wartime

technology.zh: 军事技术以复合弓骑射、鳞甲与札甲重骑、长枪冲击、机动作战和沙漠补给经验见长；攻城技术逊于罗马，器械多依赖地方工匠与临时集结。
technology.en: Military technology excelled in composite-bow horse archery, scale and lamellar armored cavalry, lance charges, mobile warfare, and desert logistics; siege capability lagged behind Rome and often depended on local craftsmen and ad hoc assembly.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自内部贵族叛离和法尔斯地区萨珊势力崛起；西方仍须防备罗马帝国干涉亚美尼亚与美索不达米亚，东北边地则面临草原与部族压力。
threats.en: The chief threats came from internal noble defections and the rise of Sasanian power in Persis; in the west, Rome remained a danger through intervention in Armenia and Mesopotamia, while northeastern frontiers faced steppe and tribal pressures.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 重骑兵标志性武器，冲击力极强
description.en: Signature heavy cavalry weapon with tremendous charge impact

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

description.zh: 单兵弩与床弩，中国军队核心远程武器
description.en: Infantry crossbow and mounted crossbow, core ranged weapon

populationDescription.zh: 人口约八百万，主要集中于两河流域灌溉区、米底与伊朗西部高原、以及若干绿洲和商路城市。战争与地方割据使部分边区人口流动加剧。
populationDescription.en: The population was about eight million, concentrated mainly in the irrigated zones of Mesopotamia, Media, the western Iranian plateau, and a series of oasis and trade-route cities. Warfare and regional fragmentation increased population movement in some frontier areas.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、七大贵族家族、地方诸侯与高级祭司；中间层包括城市地主、商人、手工业者和低级军功贵族；底层为农民、佃户、牧民、劳役民和奴隶。帝国不同地区在奴役形式与依附关系上差异较大。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of the royal house, major noble families, regional lords, and senior priests; the middle ranks included urban landholders, merchants, artisans, and lesser military nobility; the lower strata were peasants, tenants, pastoralists, labor dependents, and slaves. Forms of servitude and dependency varied considerably across the empire.

allies.zh: 名义上的盟友与附庸包括部分亚美尼亚派系、阿迪亚贝尼、哈特拉及若干阿拉伯边地势力，但其忠诚多受局势左右。
allies.en: Nominal allies and dependents included some Armenian factions, Adiabene, Hatra, and several Arab frontier powers, though their loyalty was highly contingent on circumstances.

enemies.zh: 主要对手为罗马帝国，以及国内新兴的萨珊势力；在边境上也常与不稳定的部族集团和叛离诸侯对抗。
enemies.en: Its principal adversaries were the Roman Empire and the rising Sasanian power within Iran; on the borders it also contended with unstable tribal groupings and rebellious magnates.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以维持亚美尼亚缓冲地位、保护两河与伊朗贸易线、平衡罗马压力并安抚东方附庸为核心。220年时外交更趋防御和应急，重心转向抑制内部解体。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on preserving Armenia as a buffer, protecting Mesopotamian and Iranian trade lines, balancing Roman pressure, and managing eastern dependents. By 220 CE it had become increasingly defensive and reactive, with priority shifting toward preventing internal disintegration.

era.zh: 古典时代晚期
era.en: Late Classical Age

keyInnovations.zh: 复合弓骑战体系、重装具装骑兵、拱券与前厅式建筑、改良灌溉与运河利用、长途商队站网络，以及结合希腊化与伊朗传统的铸币与宫廷艺术
keyInnovations.en: Composite-bow mounted warfare, armored cataphract cavalry, vaulted and iwan-style architecture, improved irrigation and canal use, long-distance caravan station networks, and coinage and court art blending Hellenistic and Iranian traditions

strengths.zh: 地处欧亚贸易枢纽，拥有富庶的两河农业区、强大的骑兵传统、深厚的贵族军事资源和多元文化整合能力。
strengths.en: It occupied a strategic Eurasian trade crossroads, possessed the wealthy agricultural base of Mesopotamia, a formidable cavalry tradition, deep aristocratic military resources, and strong capacity for multicultural integration.

weaknesses.zh: 中央集权薄弱，王位继承不稳，贵族割据严重，附庸忠诚易变，财政征收能力下降，难以在罗马与萨珊双重压力下维持统一。
weaknesses.en: Centralization was weak, succession unstable, noble fragmentation severe, vassal loyalty unreliable, and fiscal extraction declining, making it difficult to maintain unity under simultaneous Roman and Sasanian pressure.

outlook.zh: 前景黯淡。若无法压制法尔斯的萨珊势力并重建对贵族和税源的控制，安息帝国将在数年内失去核心统治地位，并被更具组织力的新伊朗王朝取代。
outlook.en: The outlook was bleak. Unless it could suppress the Sasanian power in Persis and rebuild control over nobles and revenues, the Parthian Empire was likely to lose its core authority within a few years and be replaced by a more organized new Iranian dynasty.

description.zh: 安息帝国仍统治伊朗高原与两河流域，但内部贵族割据，萨珊势力正在崛起。
description.en: Parthia still ruled Iran and Mesopotamia, but noble fragmentation weakened the empire as the Sasanians were rising.

governmentForm.zh: 由孙氏宗族主导的世袭君主制，保留汉代官僚制度并结合地方军政统治。
governmentForm.en: A hereditary monarchy dominated by the Sun clan, retaining Han-style bureaucracy while combining it with regional military-administrative rule.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由宗室与将门、士族官僚、郡县豪强、自由农民、屯田军户、工匠、商人及少量奴婢构成。江东本地豪族与北来士人共同塑造统治秩序。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal clan and military families, gentry officials, local magnates, free peasants, military-agricultural colonists, artisans, merchants, and a smaller number of slaves and bonded dependents. Native Jiangdong elites and émigré northern literati together shaped the ruling order.

rulingClass.zh: 孙氏宗室、追随孙策与孙权起家的将领集团，以及顾、陆、朱、张等江东士族。
rulingClass.en: The Sun royal clan, the generals who rose with Sun Ce and Sun Quan, and leading Jiangdong gentry lineages such as the Gu, Lu, Zhu, and Zhang.

succession.zh: 原则上由孙氏家族父死子继或宗室近支承继，但在220年仍高度依赖君主指定与重臣支持，制度尚未完全稳定。
succession.en: In principle succession passed within the Sun family by father-to-son inheritance or to a close agnatic relative, but in 220 it still depended heavily on the ruler’s designation and the backing of senior ministers, so the system was not yet fully stabilized.

structure.zh: 吴国在形式上沿袭东汉州郡县体系，中央设尚书类决策机构、文武属官与中军幕府；地方则以扬州、荆州所辖郡县为基础，由太守、都尉、县令县长治理，并在边防与要地由将军兼领军政。
structure.en: Wu formally continued the Eastern Han provincial-commandery-county system. The center used a secretariat-style decision apparatus, civil and military officials, and the commander’s staff. Locally, administration rested on the commanderies and counties of Yang and Jing Provinces, governed by administrators, military commandants, and county magistrates, while frontier and strategic regions were often managed by generals holding both military and civil authority.

function.zh: 处理诏令、奏章、官员任命与中央政务协调，是君主决策的核心文书机构。
function.en: Handled edicts, memorials, appointments, and coordination of central administration; it was the core documentary organ of royal decision-making.

function.zh: 统筹都督诸军、京口与江防兵力调动、军令传达及战时部署。
function.en: Coordinated the regional commands, troop movements in the lower Yangtze defense zone, transmission of orders, and wartime deployment.

function.zh: 负责田租、户调、军粮、仓储、漕运与财政核算。
function.en: Managed land tax, household levies, military grain, granaries, river transport, and fiscal accounting.

function.zh: 审理重大案件，执行汉律，管理刑罚与狱政。
function.en: Tried major cases, enforced Han law, and managed punishments and prisons.

function.zh: 经营屯田、水利、造船、兵器和官营工场。
function.en: Oversaw agricultural colonies, irrigation, shipbuilding, weapons production, and state workshops.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依州、郡、县三级展开，但战时色彩浓厚。江东腹地郡县多由文官与地方士族合作治理，荆州前线与山越地区则常由将领兼治。公安、武昌、建业等战略节点拥有更强军事管制。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through the province-commandery-county hierarchy, but with a strong wartime character. In the Jiangdong core, commanderies and counties were commonly governed through cooperation between civil officials and local gentry, while the Jing frontier and Shanyue zones were often ruled directly by military commanders. Strategic nodes such as Gong'an, Wuchang, and Jianye had heavier military oversight.

legalSystem.zh: 法律基础仍为汉律及令式，强调户籍、赋役、军法和叛乱惩治。实际司法常受地方豪强影响，但中央努力通过廷尉和刺史纠察维持秩序。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was still based on Han law and ordinances, with strong emphasis on household registration, taxation and labor service, military discipline, and punishment of rebellion. In practice, justice was often influenced by local magnates, but the center sought to maintain order through judicial offices and provincial inspectors.

taxationSystem.zh: 赋税以田租、户调和徭役为主，并辅以盐铁、山泽、关津和官营手工业收入。战时广泛依赖屯田、军粮征发及对新附人口的编户。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied primarily on land tax, household levies, and corvée labor, supplemented by revenue from salt and iron, mountains and marshes, transit points, and state workshops. In wartime the state depended heavily on military-agricultural colonies, grain requisitions, and registration of newly submitted populations.

religion.zh: 以祖先崇拜、国家祭祀、天地山川之礼和民间神灵信仰为主，道教早期形态正在扩展，佛教已传入但影响仍有限。
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor worship, state ritual, sacrifices to Heaven, Earth, mountains, and rivers, and popular cults. Early Daoist forms were expanding, while Buddhism had entered the region but remained limited in influence.

philosophy.zh: 官方思想仍以汉代经学化儒家为核心，重视忠、孝、名分与治国秩序；同时实际政治高度务实，兼采法家行政手段与兵家谋略。
philosophy.en: Official thought remained centered on Han-style Confucian classicism, valuing loyalty, filiality, hierarchy, and orderly government; at the same time, practical politics was highly pragmatic, borrowing administrative methods from Legalism and strategic thinking from military traditions.

writingSystem.zh: 使用汉字，公文多用隶书向楷化过渡的书写形式，简牍与帛书并行，官方档案制度较为发达。
writingSystem.en: The writing system was Chinese characters. Official documents commonly used late clerical scripts transitioning toward regularized forms; bamboo slips and silk manuscripts were both in use, and record-keeping was relatively well developed.

culturalAchievements.zh: 吴地在水军、造船、江海交通、地方史地知识和音乐舞乐方面表现突出；江东士族文化逐渐成熟，并吸纳北方流亡士人传统，为后来的建业文化中心奠基。
culturalAchievements.en: Wu was notable for naval arts, shipbuilding, river and maritime communications, regional geographic knowledge, and musical performance traditions. Jiangdong gentry culture was maturing and absorbing traditions brought by northern émigré elites, laying foundations for the later cultural prominence of Jianye.

languageFamily.zh: 汉藏语系汉语族；官方与书面语言为文言汉语，地方口语属中古早期江东汉语，并夹杂部分越地底层语言成分。
languageFamily.en: Sino-Tibetan, Sinitic branch; the official and written language was Literary Chinese, while local speech belonged to early medieval Jiangdong varieties of Chinese with some substrate elements from older Yue languages.

unit.en: shi grain equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 吴国经济在220年处于东亚最有活力的区域之一。江南稻作、渔盐、桑麻、木材、铜铁、造船与沿江贸易支撑了繁荣。与中原相比，其人口密度较低，但资源开发潜力高，战乱破坏相对较轻，因此总体呈扩张态势。
gdpDescription.en: In 220, Wu possessed one of the most dynamic regional economies in East Asia. Southern rice agriculture, fishing and salt, silk and hemp, timber, copper and iron, shipbuilding, and Yangtze trade supported prosperity. Compared with the Central Plains, population density was lower, but resource potential was high and wartime devastation comparatively lighter, so the overall economy was expanding.

mainIndustries.zh: 水稻农业、渔业、盐业、桑蚕与纺织、木材采伐、造船、铜铁冶炼、漆器、陶器、军需制造。
mainIndustries.en: Rice agriculture, fishing, salt production, sericulture and textiles, timber extraction, shipbuilding, copper and iron metallurgy, lacquerware, pottery, and military manufacturing.

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、丝织品、麻布、盐、鱼干、木材、漆器、铜器、铁器、船只、药材、珍珠与南方山海特产。
tradeGoods.en: Rice, silk textiles, hemp cloth, salt, dried fish, timber, lacquerware, bronze goods, iron tools, ships, medicinal materials, pearls, and specialty products from southern mountains and coasts.

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要表现为土地使用权、稻谷存量、农具、家畜和宗族网络；江东豪族则拥有大片田庄、佃客、舟船、部曲和仓储，财富差距显著。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was mainly expressed in land-use rights, grain stocks, tools, livestock, and lineage connections; Jiangdong magnates possessed large estates, tenants, boats, private retainers, and granaries, creating a marked wealth gap.

unit.en: shi grain equivalent per household per year

economicSystem.zh: 以小农农业和地方豪族庄园为基础，辅以国家屯田、官营盐铁工场和军事后勤体系的混合经济。市场交换活跃，但国家对战略资源和运输节点保持较强控制。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on peasant farming and gentry estates, supplemented by state agricultural colonies, government salt and iron workshops, and a military logistics system. Market exchange was active, but the state maintained strong control over strategic resources and transport nodes.

source.en: Household levies and commuted labor dues
State agricultural colonies and granary operations
Transit dues, mountain-marsh revenues, and miscellaneous taxes

category.en: Navy, shipbuilding, and river defense works

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库存量以粮食、布帛、铜钱和军需物资为主，而非纯金银。吴国财政相对稳健，受益于江南生产增长与较完整的河运体系，但荆州前线和持续备战使储备难以迅速累积。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury holdings consisted mainly of grain, textiles, copper cash, and military stores rather than bullion alone. Wu’s finances were relatively sound, benefiting from southern production growth and a fairly intact river transport system, but the Jing frontier and constant military preparedness limited rapid reserve accumulation.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向稳健扩张：保护农业基础，发展屯田和漕运，确保军粮优先，同时通过笼络士族与安置北方流民扩大税源。对战略物资采取较强国家干预。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored cautious expansion: protecting the agricultural base, developing state colonies and grain transport, prioritizing military provisioning, and enlarging the tax base through accommodation of local gentry and settlement of northern refugees. Strategic materials were subject to strong state intervention.

description.zh: 吴军最具特色的主力，依托长江与江南水网，负责江防、运输、两栖作战和火攻战术，配备大量战船与熟练舟师。
description.en: Wu’s most distinctive core force, based on the Yangtze and southern waterways, responsible for river defense, transport, amphibious operations, and fire tactics, equipped with numerous warships and trained mariners.

description.zh: 由郡县兵、屯田兵和将领部曲构成，是守城、山地作战和前线主战力量，武器包括矛、戟、弩、刀盾。
description.en: Composed of commandery troops, colony soldiers, and generals’ retainers; it formed the main force for garrison duty, mountain warfare, and frontline combat, using spears, halberds, crossbows, sabers, and shields.

description.zh: 骑兵规模较魏为小，主要用于侦察、传令、追击和荆州平原地带机动，马匹资源相对不足。
description.en: Smaller in scale than Wei cavalry, it was used mainly for scouting, communications, pursuit, and mobility in the plains of Jing Province; horse resources were comparatively limited.

description.zh: 包括招抚后的山越兵和地方守备力量，熟悉丘陵森林环境，适用于治安、边地控制和轻装作战。
description.en: Included pacified Shanyue troops and local garrison elements, familiar with hilly and forested terrain and useful for policing, frontier control, and light warfare.

technology.zh: 军事技术承袭东汉晚期水平，强项在弩、甲胄、战船、江河运输和筑城守御。吴军尤其擅长水战、夜袭、火攻与依托地形机动。
technology.en: Military technology followed late Eastern Han standards, with strengths in crossbows, armor, warships, river transport, and fortified defense. Wu forces were especially proficient in naval warfare, night attacks, fire tactics, and maneuver based on terrain.

threats.zh: 首要威胁为北方曹魏在长江上游与淮南方向的压力；次要威胁包括荆州争夺、山越反复叛服以及内部将领与地方豪族势力过强带来的统治风险。
threats.en: The primary threat was pressure from Cao Wei in the upper Yangtze and Huainan regions. Secondary threats included the contest over Jing Province, recurring unrest and submission among the Shanyue, and the political risk posed by overly powerful generals and local magnate families.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气尚可，常规水平
description.en: Adequate morale, average cohesion

description.zh: 单兵弩与床弩，中国军队核心远程武器
description.en: Infantry crossbow and mounted crossbow, core ranged weapon

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 水战拍竿，击毁敌船上层结构
description.en: Ship-mounted swinging beam to smash enemy superstructures

description.zh: 覆盖全身的重装骑兵铁甲
description.en: Full-body heavy cavalry iron barding

populationDescription.zh: 吴国人口约230万，主要集中于长江中下游、江东平原、太湖流域及荆州东部。相较中原，人口仍显稀疏，但因北方战乱带来的流民南迁和持续开发，人口处于增长阶段。
populationDescription.en: Wu’s population was about 2.3 million, concentrated in the middle and lower Yangtze, the Jiangdong plain, the Taihu basin, and eastern Jing Province. Compared with the Central Plains, settlement was still relatively sparse, but population was growing due to southward migration from the northern wars and ongoing frontier development.

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括王室与宗室、将领与士族官僚、地方豪强与大地主、自耕农与佃农、屯田军户、工匠、商贾、舟人渔民，以及编附中的山越与其他边缘群体。
socialClasses.en: The main social strata included the royal house and princely kin, generals and gentry officials, local magnates and great landholders, owner-peasants and tenant farmers, military colonist households, artisans, merchants, boatmen and fishers, and incorporated Shanyue and other marginal groups.

allies.zh: 名义上与蜀汉维持反魏联盟倾向，虽在220年关系并非完全稳固；并通过招抚岭南与南方部族、保持海上接触来扩大影响。
allies.en: Nominally, Wu leaned toward an anti-Wei alignment with Shu Han, though relations in 220 were not yet fully stable; it also expanded influence by conciliating Lingnan and southern tribal groups and by maintaining maritime contacts.

enemies.zh: 最大敌手是曹魏；在荆州问题上对蜀汉亦存潜在竞争与不信任。边境山越叛乱势力也是长期安全威胁。
enemies.en: Its principal enemy was Cao Wei; over Jing Province it also harbored latent rivalry and mistrust toward Shu Han. Rebellious Shanyue groups on the borders were another long-term security threat.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以保卫长江防线、巩固江东与荆州既得地盘、伺机联蜀制魏为核心；对南方与海外则采取更灵活的羁縻和贸易并用政策，以获取资源、人口与战略纵深。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on defending the Yangtze line, consolidating holdings in Jiangdong and Jing Province, and opportunistically aligning with Shu against Wei. Toward the south and overseas regions, Wu employed a more flexible mix of loose suzerainty and trade to acquire resources, population, and strategic depth.

era.zh: 三国时代早期铁器与帆橹战船时代
era.en: Early Three Kingdoms age of iron technology and sail-oar warships

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟铁器农具与兵器、强弩体系、江河战船建造、屯田组织、水利灌溉、漕运调度和湿润南方环境下的稻作扩展技术。
keyInnovations.en: Mature iron agricultural tools and weapons, organized heavy crossbow use, river warship construction, agricultural-colony organization, irrigation works, grain-transport logistics, and techniques for expanding wet-rice cultivation in the humid south.

strengths.zh: 吴国拥有长江天险、强大水军、相对稳定的江南腹地、不断增长的人口与经济，以及能整合本地豪族和流亡士人的政治基础。其地理条件使其极难被快速征服。
strengths.en: Wu possessed the natural barrier of the Yangtze, a powerful navy, a relatively stable southern core, growing population and economy, and a political base capable of integrating native magnates and émigré elites. Its geography made it extremely difficult to conquer quickly.

weaknesses.zh: 骑兵薄弱，北伐能力受限；政权仍处建构阶段，地方豪族、将门与山越问题牵制中央；荆州前线漫长，且在魏蜀之间需要持续平衡外交。
weaknesses.en: Its cavalry was weak, limiting northern offensive capacity; the regime was still in a formative stage, with local magnates, military families, and the Shanyue problem constraining the center; the Jing frontier was long and exposed, and diplomacy had to be constantly balanced between Wei and Shu.

outlook.zh: 220年的吴国前景总体乐观。只要孙权能够稳定继承秩序、抑制内部离心，并继续发展江南经济与水军，吴将长期维持为与魏、蜀并立的大国，并有条件在未来正式建号称帝。
outlook.en: Wu’s outlook in 220 was broadly favorable. If Sun Quan could stabilize succession, restrain centrifugal forces, and continue developing the southern economy and navy, Wu would remain a major power alongside Wei and Shu for the long term and would be well positioned to proclaim an imperial title in the future.

description.zh: 孙权控制长江中下游与江东地区，虽尚未正式称帝，但已是独立强国。
description.en: Sun Quan controlled the middle and lower Yangtze and Jiangdong; though not yet emperor, Wu was already an independent great power.

ideology.zh: 务实外交与灵活战略，在魏蜀之间求取最大利益，巩固水军霸权与江东统治根基，平衡北方流亡士族与本地豪强关系
ideology.en: Pragmatic diplomacy and flexible strategy, maximizing advantage between Wei and Shu, consolidating naval supremacy and Jiangdong rule, balancing northern émigré elites with local magnate families

relationship.zh: 孙权以孙氏宗室身份继承孙策基业，凭借个人政治手腕与对文武百官的驾驭能力稳固统治。他在蜀魏之间维持灵活外交，既联蜀抗魏又保持独立行动自由，确保了吴国的生存与发展。
relationship.en: Sun Quan inherited Sun Ce's legacy as head of the Sun clan and consolidated power through personal political skill and masterful management of civil and military officials. He maintained flexible diplomacy between Shu and Wei, aligning with Shu against Wei while preserving independent freedom of action, ensuring Wu's survival and growth.

description.zh: 孙权继承兄长孙策的江东基业后，以卓越的政治平衡术将一个军阀割据势力发展为稳定的独立政权。他善于在曹操与刘备之间左右逢源，赤壁之战联蜀抗曹的战略决策奠定了三国鼎立格局。孙权重用周瑜、鲁肃、吕蒙等军事人才，同时积极拉拢江东本地世家大族参与治国。他的统治风格以务实为主，水军建设、南方开发、海外探索和灵活外交是其施政核心。220年时，孙权虽未称帝，但已实际独立于曹魏控制之外。
description.en: After inheriting Sun Ce's Jiangdong domain, Sun Quan transformed a warlord base into a stable independent polity through exceptional political balancing. He excelled at maneuvering between Cao Cao and Liu Bei; the strategic decision to ally with Shu at Red Cliffs established the Three Kingdoms equilibrium. Sun Quan employed military talents like Zhou Yu, Lu Su, and Lü Meng while actively integrating Jiangdong local great families into governance. His rule was fundamentally pragmatic, centered on naval construction, southern development, overseas exploration, and flexible diplomacy. By 220, though not yet emperor, Sun Quan was effectively independent of Cao Wei's control.

role.zh: 最高统治者，总揽军政外交全局
role.en: Supreme ruler overseeing all military, civil, and diplomatic affairs

role.zh: 夺取荆州的关键军事统帅
role.en: Key military commander in the seizure of Jingzhou

role.zh: 重要外交使臣与孙权亲信
role.en: Key diplomatic envoy and trusted confidant of Sun Quan

ideology.zh: 依托江东世家根基参与国政，注重地方利益与宗族权势，主张务实防御而非冒险北进
ideology.en: Participating in state governance through deep-rooted Jiangdong family networks, prioritizing local interests and clan power, favoring pragmatic defense over risky northern campaigns

relationship.zh: 江东四大族顾、陆、朱、张是吴国政权不可或缺的统治基础。他们掌握大量地方资源，在郡县行政中发挥关键作用，与孙氏宗族形成相互依存的合作关系，但也因此限制了中央集权的推进。
relationship.en: The four great Jiangdong families—Gu, Lu, Zhu, and Zhang—were an indispensable governing foundation of Wu. They controlled vast local resources and played critical roles in commandery administration, forming a mutually dependent relationship with the Sun clan that also constrained centralization.

description.zh: 江东士族在东汉末年已是长江以南最有势力的地方精英集团。孙策征服江东时对本地豪强采取了严厉手段，但孙权执政后转向笼络合作的策略。陆逊、顾雍、朱桓、张温等本地名门子弟逐步进入政权核心，在军事、行政与文化各领域发挥关键作用。陆逊尤为突出，他在夷陵之战（222年）中大破刘备，成为吴国最重要的军事统帅。江东士族的支持确保了吴国在南方的长期稳定，但他们对地方资源的控制也使得孙氏宗室在后期面临权力分散的隐患。
description.en: The Jiangdong gentry were the most powerful local elite group south of the Yangtze by the late Eastern Han. While Sun Ce used harsh methods in conquering Jiangdong, Sun Quan shifted to a strategy of co-optation. Scions of local great families—Lu Xun, Gu Yong, Zhu Huan, Zhang Wen—gradually entered the regime's core, playing key roles in military, administrative, and cultural spheres. Lu Xun was especially prominent; his crushing defeat of Liu Bei at the Battle of Yiling (222) made him Wu's most important military commander. The support of the Jiangdong gentry ensured Wu's long-term stability in the south, but their control over local resources also posed risks of power fragmentation for the Sun royal house in later years.

role.zh: 江东士族最杰出的军事与政治领袖
role.en: The most outstanding military and political leader of the Jiangdong gentry

role.zh: 吴国首席文官，以谦抑沉稳著称
role.en: Wu's chief civil official, known for modesty and steadiness

role.zh: 地方军事将领，朱氏家族代表
role.en: Local military commander and representative of the Zhu family

ideology.zh: 来自北方战乱的流亡士族，带来中原行政经验与文化传统，部分人主张归顺曹魏以求安定，部分人则积极融入吴政权
ideology.en: Émigré elites fleeing northern warfare, bringing Central Plain administrative experience and cultural traditions; some favored submission to Cao Wei for stability, while others actively integrated into the Wu regime

relationship.zh: 北方流亡士人在文化素养与行政经验上具有明显优势，但他们缺乏地方根基，需要依附孙氏宗室获取权力。他们与江东本土士族之间长期存在利益冲突与文化隔阂，这种紧张关系贯穿吴国始终。
relationship.en: Northern émigrés held clear advantages in cultural accomplishment and administrative experience, but lacked local roots and needed to attach themselves to the Sun clan for power. Their long-standing conflicts of interest and cultural distance with the Jiangdong gentry persisted throughout Wu's history.

description.zh: 东汉末年北方长期战乱，大量中原士族与官僚南渡避难，张昭、诸葛瑾、步骘、严畯等人即为代表。他们为吴政权带来了中原的行政管理技术、经学传统与外交才能，在吴国建设中发挥了不可替代的作用。然而，张昭等人在赤壁之战前曾主张投降曹操，这使他们在政治上始终处于微妙位置。北方流亡士人与江东本地世家的关系十分复杂——双方在争夺官位、资源分配和政策取向上时常发生冲突。孙权善于利用两派之间的竞争来维持权力平衡，但这种内部张力也在一定程度上消耗了吴国的政治凝聚力。
description.en: The prolonged northern wars of the late Eastern Han drove many Central Plain gentry and officials to flee south. Zhang Zhao, Zhuge Jin, Bu Zhi, and Yan Jun were representative figures. They brought administrative techniques, classical scholarship, and diplomatic skills that played an irreplaceable role in Wu's state-building. However, the fact that Zhang Zhao and others had advocated surrender to Cao Cao before Red Cliffs left them in a perpetually delicate political position. Relations between northern émigrés and local Jiangdong families were complex—they frequently clashed over offices, resource allocation, and policy direction. Sun Quan skillfully exploited competition between the two groups to maintain his own balance of power, but this internal tension also eroded Wu's political cohesion.

role.zh: 北方流亡士人领袖，吴国元老重臣
role.en: Leader of the northern émigrés, senior statesman of Wu

role.zh: 南方拓殖与边疆治理的关键人物
role.en: Key figure in southern colonization and frontier governance

role.zh: 经学家与行政官员
role.en: Classical scholar and administrative official

governmentForm.zh: 以汉室复兴为名义的君主制藩国政权，实际为割据王国
governmentForm.en: A monarchic regional regime claiming Han restoration, in practice an autonomous rival kingdom

socialStructure.zh: 社会以皇室与宗室、益州与荆州士族官僚、地方豪强、军户、编户齐民、工商业者与少数民族部落共同构成，中央对盆地核心区控制较强，对边郡多依赖羁縻与军事驻防。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal Liu house, gentry-official elites from Yi and Jing provinces, local magnates, military households, registered commoners, artisans and merchants, and non-Han tribal communities. Central control was strong in the Sichuan Basin core but more dependent on indirect rule and garrisons in frontier commanderies.

rulingClass.zh: 刘备集团核心将领与谋臣、随刘备入蜀的荆州士人、原益州地方士族
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of Liu Bei’s core generals and advisers, Jing Province émigré elites who followed him into Shu, and the preexisting local gentry of Yi Province

succession.zh: 原则上为父子世袭，依托刘氏宗亲血统与群臣拥戴；220年时默认继承人为太子刘禅
succession.en: Succession was in principle hereditary from father to son, grounded in Liu clan legitimacy and ministerial support; in 220 the expected heir was Crown Prince Liu Shan

structure.zh: 政权沿用东汉州郡官制与王国体制，汉中王为最高统治者，下设尚书、军府与州郡县三级行政体系；政务与军务高度结合，诸葛亮、法正、许靖等分别在决策、法制与礼仪名望上发挥作用。
structure.en: The regime largely retained Eastern Han provincial-commandery institutions within a royal kingdom framework. The King of Hanzhong was supreme ruler, under whom operated secretariat offices, military headquarters, and a three-tier provincial-commandery-county administration. Civil and military affairs were closely intertwined, with figures such as Zhuge Liang, Fa Zheng, and Xu Jing influential in policy, law, and prestige.

function.zh: 处理文书、诏令起草、官员奏报与中央政务协调
function.en: Handled documents, drafted orders, processed official memorials, and coordinated central administration

function.zh: 负责战略规划、战役调度、边防部署与军政参议
function.en: Oversaw strategic planning, campaign coordination, frontier deployment, and military consultation

function.zh: 管理盆地核心郡县的户籍、赋税、治安、仓储与道路水利
function.en: Managed household registration, taxation, public order, granaries, roads, and irrigation in the basin core commanderies

function.zh: 负责屯田、粮秣征收、官仓储备与赈济调度
function.en: Managed state farms, grain collection, official granaries, and relief distribution

function.zh: 审理案件、执行刑律、复核重罪与整顿地方司法
function.en: Tried cases, enforced penal law, reviewed major crimes, and supervised local justice

function.zh: 管理宫廷供应、兵器作坊、漆器织造、盐铁与官营手工业
function.en: Managed court supply, armories, lacquer and textile production, and state-operated craft industries including salt and iron

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以州、郡、县为基本框架，成都平原与主要盆地郡县由中央直接委任太守、县令治理；汉中及南中等边区则更多依赖都督、太守、校尉与当地豪强、夷帅合作维持秩序。
localAdmin.en: Local administration followed the province-commandery-county framework. Core basin commanderies around Chengdu were governed directly by centrally appointed administrators and county magistrates, while frontier regions such as Hanzhong and the southern territories relied more on military governors, commandery officials, and cooperation with local magnates and tribal leaders.

legalSystem.zh: 法律基本承袭东汉律令，以儒家名教为正当性基础，辅以军法与战时处置。政权新立，法制尚在整合，重视惩治叛乱、盗贼、逃亡与军纪败坏。
legalSystem.en: The legal system largely inherited Eastern Han statutes and ordinances, with Confucian moral order as its ideological basis, supplemented by military law and wartime measures. As the regime was newly consolidated, legal institutions were still being integrated, with special emphasis on punishing rebellion, banditry, desertion, and breaches of discipline.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制沿东汉旧制，以田租、户调、徭役与军需征发为主；对新附地区和边郡征收较灵活，部分以实物、劳役或地方土产缴纳。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation followed late Han practice, centered on land tax, household levies, labor service, and military requisitioning. Collection in newly acquired and frontier regions was more flexible, with some obligations paid in kind, labor, or local products.

religion.zh: 国家层面尊奉汉室宗庙祭祀、天命与祖先崇拜，民间并行地方神祇、山川祭祀、巫觋信仰与早期道教传统；佛教已传入蜀地但影响仍有限。
religion.en: At the state level, religion centered on Han dynastic ancestral rites, Heaven’s mandate, and ancestor worship.
