foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on retaining the Kabul-Gandhara-Punjab core, preserving customs revenues from trade routes, balancing neighboring powers, and maintaining influence through gifts, marriage ties, religious patronage, and limited military force.

era.zh: 古典时代晚期，处于三国时代的同时期
era.en: Late Classical Age, contemporary with the Three Kingdoms Era

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与制度优势包括高质量金币铸造、复合型跨区域货币体系、犍陀罗雕塑工艺、成熟的驮运商路组织、多语文书管理，以及融合草原骑兵与南亚作战方式的军事传统。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations and strengths included high-quality gold coinage, a mature transregional monetary system, Gandharan sculptural craftsmanship, organized caravan-route logistics, multilingual documentary administration, and a military tradition blending steppe cavalry with South Asian warfare.

strengths.zh: 贵霜帝国的优势在于地处中亚与印度之间的交通枢纽、拥有成熟的铸币传统、文化包容性强、商业网络广泛，并在犍陀罗与旁遮普保有较稳固的核心地区。
strengths.en: The empire’s strengths lay in its position astride the routes between Central Asia and India, its mature coinage tradition, strong cultural inclusiveness, broad commercial networks, and relatively secure core territories in Gandhara and the Punjab.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于疆域收缩、中央控制减弱、地方萨特拉普与附庸自主性上升、财政出现赤字倾向，且同时面临西方强邻和印度地方政权的双重压力。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were territorial contraction, weakening central control, rising autonomy among satraps and dependent rulers, a drift toward fiscal deficit, and simultaneous pressure from western powers and Indian regional states.

outlook.zh: 220年前后，贵霜仍是区域性重要强国，但已明显进入衰退阶段。若无法重建对行省、军队和商路的有效控制，未来数十年很可能进一步分裂，并在伊朗新帝国与印度地方势力之间被持续蚕食。
outlook.en: Around 220 CE, the Kushan state remained an important regional power but was clearly in decline. Unless it could rebuild effective control over provinces, the army, and the trade routes, it was likely to fragment further over the coming decades and be steadily eroded between a new Iranian empire and Indian regional powers.

description.zh: 贵霜帝国仍控制犍陀罗、喀布尔与印度西北，但版图已较鼎盛期缩小。
description.en: The Kushan Empire still held Gandhara, Kabul, and northwestern India, though reduced from its height.

dynasty.zh: 安得罗伊克什瓦库王朝

governmentForm.zh: 以王权为核心、依靠地方贵族与宗教赞助维系的早期历史时期君主制
governmentForm.en: An early historic monarchy centered on royal authority, sustained through local elites and religious patronage

socialStructure.zh: 社会以王室、武士贵族、婆罗门祭司、城市商人、工匠、乡村农民与劳役群体构成；佛教僧团在都城及下克里希纳河谷具有重要社会影响力。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal house, martial nobility, Brahmin ritual elites, urban merchants, artisans, rural cultivators, and laboring groups; Buddhist monastic communities held major social influence in the capital and lower Krishna valley.

rulingClass.zh: 王室、军事贵族、地方首领与受赐地的婆罗门精英共同构成统治阶层
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the royal family, military nobles, local chiefs, and land-endowed Brahmin elites

succession.zh: 王位通常由王族男性成员世袭继承，并以宫廷支持、军事控制与宗教合法性加以确认
succession.en: The throne was generally inherited patrilineally within the royal house, with succession validated by court backing, military control, and religious legitimacy

structure.zh: 王国政府由国王统领，中央宫廷直接控制都城周边核心地区，外围则通过总督、封臣、部族首领与地方行政官间接统治。铭文记录、土地施赠和税收征集是政务运作的核心手段。
structure.en: The kingdom was headed by the king, with the central court directly controlling the core territory around the capital and ruling outer areas indirectly through governors, feudatories, clan chiefs, and local administrators. Inscriptions, land grants, and tax collection were central instruments of governance.

function.zh: 管理宫廷、王室财产、仪式、侍从与接见事务
function.en: Managed the court, royal estates, ceremonies, attendants, and audiences

function.zh: 征收地税、贡赋、关税并监督仓储与王室收入
function.en: Collected land tax, tribute, and customs duties while supervising storage and royal income

function.zh: 统筹驻军、征召、边防、要塞与将领任命
function.en: Oversaw garrisons, levies, frontier defense, forts, and the appointment of commanders

function.zh: 负责文书、土地施赠记录、王命发布及碑刻起草
function.en: Handled documents, records of land grants, royal orders, and inscription drafting

name.zh: 宗教施赠与公共工程署
name.en: Religious Endowments and Public Works Office

function.zh: 管理对佛教寺院与婆罗门的施赠，并维护灌溉、道路与建筑工程
function.en: Managed grants to Buddhist establishments and Brahmins and maintained irrigation, roads, and construction works

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托地区长官、城镇首领、村社长老与税吏。核心河谷地区控制较强，边缘地区则较多依赖被纳入王国体系的地方家族与附属首领。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on regional officers, town heads, village elders, and tax collectors. Control was stronger in the core river valley, while peripheral areas depended more on local lineages and subordinate chiefs integrated into the kingdom.

legalSystem.zh: 法律秩序结合王命、习惯法、婆罗门法理传统与地方调解机制。财产、土地施赠、婚姻和宗教特权常由铭文、见证人与社群认可共同确认。
legalSystem.en: The legal order combined royal decree, customary law, Brahmanical legal tradition, and local mediation. Property, land grants, marriage, and religious privileges were commonly validated through inscriptions, witnesses, and communal recognition.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以农业地租和实物征收为主，辅以手工业税、市场税、渡口税和贸易关税；部分地区还通过附属首领上贡形式向王室输送资源。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was based chiefly on agrarian land revenue and payment in kind, supplemented by craft taxes, market tolls, ferry dues, and trade customs; some areas also delivered resources to the court through tributary subordinate chiefs.

religion.zh: 王国宗教生活兼具婆罗门教与佛教特色。王室强调吠edic祭祀与王权合法性，同时王后和贵族妇女对佛教寺院、塔院与僧团给予重要支持。
religion.en: Religious life combined Brahmanical and Buddhist elements. The royal house emphasized Vedic sacrifice and royal legitimacy, while queens and elite women gave important patronage to Buddhist monasteries, stupas, and monastic communities.

philosophy.zh: 思想基础以达摩、王权秩序、功德施赠与因果观念为核心，兼受婆罗门仪礼传统和佛教伦理教化影响。
philosophy.en: Its intellectual foundations centered on dharma, royal order, meritorious donation, and karmic thought, shaped by both Brahmanical ritual tradition and Buddhist ethical teaching.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用婆罗米字母的南印度地方形式，行政与碑铭多见俗语化普拉克里特语，也逐渐出现梵语 prestige 的上升。
writingSystem.en: The kingdom mainly used southern regional forms of the Brahmi script; administration and inscriptions often employed vernacularized Prakrit, while Sanskrit prestige was gradually rising.

culturalAchievements.zh: 毗阇耶补罗及其周边佛教建筑、砖石塔院、雕刻栏楯与铭文施赠传统十分突出，反映了安得罗地区在后萨达婆诃那时代的建筑与宗教艺术繁荣。
culturalAchievements.en: Vijayapuri and its environs were notable for Buddhist monuments, brick-and-stone stupa complexes, carved railings, and epigraphic traditions of donation, reflecting the architectural and religious-artistic vitality of Andhra in the post-Satavahana age.

languageFamily.zh: 以中印度雅利安语支的普拉克里特行政书写传统为主，民间亦存在早期达罗毗荼语成分，尤其与泰卢固语前身相关
languageFamily.en: Administrative writing was dominated by Middle Indo-Aryan Prakrit traditions, while popular speech also included early Dravidian elements, especially those related to the ancestors of Telugu

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以灌溉农业为基础，辅以河谷贸易、手工业生产、宗教中心带动的消费与区域转运。作为取代萨达婆诃那权威的新兴势力，伊克什瓦库在220年前后处于扩张与整合并进的繁荣阶段。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on irrigated agriculture, supplemented by river-valley trade, craft production, consumption driven by religious centers, and regional redistribution. As a rising power replacing Satavahana authority, the Ikshvakus around 220 CE were in a prosperous phase of expansion and consolidation.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作与杂粮农业、棉纺织、陶器与砖石建筑、金属加工、珠饰制作、象牙与木工、宗教建筑营造
mainIndustries.en: Rice and mixed-grain agriculture, cotton textiles, pottery and brick construction, metalworking, bead-making, ivory and woodcraft, and religious building activity

tradeGoods.zh: 棉布、珠饰、象牙制品、香料、谷物、盐、金属器、半宝石、工艺陶器与区域性奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Cotton cloth, beads, ivory goods, spices, grain, salt, metalware, semi-precious stones, fine ceramics, and regional luxury items

householdWealth.zh: 多数家庭财富集中于耕地使用权、牲畜、粮食储备、纺织工具与首饰；城市商人和宗教机构积累了更高的可转移财富。
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth lay in cultivation rights, livestock, grain stores, textile tools, and ornaments; urban merchants and religious institutions accumulated higher levels of transferable wealth.

economicSystem.zh: 经济体系属农业税收国家下的区域市场经济，王室、寺院、商人行会与地方精英共同参与资源分配与财富积累。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a regional market economy within an agrarian revenue state, in which the crown, religious institutions, merchant groups, and local elites all participated in resource distribution and wealth accumulation.

source.en: Agrarian land revenue and commuted produce

source.zh: 王室领地与矿产工坊收入

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模对一个区域性安得罗王国而言较为充实，部分以金属货币、宝物、粮食储备和可征调贡赋形式存在，而非完全以现钱保存。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was substantial for a regional Andhra kingdom, existing partly in metal coin, valuables, grain reserves, and callable tribute rather than entirely in cash.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向稳健扩张：维持军费与地方整合的同时，通过宗教施赠巩固合法性，并投资灌溉和都城建设以扩大未来税基。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored prudent expansion: maintaining military outlays and territorial integration while using religious patronage to reinforce legitimacy and investing in irrigation and capital development to enlarge the future tax base.

description.zh: 军队主体，由持矛、弓手、盾兵和轻装步卒组成，承担守城、野战与护卫交通线任务。
description.en: The core of the army, composed of spearmen, archers, shield-bearers, and light infantry for garrison duty, field combat, and road protection.

description.zh: 数量有限但机动性强，主要用于侦察、追击、传令和对地方叛乱的快速反应。
description.en: Limited in number but mobile, used mainly for reconnaissance, pursuit, dispatch carrying, and rapid response to local unrest.

description.zh: 用于震慑、冲击和王权象征，在重要会战和仪式场合尤为关键。
description.en: Used for shock, intimidation, and royal symbolism, especially in major battles and ceremonial displays.

description.zh: 由地方首领和边地据点提供，负责维持秩序、守卫通道与支援王军行动。
description.en: Supplied by local chiefs and frontier posts, these forces maintained order, guarded routes, and supported royal operations.

technology.zh: 军事技术处于南亚早期历史时期的成熟水平，使用铁制兵器、复合弓或强化木弓、攻城器具、战象和设防据点，但不具备同时代中原大型官僚军队那样的标准化程度。
technology.en: Military technology stood at a mature early historic South Asian level, using iron weapons, strong bows or composite forms, siege implements, war elephants, and fortified positions, though without the degree of standardization seen in the large bureaucratic armies of contemporary China.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自后萨达婆诃那权力真空中的地区竞争者，尤其是西部与南部的地方势力、残余萨达婆诃那集团及德干高原上的附庸不稳。
threats.en: The main threats came from regional rivals in the post-Satavahana power vacuum, especially western and southern local powers, residual Satavahana-aligned groups, and unstable feudatories on the Deccan plateau.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

description.zh: 水战拍竿，击毁敌船上层结构
description.en: Ship-mounted swinging beam to smash enemy superstructures

description.zh: 覆盖全身的重装骑兵铁甲
description.en: Full-body heavy cavalry iron barding

description.zh: 复合弓或长弓，远射主力
description.en: Composite or longbow, primary ranged weapon

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于克里希纳河流域及其灌溉农业区，乡村人口占绝大多数；都城及宗教中心吸纳了工匠、商人、僧侣和行政人员。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated chiefly in the Krishna river basin and its irrigated agricultural zones, with the overwhelming majority in villages; the capital and religious centers drew artisans, merchants, monks, and officials.

name.zh: 阿马拉瓦蒂地区城镇群
下克里希纳河谷市场城

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括王室与贵族、婆罗门与宗教精英、军人、商人和行会成员、工匠、自由农民、佃耕者及依附劳作者；女性在宗教捐赠与王室联姻中发挥显著作用。
socialClasses.en: Social strata included the royal house and nobility, Brahmins and religious elites, soldiers, merchants and guild members, artisans, free peasants, tenant cultivators, and dependent laborers; women played notable roles in religious patronage and royal alliance-building.

allies.zh: 无稳定意义上的远程正式盟友，但与部分地方首领、佛教宗教网络及安得罗沿海商人群体保持合作关系。
allies.en: There were no stable formal long-distance allies in the strict sense, but the kingdom maintained cooperative relations with certain local chiefs, Buddhist religious networks, and Andhra coastal merchant communities.

enemies.zh: 主要对手是萨达婆诃那解体后争夺德干东部的区域势力，包括西部地方政权与可能挑战其新兴霸权的邻近首领。
enemies.en: Its principal adversaries were the regional powers contesting the eastern Deccan after the breakup of Satavahana authority, including western polities and nearby chiefs capable of challenging its emerging dominance.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以巩固安得罗腹地、接收前萨达婆诃那政治遗产、控制贸易通道并通过婚姻、施赠和军事威慑整合周边势力为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on consolidating the Andhra heartland, inheriting former Satavahana political space, controlling trade routes, and integrating surrounding powers through marriage ties, patronage, and military deterrence.

era.zh: 南亚早期历史时期晚段，约当中国三国时代
era.en: Late Early Historic South Asia, roughly contemporaneous with the Chinese Three Kingdoms era

keyInnovations.zh: 砖石佛教建筑、成熟的铭文行政、灌溉农业管理、铁器生产、区域性货币流通和专业手工业组织是其关键技术与制度优势。
keyInnovations.en: Brick-and-stone Buddhist construction, mature inscriptional administration, irrigated agriculture management, iron production, regional coin circulation, and specialized craft organization were its key technological and institutional strengths.

strengths.zh: 地处富庶河谷、继承萨达婆诃那旧有政治与贸易网络、宗教赞助增强合法性、都城文化活跃且财政稳健，是其最显著优势。
strengths.en: Its greatest strengths were its position in a fertile river valley, inheritance of Satavahana-era political and trade networks, legitimacy strengthened by religious patronage, a culturally active capital, and sound finances.

weaknesses.zh: 统治基础仍在形成中，对地方附庸和贵族合作依赖较大，国家整合程度不如长期成熟帝国；若继承、军事实力或税收控制出现波动，局势容易分裂。
weaknesses.en: Its governing base was still forming, it depended heavily on cooperation from local feudatories and nobles, and it lacked the integration of a long-mature empire; if succession, military strength, or revenue control faltered, fragmentation could quickly follow.

outlook.zh: 220年前后前景总体乐观。只要王室能继续控制克里希纳河谷、稳住附属势力并维持宗教与财政支持，伊克什瓦库王国有望在短期内成为安得罗地区最重要的区域强国之一。
outlook.en: The outlook around 220 CE was generally favorable. If the royal house could continue to control the Krishna valley, stabilize subordinate powers, and sustain religious and fiscal support, the Ikshvaku kingdom was well positioned to become one of the foremost regional powers in Andhra in the near term.

description.zh: 伊克什瓦库在安得拉地区崛起，接替萨塔瓦哈那余势成为地方强权。
description.en: The Ikshvakus were rising in Andhra, replacing Satavahana authority as a significant regional power.

governmentForm.zh: 以君主为核心、依托诸省总督与附庸首领的分层王国
governmentForm.en: A stratified monarchy centered on the king and supported by provincial governors and subordinate chiefs

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、地方贵族与军政官员、婆罗门与僧团、商人行会、手工业者、农民及各类依附社群构成，种姓秩序与地方族群传统并存。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house, local nobles and military-administrative officials, Brahmins and monastic communities, merchant guilds, artisans, peasants, and dependent groups, with caste hierarchy coexisting alongside regional ethnic traditions.

rulingClass.zh: 萨塔वाह纳王室、马哈塞纳帕提等高级军政官、地方大族、受封首领与婆罗门土地受赐者
rulingClass.en: The Satavahana royal family, senior military-administrative officers such as mahasenapatis, regional landed elites, subordinate chiefs, and Brahmin beneficiaries of land grants

succession.zh: 王位原则上由王族世袭，通常在萨塔वाह纳宗室男性间承继，但实际继承常受宫廷派系、地方实力者与军政支持影响。
succession.en: The throne was in principle hereditary within the royal house, usually passing among male members of the Satavahana line, though actual succession was often influenced by court factions, regional powerholders, and military-administrative backing.

structure.zh: 中央王廷设于普拉提什塔那，王权通过王室、财政官、军职官与各地区总督实施。边远地区多依赖半自治的附庸、地方家族与旧有部族首领治理，国家整合力在220年已明显下降。
structure.en: The central court at Pratishthana ruled through the royal household, fiscal officers, military officials, and regional governors. Distant areas were often administered through semi-autonomous subordinates, local families, and existing tribal chiefs, and by 220 the kingdom’s integrative capacity had clearly weakened.

function.zh: 起草王命、保存契据、管理铭文与地方诏令传达
function.en: Drafted royal orders, preserved charters, and managed inscriptions and the transmission of provincial directives

function.zh: 征收土地税、商税、港市税与附庸贡赋，监督国库与仓储
function.en: Collected land tax, trade duties, market and port taxes, and tribute from subordinates, while supervising the treasury and granaries

function.zh: 统筹野战部队、边境防务、要塞补给与地方征发
function.en: Coordinated field forces, frontier defense, fortress supply, and local levies

function.zh: 监督阿哈拉等地方行政区、确认封地、调解附庸与地方大族关系
function.en: Oversaw local administrative districts such as aharas, confirmed landed rights, and mediated relations with subordinate chiefs and local magnates

function.zh: 监管西海岸港口、内陆商路、关税与对外贸易秩序
function.en: Supervised western seaports, inland caravan routes, customs duties, and external trade order

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以阿哈拉、地区城镇与乡村共同体为基础，王朝任命或承认的官员与地方首领并行治理。许多地区实际由世袭家族和军事长官控制，中央依靠税收、封赠和婚姻关系维持忠诚。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was based on aharas, district towns, and village communities, with royally appointed or recognized officials governing alongside local chiefs. In many areas hereditary families and military officers held practical control, while the center maintained loyalty through taxation, grants, and marriage ties.

legalSystem.zh: 法律实践结合王命、婆罗门法传统、地方习惯与行会规约。刑罚与争讼多由地方官、村社、商人行会或宗教权威处理，重大案件上达王廷或省级长官。
legalSystem.en: Legal practice combined royal commands, Brahmanical legal tradition, local custom, and guild regulations. Punishment and dispute resolution were often handled by local officers, village bodies, merchant guilds, or religious authorities, while major cases went to the royal court or provincial superiors.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依赖农业地租性质的土地税、灌溉与产出征收、市场交易税、港口关税、矿产及盐铁收益，并辅以附庸贡纳与战利收入。因地方离心，税收征解日益不均。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue depended mainly on agrarian land taxes akin to rent, levies on irrigation and produce, market taxes, port customs, mineral and salt-metal income, supplemented by tribute from subordinate polities and war gains. As regional fragmentation advanced, collection became increasingly uneven.

religion.zh: 宗教生活兼具婆罗门教、早期印度教诸神崇拜与佛教传统。王室常赞助婆罗门祭祀并行土地布施，但佛教僧院、岩窟与商人赞助网络仍然活跃，局部地区也保留地方神灵与民间信仰。
religion.en: Religious life combined Brahmanical religion, early Hindu cults, and Buddhist traditions. The court often patronized Brahmins and ritual donation, yet Buddhist monasteries, cave establishments, and merchant patronage networks remained active, while local deities and popular cults also persisted.

philosophy.zh: 思想基础以达摩、王权正当性、祭祀功德与布施伦理为核心，兼受佛教业报、出世观与僧团学术影响。宫廷与地方精英普遍以婆罗门规范解释社会秩序。
philosophy.en: The intellectual foundation centered on dharma, royal legitimacy, ritual merit, and the ethics of donation, while also reflecting Buddhist ideas of karma, renunciation, and monastic learning. Court and regional elites generally interpreted social order through Brahmanical norms.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用婆罗米文字书写普拉克里特语，部分地区也出现早期梵语化趋势。王室铭文、捐献题记、钱币传说和行会记录构成主要书写载体。
writingSystem.en: The kingdom mainly used Brahmi script to write Prakrit, with early tendencies toward Sanskritization in some areas. Royal inscriptions, donation records, coin legends, and guild documentation formed the principal written media.

culturalAchievements.zh: 萨塔वाह纳时期延续并推动了德干地区岩窟建筑、佛教供养艺术、商人捐献文化与普拉克里特铭文传统；阿旃陀早期洞窟、纳西克与卡尔拉等石窟群及精美铅币、铜币体现了这一时代的区域文化活力。
culturalAchievements.en: The Satavahana period sustained and advanced Deccan rock-cut architecture, Buddhist patronage art, merchant donation culture, and the Prakrit inscriptional tradition; the early Ajanta caves, the cave complexes at Nasik and Karla, and refined lead and copper coinage reflect the era’s regional cultural vitality.

languageFamily.zh: 行政与铭文语言主要属中古印度-雅利安语支的普拉克里特，民间同时广泛使用德拉威语与地方语言群。
languageFamily.en: The main administrative and inscriptional language belonged to the Middle Indo-Aryan Prakrit group, while Dravidian and other regional languages were also widely spoken among the populace.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业为主，辅以区域性手工业、矿产、纺织与海陆贸易。虽然王朝衰落，但德干高原与西海岸之间的交通网络仍支撑相当规模的商品流通。对外贸易较1世纪高峰已有回落，财政和市场更受地方割据影响。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was predominantly agrarian, supplemented by regional craft production, mining, textiles, and maritime and overland trade. Although the dynasty was declining, transport networks between the Deccan plateau and the western coast still supported substantial commodity circulation. External trade had retreated from its 1st-century peak, and both fiscal life and markets were increasingly affected by regional fragmentation.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻麦杂粮农业、棉纺织、珠饰与宝石加工、金属冶炼、制陶、盐业、林产、畜牧、港口贸易与内陆转运
mainIndustries.en: Mixed grain agriculture, cotton textiles, bead and gem working, metal smelting, pottery, salt production, forest products, pastoralism, port commerce, and inland transport

tradeGoods.zh: 棉布、胡椒与香料转运品、玛瑙与红玉髓珠、象牙、珍木、药材、金属器、铅币、马匹、葡萄酒、玻璃器与奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Cotton cloth, pepper and spice transit goods, agate and carnelian beads, ivory, fine woods, medicinal products, metalware, lead coinage, horses, wine, glassware, and luxury items

name.zh: 卡夏帕那与地方铅铜币

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异极大。王室、港市商人、行会领袖和受赐土地的婆罗门拥有较高财富；大多数农户仅维持中低水平自给，遇到战乱、旱情或税赋加重时极为脆弱。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. The royal house, port merchants, guild leaders, and Brahmin land grantees enjoyed considerable assets, while most peasant households remained at modest or low subsistence levels and were highly vulnerable to conflict, drought, or heavier taxation.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业税基、地方市场、行会生产和王室—地方贵族再分配为核心的前近代混合经济；货币流通活跃于商贸节点，但广阔乡村仍以实物、劳役与地方交换为主。
economicSystem.en: A premodern mixed economy centered on agrarian taxation, local markets, guild production, and redistribution through the crown and regional elites; coin use was active in commercial nodes, but much of the countryside still relied primarily on payment in kind, labor obligations, and local exchange.

source.zh: 市场税、关税与港口收入
source.en: Market taxes, customs, and port income
Tribute from subordinates and provincial remittances

category.zh: 宗教布施与土地赐予相关成本
category.en: Religious patronage and costs tied to land grants
Maintenance of roads, ports, and storage

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库仍具一定储备，但已不如王朝鼎盛时期稳固。持续军事动员、地方税源流失和高额布施削弱了中央现金与实物储备的可持续性。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury still retained meaningful reserves, but it was no longer as secure as in the dynasty’s peak period. Continued military mobilization, loss of provincial revenues, and high levels of donation weakened the sustainability of central cash and in-kind reserves.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策趋向保守与补缺并用：一方面努力维持交通税收和港口收益，另一方面通过重申附庸义务、确认封地和临时征发来弥补收入下滑，但这也加深了地方离心与税负不稳。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy combined conservatism with ad hoc extraction: the court sought to preserve transit taxes and port revenues while compensating for declining income through reaffirmed tributary obligations, confirmation of grants, and temporary levies, but this in turn deepened regional centrifugal tendencies and unstable tax burdens.

description.zh: 构成军队主体，装备长矛、盾、弓与刀剑，负责守城、野战和护卫交通线。
description.en: Formed the bulk of the army, equipped with spears, shields, bows, and swords, and used for garrison duty, field operations, and protection of communications.

description.zh: 数量有限但机动性重要，多用于边境袭扰、追击与支援地方总督作战。
description.en: Limited in number but important for mobility, used for frontier raiding, pursuit, and support of provincial commanders.

description.zh: 象队主要用于震慑、冲击与王权象征，在平原作战和阅兵中尤具效果，但维护成本高。
description.en: Elephant units served for shock, intimidation, and royal prestige, especially effective in open-ground warfare and ceremonial display, though expensive to maintain.

description.zh: 由地方首领、城镇守军和乡村征发组成，战斗力参差不齐，是衰落期维持兵力规模的关键补充。
description.en: Composed of local chiefs’ retainers, town garrisons, and rural levies, with uneven quality; they were essential to maintaining numbers during the period of decline.

commanderInChief.zh: 阎那·室利·萨塔卡尔尼国王兼最高统帅
commanderInChief.en: King Yajna Sri Satakarni as commander-in-chief

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁制冷兵器、复合或强化弓、战象运用、土木筑城与传统攻城手段为主。海上力量仅限沿岸护航与港口控制，缺乏远洋海军优势。
technology.en: Military technology was based on iron melee weapons, strong bows, the use of war elephants, earthen and masonry fortification, and traditional siege methods. Naval power was limited to coastal escort and port control, without major blue-water capabilities.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自西方的西方刹帝利王朝压力、东部与南部地方势力坐大、附庸离叛以及内部继承与财政危机。边疆虽无统一大敌全面入侵，但持续的区域战争不断消耗国力。
threats.en: The main threats came from pressure by the Western Kshatrapas in the west, the rise of eastern and southern regional powers, subordinate defections, and internal succession and fiscal crises. Although no single frontier enemy mounted a full-scale conquest at the time, continual regional warfare steadily drained the kingdom.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 水战拍竿，击毁敌船上层结构
description.en: Ship-mounted swinging beam to smash enemy superstructures

description.zh: 覆盖全身的重装骑兵铁甲
description.en: Full-body heavy cavalry iron barding

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

description.zh: 可连发十矢的机械弩，守城利器
description.en: Mechanical crossbow firing ten bolts in succession, excellent for defense

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约六百万，主要分布于德干高原农业区、西海岸贸易走廊及哥达瓦里、克里希纳等河谷。人口结构以农村为绝对多数，城市与宗教中心在交通节点相对集中。
populationDescription.en: The total population was about six million, concentrated mainly in the agrarian Deccan plateau, the western coastal trade corridor, and river valleys such as the Godavari and Krishna. The population was overwhelmingly rural, with towns and religious centers clustered at transport nodes.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与贵族居上层，其次为军政官员、婆罗门、富商与行会领袖；城镇工匠、普通商人和僧侣居中层；广大农民、佃作者、牧民、劳役者与边缘社群居下层。地方族群与种姓地位交织，社会流动有限但并非完全封闭。
socialClasses.en: The royal family and nobility stood at the top, followed by military-administrative officials, Brahmins, wealthy merchants, and guild leaders; urban artisans, ordinary traders, and monks formed the middle strata; the vast mass of peasants, tenants, pastoralists, laborers, and marginal groups occupied the lower levels. Regional identities overlapped with caste rank, and social mobility was limited though not entirely absent.

allies.zh: 无稳固大国盟友；王朝主要依赖附庸首领、婚姻关系与临时地方合作来维持势力范围。
allies.en: No stable great-power allies; the dynasty relied chiefly on subordinate chiefs, marriage ties, and temporary regional cooperation to preserve its sphere.

enemies.zh: 西方刹帝利王朝是最直接的战略对手；此外，伊克什瓦库等新兴地方政权和南方诸王国的独立倾向也削弱了萨塔वाह纳控制力。
enemies.en: The Western Kshatrapas were the most immediate strategic rival; in addition, emerging regional polities such as the Ikshvakus and the independent tendencies of southern kingdoms weakened Satavahana control.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以维持德干霸权、保护西海岸贸易、遏制西方刹帝利扩张并安抚地方附庸为核心。对远方强权如罗马、帕提亚和南海贸易圈多采取间接商业联系而非正式国与国外交。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving Deccan hegemony, protecting western coastal commerce, containing Western Kshatrapa expansion, and pacifying subordinate polities. With distant powers such as Rome, Parthia, and the maritime Southeast Asian trade sphere, the kingdom maintained mostly indirect commercial rather than formal interstate relations.

era.zh: 古典时代晚期的德干区域国家
era.en: Late Classical-era regional state of the Deccan

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的婆罗米铭文实践、岩窟开凿技术、珠饰与纺织工艺、铅铜钱币铸造、内陆与海岸联动商路组织，以及适应季风航行的港口贸易经验。
keyInnovations.en: Key developments included mature Brahmi inscriptional practice, rock-cut excavation techniques, advanced bead and textile crafts, lead-copper coin minting, integrated inland-coastal trade organization, and port-trade experience adapted to monsoon navigation.

strengths.zh: 萨塔वाह纳王国仍拥有广阔人口基础、德干核心地带的政治传统、贯通内陆与西海岸的贸易网络、成熟的地方统治经验以及较强的文化凝聚力。
strengths.en: The Satavahana kingdom still possessed a large population base, political legitimacy in the Deccan heartland, trade networks linking the interior with the western coast, mature experience in regional governance, and strong cultural cohesion.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于王权衰退、附庸离心、财政赤字扩大、地方军事化加深、继承稳定性不足，以及对关键港口和西部边区控制力下降。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in declining royal authority, centrifugal subordinate powers, widening fiscal deficits, increasing regional militarization, unstable succession, and weakening control over key ports and western frontier zones.

outlook.zh: 220年前后的萨塔वाह纳虽仍是德干名义上的主要强国，但已进入难以逆转的分裂阶段。若无持续军事成功与财政整合，王朝将在数十年内进一步瓦解，被伊克什瓦库、阿比拉、丘图及其他地方王朝取代。
outlook.en: Around 220 the Satavahanas remained nominally the principal power of the Deccan, but they had entered a stage of fragmentation that would be difficult to reverse. Without sustained military success and fiscal reintegration, the dynasty was likely to disintegrate further within decades and be replaced by polities such as the Ikshvakus, Abhiras, Chutus, and other regional dynasties.

description.zh: 萨塔瓦哈那仍是德干核心政权，但地方分裂与外敌压力日益加剧。
description.en: The Satavahanas remained the principal Deccan power, but regional fragmentation and external pressure were increasing.

governmentForm.zh: 南印度婆罗门化地方君主制，继承并改造萨塔瓦哈纳旧有的区域统治结构
governmentForm.en: A Brahmanized regional monarchy of South India that inherited and adapted older Satavahana provincial governing structures

socialStructure.zh: 社会以王室、婆罗门祭司与地主精英为上层，地方首领、军人、商人和手工业者居中，广大农民、牧民、林地部族与劳役人口构成基层。
socialStructure.en: Society was headed by the royal house, Brahmin ritual elites, and landed notables; local chiefs, soldiers, merchants, and artisans formed the middle strata; the broad base consisted of cultivators, pastoralists, forest tribes, and dependent laborers.

rulingClass.zh: 王族、婆罗门官僚、与王室结盟的地方酋长及土地控制者
rulingClass.en: The royal family, Brahmin administrators, and allied local chiefs and land-controlling elites

succession.zh: 父系世袭为主，通常由王子继位，但需获得王室宗族、婆罗门支持者与地方权贵承认
succession.en: Primarily patrilineal hereditary succession, usually by a prince of the royal line, subject to recognition by the royal clan, Brahmin backers, and regional magnates

structure.zh: 中央宫廷规模不大，围绕国王设立财政、军务、土地与宗教施赠事务；地方上依赖旧萨塔瓦哈纳体系下的地区长官、城镇首领与村社。
structure.en: The central court was modest in scale, organized around the king with responsibilities for revenue, military affairs, land management, and religious grants; locally it relied on district officers, town heads, and village communities inherited from the former Satavahana system.

function.zh: 起草铭文、土地施赠文书、王命与对地方官的指示，并保存宫廷记录
function.en: Drafted inscriptions, land-grant charters, royal orders, and instructions to local officials, while keeping court records

function.zh: 征收农业税、贸易关税、手工业课税与附属地区贡纳，管理王室仓储与钱币收入
function.en: Collected agrarian taxes, trade duties, craft levies, and tribute from subordinate areas, and managed royal granaries and coin receipts

function.zh: 统筹王室卫队、地方征召兵、边地守备与交通要道防务
function.en: Coordinated the royal guard, levied local troops, frontier garrisons, and defense of routes and strategic passes

name.zh: 土地丈量与村社事务处
name.en: Land Survey and Village Affairs Office

function.zh: 核定耕地、监督村社负担、确认免税地与宗教施赠边界
function.en: Assessed cultivated land, supervised village obligations, and confirmed tax-free estates and the boundaries of religious grants

function.zh: 管理对婆罗门、寺庙与可能存在的佛教机构的施赠，组织王室祭礼以巩固合法性
function.en: Managed grants to Brahmins, temples, and possible Buddhist establishments, and organized royal rituals to strengthen legitimacy

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以地区、城镇与村落三级为主。王室任命或认可地区长官，城镇由商人团体和地方精英协助治理，村社在税收、灌溉和治安上具有较强自治性。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was structured chiefly around districts, towns, and villages. District governors were appointed or recognized by the crown, towns were helped by merchant bodies and local notables, and village communities retained strong autonomy in taxation, irrigation, and order.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、婆罗门法传统和王命并行为主；土地、婚姻、继承与施赠纠纷多由地方长老、婆罗门和官员共同裁断，严重治安案件由王权干预。
legalSystem.en: Law combined customary practice, Brahmanical legal tradition, and royal decree; disputes over land, marriage, inheritance, and donations were often judged jointly by local elders, Brahmins, and officials, while serious breaches of order drew direct royal intervention.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以地税为核心，辅以灌溉费、牧税、林产征收、市场税、道路和渡口关税，以及对附属首领的贡纳要求。宗教施赠地产常享减免。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation centered on land revenue, supplemented by irrigation fees, pastoral dues, forest-product levies, market taxes, road and ferry customs, and tribute obligations imposed on subordinate chiefs. Religious grant lands were often partly or wholly exempt.

religion.zh: 以婆罗门教传统为主，尤其尊奉毗湿奴与湿婆等印度教早期形态，同时延续德干地区对地方神祇、祖先崇拜与部分佛教、耆那教影响的包容格局。
religion.en: Religion was predominantly Brahmanical, with devotion especially to early Hindu forms centered on Vishnu and Shiva, while still preserving a Deccan pattern of accommodation toward local deities, ancestor cults, and some Buddhist and Jain influences.

philosophy.zh: 思想上以达摩、王权合法性、祭祀功德和种姓秩序观念为主，兼受南印度与德干地区实用政治传统和商旅交流带来的多元文化影响。
philosophy.en: Its thought world was shaped by dharma, royal legitimacy, ritual merit, and caste order, while also reflecting practical political traditions of the Deccan and the cultural pluralism brought by commerce.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用婆罗米字母的南印度地方变体，行政与铭文语言多为俗语化普拉克里特，并逐渐受到梵语化趋势影响。
writingSystem.en: It mainly used southern regional variants of the Brahmi script; administrative and inscriptional language was often vernacularized Prakrit, increasingly influenced by the growing prestige of Sanskrit.

culturalAchievements.zh: 该政权的重要文化成就在于延续萨塔瓦哈纳后期的铭文施赠传统、促进婆罗门化国家仪礼、支持寺庙与祭祀网络，并推动西南德干地区的城镇与贸易文化延续。
culturalAchievements.en: Its notable cultural achievements lay in preserving the late Satavahana tradition of inscriptional donation, promoting Brahmanized state ritual, supporting temple and sacrificial networks, and sustaining the urban and commercial culture of the southwestern Deccan.

languageFamily.zh: 行政和铭文以中期印度-雅利安语的普拉克里特为主，日常社会同时广泛使用达罗毗荼语族语言，尤其与早期卡纳达语环境密切相关。
languageFamily.en: Administration and inscriptions chiefly used Middle Indo-Aryan Prakrit, while everyday society also widely employed Dravidian languages, especially in an environment closely connected with early Kannada-speaking regions.

gdpDescription.zh: 朱图王国为中等规模的区域农业—商业经济体。其财富来自稻作与旱地农业、林产、畜牧、地方手工业以及连接阿拉伯海港口与德干腹地的商路收益。与大型帝国相比总体经济体量有限，但在地区层面具备稳定财政基础。
gdpDescription.en: The Chutu Kingdom was a medium-sized regional agro-commercial economy. Its wealth came from rice and dry farming, forest products, pastoralism, local crafts, and revenues from routes linking the Arabian Sea ports with the Deccan interior. Compared to great empires its total economic scale was limited, but at the regional level it possessed a stable fiscal base.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧、林产采集、棉纺织、陶器、金属加工、珠饰制造、区域转运贸易
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, forest extraction, cotton textiles, pottery, metalworking, bead production, and regional transit trade

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、黍粟、棉布、香木、象牙、药材、胡椒与南方香料转运品、珠串、铁器、铜器、牲畜
tradeGoods.en: Rice, millets, cotton cloth, fragrant woods, ivory, medicinal products, pepper and southern spices in transit, beads, iron goods, copperware, and livestock

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要体现为土地使用权、牲畜、粮食储备、农具与家庭饰物；商人和地方首领则掌握更多货币、仓储与贵金属。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was mainly embodied in land-use rights, livestock, grain reserves, tools, and family ornaments; merchants and local chiefs controlled more coin, storage, and precious metal.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业地租和村社生产为基础，辅以王室控制的税收、地方精英的中介征收、宗教施赠免税地，以及依靠商路和市场交换形成的混合经济。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on agrarian rent and village production, combined with crown taxation, intermediary collection by local elites, tax-exempt religious grants, and market exchange tied to trade routes.

source.en: Mining, forest products, and miscellaneous receipts

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模对一个地方王国而言较为充实，既包括钱币与贵金属储备，也包括粮仓、牲畜和可动员贡赋。其稳定性依赖农业收成与商路通畅。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was fairly substantial for a regional kingdom, consisting not only of coin and precious metal reserves but also granary stores, livestock, and mobilizable tribute. Its stability depended on harvests and open trade routes.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守稳健，重视维持军政开支与宗教施赠之间的平衡，避免对村社过度榨取，以确保地方忠诚和长期税基。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative and stabilizing, aiming to balance military-administrative spending with religious patronage while avoiding excessive pressure on village communities so as to preserve local loyalty and the long-term tax base.

description.zh: 由矛兵、盾兵、弓箭手组成，是守卫都城、据点和主要道路的核心力量。
description.en: Composed of spearmen, shield-bearers, and archers, it formed the core force for guarding the capital, strongpoints, and major roads.

description.zh: 数量不大但机动性较高，用于侦察、追击、护送和快速联络。
description.en: Small in number but relatively mobile, they were used for reconnaissance, pursuit, escort, and rapid communications.

description.zh: 用于仪礼展示、战场震慑和保护君主，是象征性强于数量优势的精锐单位。
description.en: Used for ritual display, battlefield intimidation, and protection of the ruler, they were elite units whose symbolic value exceeded their numerical size.

description.zh: 由地方首领和村社在战时提供，训练水平参差不齐，但适合守备、运输与区域作战。
description.en: Provided in wartime by local chiefs and village communities, they varied in training but were useful for garrison, transport, and regional warfare.

commanderInChief.zh: 诃利提子·毗湿奴堪陀王亲自为最高统帅，日常由王室将领与地方军首分掌
commanderInChief.en: King Haritiputra Vishnuskanda served as commander-in-chief, with day-to-day command divided among royal officers and regional military leaders

technology.zh: 军备以铁制矛、剑、弓、盾、皮革或织物甲、少量金属护具和战象装备为主，无攻城技术上的重大创新，但适应德干丘陵、林地与道路节点防御。
technology.en: Military equipment centered on iron spears, swords, bows, shields, leather or textile armor, limited metal protection, and elephant gear; it showed no major siege innovations but was well adapted to the hills, forests, and route-node defense of the Deccan.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自萨塔瓦哈纳解体后德干地区权力重组，包括西方刹帝利总督势力、伊克什瓦库等邻近政权、地方酋长离心，以及贸易线路受阻造成的财政压力。
threats.en: The main threats arose from the post-Satavahana reordering of power in the Deccan, including Western Kshatrapa influence, nearby polities such as the Ikshvakus, centrifugal local chiefs, and fiscal strain if trade routes were disrupted.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

description.zh: 复合弓或长弓，远射主力
description.en: Composite or longbow, primary ranged weapon

description.zh: 单兵弩与床弩，中国军队核心远程武器
description.en: Infantry crossbow and mounted crossbow, core ranged weapon

populationDescription.zh: 人口规模在区域王国中属中等，主要分布于可耕谷地、交通节点、林缘聚落及都城周边，呈现农业村社占绝对多数、城市与市场城镇较少但作用显著的格局。
populationDescription.en: Its population was medium-sized for a regional kingdom, concentrated in cultivable valleys, transport nodes, forest-edge settlements, and the capital’s hinterland; agrarian villages formed the overwhelming majority, while cities and market towns were fewer but economically significant.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与贵族、婆罗门祭司、地方首领、商人与工匠、自由农民、佃耕者、牧民、林地部族、仆役与附属劳动力共同构成社会阶序。
socialClasses.en: The social order comprised royalty and nobles, Brahmin priests, local chiefs, merchants and artisans, free cultivators, tenant tillers, pastoralists, forest tribes, servants, and dependent laborers.

allies.zh: 无稳定意义上的远程正式盟友；更现实的是与周边地方首领、商人网络及部分南印度政权维持务实关系。
allies.en: It had no stable long-distance formal allies in the strict sense; more important were pragmatic relations with surrounding chiefs, merchant networks, and some South Indian polities.

enemies.zh: 并无持续全面战争中的绝对死敌，但与德干地区竞争性继承萨塔瓦哈纳遗产的势力存在紧张，包括西方刹帝利总督、邻近地方王朝及叛服不定的地方酋长。
enemies.en: It had no single perpetual mortal enemy in full-scale war, but it faced tensions with powers competing to inherit the Satavahana legacy in the Deccan, including the Western Kshatrapas, neighboring dynasties, and unreliable local chiefs.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以生存和区域平衡为核心：避免卷入北方大帝国冲突，通过承认、婚姻、施赠和商路合作巩固自身地位，并争取在西海岸—德干内陆贸易中保持节点优势。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was centered on survival and regional balancing: avoiding entanglement in northern imperial conflicts, using recognition, marriage ties, patronage, and trade-route cooperation to secure its position, and trying to retain nodal advantages in west-coast–Deccan commerce.

era.zh: 铁器时代晚期至古典南亚早期国家时代
era.en: Late Iron Age to early Classical South Asian state era

keyInnovations.zh: 并无革命性新发明，但熟练运用铁器农业工具、区域铸币、婆罗米铭文行政、灌溉设施维护、珠饰与纺织工艺，以及适应德干地形的道路与仓储组织。
keyInnovations.en: It produced no revolutionary inventions, but made effective use of iron agricultural tools, regional coinage, Brahmi-based inscriptional administration, irrigation maintenance, bead and textile craftsmanship, and road and storage organization suited to Deccan terrain.

strengths.zh: 朱图王国的优势在于继承萨塔瓦哈纳体系后的行政连续性、都城与内陆商路节点的地理位置、相对稳健的财政、以及婆罗门化王权提供的合法性与地方整合能力。
strengths.en: The Chutu Kingdom’s strengths lay in administrative continuity from the Satavahana system, the geographic value of its capital and interior trade nodes, relatively sound finances, and the legitimacy and integrative capacity provided by Brahmanized kingship.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于人口和军力有限、对地方首领合作的依赖较深、中央官僚规模不大、以及容易受到德干政局重组和贸易波动影响。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were limited population and military power, deep reliance on cooperation from local chiefs, a relatively small central bureaucracy, and vulnerability to Deccan political realignment and trade fluctuations.

outlook.zh: 220年时局势总体稳定，但长期前景取决于能否在后萨塔瓦哈纳世界中维持自治、控制关键税源，并抵御更强邻国或新兴地区王朝的吸纳压力。
outlook.en: In 220 CE the situation was broadly stable, but the long-term outlook depended on whether it could preserve autonomy in the post-Satavahana world, hold key sources of revenue, and resist absorption by stronger neighbors or rising regional dynasties.

description.zh: 朱荼是德干西南部的地方王朝，可能源于萨塔瓦哈那体系下的支系势力。
description.en: The Chutus were a regional dynasty in the southwestern Deccan, likely emerging from the Satavahana political sphere.

governmentForm.zh: 以王权为核心、依赖宗族贵族、地方酋长与港口商人合作治理的世袭君主制
governmentForm.en: A hereditary monarchy centered on kingship, governed through cooperation among clan nobles, local chieftains, and port merchants

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、贵族与武士首领、婆罗门祭司、商人、农民、手工业者、渔民与劳役群体构成，山区部族与沿海港市居民在经济上都很重要。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal house, nobles and warrior chiefs, Brahmin ritual specialists, merchants, cultivators, artisans, fishing communities, and laboring groups, with both hill tribes and coastal port populations playing important economic roles.

rulingClass.zh: 哲罗王族、伊伦波赖支系贵族、地方维拉尔与酋长、掌控港口与内陆贸易的显贵家族
rulingClass.en: The Chera royal house, Irumporai branch nobles, local velir chiefs, and notable families controlling ports and inland trade

succession.zh: 王位通常在王族内部世袭，受宗族资历、军事威望和贵族支持影响，并非绝对长子继承。
succession.en: Succession was generally hereditary within the royal lineage, shaped by clan seniority, military prestige, and noble backing rather than strict primogeniture.

structure.zh: 中央朝廷规模不大，围绕国王、王室亲族、军政顾问、税务征收者和港口监督者运作；地方上通过附属首领、村社与港市官员管理山地、农区和海岸贸易中心。
structure.en: The central court was relatively compact, operating around the king, royal kinsmen, military and political advisers, tax collectors, and port supervisors; locally, the realm was managed through subordinate chiefs, village communities, and port officials overseeing hill country, agrarian zones, and coastal trade centers.

function.zh: 管理王宫、仪式、使节接待、王室财产与亲族事务
function.en: Managed the palace, ceremonies, reception of envoys, royal property, and dynastic affairs

function.zh: 征收地租、关税、港口税、森林与香料贡赋，并保管粮仓与王室储备
function.en: Collected land dues, customs, port taxes, and forest and spice tribute, while maintaining granaries and royal stores

function.zh: 监督穆济里斯等港口的泊船、称量、关税、外商居留和贸易秩序
function.en: Oversaw berthing, weighing, customs, foreign merchant residence, and commercial order at ports such as Muziris

function.zh: 统筹王家卫队、战象、地方征召兵与山地通道防务
function.en: Coordinated the royal guard, war elephants, local levies, and defense of hill passes

function.zh: 处理土地、贸易、债务、继承与部族纠纷，并执行王命裁断
function.en: Handled disputes over land, trade, debt, inheritance, and tribal matters, and enforced royal judgments

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托村社、纳都式地区共同体、港口管理人和附属酋长。王权对核心贸易走廊与港口控制较强，对山地与边缘地区更多采取宗主—朝贡关系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on village communities, regional assemblies, port managers, and subordinate chiefs. Royal authority was stronger in core trade corridors and ports, while hill and peripheral regions were often governed through looser overlord-tribute relationships.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以王命、习惯法、宗族调解和宗教规范并行。商业纠纷多由港口官员与商人团体调处，乡村案件则由地方首领和长老裁决。
legalSystem.en: Law combined royal command, customary law, clan arbitration, and religious norms. Commercial disputes were often settled by port officials and merchant bodies, while rural cases were judged by local chiefs and elders.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自农产租税、港口关税、香料和林产贡赋、道路与市场征费，以及附属首领进献。海贸关税是王室财富的重要来源。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came chiefly from agrarian dues, port customs, spice and forest tribute, road and market tolls, and contributions from subordinate chiefs. Maritime customs were a major source of royal wealth.

religion.zh: 以早期南印度婆罗门教传统、地方神祇崇拜、祖先祭祀和英雄石纪念并存，亦可见耆那与佛教影响，尤其在贸易网络连接的港市中。
religion.en: Religion combined early South Indian Brahmanical traditions, local deity cults, ancestor rites, and hero-stone commemoration, with some Jain and Buddhist influence, especially in port towns linked to trade networks.

philosophy.zh: 思想以达摩、王道、赠与声望、诗歌赞颂和宗族伦理为核心，兼受婆罗门教宇宙观与南印度英雄伦理影响。
philosophy.en: Thought centered on dharma, kingship, gift-based prestige, poetic praise, and clan ethics, shaped by Brahmanical cosmology and southern heroic ideals.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用早期泰米尔语传统，书写上采用泰米尔-婆罗米文字；铭文和贸易记录可能混用俗语、祭祀语汇与Prakrit元素。
writingSystem.en: The kingdom primarily used early Tamil linguistic traditions, written in Tamil-Brahmi script; inscriptions and trade records could mix vernacular usage, ritual vocabulary, and some Prakrit elements.

culturalAchievements.zh: 属于桑伽姆时代文化圈，宫廷诗歌高度发达，赞颂战争、馈赠、爱情、山海景观与商贸繁荣；对胡椒海岸、远洋贸易和王者慷慨的描写尤其突出。
culturalAchievements.en: The Cheras belonged to the Sangam-age cultural sphere, marked by sophisticated court poetry praising war, generosity, love, landscapes, and commercial prosperity; depictions of the pepper coast, overseas trade, and royal largesse were especially prominent.

languageFamily.zh: 达罗毗荼语系
languageFamily.en: Dravidian

gdpDescription.zh: 哲罗经济在当时南亚属较高商业化水平，农业基础稳固，同时因胡椒、豆蔻、象牙、珍贵木材和转口贸易而显著受益。其财富高度集中于港口、商人和王室关税体系。
gdpDescription.en: The Chera economy was comparatively commercialized by South Asian standards of the time, with a solid agrarian base and exceptional gains from pepper, cardamom, ivory, fine timber, and entrepot trade. Wealth was strongly concentrated in ports, merchant circles, and the royal customs system.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作与椰枣种植、胡椒与香料生产、林产采集、象牙与木材贸易、珠宝加工、金属手工业、航运与港口服务
mainIndustries.en: Rice cultivation and coconut production, pepper and spice cultivation, forest extraction, ivory and timber trade, jewelry making, metal crafts, shipping, and port services

tradeGoods.zh: 黑胡椒、豆蔻、肉桂替代香材、珍木、象牙、珍珠、贝壳制品、棉布、半宝石，以及输入的金银币、葡萄酒、珊瑚、玻璃器和金属制品
tradeGoods.en: Black pepper, cardamom, aromatic substitutes, fine timber, ivory, pearls, shell goods, cotton cloth, semi-precious stones, and imports of gold and silver coin, wine, coral, glassware, and metal goods

name.zh: 罗马金币与本地铸币并用

householdWealth.zh: 多数家庭财富以土地使用权、稻谷、椰树、牲畜、渔具和首饰形式保存；沿海商人和港口中介拥有远高于内陆农户的流动财富。
householdWealth.en: Most households held wealth in land-use rights, rice stores, coconut groves, livestock, fishing gear, and jewelry; coastal merchants and port intermediaries possessed far greater liquid wealth than inland cultivators.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业为基础、以港口关税和远洋贸易为高利润部门的混合经济；王室不直接统制全部生产，但通过港口、通道和贡赋网络攫取关键收益。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on agriculture, with port customs and overseas trade forming the high-profit sector; the crown did not directly control all production, but captured key revenue through ports, routes, and tribute networks.

source.en: Port customs and maritime trade taxes

source.zh: 附属首领贡纳与林产香料收入
source.en: Tribute from subordinate chiefs and forest-spice income
Road tolls, market dues, and fines

category.en: Army, elephant corps, and frontier defense

category.zh: 港口管理、仓储与交通维护
category.en: Port administration, storage, and route maintenance

category.zh: 地方酋长补助与政治整合
category.en: Subsidies to local chiefs and political integration

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由金银币、珠宝、香料存货、象牙、谷仓储备和贡品组成。因对外贸易繁盛，哲罗王室较容易积累贵金属，但财政对港口流量波动较敏感。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of gold and silver coin, jewels, spice stocks, ivory, granary reserves, and tribute goods. Thanks to flourishing overseas trade, the Chera court could accumulate bullion relatively easily, though finances were sensitive to fluctuations in port traffic.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持港口安全、鼓励商贸到访、通过馈赠稳固地方忠诚，并以关税而非过度压榨农业作为增收重点。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized securing ports, encouraging merchant traffic, stabilizing local loyalty through gifts, and raising income more through customs than through excessive pressure on agriculture.

description.zh: 以矛、盾、剑和弓为主，是战争中的核心兵力，平时分散驻守都城、港口与要道。
description.en: Armed mainly with spears, shields, swords, and bows, this was the core wartime force and in peacetime garrisoned the capital, ports, and strategic routes.

name.zh: 地方征召军与酋长随从

description.zh: 由附属首领和地方共同体提供，战时集结迅速，但训练和装备不如王家常备部队统一。
description.en: Provided by subordinate chiefs and local communities, these troops could be raised quickly in war but were less uniformly trained and equipped than the royal standing forces.

description.zh: 战象用于冲击、威慑和王权象征，伴随精锐护卫作战，在南印度战争中具有重要心理和战术价值。
description.en: War elephants served for shock action, intimidation, and royal display, fighting alongside elite guards and carrying significant psychological and tactical value in South Indian warfare.

description.zh: 负责港口秩序、税关保护、海岸巡逻和护卫商船近岸活动，不是远洋海军，但具备近海安保功能。
description.en: Responsible for port order, customs protection, coastal patrol, and safeguarding merchant activity near shore; not a blue-water navy, but capable of littoral security.

commanderInChief.zh: 曼塔兰·哲罗·伊伦波赖国王

technology.zh: 军备以铁制兵器、弓箭、盾牌、战象和木制攻守器具为主，善于山地通道作战和热带环境下机动，但缺乏大型攻城技术与复杂常备军体系。
technology.en: Military technology relied on iron weapons, bows, shields, war elephants, and wooden siege or defensive implements; the Cheras were effective in hill-pass warfare and tropical campaigning, but lacked advanced large-scale siege techniques and a deeply institutionalized standing army.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与朱罗、潘地亚等南印度竞争者的边境冲突、港口争夺和内陆通道控制，以及附属首领可能的离心倾向。
threats.en: Main threats came from frontier clashes with South Indian rivals such as the Cholas and Pandyas, competition over ports and inland routes, and the centrifugal tendencies of subordinate chiefs.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 复合弓或长弓，远射主力
description.en: Composite or longbow, primary ranged weapon

description.zh: 覆盖全身的重装骑兵铁甲
description.en: Full-body heavy cavalry iron barding

description.zh: 可连发十矢的机械弩，守城利器
description.en: Mechanical crossbow firing ten bolts in succession, excellent for defense

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布在西南海岸平原、河谷、港口带与山麓地带，内陆森林和高地人口较稀疏。由于海贸繁荣，沿海存在一定数量外来商人与侨居群体。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the southwest coastal plain, river valleys, port belt, and foothill zones, while inland forests and uplands were more sparsely settled. Maritime trade brought a noticeable presence of foreign merchants and resident trading communities along the coast.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王族、贵族、祭司和大商人；中层为自由农民、地方武士、航海者与工匠；下层为佃作者、劳役群体、渔民和依附性较强的服务人口。社会身份深受宗族、职业和地方习惯影响。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of the royal house, nobles, ritual specialists, and major merchants; the middle strata included free cultivators, local warriors, sailors, and artisans; the lower strata comprised tenant laborers, dependent workers, fisherfolk, and service groups. Social identity was strongly shaped by clan, occupation, and local custom.

allies.zh: 无严格意义上的固定远程盟友；与部分南印度地方首领、阿拉伯海商人与罗马埃及商人保持互利关系。
allies.en: There were no strict permanent long-distance allies; the Cheras maintained mutually beneficial relations with some South Indian local chiefs, Arabian Sea merchants, and traders from Roman Egypt.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对者为朱罗与潘地亚等竞争王国；在贸易与边地问题上也可能与西海岸其他地方势力发生摩擦。
enemies.en: The principal adversaries were rival kingdoms such as the Cholas and Pandyas; friction could also arise with other west-coast powers over trade and frontier matters.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制香料海岸、保障商路、吸引外商、维护港口中立交易环境并压制邻近竞争者为核心。对于远方大国，哲罗更重视商业联系而非正式同盟。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on controlling the spice coast, securing trade routes, attracting overseas merchants, maintaining neutral port conditions for exchange, and checking nearby rivals. Toward distant great powers, the Cheras prioritized commerce over formal alliance.

era.zh: 南印度桑伽姆时代晚期与印度洋古典贸易时代
era.en: Late South Indian Sangam Age and Classical Indian Ocean trade era

keyInnovations.zh: 季风航海知识、港口称量与仓储管理、胡椒与山地香料贸易组织、铁器生产、珠宝与玻璃再加工，以及适应热带海贸的造船与近岸运输技术
keyInnovations.en: Monsoon navigation knowledge, port weighing and storage management, organized pepper and upland spice trade, iron production, jewelry and glass reworking, and shipbuilding and coastal transport techniques suited to tropical maritime exchange

strengths.zh: 哲罗的最大优势在于控制高价值香料产区与面向阿拉伯海、红海的贸易港口，能够将地理位置转化为关税、贵金属流入和政治声望。其文化活力和港口开放性也增强了王国吸引力。
strengths.en: The Cheras' greatest strengths lay in their control of high-value spice-producing regions and ports facing the Arabian Sea and Red Sea, allowing them to convert geography into customs income, bullion inflow, and political prestige. Their cultural vitality and openness of the ports also increased the kingdom's attractiveness.

weaknesses.zh: 王国的统合程度不如高度官僚化帝国，对地方首领依赖较深；财政和王权稳定性又受海贸波动、港口淤积、竞争王国袭扰和内陆控制有限等因素影响。
weaknesses.en: The kingdom was less integrated than highly bureaucratic empires and depended heavily on local chiefs; fiscal health and royal stability were also vulnerable to fluctuations in maritime trade, port silting, harassment by rival kingdoms, and limited penetration into the interior.

outlook.zh: 220年时哲罗王国总体稳定，若能继续掌握胡椒出口和主要港口，其财富与影响仍可维持；但长期前景取决于海贸线路、港口环境变化以及与朱罗、潘地亚竞争的结果。
outlook.en: In 220 CE the Chera kingdom was broadly stable, and if it continued to control pepper exports and key ports, its wealth and influence could be sustained; however, its longer-term outlook depended on trade routes, changing port conditions, and the outcome of competition with the Cholas and Pandyas.

description.zh: 切拉控制印度西南香料海岸，是印度洋贸易的重要参与者。
description.en: The Cheras controlled the southwest spice coast and were major participants in Indian Ocean trade.

governmentForm.zh: 以世袭君主为核心、依托地方酋长与宫廷官员治理的泰米尔君主制
governmentForm.en: A Tamil hereditary monarchy centered on a king, administered through local chieftains and court officials

socialStructure.zh: 社会以王室、贵族与维拉尔武士土地持有者为上层，其下为祭司、商人、农民、牧民、工匠、渔民与劳役群体；地方氏族和职业共同体在社会组织中具有重要地位。
socialStructure.en: Society was headed by the royal family, nobles, and velir warrior-landholders, followed by priests, merchants, cultivators, pastoralists, artisans, fishing communities, and laboring groups; local clans and occupational communities played an important role in social organization.

rulingClass.zh: 朱罗王室、宫廷贵族、维拉尔诸侯与高级祭司顾问
rulingClass.en: The Chola royal house, court nobility, velir chieftains, and senior priestly advisers

succession.zh: 王位通常由王族男性世袭继承，但在王室分支竞争、内战和贵族支持影响下，继承并不总是稳定。
succession.en: The throne normally passed hereditarily within the royal male line, but succession was often unstable because of rival royal branches, civil conflict, and noble backing.

structure.zh: 中央政权规模较小，以国王宫廷为核心，依靠大臣、军事随从、税吏、港口监督者和地方首领共同维持统治；边远地区更多采取宗主权与贡赋关系，而非严密官僚直辖。
structure.en: The central state was relatively compact, built around the king's court and sustained by ministers, military retainers, tax collectors, port supervisors, and local chiefs; outlying areas were often held through overlordship and tribute rather than tight bureaucratic rule.

function.zh: 管理王宫、仪式、使节接待、王室仓储与宫廷文书
function.en: Managed the palace, ceremonies, diplomatic reception, royal storehouses, and court records

function.zh: 征收谷物、土地贡赋、关税与港口税，并管理粮仓与再分配
function.en: Collected grain dues, land tribute, customs, and port taxes, and managed granaries and redistribution

function.zh: 统筹王室卫队、战时征召、要塞与边境警戒，并协调属下酋长兵力
function.en: Coordinated the royal guard, wartime levies, forts and border watch, and the forces of subordinate chiefs

function.zh: 监督河港和海岸贸易点、征收商税、保障航运并管理外来商人
function.en: Oversaw river ports and coastal trade points, levied merchant taxes, protected shipping, and supervised foreign traders

function.zh: 维系与地方酋长、村社首领和附属地区的关系，核定贡赋与服役义务
function.en: Maintained ties with local chiefs, village heads, and subordinate territories, and assessed tribute and service obligations

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以村社、氏族首领和地区酋长为基础。王权通过任命代理人、征税人和军事代表介入核心区域，而在外围更多依赖盟约、婚姻和朝贡关系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on villages, clan heads, and regional chiefs. Royal authority intervened more directly in core zones through agents, tax collectors, and military representatives, while peripheral areas were governed more through alliance, marriage, and tribute ties.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以王命、习惯法、氏族规范和宗教伦理并行为主。争端多由地方长老、酋长或王廷裁断，刑罚包括罚金、赔偿、没收和武力惩治。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on royal command, customary practice, clan norms, and religious ethics. Disputes were often judged by local elders, chiefs, or the royal court, with punishments including fines, compensation, confiscation, and coercive enforcement.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以农业贡赋为主，包括稻谷、牲畜、劳役和地方贡品；另有贸易关税、港口税、市场税和战时附加征发。实物征收比货币征收更常见。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was centered on agrarian tribute, including paddy, livestock, labor service, and regional produce; trade customs, port duties, market taxes, and wartime extractions also existed. Collection in kind was more common than monetary taxation.

religion.zh: 宗教以早期泰米尔多神崇拜为主，敬奉穆鲁干、湿婆、毗湿奴、科特拉瓦伊等神灵，并有祖先崇拜、英雄石传统及对圣树、河流和山丘的祭祀。耆那教与佛教在商路与城市环境中也有一定影响。
religion.en: Religion was primarily early Tamil polytheism, honoring deities such as Murugan, Shiva, Vishnu, and Korravai, along with ancestor worship, the hero-stone tradition, and cults of sacred trees, rivers, and hills. Jainism and Buddhism also had some influence in trading and urban settings.

philosophy.zh: 思想以泰米尔英雄伦理、赠与声望、王道观念与达摩观相结合；诗歌传统强调荣誉、忠诚、爱情、战争与慷慨，婆罗门教思想逐渐增强其在王权合法性中的作用。
philosophy.en: Thought combined Tamil heroic ethics, gift-based prestige, ideals of kingship, and concepts of dharma; poetic tradition emphasized honor, loyalty, love, war, and generosity, while Brahmanical ideas were increasingly important in legitimizing royal rule.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用泰米尔-婆罗米文字记录铭文、商业标记与简短文书，口传诗歌传统仍占主导地位。
writingSystem.en: Tamil-Brahmi was the principal script for inscriptions, commercial marks, and short records, while oral poetic tradition remained dominant.

culturalAchievements.zh: 朱罗文化属于桑伽时代文明圈，以泰米尔诗歌、英雄赞歌、宫廷赞助、珠饰工艺、纺织、港市文化和对内陆与海洋贸易网络的参与著称。
culturalAchievements.en: Chola culture belonged to the Sangam-age civilization, notable for Tamil poetry, heroic panegyrics, court patronage, bead craftsmanship, textiles, port culture, and participation in inland and maritime trade networks.

languageFamily.zh: 达罗毗荼语系，以古泰米尔语为主
languageFamily.en: Dravidian language family, primarily Old Tamil

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以稻作农业为基础，辅以畜牧、渔业、手工业和区域海贸。乌赖尤尔及东海岸贸易节点通过纺织品、珍珠、宝石和香料连接印度洋市场。整体财富在南印度诸国中较为可观，但国家对资源的直接控制仍有限。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on wet-rice agriculture, supplemented by pastoralism, fishing, handicrafts, and regional maritime trade. Uraiyur and east-coast trading nodes linked textiles, pearls, gemstones, and spices to the wider Indian Ocean market. Overall wealth was considerable among South Indian polities, though direct state control over resources remained limited.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻米种植、棉纺织、染色、珍珠与海产、制盐、冶铁、珠饰加工、陶器、内河与沿海贸易
mainIndustries.en: Rice cultivation, cotton weaving, dyeing, pearls and marine products, salt production, ironworking, bead processing, pottery, and riverine and coastal trade

tradeGoods.zh: 棉布、细织物、珍珠、宝石、象牙、胡椒与香料、盐、铁器、贝壳制品，以及输入的罗马金币、酒、玻璃器和珊瑚
tradeGoods.en: Cotton cloth, fine textiles, pearls, gemstones, ivory, pepper and spices, salt, iron goods, shell products, and imports such as Roman gold coins, wine, glassware, and coral

name.zh: 混合货币与实物交换体系
name.en: Mixed coinage and commodity exchange system

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距显著。农户通常拥有少量土地、牲畜和粮储；商人、珠宝商、盐商与港口中介可积累较多动产；贵族与酋长则掌控土地、战利品和贡赋流入。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Peasant families generally held small plots, livestock, and grain stores; merchants, jewel traders, salt dealers, and port intermediaries could accumulate more movable wealth; nobles and chiefs controlled land, spoils, and tribute inflows.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业村社为基础、由王室再分配、酋长征收和市场交换并存的区域性贡赋经济，兼具活跃的海上商业成分。
economicSystem.en: A regional tribute economy based on agrarian villages, with royal redistribution, chieftain exaction, and market exchange coexisting alongside a lively maritime commercial sector.

source.zh: 市场税、罚金与杂项收入
source.en: Market taxes, fines, and miscellaneous income

category.zh: 地方盟约、赠赐与酋长安抚
category.en: Local alliances, gifts, and chieftain pacification

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由现金、金银饰物、进口金币、珠宝、谷仓存粮与可动员贡品共同构成。因内战与地方冲突，储备虽仍可观，但承压明显。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of cash, precious ornaments, imported gold coins, jewels, granary reserves, and mobilizable tribute goods. Because of civil strife and regional conflict, reserves remained significant but were under visible pressure.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持王权与战争动员，通过提高贡赋、强化港税和向地方精英分配战利品与礼物来稳固统治；其代价是财政波动较大，和平时期盈余有限。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized preserving royal authority and wartime mobilization, using increased tribute, stronger port taxation, and the redistribution of spoils and gifts to local elites; the cost was substantial volatility and limited peacetime surplus.

description.zh: 以持矛、剑、盾和弓的步兵为主体，是战时主力，既包括王室直属兵，也包括酋长提供的部众。
description.en: The main fighting arm, composed of spearmen, swordsmen, shield-bearers, and archers, including both royal troops and contingents supplied by chiefs.

description.zh: 用于冲击敌阵、震慑对手和彰显王权，多配有骑象战士与护卫。
description.en: Used to break enemy formations, intimidate opponents, and display royal prestige, usually with mahouts and guards.

description.zh: 数量较少，主要用于侦察、追击、通信与快速打击，马匹部分依赖外部贸易输入。
description.en: Relatively limited in number, used for reconnaissance, pursuit, communication, and rapid strikes; horses were partly dependent on external trade.

description.zh: 由地方村社和附属首领临时征发，战斗力参差不齐，但在内战和区域争夺中不可或缺。
description.en: Raised temporarily from villages and subordinate chiefs, uneven in quality but indispensable in civil wars and regional contests.

description.zh: 以轻型船只、运输船和护航船为主，用于沿岸运输、河口巡逻、护商与有限的两栖行动。
description.en: Comprised mainly of light craft, transports, and escort vessels for coastal transport, estuary patrol, merchant protection, and limited amphibious operations.

commanderInChief.zh: 内敦基利王亲掌最高军权，依靠王族将领与地方酋长统兵
commanderInChief.en: King Nedunkilli held supreme command, relying on royal kinsmen and local chieftains as field commanders

technology.zh: 军备处于南印度铁器时代后期水平，普遍使用铁制矛、剑、箭镞、皮盾与木盾，配合战象、攻围和机动突袭。海军技术以季风航行经验、木船建造和近岸航运为强项。
technology.en: Military technology reflected the late South Indian Iron Age, with widespread use of iron spears, swords, arrowheads, leather and wooden shields, combined with war elephants, sieges, and mobile raids. Naval strengths lay in monsoon navigation, wooden shipbuilding, and coastal sailing.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与潘地亚、哲罗及朱罗王族内部对手的持续冲突，以及地方酋长叛离、贸易通道受扰和港口控制权争夺。
threats.en: The main threats came from continuing conflict with the Pandyas, the Cheras, and rival Chola claimants, as well as defection by local chiefs, disruption of trade routes, and struggles over port control.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

description.zh: 覆盖全身的重装骑兵铁甲
description.en: Full-body heavy cavalry iron barding

description.zh: 水战拍竿，击毁敌船上层结构
description.en: Ship-mounted swinging beam to smash enemy superstructures

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布在卡韦里河流域及其灌溉农区、内陆村庄、沿海渔业聚落和若干贸易城镇。人口密度在稻作区较高，外围森林与干旱地带较低。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the Kaveri basin and its irrigated agrarian zones, inland villages, coastal fishing settlements, and a few trade towns. Density was highest in the rice-growing districts and lower in forested and drier peripheral areas.

name.zh: 科杜姆巴卢尔地区城镇

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括王室与贵族、维拉尔酋长、婆罗门与祭司、商人与航海者、工匠、农民、牧民、渔民及依附性劳役群体；社会等级存在，但尚未形成后世那种更完备的固定种姓结构。
socialClasses.en: Major strata included the royal family and nobility, velir chiefs, Brahmins and ritual specialists, merchants and seafarers, artisans, cultivators, pastoralists, fishers, and dependent laboring groups; hierarchy existed, but the later more rigid caste order was not yet fully developed.

allies.zh: 无稳定大型盟友；主要依靠与部分地方酋长、商人团体及临时结盟对象维持力量平衡。
allies.en: No stable major allies; the kingdom relied mainly on selected local chiefs, merchant interests, and temporary partners to maintain balance.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对力量为潘地亚、哲罗以及朱罗内部敌对王族支系；在贸易与边境上也与若干沿海与内陆首领存在摩擦。
enemies.en: The principal enemies were the Pandyas, the Cheras, and rival branches within the Chola line; there were also frictions with various coastal and inland chiefs over trade and borders.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维护卡韦里流域核心地位、争夺泰米尔地区霸权、掌控贸易节点并吸引海外商人为中心。对更远方国家多通过间接贸易联系而非正式外交往来。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving dominance in the Kaveri basin, competing for hegemony in the Tamil region, controlling trade nodes, and attracting overseas merchants. More distant states were engaged mainly through indirect trade rather than formal diplomacy.

era.zh: 南印度桑伽时代晚期铁器文明
era.en: Late Sangam-age Iron Age civilization of South India

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的稻作灌溉、铁器冶炼与兵器制造、棉纺与染织、珠饰加工、近海航运、季风贸易组织，以及利用泰米尔-婆罗米进行铭刻与商贸标识。
keyInnovations.en: Key developments included mature rice irrigation, iron smelting and weapon-making, cotton weaving and dyeing, bead craftsmanship, coastal navigation, monsoon trade organization, and the use of Tamil-Brahmi for inscriptions and commercial marking.

strengths.zh: 朱罗王国拥有肥沃的卡韦里农业基础、活跃的海上与区域贸易、悠久的王室声望以及可动员的地方武力网络，在南印度竞争格局中仍具重要地位。
strengths.en: The Chola Kingdom possessed the fertile agricultural base of the Kaveri, active maritime and regional trade, long-standing royal prestige, and a mobilizable network of local armed support, giving it an important place in South Indian power politics.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于中央官僚能力有限、对地方酋长依赖较强、继承不稳、内战频发，并且财政与军力容易因持续冲突而被迅速消耗。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in limited central bureaucracy, heavy dependence on local chiefs, unstable succession, frequent civil conflict, and finances and military strength that could be rapidly drained by prolonged warfare.

outlook.zh: 220年时朱罗仍是重要的泰米尔王国，但正处于内外冲突压力之下。若能重新统一王室、稳固对河谷与港口的控制，仍可维持区域强国地位；若内斗延续，则可能被潘地亚、哲罗或地方势力进一步削弱。
outlook.en: In 220 CE the Cholas remained a significant Tamil kingdom, but one under pressure from both internal and external conflict. If the royal line could be reunified and control over the river basin and ports strengthened, the kingdom could remain a major regional power; if factional struggle continued, it risked further weakening at the hands of the Pandyas, the Cheras, or local powers.

description.zh: 朱罗是泰米尔地区古老王国之一，在区域争霸与海贸中占据一席之地。
description.en: The Cholas were one of the ancient Tamil kingdoms, active in regional rivalries and maritime trade.

governmentForm.zh: 以世袭君主为核心的泰米尔地区王国，依靠宫廷、地方酋长与港口网络统治
governmentForm.en: A hereditary Tamil monarchy centered on the king, ruling through the court, local chieftains, and a network of ports

socialStructure.zh: 社会以王室与贵族、武士与地方首领、祭司与诗人、商人、农民、牧民、渔民、珍珠采集者及劳役群体构成，宗族、职业与地域联系强烈
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house and nobles, warriors and local chiefs, priests and poets, merchants, cultivators, pastoralists, fisherfolk, pearl-divers, and laboring groups, with strong kinship, occupational, and regional ties

rulingClass.zh: 潘地亚王室、宫廷贵族、军事首领、拥有土地与税权的地方酋长，以及与王室合作的祭司和大商人
rulingClass.en: The Pandya royal family, court nobles, military leaders, local chieftains holding land and revenue rights, and allied priests and major merchants

succession.zh: 王位通常由王族世袭继承，多在父子或近支宗族之间传递，但需得到宫廷精英与地方首领承认
succession.en: The throne was normally hereditary within the royal house, usually passing between father and son or close agnatic kin, but required acceptance by court elites and local chiefs

structure.zh: 中央政府围绕国王与马杜赖宫廷运作，下设财政、军务、港务、司法与礼仪等职能；地方由附属酋长、村社首领与港口官员执行王命并征收赋税
structure.en: The central government operated around the king and the Madurai court, with functions for finance, military affairs, ports, justice, and ritual; locally, subordinate chiefs, village leaders, and port officials enforced royal authority and collected revenue

function.zh: 起草诏令、记录贡赋、保存盟约与宫廷事务文书
function.en: Drafted royal orders, recorded tribute and taxes, and kept agreements and court records

function.zh: 管理地税、港口关税、市场税、贡赋及王室仓储
function.en: Managed land revenue, port customs, market dues, tribute, and royal granaries

function.zh: 统筹王家卫队、步兵、象兵、边地守备与战时征召
function.en: Coordinated the royal guard, infantry, elephant corps, frontier garrisons, and wartime levies

function.zh: 监管科尔凯等港口、珍珠渔场、对外贸易与外商税收
function.en: Supervised ports such as Korkai, pearl fisheries, foreign commerce, and taxes on overseas merchants

function.zh: 处理刑事案件、财产争议、商贸纠纷与地方申诉
function.en: Handled criminal cases, property disputes, commercial conflicts, and local petitions

function.zh: 主持王室祭祀、节庆、馈赠诗人和宗教机构的典礼安排
function.en: Oversaw royal rituals, festivals, and ceremonial patronage to poets and religious establishments

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理并非高度官僚化，而是由王国直辖地区、附属酋邦、村社 assemblies 与港口管理点共同构成；王权在马杜赖周边最强，在边缘地区更多依赖盟属首领
localAdmin.en: Local administration was not highly bureaucratized, but combined directly controlled royal districts, subordinate chiefdoms, village assemblies, and port authorities; royal power was strongest around Madurai and relied more on allied chiefs in peripheral zones

legalSystem.zh: 法律以王命、习惯法、宗族规范、商贸惯例和宗教性誓约为基础；国王被视为最高裁断者，地方争端常由长老、酋长或寺祭群体调解
legalSystem.en: Law rested on royal decree, customary practice, kin norms, mercantile usage, and religious oath-taking; the king was the highest judge, while local disputes were often mediated by elders, chiefs, or ritual authorities

taxationSystem.zh: 财政来源包括农产税、灌溉地租、港口关税、市场税、珍珠渔场收益、附属首领贡赋及战利品；税收常以粮食、珍珠、牲畜、布匹和钱币混合征纳
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came from agrarian taxes, rents on irrigated land, port customs, market dues, pearl fishery income, tribute from subordinate chiefs, and war booty; taxation was collected in mixed forms such as grain, pearls, livestock, textiles, and coin

religion.zh: 宗教以早期印度教的湿婆、毗湿奴、穆鲁干、科特拉维等神祇崇拜为主，并与英雄崇拜、祖先祭祀、地方神灵信仰共存；耆那教和佛教在商路与城市中也有一定影响
religion.en: Religion was centered on early Hindu worship of deities such as Shiva, Vishnu, Murugan, and Korravai, alongside hero cults, ancestor rites, and local goddesses; Jainism and Buddhism also had some influence in urban and mercantile settings

philosophy.zh: 思想传统融合达摩观念、王道伦理、英雄荣誉、诗歌礼赠文化与南印度地方宗教观念；宫廷文学强调名誉、慷慨、爱情与战争
philosophy.en: Intellectual life blended ideas of dharma, royal ethics, heroic honor, poetic patronage, and South Indian local religious concepts; court literature emphasized fame, generosity, love, and war

writingSystem.zh: 书写主要使用泰米尔-婆罗米文字，行政与贸易中也接触婆罗米书写传统；铭文、陶片题记与商业标记较常见
writingSystem.en: Writing mainly used the Tamil-Brahmi script, with contact with broader Brahmi traditions in administration and trade; inscriptions, pottery graffiti, and commercial marks were common

culturalAchievements.zh: 潘地亚宫廷是泰米尔诗歌传统的重要中心之一，与桑伽姆文学传统密切相关；珍珠贸易、城市市场、王室馈赠与诗人赞歌共同塑造了高度活跃的宫廷文化
culturalAchievements.en: The Pandya court was one of the major centers of classical Tamil poetic culture, closely associated with the Sangam tradition; pearl trade, urban markets, royal gift-giving, and bardic praise poetry fostered a vibrant court culture

languageFamily.zh: 达罗毗荼语系，以古泰米尔语为主
languageFamily.en: Dravidian language family, primarily Old Tamil

unit.zh: 罗马银第纳里乌斯等值
罗马银第纳里乌斯等值/人/年
unit.en: Roman silver denarii equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以稻作农业、畜牧、内陆与海港贸易、珍珠采集和手工业为核心。按南印度古代王国规模估算，潘地亚在220年属于富庶但非超大型帝国的区域强国，财富高度集中在王室、港口商人与地方精英手中。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was centered on wet-rice agriculture, pastoralism, inland and maritime trade, pearl extraction, and craft production. By the standards of ancient South Indian kingdoms, the Pandya realm in 220 CE was prosperous but not an enormous empire, with wealth heavily concentrated in the royal house, port merchants, and local elites.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻米种植、珍珠采集、渔业、盐业、纺织、珠饰加工、金属工艺、象牙与宝石贸易、海运和市场中转
mainIndustries.en: Rice cultivation, pearl diving, fishing, salt production, textiles, bead-making, metalworking, ivory and gem trade, shipping, and market redistribution

tradeGoods.zh: 珍珠、贝壳、细布、棉织物、香料、象牙、宝石、黑胡椒、盐、鱼制品，以及输入的金银币、酒、玻璃器和珊瑚
tradeGoods.en: Pearls, shell products, fine cloth, cotton textiles, spices, ivory, gemstones, black pepper, salt, fish products, and imported gold and silver coin, wine, glassware, and coral

name.zh: 潘地亚钱币与罗马输入钱币并用
name.en: Pandyan coinage alongside imported Roman coin

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富多以土地使用权、粮食存量、牲畜、渔具、首饰和宗族支持体现；沿海商人与珍珠业相关家庭明显更富
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was usually expressed through access to land, grain stocks, livestock, fishing gear, ornaments, and kin support; coastal merchants and pearl-related households were notably richer

unit.zh: 罗马银第纳里乌斯等值/劳动者/年
unit.en: Roman silver denarii equivalent per worker per year

unit.zh: 罗马银第纳里乌斯等值/年
unit.en: Roman silver denarii equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以农业为基础、由王室再分配和港口商业驱动的混合经济；货币使用增长，但实物税与礼赠交换仍然十分重要
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on agriculture and driven by royal redistribution and port commerce; monetization was increasing, but payment in kind and gift exchange remained very important

unit.zh: 罗马银第纳里乌斯等值/年
unit.en: Roman silver denarii equivalent per year

unit.en: Roman silver denarii equivalent per year

unit.en: Roman silver denarii equivalent per year

source.en: Tribute from subordinate chiefs and forfeitures

category.zh: 灌溉、仓储与道路港口维护
category.en: Irrigation, storage, roads, and port maintenance

unit.zh: 罗马银第纳里乌斯等值
罗马银第纳里乌斯等值
罗马银第纳里乌斯等值

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由钱币、金银器、珠宝、珍珠存货、粮仓储备和可随时征调的贡赋构成。沿海贸易使王室较易积累贵金属，但财富也易受海贸波动影响。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of coin, precious metal objects, jewels, pearl stocks, granary reserves, and tribute that could be called up when needed. Maritime trade allowed the crown to accumulate bullion relatively easily, though this wealth was also vulnerable to shifts in overseas commerce.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策总体稳健，重视维持港口畅通、控制珍珠收益、保障宫廷威望与维持对地方首领的赏赐平衡
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was generally prudent, emphasizing open ports, control of pearl revenues, maintenance of court prestige, and a balanced system of rewards to local chiefs

description.zh: 军队主力，由持矛、剑、盾和弓的步兵组成，承担野战与守城任务
description.en: The main arm of the army, composed of spear, sword, shield, and bow-armed foot soldiers for field operations and garrison duty

description.zh: 由附属酋长和村社提供，战时扩充兵力，训练和装备参差不齐
description.en: Raised by subordinate chiefs and village communities in wartime, increasing numbers but with uneven training and equipment

description.zh: 用于冲击、威慑和王室仪仗，兼有精锐近卫功能
description.en: Used for shock, intimidation, and royal display, while also serving as elite guard units

description.zh: 规模较小，部分马匹依赖外来贸易获得，主要用于侦察、追击和联络
description.en: Relatively small in number, with some horses obtained through trade, mainly used for reconnaissance, pursuit, and communications

description.zh: 负责港口秩序、近海巡防、缉私与保护珍珠渔场
description.en: Responsible for port order, coastal patrol, anti-smuggling, and protection of pearl fisheries

technology.zh: 军备以铁制兵器、弓箭、盾牌、战象和木制攻守器械为主，无高度机械化攻城技术，但适应南印度地形与短程作战
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron weapons, bows, shields, war elephants, and wooden siege or defensive devices, lacking highly sophisticated siege machinery but well adapted to South Indian terrain and short-range campaigning

unit.zh: 罗马银第纳里乌斯等值/年
unit.en: Roman silver denarii equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与朱罗、哲罗之间的竞争、地方首领叛离、海岸贸易受阻，以及内陆通道控制权争夺
threats.en: Main threats came from rivalry with the Cholas and Cheras, defection of local chiefs, disruption to coastal trade, and struggles over inland routes

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

description.zh: 覆盖全身的重装骑兵铁甲
description.en: Full-body heavy cavalry iron barding

description.zh: 复合弓或长弓，远射主力
description.en: Composite or longbow, primary ranged weapon

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布在马杜赖周边农业区、沿海港口与渔业带，以及河谷和灌溉聚落。整体人口密度在南印度属于较高水平，但仍以乡村居住为主。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the agrarian zone around Madurai, coastal ports and fishing belts, and riverine and irrigated settlements. Population density was relatively high by South Indian standards, though the realm remained predominantly rural.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、贵族、酋长、祭司与富商；中层包括自由农、手工业者、船主、诗人和武士；下层包括佃作者、劳工、渔民、珍珠采集者与依附性较强的服务群体
socialClasses.en: Upper strata included the royal family, nobles, chiefs, priests, and wealthy merchants; middle strata included free cultivators, artisans, shipowners, poets, and warriors; lower strata included tenant cultivators, laborers, fishers, pearl-divers, and more dependent service groups

allies.zh: 无稳定意义上的正式大国盟友，但与部分海商集团、南印度地方首领及印度洋贸易伙伴保持务实合作
allies.en: There were no permanent great-power allies in a formal sense, but the kingdom maintained pragmatic cooperation with maritime merchant groups, South Indian local chiefs, and Indian Ocean trade partners

enemies.zh: 主要竞争者为朱罗与哲罗，两者在贸易通道、边境地带和声望上与潘地亚长期角逐
enemies.en: The principal rivals were the Cholas and Cheras, with whom the Pandyas competed over trade routes, border zones, and prestige

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持港口贸易、吸引海外商人、保护珍珠渔场、平衡南印度诸强和确保地方首领归附为重点
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on maintaining port trade, attracting overseas merchants, protecting pearl fisheries, balancing South Indian powers, and ensuring the loyalty of local chiefs

era.zh: 南印度早期历史时代，属泰米尔桑伽姆晚期传统，与同时期三国时代大体并行
era.en: Early historic South India, belonging to the later Sangam-age tradition and broadly contemporary with the Three Kingdoms era

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的铁器使用、灌溉农业、海港组织、珠饰加工、长距离海上贸易网络，以及适应热带海岸环境的珍珠采集技术
keyInnovations.en: Mature iron use, irrigated agriculture, organized port management, bead-making, long-distance maritime trade networks, and pearl-diving techniques adapted to tropical coastal waters

strengths.zh: 控制著名珍珠产区、拥有活跃海贸港口、马杜赖宫廷文化声望高、农业与海洋经济互补，王权在核心区较稳固
strengths.en: The kingdom controlled famous pearl-producing zones, possessed active maritime ports, enjoyed high cultural prestige at the Madurai court, combined agrarian and maritime wealth, and maintained fairly solid royal authority in its core territory

weaknesses.zh: 政治整合度不如大型帝国，依赖地方首领合作；骑兵薄弱，区域间控制不均；财政对海贸和珍珠收入依赖较大，易受竞争与航路变化影响
weaknesses.en: Political integration was weaker than in major empires and depended on cooperation from local chiefs; cavalry was limited, control was uneven across regions, and finances depended heavily on maritime trade and pearl income, making them sensitive to competition and route changes

outlook.zh: 220年前后的潘地亚王国总体稳定且富有区域影响力。若能维持港口繁荣并压制朱罗、哲罗竞争，其地位可继续稳固；若海贸衰退或地方首领离心，王国实力将迅速下降。
outlook.en: Around 220 CE, the Pandya kingdom was generally stable and regionally influential. If it could preserve port prosperity and contain Chola and Chera rivalry, its position could remain secure; if maritime trade declined or local chiefs drifted away, royal power would weaken quickly.

description.zh: 潘地亚以马杜赖为中心，是泰米尔南部的重要王国与珍珠贸易中心。
