description.en: Centered on Madurai, the Pandyas were a major kingdom in southern Tamilakam and a hub of pearl trade.

governmentForm.zh: 以王權為核心的印度化早期海岸王國，結合地方酋長、宮廷官員與港市網絡統治
governmentForm.en: A king-centered early Indianized coastal monarchy combining local chieftains, court officials, and port-town networks

socialStructure.zh: 社會由王室與貴族、地方首領、祭司與婆羅門、商人與工匠、農民、漁民及依附人口構成；山地與沿海社群保持較強地方性
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house and nobles, local chiefs, priests and Brahmins, merchants and artisans, peasants, fisherfolk, and dependent populations; upland and coastal communities retained strong local identities

rulingClass.zh: 王族、沿海城邑貴族、控制河口與貿易節點的地方首領，以及受王室庇護的宗教精英
rulingClass.en: The ruling class comprised the royal clan, urban coastal nobles, local chiefs controlling estuaries and trade nodes, and religious elites patronized by the crown

succession.zh: 以王族世襲為主，實際繼承常受宮廷支持、地方豪強認可與軍事實力影響
succession.en: Succession was principally hereditary within the royal line, though actual accession depended on court backing, recognition by regional magnates, and military strength

structure.zh: 林邑在220年仍屬早期國家形態，中央宮廷直接控制都城、周邊平原與主要港口，對外圍地區則透過附庸首領、納貢與婚盟實施間接統治
structure.en: In 220 CE Linyi remained an early state formation: the central court directly controlled the capital, nearby plains, and major ports, while outer districts were governed indirectly through subordinate chiefs, tribute, and marriage alliances

function.zh: 管理宮廷、王室土地、儀典、使節接待與王命傳達
function.en: Managed the palace, royal lands, ceremonies, reception of envoys, and communication of royal orders

function.zh: 徵收稻穀、林產、海產與手工品，管理糧倉與實物稅轉運
function.en: Collected rice, forest products, marine goods, and crafts, and managed granaries and transport of payments in kind

function.zh: 監督海港、市場、商旅登記、航路安全與關稅徵收
function.en: Supervised harbors, markets, merchant registration, sea-lane security, and customs collection

function.zh: 統籌王家衛隊、地方兵役、要塞守備、海岸巡邏與戰時徵發
function.en: Coordinated the royal guard, local levies, fort garrisons, coastal patrols, and wartime mobilization

function.zh: 主持王室祭儀、管理神廟供奉、曆法吉日與王權神聖化儀式
function.en: Oversaw royal rites, temple endowments, calendrical observances, and ceremonies sacralizing kingship

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以河谷、港口與聚落為核心，由王室任命或承認的首領治理；中央派出稅吏、倉官與軍使監督，但多依賴地方族群權威與人際網絡
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on river valleys, ports, and settlements governed by chiefs appointed or recognized by the king; the center sent tax collectors, granary officials, and military agents, but relied heavily on local clan authority and personal networks

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要由習慣法、王命、賠償慣例與宗教誓約構成，尚未形成完整成文法典；土地、水利、婚姻、債務與貢賦爭議多由地方首領或宮廷裁決
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based mainly on customary law, royal decrees, compensation practices, and religious oaths rather than a full written code; disputes over land, irrigation, marriage, debt, and tribute were settled by local chiefs or the court

taxationSystem.zh: 稅制以實物徵收為主，包括稻穀、椰產、魚鹽、沉香、象牙、犀角、木材與勞役；港口另徵關稅、市稅及停泊費
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was primarily collected in kind, including rice, coconut products, fish and salt, agarwood, ivory, rhinoceros horn, timber, and labor service; ports also levied customs duties, market tolls, and anchorage fees

religion.zh: 宗教處於本地祖靈與自然崇拜同印度宗教觀念融合的階段，濕婆與其他婆羅門教神祇影響開始增強，同時保留海神、山神與祖先祭祀
religion.en: Religion was in a phase of fusion between indigenous ancestor and nature worship and Indian religious ideas; the influence of Shiva and other Brahmanical deities was growing, while sea spirits, mountain spirits, and ancestor cults remained important

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成系統化哲學傳統，政治與倫理觀念主要來自王權神授、祖先榮耀、地方習俗，以及經由海上交流傳入的印度宇宙觀與王道觀念
philosophy.en: No fully systematized philosophical tradition yet existed; political and ethical ideas drew chiefly from sacral kingship, ancestral prestige, local custom, and Indian cosmological and royal ideals transmitted through maritime exchange

writingSystem.zh: 書寫處於早期發展階段，可能開始接觸並採用婆羅米系梵文書寫形式於宗教與王權場合，但行政與日常生活多仍依口傳與地方記錄習慣
writingSystem.en: Writing was at an early stage of development; Brahmi-derived Sanskrit writing forms were likely beginning to be adopted for religious and royal contexts, while administration and everyday life still depended largely on oral practice and local record habits

culturalAchievements.zh: 林邑已形成海岸港市文化、早期磚木宗教建築傳統、印度式王號與儀典、精於航海與區域交易，並在本地樂舞與祭儀中展現鮮明特色
culturalAchievements.en: Linyi had developed a coastal port culture, early brick-and-timber religious architecture, Indian-style royal titulature and ceremony, skill in seafaring and regional trade, and distinctive local music, dance, and ritual traditions

languageFamily.zh: 主要語言屬南島語系占語支前身，並受南亞與印度文化語彙影響
languageFamily.en: The main language belonged to the Austronesian lineage ancestral to Cham, with additional vocabulary influenced by South and Indian cultural contacts

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 林邑經濟規模在東南亞早期政權中屬中等偏強，依賴濕稻農業、漁鹽業、林產採集與沿海轉口貿易。雖不及大型帝國，但因控制中部越南海岸航點而具區域活力
gdpDescription.en: Linyi's economy was moderately strong by the standards of early Southeast Asian polities, relying on wet-rice agriculture, fishing and salt making, forest extraction, and coastal entrepôt trade. Though far smaller than major empires, it was regionally dynamic because it controlled sailing points along the central Vietnamese coast

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作農業、漁業、鹽業、木材與香料林產採集、造船、陶器、編織、象牙與角料貿易
mainIndustries.en: Rice agriculture, fishing, salt production, timber and aromatic forest extraction, boatbuilding, pottery, weaving, and trade in ivory and horn products

tradeGoods.zh: 出口稻米、鹽、魚乾、沉香、木材、象牙、犀角、龜甲、珍奇鳥獸與奴隸；進口珠飾、金屬器、鐵器、紡織品、玻璃珠、宗教用品與奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Exports included rice, salt, dried fish, agarwood, timber, ivory, rhinoceros horn, turtle shell, exotic animals, and slaves; imports included beads, metalwares, iron goods, textiles, glass beads, ritual items, and luxury products

householdWealth.zh: 大多數家庭財富有限，以稻田、漁具、牲畜、小舟與家屋為主；沿海商人、地方首領與宮廷近臣則積累珠寶、金屬器、奴婢與進口奢侈品
householdWealth.en: Most households possessed modest wealth centered on rice plots, fishing gear, livestock, small boats, and dwellings; coastal merchants, local chiefs, and palace retainers accumulated jewelry, metal goods, dependents, and imported luxuries

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 經濟體制為以農業村社為基礎、由王室與地方首領抽取貢賦的再分配體系，並輔以港口市場交易與長途海上交換
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a redistributive order based on agrarian village communities from which the crown and local chiefs extracted tribute, supplemented by port-market exchange and long-distance maritime trade

treasuryDescription.zh: 國庫規模不大，且多以稻穀、鹽、香木、珠飾、金屬器與貢品形式儲存，而非統一現金；其穩定性高度依賴港稅與地方效忠
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not large and was stored largely as rice, salt, aromatic wood, beads, metal goods, and tribute items rather than as uniform cash; its stability depended heavily on customs revenue and provincial loyalty

fiscalPolicy.zh: 財政政策傾向維持王室威望、贍養武裝與獎酬地方首領，避免對核心農村過度榨取，以免削弱糧食供應與忠誠
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed to sustain royal prestige, support armed forces, and reward local chiefs while avoiding excessive extraction from core farming communities that could undermine food supply and loyalty

description.zh: 以長矛、盾牌、短劍與弓為主，負責都城守衛、平原作戰與鎮壓叛亂
description.en: Armed mainly with spears, shields, short blades, and bows, they guarded the capital, fought in the plains, and suppressed rebellions

description.zh: 由地方首領在戰時動員，熟悉河谷、山地與叢林地形，但裝備與紀律不一
description.en: Mobilized by local chiefs in wartime, they were familiar with river valleys, uplands, and jungle terrain, though uneven in equipment and discipline

description.zh: 使用小型與中型木船巡邏海岸、護航商旅、突襲鄰近港口與運送兵力
description.en: Using small and medium wooden craft, they patrolled the coast, escorted merchants, raided nearby ports, and transported troops

description.zh: 直屬國王的精銳侍從與親兵，兼具儀仗、警衛與戰場突擊功能
description.en: Elite retainers directly attached to the king, serving ceremonial, protective, and battlefield shock roles

technology.zh: 軍事技術以鐵製兵器、竹木弓矛、藤盾、木柵要塞與沿海舟船為核心，善於熱帶環境機動與近岸作戰，但缺乏重型攻城器與大規模制式裝備
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron weapons, bamboo-and-wood bows and spears, rattan shields, timber palisades, and coastal vessels; forces were adept in tropical mobility and littoral warfare but lacked heavy siege equipment and large-scale standardized gear

threats.zh: 主要威脅來自北方漢地政權或其地方勢力的干預、與南方及內陸部族的衝突、港口控制權競爭，以及新生王權對地方首領約束不足所引發的內亂
threats.en: Principal threats came from intervention by northern Chinese regimes or their local authorities, conflicts with southern and inland tribal groups, competition over port control, and internal disorder caused by the young monarchy's limited hold over regional chiefs

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 单兵弩与床弩，中国军队核心远程武器
description.en: Infantry crossbow and mounted crossbow, core ranged weapon

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

description.zh: 复合弓或长弓，远射主力
description.en: Composite or longbow, primary ranged weapon

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布於中部越南沿海平原、河口、潟湖與若干內陸河谷。沿海聚落稠密，山地居民則較分散，整體人口在當時區域內屬可觀規模
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in the coastal plains, estuaries, lagoons, and certain inland river valleys of central Vietnam. Coastal settlements were relatively dense, while upland peoples were more dispersed; overall population was substantial for the region at the time

socialClasses.zh: 社會階層大致分為王族與貴族、地方酋長、祭司與宗教人士、商人與工匠、自由農民與漁民、依附勞動者與奴隸；族群與地域差異對身份有重要影響
socialClasses.en: Social classes were broadly divided into royalty and nobles, local chieftains, priests and religious specialists, merchants and artisans, free farmers and fisherfolk, and dependent laborers and slaves; ethnic and regional distinctions strongly shaped status

allies.zh: 林邑尚無穩固的正式盟邦，實際上與南方海上商人網絡、若干地方酋邦及可能的扶南商路保持互惠關係
allies.en: Linyi had no firmly institutionalized formal allies; in practice it maintained mutually beneficial ties with southern maritime merchant networks, certain local chiefdoms, and possibly routes connected to Funan

enemies.zh: 主要對立對象是北方中國勢力及其在交州、日南一帶的影響，同時也可能與鄰近沿海或內陸首領因貢賦、港口與邊界發生衝突
enemies.en: Its main adversaries were northern Chinese powers and their influence in Jiaozhou and the Rinan region, while neighboring coastal or inland chiefs could also become enemies over tribute, ports, and frontiers

foreignPolicy.zh: 對外政策以鞏固獨立、控制海岸節點、吸引商旅與在必要時以朝貢、示好或武力維持自主為主，具有靈活的海上外交特徵
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on consolidating independence, controlling coastal nodes, attracting merchants, and preserving autonomy through tribute, conciliation, or force as circumstances required, reflecting a flexible maritime diplomacy

era.zh: 三國時代的東南亞早期國家形成期
era.en: Early Southeast Asian state-formation phase during the Three Kingdoms Era

keyInnovations.zh: 關鍵技術包括濕稻耕作、水利管理、沿海航海與季風利用、木船建造、鐵器使用、鹽製造、磚木建築與來自印度世界的宗教象徵技術
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies included wet-rice cultivation, water management, coastal navigation and monsoon sailing, wooden shipbuilding, ironworking, salt production, brick-and-timber construction, and religious-symbolic techniques from the Indian world

strengths.zh: 林邑的優勢在於地處海上交通要衝、港口貿易活躍、王權具新興凝聚力、能整合沿海與河谷資源，並較早吸收印度化政治與宗教形式以提升統治正當性
strengths.en: Linyi's strengths lay in its location on important sea routes, active port trade, the new integrative force of kingship, its ability to combine coastal and riverine resources, and its early adoption of Indianized political and religious forms to enhance legitimacy

weaknesses.zh: 其弱點在於中央官僚與法制尚不成熟、地方首領自主性強、軍力有限、財政依賴貢賦與商稅，且容易受北方強權壓力與海路變動影響
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were an immature central bureaucracy and legal order, strong autonomy of local chiefs, limited military scale, fiscal dependence on tribute and customs, and vulnerability to northern great-power pressure and shifts in maritime routes

outlook.zh: 220年前後的林邑前景整體向上。若王室能穩住核心港口、維持對地方首領的賞罰並拓展對外商路，則有望演變為更穩定的占婆前身政體；但若遭北方遠征或內部分裂，成長將受重挫
outlook.en: Around 220 CE Linyi's outlook was broadly favorable. If the monarchy could secure its core ports, balance reward and coercion toward local chiefs, and expand foreign trade links, it could develop into a more stable precursor of later Champa polities; but northern expeditions or internal fragmentation could sharply disrupt that growth

description.zh: 林邑在今越南中部兴起，是后世占婆国家的早期形态。
description.en: Linyi arose in central Vietnam as an early precursor to later Champa polities.

governmentForm.zh: 以港口贸易与河口平原控制为基础的早期君主制曼荼罗国家
governmentForm.en: An early monarchical mandala state based on control of ports, trade routes, and the Mekong delta lowlands

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、地方首领、祭司与文吏、商人、工匠、农民、渔民及少量奴婢构成；沿海与河道城市较为开放，吸收本地高棉语族传统与印度化礼仪。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house, local chiefs, priests and scribes, merchants, artisans, farmers, fishers, and a smaller dependent or servile population; port and riverine towns were relatively open, blending local Khmer-related traditions with Indianized court ritual.

rulingClass.zh: 王族、与王室联姻的地方酋长、婆罗门祭司及掌管贸易税与贡赋的高级官员
rulingClass.en: The ruling class comprised the royal family, local chiefs tied to the court by alliance and marriage, Brahmanical ritual specialists, and senior officials who managed trade tolls and tribute

succession.zh: 王位继承以王族内部继承为主，常受宫廷支持、地方首领承认及武力控制影响，并非严格长子继承
succession.en: Succession was primarily dynastic within the royal line, but in practice depended on court backing, recognition by regional chiefs, and military control rather than strict primogeniture

structure.zh: 中央王廷统辖都城与核心河口地带，外围地区由附属首领、港口长官与地方贵族治理；王权通过贡赋、婚盟、宗教仪式及贸易节点控制来维持。
structure.en: A central royal court governed the capital and core delta zone, while outer regions were administered by subordinate chiefs, port officials, and local nobles; royal authority was maintained through tribute, marriage alliances, ritual legitimacy, and control of trade nodes.

function.zh: 管理宫廷、王室财产、接见使节与赏赐分配
function.en: Managed the palace, royal estates, reception of envoys, and distribution of gifts and rewards

function.zh: 征收港口关税、市场税、土地贡赋与属邦贡物
function.en: Collected port customs, market dues, agrarian tribute, and tribute goods from subordinate polities

function.zh: 维护运河、河道、堤堰、仓储与航运补给
function.en: Maintained canals, river channels, embankments, storage facilities, and transport provisioning

function.zh: 主持王室祭祀、保存契约与早期铭文、管理通事与书吏
function.en: Oversaw royal rituals, preserved agreements and early inscriptions, and managed interpreters and scribes

function.zh: 统筹王家卫队、河舟武装、征召兵与边境防务
function.en: Coordinated the royal guard, riverine forces, levies, and frontier defense

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托港口城镇、河口聚落与内陆首领领地。王廷向各地派遣税吏、使者或驻守人员，但多数地区仍由本地贵族按习惯法与贡赋关系治理。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on port towns, delta settlements, and inland chiefdoms. The court sent tax collectors, envoys, or small garrisons to key sites, but most territories were still governed by local nobles under customary law and tribute obligations.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以王命、习惯法、宗教誓约与首领裁判为主，尚未形成高度成文化法典；商业纠纷多由港口官员、商人团体和地方长者调处。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on royal orders, customary law, ritual oaths, and judgments by chiefs rather than a fully codified legal code; commercial disputes were often settled by port officials, merchant groups, and local elders.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以实物贡赋与贸易税为核心，包括稻米、鱼产、林木、象牙、香料、珠宝、手工业品及港口关税；部分劳役用于水利、运输和宫廷工程。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation centered on in-kind tribute and trade duties, including rice, fish products, timber, ivory, aromatics, gems, crafted goods, and port customs; labor service was also levied for hydraulic works, transport, and royal construction.

religion.zh: 宗教以本地祖灵、自然神与王权祭祀为基础，同时吸收婆罗门教与早期印度宗教要素；部分地区可能已有早期佛教影响，但尚非主导。
religion.en: Religion was grounded in local ancestor cults, nature spirits, and royal rites, while also incorporating Brahmanical and early Indian religious elements; some areas may already have seen early Buddhist influence, though it was not yet dominant.

philosophy.zh: 思想体系主要体现为王权神圣化、礼仪秩序、祖先传统与来自印度的政治—宗教观念结合，重视航运、互惠与贡献关系。
philosophy.en: Its worldview combined sacral kingship, ritual order, ancestral tradition, and Indian political-religious ideas, with strong emphasis on seafaring exchange, reciprocity, and tribute relations.

writingSystem.zh: 书写仍属早期阶段，宫廷与宗教场合开始使用源自印度的梵文与婆罗米体系书写；日常治理仍大量依赖口传、记忆与符号性记录。
writingSystem.en: Writing was still at an early stage; Sanskrit and Brahmi-derived scripts were beginning to appear in courtly and religious contexts, while everyday administration still relied heavily on oral transmission, memory, and symbolic recordkeeping.

culturalAchievements.zh: 扶南在220年前后最大的文化成就在于形成跨海与河网相连的港市文明、引入印度化宫廷礼仪、发展早期运河与交通体系，并作为中国与南亚之间的重要中转文化区。
culturalAchievements.en: Funan’s major cultural achievement around 220 CE was the creation of a port-based civilization linked by sea routes and river networks, the adoption of Indianized court ritual, the development of early canal and transport systems, and its role as a major cultural intermediary between China and South Asia.

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言属南亚语系孟—高棉语支，宫廷与贸易活动中可能并用古高棉相关语言、马来海商语和少量梵语术语
languageFamily.en: The main language belonged to the Austroasiatic Mon-Khmer branch, while court and trade activity likely involved an early Khmer-related language, Malay maritime speech, and a limited Sanskrit vocabulary

unit.zh: 银币第纳里乌斯等值/人

gdpDescription.zh: 扶南经济在东南亚早期国家中相当发达，农业、渔业与手工业支撑人口，而其真正优势在于转口贸易、港口税收与控制连接中国、南亚及南海航道的节点。
gdpDescription.en: Funan’s economy was relatively advanced for an early Southeast Asian state: agriculture, fishing, and craft production sustained the population, but its distinctive strength lay in entrepôt trade, port customs, and control of routes linking China, South Asia, and the South China Sea.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、渔业、盐业、林木采集、象牙与犀角贸易、珠宝与贝饰加工、金属工艺、造船与航运服务
mainIndustries.en: Wet-rice agriculture, fishing, salt production, timber extraction, ivory and rhinoceros horn trade, bead and shell ornament production, metalworking, shipbuilding, and maritime transport services

tradeGoods.zh: 输出稻米、鱼干、盐、木材、香木、象牙、犀角、龟甲、珠宝、热带产物与奴役劳动力；输入纺织品、金属器、玻璃珠、印度宗教用品、奢侈品与中国货物。
tradeGoods.en: Exports included rice, dried fish, salt, timber, aromatic woods, ivory, rhinoceros horn, tortoiseshell, gems, tropical products, and servile labor; imports included textiles, metal goods, glass beads, Indian religious objects, luxury wares, and Chinese goods.

name.zh: 金银衡量与货物交换并用

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距较大。普通农渔家庭多以稻谷、舟具、渔具、牲畜与木屋为主要资产；港口商人、地方首领与王室则集中掌握金银、珠宝、进口织物与奴婢。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was unevenly distributed. Ordinary farming and fishing families held wealth mainly in rice stores, boats, nets, livestock, and wooden houses, while port merchants, local chiefs, and the royal court concentrated bullion, gems, imported textiles, and dependents.

unit.zh: 银币第纳里乌斯等值/年
银币第纳里乌斯等值/年

economicSystem.zh: 经济体系为农业基础上的港市转口经济，王权通过控制河口、运河、港口、贡赋和贸易中介获利，市场交换与实物再分配并存。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was an agrarian base combined with a port-entrepôt economy, in which royal power profited by controlling estuaries, canals, ports, tribute, and commercial brokerage; market exchange coexisted with in-kind redistribution.

unit.zh: 银币第纳里乌斯等值/年
银币第纳里乌斯等值/年
银币第纳里乌斯等值/年

source.en: Tribute from subordinate territories and chiefs

source.zh: 王室垄断与工商业收入

category.en: Hydraulic works, storage, and transport infrastructure

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以金银、珠宝、香料、象牙、进口奢侈品与粮食储备构成，适合港口王国的流动性需求，但也容易受航道中断和宫廷赏赐消耗影响。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of bullion, gems, aromatics, ivory, imported luxuries, and grain reserves, giving the kingdom useful liquidity for a port state but leaving it vulnerable to trade disruption and heavy court largesse.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重视维持港口畅通、安抚地方首领与保持宫廷威望，倾向以贸易税和贡物而非高度统一的地税体系支持王权。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized keeping ports open, appeasing regional chiefs, and sustaining court prestige, relying more on customs and tribute goods than on a highly uniform land-tax system.

description.zh: 以矛、盾、弓、刀为主要装备，承担都城守备、镇压叛乱与随军远征任务。
description.en: Equipped mainly with spears, shields, bows, and blades; responsible for capital defense, suppression of revolts, and field expeditions.

description.zh: 以浅吃水舟船巡逻河口、运河与沿海航线，兼具护航、运兵、缉私和阻截海盗功能。
description.en: Using shallow-draft craft to patrol estuaries, canals, and coastal routes, these forces handled escort, troop transport, anti-smuggling, and anti-piracy duties.

description.zh: 由附属首领在战争时期提供，战斗力不均，但熟悉本地水网、森林与边地环境。
description.en: Provided by subordinate chiefs in wartime; uneven in quality, but familiar with local waterways, forests, and frontier terrain.

description.zh: 数量不多但象征王权，用于震慑、冲击与护卫君主及高级将领。
description.en: Limited in number but symbolically important, used for shock action, intimidation, and protection of the king and senior commanders.

technology.zh: 军事技术以东南亚早期铁器武装、弓矛步兵、战象与河舟作战为主，缺乏重装甲与大型攻城技术，但在水网地形与机动航运方面有优势。
technology.en: Military technology was based on early Southeast Asian iron weaponry, bow-and-spear infantry, war elephants, and river craft; it lacked heavy armor and major siege technology but held advantages in watery terrain and mobile transport.

unit.zh: 银币第纳里乌斯等值/年

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自沿海海盗、属地离叛、湄公河流域竞争势力、通往马来半岛的贸易节点争夺，以及北方中国政权变动导致的海贸波动。
threats.en: Main threats came from coastal piracy, rebellion among subordinate territories, rival powers in the Mekong basin, competition over routes toward the Malay Peninsula, and fluctuations in maritime trade caused by political changes in China.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 重骑兵标志性武器，冲击力极强
description.en: Signature heavy cavalry weapon with tremendous charge impact

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

description.zh: 水战拍竿，击毁敌船上层结构
description.en: Ship-mounted swinging beam to smash enemy superstructures

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于湄公河三角洲、运河沿线、港口聚落与若干内陆贸易站，居民构成多元，包括本地农渔民、港市工匠、商人、首领部众与外来海商社群。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in the Mekong delta, canal corridors, port settlements, and several inland trade stations, and was socially mixed, including local farmers and fishers, urban artisans, merchants, retinues of chiefs, and foreign merchant communities.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王族、地方贵族和祭司；中层为商人、船主、工匠、书吏与中级头人；下层为农民、渔民、劳役者及依附人口。社会流动性在港口地区高于内陆乡村。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of the royal family, regional nobles, and priests; the middle layer included merchants, shipowners, artisans, scribes, and lesser headmen; the lower ranks were farmers, fishers, laborers, and dependents. Social mobility was greater in port zones than in inland villages.

allies.zh: 扶南并无稳定意义上的正式同盟，更多依靠与南印度海商、马来半岛港口网络及若干属地首领维持互利关系，并积极向中国吴国方向开展朝贡与通使。
allies.en: Funan had no permanent allies in the strict sense, relying instead on mutually beneficial ties with South Indian merchants, port networks on the Malay Peninsula, and subordinate chiefs, while actively pursuing tributary and diplomatic contact toward the Chinese state of Wu.

enemies.zh: 无持续全面战争中的固定大敌，但与林邑、周边海上竞争港口、叛离属地以及内陆部族势力存在紧张与冲突可能。
enemies.en: It had no single fixed enemy in a state of total war, but faced tensions and possible conflict with Linyi, rival maritime ports, rebellious dependencies, and inland tribal powers.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以保护海贸、扩大中转地位、吸纳外来商人和技术、以贡使争取中国承认，并通过礼物、婚盟和军事威慑维系区域支配为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on protecting maritime commerce, expanding Funan’s role as an entrepôt, attracting foreign merchants and techniques, seeking recognition from China through embassies, and maintaining regional dominance through gifts, marriage alliances, and military deterrence.

era.zh: 三国时代的东南亚早期国家阶段
era.en: Early Southeast Asian state development during the Three Kingdoms Era

keyInnovations.zh: 运河开凿与河港联通、适应浅水环境的舟船技术、热带稻作与水资源管理、珠饰与金属工艺、吸收印度书写和礼仪制度
keyInnovations.en: Canal excavation and river-port integration, boat technology suited to shallow waters, tropical rice cultivation and water management, bead and metal craftsmanship, and the adoption of Indian writing and court ritual systems

strengths.zh: 扶南的最大优势是地处海陆交通中介地带，能够把湄公河资源、南海航线与印度洋贸易连接起来；王权对港口和水道的控制带来财政收入、文化吸纳力与地区影响力。
strengths.en: Funan’s greatest strength was its position as an intermediary linking Mekong resources, South China Sea routes, and Indian Ocean trade; royal control over ports and waterways generated revenue, cultural receptivity, and regional influence.

weaknesses.zh: 其统治结构较为松散，对地方首领依赖较大；政治整合、文字行政与常备军规模有限，经济又高度依赖海贸，一旦航线受阻或属地叛离，王权稳定性就会下降。
weaknesses.en: Its political structure was relatively loose and heavily dependent on local chiefs; administrative integration, written bureaucracy, and standing forces were limited, while the economy depended strongly on maritime trade, so disruption of routes or defection of dependencies could undermine royal stability.

outlook.zh: 220年前后的扶南正处于兴盛阶段。若能继续控制港口、扩展对马来半岛和湄公河流域的影响，并维持对中国与印度商路的吸引力，仍有望保持数代繁荣；但长期前景取决于内部整合能力与区域竞争压力。
outlook.en: Around 220 CE, Funan was in a thriving phase. If it could continue to control ports, extend influence over the Malay Peninsula and Mekong basin, and remain attractive to Chinese and Indian trade, it could preserve prosperity for generations; its long-term future, however, depended on stronger internal integration and its ability to withstand regional competition.

description.zh: 扶南是湄公河下游早期国家，通过海上贸易联系印度与中国。
description.en: Funan was an early Mekong polity linking India and China through maritime trade.

governmentForm.zh: 由多个有城墙的骠人城邦组成的分散君主制网络，各城由世袭统治家族治理，并通过贸易、宗教与亲族关系维系联系
governmentForm.en: A decentralized network of walled Pyu monarchic city-states, each governed by hereditary ruling houses and linked through trade, religion, and kinship ties

socialStructure.zh: 社会以城邦居民、乡村农民、工匠、商人、宗教人员与附属劳役群体构成；贵族与祭祀阶层居上，农业人口占多数
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of urban residents, rural cultivators, artisans, merchants, religious personnel, and dependent laboring groups; nobles and ritual elites stood at the top, while agricultural people formed the majority

rulingClass.zh: 地方王族、城邦贵族、军事首领与掌管礼仪和文字的宗教精英
rulingClass.en: Local royal families, city-state nobility, military leaders, and religious elites who controlled ritual and literacy

succession.zh: 通常为家族世袭，偏向父系继承，但在具体城邦中也可能因宗族协商、婚姻联盟或地方实力而调整
succession.en: Usually hereditary within ruling families, tending toward patrilineal succession, though in practice succession could be shaped by clan negotiation, marriage alliances, or local power balances

structure.zh: 骠地并非统一帝国，而是若干城邦的区域体系。各城设君主与宫廷，统辖周边灌溉农业区、村社和贸易节点，并在宗教、度量、礼仪和外贸上呈现共同文化特征
structure.en: Pyu territory was not a unified empire but a regional system of city-states. Each city had its own ruler and court, governing surrounding irrigated farmland, villages, and trade nodes, while sharing broad cultural patterns in religion, weights, rituals, and external trade

function.zh: 管理王室事务、礼仪、使节接待、仓储与宫廷工匠
function.en: Managed royal affairs, court ritual, reception of envoys, storehouses, and palace craftsmen

function.zh: 征收谷物、劳役和地方贡赋，管理粮仓与灾年调剂
function.en: Collected grain, labor dues, and local tribute, and managed granaries and redistribution in bad years

function.zh: 维护水渠、堤坝、道路、城墙和城门，组织公共劳役
function.en: Maintained canals, embankments, roads, walls, and gates, and organized public labor

function.zh: 监管市场、商税、度量衡、货物流通与外来商旅
function.en: Oversaw markets, commercial taxes, weights and measures, circulation of goods, and foreign merchants

function.zh: 主持祭仪、保管铭文与记录、协调宗教场所和教育性书写活动
function.en: Conducted rituals, kept inscriptions and records, and coordinated religious sites and educational writing

function.zh: 组织城防、驻军、武器储备与边道巡防
function.en: Organized city defense, garrisons, weapons storage, and patrols along frontier routes

localAdmin.zh: 城邦直接控制城内坊区、周边村落与灌溉地带。乡里多由地方首领、宗族长者和王廷任命的征收人员共同管理，边远地区则通过朝贡与盟附方式维系统治
localAdmin.en: The city-states directly controlled urban wards, nearby villages, and irrigated zones. Rural areas were often managed jointly by local headmen, clan elders, and royal collectors, while more distant areas were held through tribute and subordinate alliances

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王命、赔偿原则与宗教规范为基础，重视财产、灌溉秩序、婚姻与贸易纠纷的调解，严重案件由城主或贵族裁决
legalSystem.en: Law rested on customary practice, royal orders, compensation principles, and religious norms, with emphasis on mediating disputes over property, irrigation order, marriage, and trade; serious cases were decided by the city lord or nobles

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以谷物实物税、劳役、手工业贡纳、市场税和过路关税为主；附属村社及周边小聚落向中心城邦输送粮食、牲畜、纺织品与木材
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied mainly on grain in kind, labor service, craft tribute, market dues, and transit tolls; subordinate villages and smaller settlements supplied food, livestock, textiles, and timber to the central city-state

religion.zh: 地方祖灵与自然崇拜仍具重要地位，同时印度文化影响日益增强，婆罗门仪式、早期佛教观念和南亚宇宙秩序观念已开始进入骠人上层社会
religion.en: Local ancestor and nature cults remained important, while Indian cultural influence was increasing; Brahmanical rites, early Buddhist ideas, and South Asian cosmological concepts had begun to enter Pyu elite society

philosophy.zh: 以宗教伦理、王权合法性、功德观念和秩序观为核心，尚未形成高度系统化的本土成文哲学传统
philosophy.en: Thought centered on religious ethics, royal legitimacy, merit, and social order, without yet developing a highly systematized indigenous written philosophical tradition

writingSystem.zh: 书写仍处于早期发展阶段，受到印度婆罗米系文字传统影响；在220年左右，文字使用可能主要限于宗教、礼仪和标识性记录，尚未形成后世成熟的骠文文献体系
writingSystem.en: Writing was still in an early stage of development and influenced by Indian Brahmi-derived traditions; around 220 CE its use was likely limited mainly to religious, ritual, and identifying records rather than the fuller Pyu written corpus seen later

culturalAchievements.zh: 成就主要体现在有城墙的城市建设、砖结构建筑、灌溉农业组织、珠玉与金属工艺、对外贸易网络，以及对印度礼仪与宗教元素的本地化吸收
culturalAchievements.en: Major achievements lay in walled urban construction, brick architecture, organized irrigated agriculture, bead and metal craftwork, external trade networks, and the local adaptation of Indian ritual and religious elements

languageFamily.zh: 汉藏语系藏缅语族骠语支
languageFamily.en: Sino-Tibetan, Tibeto-Burman, Pyu branch

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以稻作农业为核心，辅以畜养、手工业和区域贸易。总体规模在内陆东南亚属较发达的城邦经济，但仍以实物生产和再分配为主，货币化有限
gdpDescription.en: The economy was centered on rice agriculture, supported by herding, crafts, and regional trade. By inland Southeast Asian standards it was a relatively developed urban polity economy, though still dominated by production in kind and redistribution with limited monetization

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、灌溉工程、制陶、纺织、珠饰制作、金属加工、木材采集、盐与地方交换贸易
mainIndustries.en: Rice farming, irrigation works, pottery, weaving, bead making, metalworking, timber extraction, salt, and local exchange trade

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、棉织物、珠饰、漆木制品、木材、象牙、盐、铜器、半宝石，以及从印度洋与陆路网络转运的奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Rice, cotton textiles, beads, lacquered wood products, timber, ivory, salt, copper goods, semi-precious stones, and luxury items transshipped from Indian Ocean and overland networks

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异明显。普通农户财富主要体现在土地使用权、牛只、谷仓和劳动力；城市精英则掌握珠饰、金属器、奴役性依附人口与储粮
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied considerably. Ordinary farming families held wealth chiefly in land-use rights, cattle, granaries, and labor, while urban elites controlled beads, metal goods, dependent laborers, and stored grain

economicSystem.zh: 以王廷再分配、村社贡赋和市场交换并存的农业城邦经济，外贸对精英消费和文化输入尤其重要
economicSystem.en: An agrarian city-state economy combining court redistribution, village tribute, and market exchange, with foreign trade especially important for elite consumption and cultural imports

source.en: Labor dues and public service valuation

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备主要以粮仓储粮、金属器、珠饰、牲畜与可调动物资形式存在，而非大量标准化钱币。稳定时期可支持修渠、筑城与赈灾
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury reserves were held mainly as granary stocks, metal goods, beads, livestock, and mobilizable supplies rather than large quantities of standardized coin. In stable times these reserves could support canal repair, fortification, and famine relief

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重维持灌溉、城防和王权礼仪，通过实物征收和劳役动员保障基本秩序，在丰年积储、歉年调剂
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized irrigation, defense, and royal ritual, using collection in kind and labor mobilization to maintain order, storing surpluses in good years and redistributing in poor ones

description.zh: 负责守卫城墙、城门、仓储和宫廷，装备长矛、盾牌、弓箭与短兵器
description.en: Responsible for guarding walls, gates, storehouses, and the court, equipped with spears, shields, bows, and sidearms

description.zh: 由周边村社在战时征发，作战能力有限，主要用于运输、筑垒与支援作战
description.en: Raised from surrounding villages in wartime, of limited combat effectiveness and used mainly for transport, fieldworks, and support

description.zh: 由贵族随从和精锐战士组成，用于快速应对邻近冲突、护送商路和镇压叛乱
description.en: Composed of noble retainers and elite warriors, used for rapid response to nearby conflicts, protecting trade routes, and suppressing unrest

name.en: Elephant Corps and Ceremonial War Force

description.zh: 数量不多，兼具威慑、礼仪和有限战场冲击功能，主要为上层权威象征
description.en: Small in number, combining deterrent, ceremonial, and limited battlefield shock roles, serving chiefly as symbols of elite authority

commanderInChief.zh: 各城邦城主亲自统军或委任军事首领
commanderInChief.en: Each city lord commanded personally or delegated to a military chief

technology.zh: 军事技术以青铜和铁制兵器、弓矛盾牌、砖土城防和战象运用为主，没有与中原或罗马同等级的复杂攻城体系
technology.en: Military technology centered on bronze and iron weapons, bows, spears, shields, brick-and-earth fortifications, and the use of war elephants, without the complex siege systems of China or Rome

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自城邦间竞争、山地与边缘族群袭扰、贸易路线争夺以及对灌溉地和劳动力的控制冲突；尚未面临大型帝国级直接征服压力
threats.en: Major threats came from rivalry among city-states, raids by upland and peripheral groups, competition over trade routes, and conflicts over irrigation lands and labor; there was not yet direct pressure from conquest by a major empire

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

description.zh: 单兵弩与床弩，中国军队核心远程武器
description.en: Infantry crossbow and mounted crossbow, core ranged weapon

description.zh: 复合弓或长弓，远射主力
description.en: Composite or longbow, primary ranged weapon

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于上缅甸干区的河谷、灌溉农田和若干有城墙中心，属于多城邦、多聚落的区域人口体系
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the river valleys, irrigated fields, and several walled centers of Upper Burma, forming a multi-city-state and multi-settlement regional population system

socialClasses.zh: 大体可分为王族与贵族、祭祀与文书人员、商人和工匠、自由农民、附属村社居民以及依附劳役人口。社会流动有限，但贸易和宗教服务提供少量上升渠道
socialClasses.en: Society can broadly be divided into royals and nobles, ritual and scribal personnel, merchants and artisans, free cultivators, residents of subordinate villages, and dependent laborers. Social mobility was limited, though trade and religious service offered some avenues for advancement

allies.zh: 无固定意义上的统一盟友体系，但与印度东岸港口网络、孟人地区及更南方海上贸易中介保持友好交换关系
allies.en: There was no fixed alliance system in a unified sense, but Pyu polities maintained friendly exchange relations with port networks on the east coast of India, Mon areas, and southern maritime intermediaries

enemies.zh: 无明确长期宿敌，主要对手是邻近竞争城邦、边缘山地群体和阻断贸易的地方势力
enemies.en: There was no clearly defined long-term archenemy; the principal opponents were neighboring rival city-states, upland groups, and local forces that disrupted trade

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以保障商路、吸纳外来宗教与工艺、维持区域平衡和避免被强大外部势力卷入为主，偏向贸易导向与礼仪性外交
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on securing trade routes, absorbing foreign religions and crafts, maintaining regional balance, and avoiding entanglement with stronger outside powers, tending toward trade-oriented and ceremonial diplomacy

era.zh: 铁器时代晚期的早期城市化阶段
era.en: Late Iron Age early urbanization stage

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括灌溉组织、砖城与护城设施、铁制农具和兵器、珠饰与窑烧工艺，以及受印度影响的礼仪、历法和初步书写传统
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies included irrigation organization, brick cities and defensive works, iron agricultural tools and weapons, bead and kiln craft production, and Indian-influenced ritual, calendrical, and early writing traditions

strengths.zh: 优势在于稳定的稻作基础、多个有城墙中心形成的互补网络、区域贸易位置优越，以及对印度文化要素的选择性吸收提升了统治合法性与文化层次
strengths.en: Strengths included a stable rice-growing base, a complementary network of multiple walled centers, an advantageous position in regional trade, and the selective adoption of Indian cultural elements that enhanced legitimacy and prestige

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于政治分散、军力有限、文字行政尚不成熟、对灌溉和地方协作依赖高，且不同城邦之间容易因资源与商路而竞争
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses lay in political fragmentation, limited military power, an only partly developed written administration, heavy dependence on irrigation and local cooperation, and competition among city-states over resources and trade routes

outlook.zh: 220年前后的骠国总体稳定，并处于持续城市化和印度化加深阶段。若贸易与灌溉维持顺畅，未来数世纪有望形成更成熟的城邦文明与更明显的宗教书写传统
outlook.en: Around 220 CE the Pyu world was broadly stable and undergoing continued urbanization and deeper Indianization. If trade and irrigation remained secure, the following centuries were likely to see a more mature city-state civilization and a more visible religious and written tradition

description.zh: 上缅甸的骠人城邦构成区域性都市网络，逐渐吸收印度文化影响。
description.en: The Pyu urban polities of Upper Burma formed a regional network increasingly influenced by Indian culture.

governmentForm.zh: 分立的部族联盟与小王国体制，由氏族首领、地方豪族与祭祀权威共同统治
governmentForm.en: A fragmented system of tribal confederacies and petty kingdoms ruled by clan chiefs, local magnates, and ritual authorities

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、村落与地方邦国为基本单位，分为王族与豪族、祭司与巫觋、自由农民、工匠、渔民及少量依附人口
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around clans, villages, and local polities, divided into royal and chiefly lineages, ritual specialists and shamans, free farmers, artisans, fishers, and a smaller dependent population

rulingClass.zh: 各地王族、氏族首领、掌握青铜器与铁器资源的豪族，以及主持祭祀的精英家族
rulingClass.en: Regional royal lineages, clan chiefs, magnates controlling bronze and iron resources, and elite families overseeing ritual life

succession.zh: 继承多依氏族传统而定，常见父子、兄弟或同族支系继承，并受部族共议与祭祀合法性影响
succession.en: Succession followed clan custom, often passing to sons, brothers, or collateral kin, and was shaped by elite consensus and ritual legitimacy

structure.zh: 倭地并无统一中央官僚国家，而是由数十至上百个邦国构成；各邦国以王或君长为核心，下有氏族长、聚落首领、祭司和负责贡赋与仓储的家臣性人员
structure.en: Wa was not a unified centralized bureaucratic state but a patchwork of dozens to over a hundred polities; each was centered on a king or chief, with clan elders, settlement heads, ritual specialists, and retainers handling tribute and storage

function.zh: 辅佐地方王处理盟约、婚姻、争端调停与对外使节事务
function.en: Assisted local kings with alliances, marriage diplomacy, dispute mediation, and foreign envoys

function.zh: 主持祖灵、农耕、战争与季节祭仪，并维持统治合法性
function.en: Conducted ancestral, agricultural, martial, and seasonal rites while sustaining political legitimacy

function.zh: 征收稻米、布帛、鱼盐、手工业品，并管理公共仓储与再分配
function.en: Collected rice, textiles, fish, salt, and handicrafts, managing communal granaries and redistribution

function.zh: 组织部族武装、守卫聚落、调集战士和管理武器储备
function.en: Organized tribal forces, guarded settlements, mobilized warriors, and maintained weapon stores

function.zh: 协调沿海航行、渡海贸易、接待朝鲜半岛与中国来客，并管理交换品
function.en: Coordinated coastal navigation, overseas trade, reception of visitors from the Korean Peninsula and China, and managed exchange goods

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依村落与氏族而行，聚落首领向区域王权纳贡；在较强邦国中，外围村社受中枢聚落监督，但整体仍松散而因地制宜
localAdmin.en: Local administration worked through villages and clans, with settlement leaders rendering tribute to regional rulers; in stronger polities, outer communities were supervised from central settlements, though the system remained loose and highly local

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要是习惯法、氏族规约与首领裁断的结合，重视血亲关系、赔偿、和解与祭祀性誓约，严重冲突则可能引发部族战争
legalSystem.en: Law consisted chiefly of customary practice, clan rules, and chiefly judgment, emphasizing kinship, compensation, reconciliation, and ritual oaths; severe disputes could escalate into inter-polity warfare

taxationSystem.zh: 税赋主要以实物贡纳形式征收，包括稻米、谷物、布、麻、鱼盐、工艺品与劳役，而非统一货币税制
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was collected primarily as tribute in kind, including rice, grain, cloth, hemp, fish, salt, craft goods, and labor service rather than a uniform monetary tax

religion.zh: 以祖灵崇拜、自然神灵、山川海洋祭祀和巫觋仪式为主，属于后世神道形成前的早期信仰传统
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor worship, nature spirits, rites to mountains, rivers, and the sea, and shamanic practices, representing an early tradition before later formalized Shinto

philosophy.zh: 尚无成体系成文哲学，社会观念强调祖先、氏族忠诚、祭祀秩序、农耕时序与首领德威；中国思想影响尚有限但已开始传入
philosophy.en: There was no fully developed written philosophy; social thought emphasized ancestors, clan loyalty, ritual order, agricultural timing, and chiefly prestige, while Chinese ideas had begun to arrive but remained limited

writingSystem.zh: 倭地本土尚未普遍使用文字；与大陆交流时可能借助识字的渡来人或少量汉字知识，但社会整体仍属前文字阶段
writingSystem.en: Wa had not yet adopted writing for broad native use; dealings with the continent may have relied on literate immigrants or limited knowledge of Chinese characters, but society as a whole remained pre-literate

culturalAchievements.zh: 弥生文化已形成成熟水稻农业、环壕聚落、青铜礼器、铁器使用、织造技术、木构建筑与多样墓制；区域精英通过祭器、冢墓和大型聚落展示权威
culturalAchievements.en: Yayoi culture had developed advanced wet-rice agriculture, moated settlements, bronze ritual objects, use of iron tools, weaving, timber architecture, and varied burial practices; regional elites displayed authority through ritual objects, tombs, and large settlements

languageFamily.zh: 原始日语—日本语系早期形态，可能已呈现列岛内部方言分化
languageFamily.en: An early form of Proto-Japonic within the Japonic language family, likely already showing dialectal variation across the archipelago

unit.en: kg grain equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以稻作农业为核心，辅以粟、黍、豆类种植、渔猎、采集、盐业、织造和小规模金属加工。生产力较此前绳纹时代显著提高，但地区差异很大，畿内与北九州较为富庶。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was centered on rice agriculture, supplemented by millet, beans, fishing, gathering, salt-making, weaving, and small-scale metalworking. Productivity was far above the earlier Jomon period, though regional disparities were considerable, with Kinai and northern Kyushu relatively prosperous.

mainIndustries.zh: 水稻种植、渔业、盐业、纺织、木工、制陶、铁器加工、青铜礼器制造与沿海运输
mainIndustries.en: Wet-rice cultivation, fishing, salt production, textiles, woodworking, pottery, ironworking, bronze ritual production, and coastal transport

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、布帛、麻、鱼干、盐、珍珠、贝器、木材、漆器、铁器、青铜器，以及自朝鲜半岛输入的铁材与技术
tradeGoods.en: Rice, cloth, hemp, dried fish, salt, pearls, shell goods, timber, lacquerware, iron tools, bronze goods, and iron materials and techniques imported from the Korean Peninsula

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在稻田使用权、粮食储备、农具、织物、住宅与亲族网络；豪族则积累青铜器、铁器、仓储、船只和礼器
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth lay mainly in paddy-use rights, grain stores, tools, textiles, dwellings, and kin networks; elite families accumulated bronze objects, iron tools, granaries, boats, and ritual goods

unit.zh: 千克谷物当量／人／年可支配剩余
unit.en: kg grain equivalent per person per year disposable surplus

economicSystem.zh: 以村社农业、自给自足、部族再分配和区域交换为基础，少数沿海政体通过朝鲜海峡贸易取得更高地位
economicSystem.en: The economy rested on village agriculture, subsistence production, tribal redistribution, and regional exchange, with some coastal polities gaining higher status through trade across the Korea Strait

source.en: Labor service and public works levies

source.zh: 贸易中转与外来珍品进献
source.en: Trade transit and presentation of foreign valuables

treasuryDescription.zh: 所谓国库主要体现为各邦国谷仓储备、布帛积存、武器、礼器与少量外来珍品，并非统一现金库藏
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury mainly took the form of granary reserves, stored textiles, weapons, ritual objects, and a small stock of imported valuables rather than a unified cash hoard

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持仓储、酬赏部众、支持祭祀与应急动员为主，重视实物积累与再分配，不追求抽象预算平衡
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on maintaining stores, rewarding followers, supporting rituals, and preserving capacity for emergency mobilization, emphasizing accumulation and redistribution in kind rather than abstract budget balance

description.zh: 由各地王与豪族直接控制的核心武装，负责护卫中心聚落、首领住宅和仓储
description.en: Core forces directly controlled by regional kings and magnates, guarding central settlements, chiefly compounds, and granaries

description.zh: 战时由农民与氏族成员征集，装备矛、弓、盾与铁制刀剑，是主要作战力量
description.en: Raised in war from farmers and clan members, armed with spears, bows, shields, and iron blades, forming the main fighting force

name.en: Coastal Boat Forces and Seaborne Warriors

description.zh: 主要分布于北九州与濑户内海沿岸，用于护航、袭扰、跨海联络与小规模登陆作战
description.en: Concentrated in northern Kyushu and along the Inland Sea, used for escort, raiding, cross-strait communication, and small amphibious operations

commanderInChief.zh: 各邦国之王与氏族首领分别统军，无统一全国统帅
commanderInChief.en: Each polity's king or clan chief commanded its own forces; there was no unified national commander-in-chief

technology.zh: 军事技术处于铁器时代早期至中期水平，常用长矛、短剑、铁刀、弓箭、木盾、皮甲或简易护身装备，防御依赖环壕聚落与木栅
technology.en: Military technology stood at an early to middle Iron Age level, using spears, short swords, iron blades, bows, wooden shields, leather or simple protective gear, while defense relied on moated settlements and wooden palisades

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自倭地内部邦国竞争、沿海掠夺、朝鲜海峡航路冲突以及资源与威望争夺，而非持续的大规模外敌入侵
threats.en: The main threats came from rival Wa polities, coastal raiding, conflict on Korea Strait sea routes, and competition for resources and prestige rather than sustained large-scale foreign invasion

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于北九州、濑户内海沿岸、近畿平原及部分西日本低地稻作区，东北与山地地区人口较稀疏
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in northern Kyushu, the Inland Sea coast, the Kinai plain, and other lowland rice-growing areas of western Japan, while the northeast and mountain regions remained more sparsely settled

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括王族与豪族、祭司与巫者、武装随从、自由农民、工匠与商旅、渔民和依附人口；阶层差距正在扩大但仍未形成后世严格官僚贵族制
socialClasses.en: Social strata included royal and chiefly elites, priests and shamans, armed retainers, free farmers, artisans and traders, fishers, and dependent people; inequalities were widening, though a later rigid bureaucratic aristocracy had not yet formed

allies.zh: 无统一全国盟友体系；部分北九州与西日本邦国同朝鲜半岛南部诸政体保持贸易与婚盟联系，并尝试与魏建立朝贡往来
allies.en: There was no unified nationwide alliance system; some polities in northern Kyushu and western Japan maintained trade and marriage ties with southern Korean polities and sought tributary relations with Wei

enemies.zh: 主要敌对对象是彼此竞争的倭国邦国，另有海上贸易冲突中的地方势力；与中国三国并无直接大规模战争
enemies.en: The main enemies were rival Wa polities and local powers competing over maritime trade; Wa had no direct large-scale war with the Chinese Three Kingdoms

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以获取威望、铁材、器物和政治承认为目标，通过遣使、交换珍品、婚盟与海上中介贸易提升本地王权地位
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed at acquiring prestige, iron, goods, and political recognition through envoys, exchange of valuables, marriage alliances, and maritime intermediary trade to elevate local rulership

era.zh: 弥生时代晚期，处于三国时代东亚交流背景下
era.en: Late Yayoi period within the wider East Asian context of the Three Kingdoms Era

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟水田灌溉、铁制农具与兵器、青铜礼器、织机与纺织、木栅环壕防御聚落、沿海航行技术以及来自大陆的冶金与组织经验输入
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included mature wet-field irrigation, iron agricultural tools and weapons, bronze ritual objects, looms and textile production, moated and palisaded settlements, coastal navigation, and imported metallurgical and organizational knowledge from the continent

strengths.zh: 倭地拥有不断扩展的稻作基础、灵活的海上交通、强大的氏族网络和逐渐增长的区域精英权力；部分邦国能够通过对外贸易与祭祀权威整合周边聚落
strengths.en: Wa possessed an expanding rice base, flexible maritime connectivity, strong clan networks, and growing regional elite power; some polities could integrate surrounding settlements through foreign trade and ritual authority

weaknesses.zh: 政治高度分裂，缺乏统一法制、常备官僚机构和全国军队；文字使用有限，行政能力受制于口头传统，内部战争与资源竞争频繁
weaknesses.en: Politics remained highly fragmented, lacking unified law, a standing bureaucracy, and a nationwide army; limited use of writing constrained administration, while internal warfare and resource competition were frequent

outlook.zh: 短期内倭地仍将维持多邦国并立格局，但随着近畿与北九州强权竞争、对魏等大陆政权联系加深，以及祭祀与军事整合增强，未来数代中出现更强的区域霸权者可能性上升
outlook.en: In the short term Wa would likely remain a mosaic of many polities, but as stronger centers in Kinai and northern Kyushu competed, ties with continental powers such as Wei deepened, and ritual and military integration increased, the rise of a more dominant regional hegemon over the coming generations became more likely

description.zh: 日本列岛处于弥生后期，分布着众多倭人小国，尚未完成统一。
description.en: The Japanese archipelago in the late Yayoi period contained many Wa polities and was not yet unified.

governmentForm.zh: 以王族贵族联盟为核心的早期世袭君主制，正在由辰韩诸小国联盟向更集中的王国转变
governmentForm.en: An early hereditary monarchy centered on a royal-aristocratic coalition, transitioning from a confederation of former Jinhan polities toward a more centralized kingdom

socialStructure.zh: 社会以王族、六部贵族、地方首领、自由民农户、工匠、依附民与奴婢构成，血缘与部族出身对地位具有决定性影响
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal house, the aristocrats of the Six Bu, local chiefs, free peasant households, artisans, dependents, and slaves, with lineage and tribal origin strongly shaping status

rulingClass.zh: 朴氏王族与金、昔等主要贵族集团及六部首领
rulingClass.en: The Pak royal house together with major aristocratic groups such as the Kim and Seok lineages and the heads of the Six Bu

succession.zh: 王位原则上由王族内部继承，但在这一时期仍深受贵族共议与王族支系平衡影响，并非严格长子继承
succession.en: The throne was in principle inherited within the royal house, but in this period succession was still heavily shaped by aristocratic consensus and balance among royal branches rather than strict primogeniture

structure.zh: 中央政权规模尚小，以国王为核心，依托六部贵族会议、宫廷随从与地方首领网络治理；中央对外围地区的控制正在加强但仍不均衡
structure.en: The central government remained relatively small, centered on the king and supported by the aristocratic councils of the Six Bu, palace retainers, and networks of local chiefs; central control over outlying areas was strengthening but still uneven

function.zh: 负责君主近侍、王命发布、宫廷礼仪、王室仓储与使者接待
function.en: Handled royal attendants, proclamation of royal orders, court ritual, royal storehouses, and reception of envoys

function.zh: 协调首都及周边六部的人口、土地、劳役、司法调解与贵族事务
function.en: Coordinated population, land, labor service, legal mediation, and aristocratic affairs for the Six Bu of the capital region

function.zh: 征集谷物、布帛、地方土产与劳役，管理储备并分配给王室、官员与军队
function.en: Collected grain, cloth, local products, and labor obligations, managing reserves and distributing them to the royal house, officials, and troops

function.zh: 组织王都守军、征发地方兵、修筑防御设施并监视与伽倻、百济及倭相关边境动向
function.en: Organized the capital guard, levied local troops, maintained defensive works, and monitored frontier developments involving Gaya, Baekje, and Wa

function.zh: 主持王室祖先祭祀、山川神祭、季节仪礼与国家性盟誓活动
function.en: Oversaw royal ancestral rites, sacrifices to mountain and river spirits, seasonal ceremonies, and state oaths

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政主要依赖被纳入新罗体系的村落首领与区域酋长。王都附近控制较直接，外围地区多保留旧有首领与自治传统，以贡纳、服役与军事随从形式向王权臣属
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied chiefly on village headmen and regional chiefs incorporated into the Silla order. Control was more direct near the capital, while peripheral areas often retained older leadership and local autonomy, submitting to royal authority through tribute, service, and military attendance

legalSystem.zh: 法律仍以习惯法、王命、部族调解和贵族仲裁为主，重视身份差序、赔偿、劳役与对叛乱和背盟的惩罚，尚未形成后世成文法典体系
legalSystem.en: Law was still based mainly on customary law, royal commands, tribal mediation, and aristocratic arbitration, emphasizing status distinctions, compensation, labor penalties, and punishment for rebellion or breach of allegiance; it had not yet developed into the later formal codified system

taxationSystem.zh: 赋役以实物和劳役为主，包括谷物、麻布、地方特产、手工业品及筑城、运输、守备等徭役；王室亦通过附属聚落的定期贡纳获取资源
taxationSystem.en: Taxation and obligations were mainly in kind and labor, including grain, hemp cloth, local specialties, craft goods, and corvée for fortification, transport, and guard duty; the royal house also drew resources through regular tribute from subordinate settlements

religion.zh: 以本土萨满式信仰、祖先崇拜与山川自然神崇拜为主，国家祭祀对王权合法性极为重要；佛教尚未正式传入并成为国教
religion.en: Religion was centered on indigenous shamanic practices, ancestor worship, and veneration of mountain and nature spirits, with state rites crucial to royal legitimacy; Buddhism had not yet been formally adopted as a state religion

philosophy.zh: 思想世界以氏族伦理、祖先传统、祭祀秩序和实用政治经验为主，尚未深度制度化吸收中国式儒学，但已通过对华接触受到部分礼制观念影响
philosophy.en: The intellectual world was shaped by clan ethics, ancestral tradition, ritual order, and practical political experience; it had not yet deeply institutionalized Chinese-style Confucian learning, though contact with China had begun to transmit some ritual concepts

writingSystem.zh: 官方和外交层面已开始接触并有限使用汉字，但日常行政与地方社会仍主要依赖口传、记忆和实物凭证，识字范围狭窄
writingSystem.en: Chinese characters had begun to be encountered and used in limited official and diplomatic contexts, but everyday administration and local society still relied mainly on oral transmission, memory, and material tokens, with literacy confined to a narrow elite

culturalAchievements.zh: 逐步形成以金城为中心的王都文化、王陵与木槨墓传统、青铜与铁器工艺、装饰性首饰制作，以及将原辰韩诸共同体整合为更明确新罗身份的政治文化
culturalAchievements.en: Cultural achievements included the gradual formation of a capital culture centered on Geumseong, royal and elite wooden-chamber tomb traditions, bronze and iron craftsmanship, ornamental jewelry production, and a political culture increasingly integrating former Jinhan communities into a clearer Silla identity

languageFamily.zh: 古韩语支，通常归入朝鲜语系前身
languageFamily.en: Proto-Koreanic, generally understood as ancestral to the Koreanic language family

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业和地方性资源征集为基础，市场交换有限但正在增长。金属器生产和区域贸易对国家整合日益重要，但整体仍属早期农业王国经济
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on agriculture and the collection of local resources, with market exchange limited but growing. Metal production and regional trade were becoming increasingly important to state integration, but the economy remained that of an early agrarian kingdom

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作与旱作农业、铁器冶炼与锻造、制陶、纺织、木工、盐与海产采集、马匹与役畜饲养
mainIndustries.en: Wet-rice and dry-field agriculture, iron smelting and forging, pottery, textile production, woodworking, salt and marine harvesting, and the keeping of horses and draft animals

tradeGoods.zh: 铁器、铁锭、麻布、丝织输入品、陶器、盐、鱼干、皮革、木材、装饰珠玉与地方特产
tradeGoods.en: Iron tools, iron ingots, hemp cloth, imported silk goods, pottery, salt, dried fish, leather, timber, ornamental beads, and local specialty products

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要体现为耕地使用权、粮食储备、农具、织具、家畜与家族劳动力；贵族财富则集中于土地控制、附属人口、武器、马匹和奢侈品
householdWealth.en: For ordinary households, wealth lay mainly in access to arable land, grain stores, tools, looms, livestock, and family labor; aristocratic wealth was concentrated in land control, dependent populations, weapons, horses, and prestige goods

economicSystem.zh: 以农业贡赋和劳役为核心、夹杂地方交换与区域手工业流通的再分配型经济
economicSystem.en: A redistributive economy centered on agrarian tribute and labor obligations, combined with local exchange and regional circulation of craft goods

source.zh: 地方土产与手工业贡纳
贸易、关津与王室直辖收益
source.en: Trade, transit dues, and royal domain income

category.en: Officials and rewards to local chiefs

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要以谷物储备、布帛、铁材、兵器和王室掌握的实物财富构成，而非铸币金银。稳定年份可形成适度积储，用于赈灾、战争动员和贵族赏赐
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted mainly of grain reserves, cloth, iron stock, weapons, and other material wealth controlled by the royal house rather than coined gold or silver. In stable years it could build moderate stores for famine relief, wartime mobilization, and aristocratic rewards

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调积谷备荒、维持王都与边备、通过赏赐整合地方首领，并避免过度榨取新纳入地区以保持政治稳定
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized stockpiling grain against scarcity, sustaining the capital and frontier defenses, integrating local chiefs through rewards, and avoiding excessive extraction from newly incorporated areas in order to preserve political stability

description.zh: 负责金城王宫、仓储和王族安全的常备兵力，由王室近侍与精锐步兵组成
description.en: A standing force responsible for the royal palace, granaries, and the security of the royal house at Geumseong, composed of royal retainers and elite infantry

description.zh: 由各部与附属聚落在战时征发的主力，装备以矛、盾、弓与铁制短兵器为主
description.en: The main wartime force levied from the various Bu and subordinate settlements, armed primarily with spears, shields, bows, and iron sidearms

description.zh: 数量不大，主要用于侦察、传令、追击与贵族武装护卫，显示骑兵在新罗尚属辅助力量
description.en: Small in number, they were used mainly for scouting, dispatch riding, pursuit, and aristocratic escort, showing that cavalry remained an auxiliary arm in Silla

description.zh: 负责边地城寨、渡口与沿海警戒，防范伽倻、倭系海上活动及地方叛乱
description.en: Responsible for frontier forts, river crossings, and coastal vigilance against Gaya, Wa-related maritime activity, and local unrest

technology.zh: 军备以铁制矛、刀剑、箭镞、木盾、皮甲和简易防御工事为主，具备一定冶铁能力；战争方式偏重步兵集结、城寨防守和部族首领率众作战
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron spears, swords, arrowheads, wooden shields, leather armor, and simple fortifications, supported by a meaningful ironworking base; warfare emphasized infantry musters, fort defense, and contingents led by tribal chiefs

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自西方百济势力扩张、南方伽倻诸国的竞争、倭系海上势力干预，以及内部新纳入地区忠诚度不足
threats.en: The main threats came from the expansion of Baekje to the west, rivalry with the Gaya polities to the south, intervention by Wa-linked maritime forces, and uncertain loyalty in newly incorporated internal regions

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 单兵弩与床弩，中国军队核心远程武器
description.en: Infantry crossbow and mounted crossbow, core ranged weapon

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于庆州盆地及其周边河谷、沿海平原和若干内陆聚落，整体仍以农村为主。随着王权扩展，原辰韩各共同体正逐步被纳入统一人口结构
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in the Gyeongju basin, nearby river valleys, coastal plains, and several inland settlements, with society still overwhelmingly rural. As royal power expanded, former Jinhan communities were gradually being incorporated into a more unified population structure

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王族与贵族，下有部族首领和武装随从，中层为自由农民与手工业者，下层为依附民、俘虏与奴婢。社会分层日益固定，但尚未发展为后世成熟骨品制
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the royal house and aristocracy, below them tribal chiefs and armed retainers, then free peasants and artisans, and at the bottom dependents, captives, and slaves. Social hierarchy was becoming more fixed, but had not yet developed into the mature later bone-rank system

allies.zh: 无稳固的远程正式盟友；在朝鲜半岛南部与部分伽倻或地方势力可能存在临时合作，以牵制百济或区域竞争者
allies.en: No firm long-distance formal allies; in southern Korea there may have been temporary cooperation with certain Gaya or local forces to check Baekje or other regional rivals

enemies.zh: 主要对手为百济及部分竞争性的伽倻政体；与倭系势力关系复杂，既有贸易往来也可能存在军事支持敌对方的情况。与高句丽接触较间接但需关注其半岛北部影响
enemies.en: The principal rivals were Baekje and some competing Gaya polities; relations with Wa-linked forces were complex, involving both trade and possible military support for hostile parties. Contact with Goguryeo was more indirect, but its influence in the peninsula's north remained noteworthy

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以半岛南东部安全与内部整合为中心，优先压制邻近竞争者、扩大对辰韩旧域的控制，并通过有限朝贡与间接接触了解中国三国局势以提升合法性
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on security in the southeastern peninsula and internal consolidation, prioritizing the suppression of nearby rivals, expanding control over former Jinhan territory, and using limited tribute or indirect contacts to understand the Three Kingdoms situation in China and enhance legitimacy

era.zh: 朝鲜三国时代早期铁器时代国家
era.en: Early Iron Age state of the Korean Three Kingdoms era

keyInnovations.zh: 较成熟的铁器锻造、稻作农业与灌溉技术扩展、木槨墓营建、织造与制陶改进、山城和土木防御设施建设，以及对汉字文化和中国制度技术的初步吸收
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included fairly mature iron forging, expanding wet-rice cultivation and irrigation techniques, wooden-chamber tomb construction, improvements in weaving and pottery, the building of hill forts and earthen defenses, and the early absorption of Chinese writing culture and institutional techniques

strengths.zh: 地处东南、腹地相对稳固，王权能够借六部结构逐步整合地方；冶铁和农业基础为军事与财政提供支撑；政治局势在220年总体稳定
strengths.en: Its southeastern position provided a relatively secure core, and royal power could progressively integrate local society through the Six Bu structure; ironworking and agriculture supported military and fiscal capacity; the political situation in 220 was broadly stable

weaknesses.zh: 中央制度仍较原始，对外围控制有限；贵族共治色彩浓厚，王位继承潜藏争议；面对百济、伽倻与海上势力时资源和机动能力不足
weaknesses.en: Central institutions were still rudimentary and control over peripheral areas remained limited; aristocratic co-rule was strong, leaving succession open to dispute; against Baekje, Gaya, and maritime forces, resources and mobility were constrained

outlook.zh: 若能继续稳固金城核心、吸纳地方首领并改善军事组织，新罗有望在未来数代由区域性王国成长为半岛重要强权；但短期内仍须谨慎应对百济与南方诸势力的竞争
outlook.en: If it could continue to secure the Geumseong core, incorporate local chiefs, and improve military organization, Silla had the potential over the next several generations to grow from a regional kingdom into a major peninsular power; in the short term, however, it still had to navigate competition from Baekje and southern polities with care

description.zh: 新罗是朝鲜半岛东南部的王国，正逐渐整合辰韩诸势力。
description.en: Silla was a southeastern Korean kingdom gradually consolidating the former Jinhan polities.

governmentForm.zh: 以王權為核心的早期世襲君主制，結合部族貴族聯盟與領域官治
governmentForm.en: An early hereditary monarchy centered on royal authority, combining a tribal-aristocratic confederative order with territorial administration

socialStructure.zh: 社會由王族、貴族部族首領、地方豪族、自由民農戶、手工業者、商人與下層依附民構成；王室正逐步將原先的部族聯盟改造為更集中化的國家秩序。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house, aristocratic clan chiefs, regional magnates, free peasant households, artisans, merchants, and lower dependent populations; the royal court was gradually transforming an older tribal confederation into a more centralized state order.

rulingClass.zh: 扶餘系王族與漢江流域諸貴族氏族
rulingClass.en: The Buyeo-descended royal family and aristocratic clans of the Han River basin

succession.zh: 王位由王族世襲，通常在直系男性宗親中承繼，但需獲主要貴族承認與支持
succession.en: The throne was hereditary within the royal house, usually passing among direct male royal kin, but required recognition and support from leading aristocrats

structure.zh: 220年的百濟政府仍屬早期王國體制。中央以國王與宮廷為核心，依靠王族、內臣與部族貴族共同治理；地方則由城邑首領與豪族管理。制度化官僚尚未達後世成熟程度，但已出現分工明確的宮廷、軍事、財賦與禮儀管理職能。
structure.en: In 220 CE, Baekje's government remained that of an early kingdom. The center was organized around the king and court, ruling through royal kinsmen, inner officials, and tribal aristocrats; the localities were administered by fortress chiefs and regional magnates. A fully institutionalized bureaucracy had not yet emerged in later mature form, but distinct court, military, revenue, and ritual functions were already present.

function.zh: 管理宮廷事務、王命傳達、王室財產、侍從與禮儀安排
function.en: Managed palace affairs, transmission of royal orders, royal property, attendants, and court ceremony

function.zh: 統籌近衛軍、地方動員、邊防警戒與武器儲備
function.en: Oversaw the royal guard, local mobilization, frontier watch, and weapon stores

function.zh: 登記戶口、徵收穀物與布帛、徵發勞役並管理倉儲
function.en: Registered households, collected grain and cloth taxes, levied labor service, and supervised granaries

function.zh: 主持祖先祭祀、天神與山川祭典，鞏固王權神聖性
function.en: Conducted ancestral rites and sacrifices to heaven, mountains, and rivers, reinforcing the sacred legitimacy of kingship

function.zh: 處理與中國諸政權、倭地及朝鮮半島諸國的使節往來與情報事務
function.en: Handled embassies and intelligence concerning Chinese regimes, Wa polities, and other states on the Korean peninsula

localAdmin.zh: 地方以城邑為核心，由王室任命或承認的城主、將領與地方豪族共治；邊區與新附地區控制力較弱，常依賴盟屬關係與人質、婚姻、屯兵等手段維繫。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was centered on fortified settlements, governed jointly by royal appointees or recognized local chiefs, military commanders, and regional magnates; control over frontier and newly attached areas was weaker and often maintained through alliance ties, hostages, marriage, and garrisoning.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要依習慣法、王命與貴族裁斷運行，重視對叛亂、盜竊、逃亡與違抗徵發的懲治；尚未形成後世成文法典式的完整法制。
legalSystem.en: The legal system operated mainly through customary law, royal commands, and aristocratic adjudication, placing emphasis on punishing rebellion, theft, desertion, and refusal of corvée or mobilization; it had not yet developed into a fully codified legal order of later centuries.

taxationSystem.zh: 賦役以戶為基本單位，主要徵收穀物、布帛、地方土產與勞役；軍役亦屬重要負擔。王室對核心地區徵收較穩定，對外圍地區則常以貢納與服屬形式取得資源。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation and service were organized by household, with grain, cloth, local products, and labor service as the main dues; military service was also a major obligation. The royal court extracted revenue more regularly from core regions, while outer areas often provided resources in the form of tribute and submission.

religion.zh: 220年的百濟宗教以本土薩滿信仰、祖先崇拜與山川祭祀為主，王室透過祭天與祖靈儀式強化統治正當性。佛教尚未正式傳入國家層面。
religion.en: Baekje's religion in 220 CE was dominated by indigenous shamanic belief, ancestor worship, and sacrifices to mountains and rivers; the royal house reinforced its legitimacy through rites to heaven and royal ancestors. Buddhism had not yet been officially introduced at the state level.

philosophy.zh: 思想以部族傳統、祖先倫理、戰士榮譽與實用王道觀為主，並可能透過與中原及遼東交流接觸到早期漢文化中的政治倫理觀念，但尚未深度制度化。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was shaped chiefly by tribal tradition, ancestral ethics, warrior honor, and practical notions of kingship; through contact with the Chinese mainland and Liaodong, early Han political-ethical ideas were probably known, though not yet deeply institutionalized.

writingSystem.zh: 百濟本土語言已存在，但正式記錄與對外交涉在少數場合可能使用漢字或借用漢文書寫方式；在220年，書寫仍限於王室與少數菁英，普及度很低。
writingSystem.en: The native Baekje language was in use, but formal record-keeping and external communication may in limited contexts have employed Chinese characters or adapted Literary Chinese writing; in 220 CE literacy remained restricted to the royal court and a small elite.

culturalAchievements.zh: 百濟已形成早期王都文化、木土結合的城郭建築、王室祭祀傳統、精緻的青銅與鐵製工藝，以及吸收漢江流域農耕社會與北方扶餘系傳統的複合文化基礎。
culturalAchievements.en: Baekje had already developed an early royal-capital culture, fortification architecture combining timber and earthwork construction, royal ritual traditions, refined bronze and iron craftsmanship, and a composite cultural base blending Han River agricultural society with northern Buyeo-derived traditions.

languageFamily.zh: 韓系語言，通常歸入古朝鮮半島語言群，與高句麗、新羅及倭地語言存在若干接觸與相近性
languageFamily.en: Koreanic, usually placed within the ancient linguistic complex of the Korean peninsula, with some contact and affinities with the languages of Goguryeo, Silla, and Wa

gdpDescription.zh: 百濟經濟以漢江流域農業剩餘為基礎，結合河運、手工業、鐵器製造與區域交換。以220年的朝鮮半島標準而言屬較強王國經濟，但仍以自然經濟與貢納體系為主，市場化有限。
gdpDescription.en: Baekje's economy was based on agricultural surplus from the Han River basin, combined with river transport, handicrafts, iron production, and regional exchange. By Korean peninsula standards in 220 CE it was a comparatively strong royal economy, but it remained primarily a natural economy structured by tribute and exaction, with limited marketization.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作與雜穀農業、漁鹽生產、鐵器鍛造、青銅工藝、木工、陶器、紡織與地方舟運
mainIndustries.en: Rice and millet agriculture, fishing and salt production, iron forging, bronze work, carpentry, pottery, textile production, and regional boat transport

tradeGoods.zh: 穀物、鹽、魚乾、布帛、鐵器、兵器、馬匹、皮革、木材、陶器，以及透過中介取得的中國系奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, salt, dried fish, cloth, iron tools, weapons, horses, leather, timber, pottery, and Chinese-derived prestige goods obtained through intermediated exchange

unitName.en: bolts of cloth and grain measures

householdWealth.zh: 核心地區農戶通常擁有住房、農具、少量鐵器、布帛與家畜；貴族則控制土地、附屬民、倉儲與武裝。整體財富分布不均，王都與漢江流域最富。
householdWealth.en: Households in the core region typically possessed dwellings, farm tools, some iron implements, cloth, and livestock; aristocrats controlled land, dependent labor, stores, and armed retainers. Wealth was unevenly distributed overall, with the capital and Han River basin the richest areas.

economicSystem.zh: 以農業貢納、勞役徵發與地方再分配為核心的早期王國經濟，並伴隨有限的區域貿易與政治性禮物交換
economicSystem.en: An early royal economy centered on agricultural tribute, labor exaction, and local redistribution, accompanied by limited regional trade and politically structured gift exchange

source.zh: 地方貢納與附屬部族獻納

source.en: Salt, fisheries, and river transport income

treasuryDescription.zh: 國庫主要以糧倉、布帛、武器、鹽與其他實物形式儲存，而非金屬貨幣；在王都與若干要塞城邑設有儲備。就早期王國而言，財政狀況穩定，足以應付常規防務與短期戰事。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was held chiefly in the form of granary reserves, cloth, weapons, salt, and other goods in kind rather than metallic currency; stocks were maintained in the capital and in several strategic fortresses. For an early kingdom, the fiscal position was stable and sufficient for routine defense and short campaigns.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 財政政策以維持王都供應、保障軍糧、鞏固貴族支持與加強漢江流域控制為優先；在穩定時期偏向積穀與保存實物儲備，而非擴張性開支。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized provisioning the capital, securing military grain supplies, maintaining aristocratic support, and strengthening control over the Han River basin; in stable times it favored stockpiling grain and preserving in-kind reserves rather than expansive spending.

unit.en: bolts of cloth and grain measures

description.zh: 由王都與核心地區動員，負責宮廷護衛、城防與主要野戰行動，是百濟軍力骨幹。
description.en: Drawn from the capital and core districts, this branch handled palace security, fortress defense, and principal field operations, forming the backbone of Baekje's military.

description.zh: 駐守地方城邑與要害渡口，平時兼具治安與運輸護衛功能，戰時可集中支援前線。
description.en: Stationed in regional fortresses and key river crossings, they also served policing and transport-protection roles in peacetime and could be concentrated to reinforce the front in war.

description.zh: 由貴族與有馬資源的上層提供，機動性較高，適合偵察、突擊與追擊。
description.en: Provided by aristocrats and elites with access to horses, these troops offered greater mobility and were suited to scouting, shock action, and pursuit.

description.zh: 沿漢江及西海岸活動，負責渡運、巡邏、補給與有限兩棲作戰。
description.en: Operating along the Han River and the western coast, they handled transport, patrol, supply, and limited amphibious operations.

technology.zh: 軍事技術以鐵製刀矛、弓箭、皮甲與木盾為主，配合土木城郭、防柵、壕溝與河運支援；騎兵規模有限但日益重要。
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron swords and spears, bows, leather armor, and wooden shields, supported by earthen-and-timber fortifications, palisades, ditches, and river transport; cavalry remained limited in size but was growing in importance.

threats.zh: 主要威脅來自北方高句麗的擴張壓力，以及與新羅、伽倻諸勢力在半島南中部的競爭；同時需警惕漢江流域內部豪族不穩與邊緣部族反覆。
threats.en: The main threats came from the expansionary pressure of Goguryeo to the north and rivalry with Silla and the Gaya polities in the south-central peninsula; the kingdom also had to watch for instability among Han River basin magnates and fluctuating frontier groups.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

description.zh: 重骑兵标志性武器，冲击力极强
description.en: Signature heavy cavalry weapon with tremendous charge impact

populationDescription.zh: 人口約五十萬，主要集中於漢江流域與其支流平原、河口與若干沿海地帶。王都及其周邊人口最稠密，外圍地區則由城邑與村落點狀分布。
populationDescription.en: The population was about 500,000, concentrated chiefly in the Han River basin, its tributary plains, estuarine zones, and some coastal areas. The capital and its surroundings were the most densely settled, while outer regions were distributed in fortress-settlement clusters and villages.

socialClasses.zh: 王族與大貴族居於頂層，其下為地方豪族與武士性從屬集團；大多數人口為農民，另有漁民、工匠、商旅與依附民。戰俘與罪犯可能淪為奴婢或類似不自由身分。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the royal house and great aristocrats, followed by regional magnates and martial retainer groups; the great majority of the population were peasants, alongside fishers, artisans, traders, and dependent people. Captives and criminals could fall into servile or near-unfree status.

allies.zh: 無穩固的長期正式同盟；在實際政治上，百濟可能與半島南部若干伽倻勢力及海上交流網中的倭地首長維持友好或互利關係，以牽制新羅與高句麗。
allies.en: There was no firm long-term formal alliance; in practical politics Baekje likely maintained friendly or mutually beneficial relations with some Gaya polities in southern Korea and with chiefs in the maritime networks of Wa, partly to counter Silla and Goguryeo.

enemies.zh: 主要敵手為高句麗；與新羅存在競逐與邊界緊張，對若干未完全整合的周邊部族亦保持警戒。
enemies.en: The principal rival was Goguryeo; Baekje also had competitive and tense relations with Silla and remained wary of nearby groups not yet fully integrated into the kingdom.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以鞏固漢江流域霸權、阻止高句麗南進、向南拓展影響並維持海上交通為中心；對中國三國局勢則採間接觀望與選擇性接觸，以獲取聲望與物資。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on consolidating supremacy in the Han River basin, checking Goguryeo's southward advance, extending influence southward, and maintaining maritime communications; toward the Three Kingdoms of China, Baekje likely adopted indirect observation and selective contact to gain prestige and material benefits.

era.zh: 朝鮮半島三國時代早期鐵器文明階段
era.en: Early Iron Age phase of the Three Kingdoms Era on the Korean peninsula

keyInnovations.zh: 鐵製農具與武器廣泛使用、木土城郭技術、河運與沿海航行能力提升、農業灌溉與穀倉管理經驗增進，以及對漢式器物與組織觀念的有限吸收。
keyInnovations.en: Key advances included widespread use of iron agricultural tools and weapons, timber-and-earth fortification techniques, improved riverine and coastal navigation, better irrigation and granary management, and limited adoption of Han-style objects and organizational ideas.

strengths.zh: 百濟在220年擁有漢江流域的農業資源、良好的水陸交通、上升中的王權與較強的軍事動員能力，並具備向南方與海上擴展的地理優勢。
strengths.en: In 220 CE Baekje possessed the agricultural resources of the Han River basin, strong land-and-water communications, rising royal authority, and relatively capable military mobilization, as well as geographic advantages for southward and maritime expansion.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱點在於政治整合仍未完全、地方豪族權力較大、官僚制度尚屬初步，且北有高句麗壓力、東南有新羅與伽倻競爭，使長期安全依舊脆弱。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in incomplete political integration, the considerable power of regional magnates, and a still-incipient bureaucratic order; pressure from Goguryeo in the north and competition from Silla and Gaya in the southeast kept long-term security fragile.

outlook.zh: 若能持續整合漢江流域、強化王室對地方的控制並利用海上交流擴展資源，百濟有望在未來數十年間成為半島西南部最具影響力的王國之一；但若北方戰事失利或內部貴族離心，國勢也可能迅速受挫。
outlook.en: If it could continue integrating the Han River basin, strengthen royal control over the local elites, and exploit maritime exchange to expand its resource base, Baekje was well positioned to become one of the most influential kingdoms in southwestern Korea in the coming decades; however, setbacks in northern warfare or aristocratic fragmentation could quickly weaken the realm.

description.zh: 百济据汉江流域发展，逐渐成为朝鲜半岛西南的重要王国。
description.en: Baekje developed in the Han River basin and was becoming a major kingdom in southwestern Korea.

governmentForm.zh: 部族贵族君主制，中央王权正在加强
governmentForm.en: A tribal-aristocratic monarchy with strengthening central royal authority

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王族、五部贵族、地方豪强、军户、自由平民农民、工匠、商人及少量奴婢构成。高句丽仍保留强烈的部族与地域组织传统，但王室通过任命官员、控制要塞和征发兵役逐步整合各部。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal house, the aristocracy of the Five Tribes, local strongmen, military households, free peasant farmers, artisans, merchants, and a smaller dependent or servile population. Goguryeo still retained strong tribal and regional forms of organization, but the royal house was increasingly integrating the realm through appointed officials, fortress control, and military levy obligations.

rulingClass.zh: 王族与五部贵族集团
rulingClass.en: The royal clan and the aristocratic elites of the Five Tribes

succession.zh: 王位通常在王族内部世袭，由贵族承认与拥立；并非绝对长子继承，兄终弟及或旁支继承在政治危机时亦可发生。
succession.en: The throne was hereditary within the royal house, but legitimacy depended on aristocratic recognition and support; succession was not strict primogeniture, and brother-to-brother or collateral succession could occur in times of political tension.

structure.zh: 220年的高句丽政体兼具宫廷王权与部族贵族联盟两种性质。国王依托王族、内廷近臣、五部首领及地方城主治理国家。中央对核心地区控制较强，对边疆和新附地区则更多依赖军事据点、地方豪族与附属集团。
structure.en: In 220 CE, Goguryeo's state combined a royal court monarchy with a confederated tribal-aristocratic order. The king ruled through the royal clan, close court attendants, the leaders of the Five Tribes, and local fortress lords. Central control was stronger in the core territories, while frontier and recently incorporated zones were governed more through military strongholds, local magnates, and subordinate groups.

function.zh: 负责王命起草、宫廷仪礼、侍从管理、王室财产与近侍事务，是国王直接施政的核心机构。
function.en: Handled royal orders, court ritual, attendants, royal estates, and the immediate affairs of the palace; it formed the king's core governing apparatus.

name.en: Council of the Five Tribal Aristocracies

function.zh: 协调各部利益，参与王位承认、军事动员、地方治理与重大对外决策。
function.en: Coordinated the interests of the major tribal aristocracies and took part in royal recognition, military mobilization, local administration, and major foreign policy decisions.

function.zh: 管理山城、边防、驻军、粮秣运输与边境警戒，对辽东及鸭绿江流域尤为重要。
function.en: Managed mountain fortresses, border defense, garrisons, supply transport, and frontier security, especially in Liaodong and along the Yalu basin.

name.en: Local District, Fortress, and Granary Administration

function.zh: 负责户口、土地、赋役、地方仓储、道路与基层征发。
function.en: Handled households, land, taxes and labor service, local granaries, roads, and basic levies.

function.zh: 主持祖先祭祀、山川祭礼、国家庆典以及与王权合法性相关的宗教事务。
function.en: Oversaw ancestral rites, mountain and river sacrifices, state ceremonies, and religious functions tied to royal legitimacy.

name.en: State Workshops and Military Supply Office

function.zh: 生产兵器、甲胄、车具、木石构件，并统筹官营工坊与战略物资。
function.en: Produced weapons, armor, wagon gear, wood and stone works, and supervised state workshops and strategic materiel.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以城、邑、要塞和部落辖区为核心。核心地区由王室或中央任命的官员与城主管理，边疆地区则由军事长官、地方豪族和被纳入体系的首领共同维持秩序。行政与军事往往不分，守城官兼具征税、司法与征兵功能。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on fortresses, settlements, walled districts, and tribal territories. In the core regions, officials and fortress commanders appointed by the court or royal clan held authority; in frontier areas, military officers, local magnates, and incorporated chieftains jointly maintained order. Civil and military administration often overlapped, with fortress commanders also handling taxation, justice, and conscription.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王命和贵族共识为基础，重视军纪、反叛惩处、财产争议、婚姻与部族秩序。刑罚较严，适用于盗窃、叛乱、逃役及违抗王命等行为；地方审判受贵族与城主影响很大。
legalSystem.en: The legal order rested on customary law, royal commands, and aristocratic consensus, with emphasis on military discipline, punishment of rebellion, property disputes, marriage, and tribal order. Penalties were often severe for theft, revolt, draft evasion, and disobedience to royal authority; local justice was strongly shaped by noble and fortress-lord influence.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依赖谷物、布帛、畜产、地方特产和劳役征发。核心农区缴纳粮赋，边疆与山地则多以猎产、皮毛、铁器、木材或军役形式承担义务。王室还直接控制部分土地、山林、关隘与贡赋来源。
taxationSystem.en: State revenue relied mainly on collections in grain, cloth, livestock products, local specialties, and labor service. Core farming districts paid grain dues, while frontier and upland zones often fulfilled obligations through hunting products, furs, iron goods, timber, or military service.
