The royal house also drew income from directly controlled lands, forests, passes, and tribute flows.

religion.zh: 高句丽宗教以祖先崇拜、天神与山川祭祀、萨满式信仰及国家祭礼为主。王权与祭祀密切相连，山岳、河流和战神性格的自然神灵在政治文化中地位很高。佛教尚未正式成为国教，汉地宗教观念与边疆信仰则通过接触逐步传入。
religion.en: Goguryeo religion centered on ancestor worship, heavenly and mountain-river cults, shamanic-style beliefs, and state rituals. Royal power was closely tied to sacrificial practice, and natural deities associated with mountains, rivers, and martial potency held major political-cultural importance. Buddhism had not yet become established as a state religion, though Chinese religious ideas and frontier cults were gradually entering through contact.

philosophy.zh: 主导思想并非系统哲学，而是王权合法性、武勇伦理、宗族义务与实用政治经验的结合。来自中原的儒家礼制观念在外交、文书与宫廷秩序中开始产生影响，但尚未完全取代本土传统。
philosophy.en: The dominant intellectual outlook was not a formal philosophy but a blend of royal legitimacy, martial values, clan obligation, and practical statecraft. Confucian notions from the Chinese world were beginning to influence diplomacy, writing, and court order, but had not yet displaced indigenous traditions.

writingSystem.zh: 官方书写主要借用汉字，用于外交、记录、刻铭与部分行政文书；口语仍为高句丽语。文字使用集中于王廷、贵族和与中原往来的精英层。
writingSystem.en: Official writing primarily used Classical Chinese characters for diplomacy, records, inscriptions, and some administrative documents, while the spoken language remained Goguryeo speech. Literacy and writing were concentrated among the court, aristocracy, and elites engaged with the Chinese world.

culturalAchievements.zh: 高句丽在220年前后最突出的文化成就是山城与都城营建、木石混合防御工程、早期王陵传统、壁画与装饰艺术的萌芽、尚武宴饮与歌舞传统，以及将北方山地生活方式与汉地制度文化相结合的能力。
culturalAchievements.en: Around 220 CE, Goguryeo's most notable cultural achievements were fortress and capital construction, mixed timber-and-stone defensive engineering, the development of royal tomb traditions, the early emergence of mural and decorative arts, martial feasting and song-dance traditions, and its ability to combine northern upland lifeways with institutional influences from the Chinese world.

languageFamily.zh: 高句丽语，通常被视为古代东北亚语言体系的一支，与朝鲜半岛北部及满洲南部语言环境相关
languageFamily.en: The Goguryeo language, generally regarded as part of the ancient Northeast Asian linguistic sphere and connected to the language environment of northern Korea and southern Manchuria

unit.en: shi of grain equivalent per year
shi of grain equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 高句丽经济在东北亚属于强势区域型经济体，以农业、畜养、渔猎、铁器生产和战争动员能力见长。其总量不及中原大帝国，但在其人口规模下已具备相当高的军事与工程组织能力。经济繁荣建立在鸭绿江、浑江流域农业，辽东交通控制，以及边贸与贡赋网络之上。
gdpDescription.en: Goguryeo's economy was a strong regional one by Northeast Asian standards, notable for agriculture, animal husbandry, fishing and hunting, iron production, and war mobilization capacity. Its overall output was far below that of the great Chinese empires, but for its population size it had considerable military and engineering organization. Its prosperity rested on the agriculture of the Yalu and Hun river basins, control of Liaodong communications, and networks of frontier trade and tribute.

mainIndustries.zh: 粟、黍、稻及豆类农业，马牛畜养，渔猎采集，铁器冶炼，木材与山林资源开发，兵器制造，盐与皮毛贸易
mainIndustries.en: Millet, sorghum, rice, and bean agriculture; horse and cattle raising; fishing, hunting, and gathering; iron smelting; timber and forest extraction; weapons production; and trade in salt and furs

tradeGoods.zh: 铁器、马匹、皮毛、木材、蜂蜜、布帛、山地产物、盐、粮食，以及从中原输入的丝绸、漆器、金属制品与奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Iron goods, horses, furs, timber, honey, cloth, upland products, salt, and grain, along with imported silk, lacquerware, metal goods, and luxuries from the Chinese world

name.zh: 谷物与布帛并用，兼行汉钱
name.en: Grain and cloth exchange with limited use of Chinese coinage

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要体现为耕地、粮仓、牲畜、农具和家庭劳动力。边地居民还依赖狩猎装备、马匹及山林资源。贵族家庭则拥有大片田地、附庸人口、武器储备和宴饮消费能力。
householdWealth.en: For ordinary households, wealth was mainly measured in cultivated land, stored grain, livestock, tools, and family labor. Frontier households also relied on hunting equipment, horses, and access to forest resources. Aristocratic households possessed larger tracts of land, dependent labor, arms stores, and the means for conspicuous feasting.

unit.en: shi of grain equivalent per household per year
shi of grain equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以王权控制、贵族支配和地方自给为特征的混合经济。农业是基础，国家通过贡赋、劳役、战利品和边境贸易获取额外资源。市场交换存在，但尚未形成高度货币化的城市商业经济。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy characterized by royal control, aristocratic dominance, and local self-sufficiency. Agriculture formed the base, while the state obtained additional resources through tribute, labor service, war booty, and frontier trade. Market exchange existed, but a highly monetized urban commercial economy had not yet developed.

unit.en: shi of grain equivalent per year
shi of grain equivalent per year
shi of grain equivalent per year

source.en: Frontier trade, tolls, and market income

source.zh: 王室直属土地与林泽收益

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要以谷物、布帛、军需、金属器与牲畜等实物形式存在，而非单纯货币。国内城及主要山城中设有仓储，既服务平时王廷，也保障战时持久守御。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury existed mainly in kind—grain, cloth, military stores, metal goods, and livestock—rather than as pure money. Granaries and depots at Gungnae and major mountain fortresses supported both routine court needs and prolonged wartime defense.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重储粮、备战和维持贵族忠诚。王廷通常避免对核心人口施加过度货币税，而以实物征收、季节性劳役和军事义务为主；在边疆扩张时期，则通过战利品、迁徙人口和控制贸易线补充财政。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized grain storage, war readiness, and maintaining aristocratic loyalty. The court generally avoided imposing excessive monetary taxes on the core population, relying instead on in-kind collections, seasonal labor service, and military duties; in periods of frontier expansion it also supplemented finances through booty, relocated populations, and control of trade routes.

description.zh: 驻守国内城及王室周边地区，负责宫廷安全、快速镇压内乱和支援国王亲征。
description.en: Stationed at Gungnae and around the royal domain, responsible for palace security, rapid suppression of unrest, and support for royal campaigns.

description.zh: 高句丽军队主力，善于山地、防御作战与攻守要塞，配备矛、弓、盾及部分甲胄。
description.en: The main body of Goguryeo's army, skilled in mountain warfare, defensive operations, and fortress attack and defense, equipped with spears, bows, shields, and some armor.

description.zh: 用于侦察、突袭、边境追击与辽东平原机动作战，是高句丽对外战争的重要兵种。
description.en: Used for reconnaissance, raiding, pursuit on the frontier, and mobile fighting on the Liaodong plains; it was a crucial arm in external warfare.

description.zh: 由各地城邑、部族与豪强提供，平时耕作或守备，战时集中动员。
description.en: Provided by local fortresses, tribal units, and magnates; they farmed or guarded locally in peacetime and were mobilized in war.

commanderInChief.zh: 山上王为最高统帅，实际指挥依赖王族将领与部族贵族将帅
commanderInChief.en: King Sansang served as commander-in-chief, with practical command exercised through royal kinsmen and aristocratic tribal generals

technology.zh: 高句丽军事技术以复合地形防御、山城体系、骑射、铁兵器和木石工事为核心。攻守城能力较强，善于利用险要地形、河谷与关隘消耗敌军。海军尚不突出，但河运与渡河作战具备一定能力。
technology.en: Goguryeo military technology centered on composite terrain defense, mountain-fortress systems, mounted archery, iron weapons, and timber-stone fortification works. It possessed strong siege and anti-siege capabilities and excelled at using difficult terrain, river valleys, and passes to wear down enemies. Naval power was limited, though river transport and crossing operations were known.

unit.en: shi of grain equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自辽东方向的曹魏势力扩张、北方与西北草原及山地集团的袭扰，以及朝鲜半岛南部百济、新罗、伽倻势力的竞争。高句丽同时也需防范内部贵族离心和边地附属集团不稳。
threats.en: The main threats came from the expanding power of Cao Wei in Liaodong, raiding or pressure from northern and northwestern steppe and upland groups, and competition from Baekje, Silla, and Gaya in southern Korea. Goguryeo also had to guard against internal aristocratic fragmentation and instability among frontier subordinate groups.

description.zh: 精锐训练标准，实战演习频繁
description.en: Elite standards, frequent live exercises

description.zh: 士气较高，部队凝聚力良好
description.en: Fairly high morale, good unit cohesion

description.zh: 大型配重式投石机，攻城主力
description.en: Large counterweight trebuchet, primary siege weapon

description.zh: 单兵弩与床弩，中国军队核心远程武器
description.en: Infantry crossbow and mounted crossbow, core ranged weapon

description.zh: 覆盖全身的重装骑兵铁甲
description.en: Full-body heavy cavalry iron barding

description.zh: 可连发十矢的机械弩，守城利器
description.en: Mechanical crossbow firing ten bolts in succession, excellent for defense

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

populationDescription.zh: 220年高句丽人口约70万，已是东北亚较大规模的区域国家之一。人口主要集中于鸭绿江、浑江和辽东若干河谷平地及要塞周边，山区分布较疏但军事意义重大。
populationDescription.en: In 220 CE, Goguryeo's population was about 700,000, making it one of the larger regional states in Northeast Asia. Population was concentrated in the Yalu and Hun river basins and selected plains and fortress zones of Liaodong, while the uplands were more thinly settled but strategically important.

socialClasses.zh: 社会等级大体可分为王族、部族贵族、地方豪强与军功精英、自由农民、工匠与商贩、附庸民及奴婢。阶层流动有限，但军功、王室恩赏和边疆扩张可为部分家庭带来上升机会。
socialClasses.en: Social hierarchy was broadly divided into the royal house, tribal aristocracy, local magnates and military elites, free farmers, artisans and traders, dependent peoples, and servile groups. Mobility was limited, but military service, royal favor, and frontier expansion could provide some families with opportunities to rise.

allies.zh: 无稳定意义上的正式同盟；与部分边疆部族、附属集团及对魏持敌意的地方势力保持临时合作。
allies.en: No stable formal allies in the strict sense; Goguryeo maintained temporary cooperation with certain frontier tribes, subordinate groups, and local forces hostile to Wei.

enemies.zh: 主要对手是辽东及中原北方的曹魏势力；同时与百济存在长期竞争，与新罗、伽倻关系复杂且多变。
enemies.en: Its principal rival was the Cao Wei power in Liaodong and northern China; it also faced long-term competition with Baekje, while relations with Silla and Gaya were complex and changeable.

foreignPolicy.zh: 高句丽奉行积极防御与机会扩张并行的外交政策。一方面通过山城防线和边军遏制中原政权东进，另一方面争取控制辽东通道与半岛北部势力范围，并通过朝贡、使节往来与有限贸易在强邻之间维持回旋空间。
foreignPolicy.en: Goguryeo pursued a foreign policy combining active defense with opportunistic expansion. It sought to block eastward pressure from Chinese states through fortress lines and frontier armies, while also aiming to control routes in Liaodong and influence northern Korea. Through tribute missions, embassies, and limited trade, it tried to preserve room for maneuver among stronger neighbors.

era.zh: 三国时代前期的东北亚铁器国家阶段
era.en: An early Three Kingdoms era Northeast Asian iron-age state phase

keyInnovations.zh: 山城与复合防御体系、铁制兵器与农具推广、骑兵与骑射战术、木石建筑工程、仓储与边防道路组织、汉字文书和铭刻的有限制度化应用
keyInnovations.en: Mountain-fortress and layered defense systems, broad use of iron weapons and tools, cavalry and mounted archery tactics, timber-stone construction engineering, granary and frontier road organization, and limited institutional use of Chinese writing for documents and inscriptions

strengths.zh: 高句丽的优势在于强悍的军事传统、复杂山地环境下的防御能力、辽东与朝鲜北部之间的战略地位，以及在王权与部族贵族之间逐步形成的国家组织。其人口虽不算庞大，但动员效率高，且具备持续修筑要塞和进行边疆战争的能力。
strengths.en: Goguryeo's strengths lay in its hard martial tradition, its defensive power in complex mountain terrain, its strategic position between Liaodong and northern Korea, and the state structure emerging between kingship and tribal aristocracy. Its population was not enormous, but it mobilized efficiently and could sustain fortress construction and frontier warfare.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于贵族势力仍强，王位继承与内部团结存在不确定性；经济货币化程度较低，对农业收成、劳役和边疆控制依赖较大；若遭遇中原强国长期进攻或内部贵族分裂，国家承压明显。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included the continued power of the aristocracy, uncertainty in succession and internal cohesion, relatively low monetization of the economy, and heavy dependence on harvests, labor obligations, and frontier control. If faced with sustained assault from a major Chinese power or serious aristocratic division, the kingdom would come under heavy strain.

outlook.zh: 220年的高句丽处于兴盛而紧张的上升阶段。若王室能继续整合贵族、巩固辽东与鸭绿江流域防线，并有效处理与魏及半岛诸国的关系，则有望继续成为东北亚最强大的国家之一；但其未来也将取决于能否在对外战争中避免过度消耗。
outlook.en: In 220 CE, Goguryeo was in a flourishing yet tense phase of ascent. If the royal house could continue integrating the aristocracy, consolidate its defenses in Liaodong and the Yalu basin, and manage relations with Wei and the peninsular states effectively, it could remain one of the strongest powers in Northeast Asia. Its future, however, would depend on avoiding overextension in external wars.

description.zh: 高句丽控制朝鲜半岛北部与辽东周边，是东北亚强国之一。
description.en: Goguryeo controlled northern Korea and adjacent Liaodong and was one of Northeast Asia's major powers.

governmentForm.zh: 以君主为核心的区域性贸易王国，依靠宫廷、贵族、地方总督与港口官员共同治理。
governmentForm.en: A monarch-centered regional trading kingdom governed through the royal court, aristocracy, provincial governors, and port officials.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与贵族居于顶层，其下为祭司、军事首领、商人、自由农民与牧民，底层包括依附劳工与奴隶；高地农业社群与红海商贸社群并存。
socialStructure.en: Society was headed by the royal house and nobility, followed by priests, military leaders, merchants, free peasants and pastoralists, with dependent laborers and slaves at the bottom; highland agrarian communities coexisted with Red Sea commercial groups.

rulingClass.zh: 阿克苏姆王室、高地贵族、军事首领以及掌控贸易与税收的宫廷官员。
rulingClass.en: The Aksumite royal house, highland nobility, military chiefs, and court officials who controlled trade and taxation.

succession.zh: 王位通常由王室内部世袭继承，但具体继承常受贵族、军事支持与宫廷联盟影响。
succession.en: The throne was generally hereditary within the royal house, though actual succession was shaped by noble backing, military support, and court alliances.

structure.zh: 中央政权围绕国王展开，首都宫廷负责军事、贡赋、外交与贸易监督；地方由附属首领、总督和港口官共同执行王命。
structure.en: Central authority revolved around the king, with the capital court directing military affairs, tribute, diplomacy, and trade oversight; local rule was carried out by subordinate chiefs, governors, and port officials.

function.zh: 管理王令、礼仪、使节接待与中央行政协调。
function.en: Managed royal decrees, ceremony, reception of envoys, and central administrative coordination.

function.zh: 征收粮食、牲畜、象牙与地方贡物，并管理储备。
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, ivory, and provincial tribute, and managed reserves.

function.zh: 监督阿杜利斯等港口的贸易、关税、度量衡与商旅登记。
function.en: Supervised trade, customs, weights and measures, and merchant registration at ports such as Adulis.

function.zh: 统筹王室亲军、地方征召、边境防务与远征准备。
function.en: Coordinated the royal guard, provincial levies, frontier defense, and expeditionary preparations.

function.zh: 处理与南阿拉伯、库施及罗马埃及商人和使者的关系。
function.en: Handled relations with South Arabia, Kush, and merchants and envoys from Roman Egypt.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政结合高地定居区域的王室任命官员与附属部族首领。港口、商路节点和肥沃农业区受到更直接控制，边缘地区则多以朝贡和军事服从维系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration combined royal appointees in settled highland zones with subordinate tribal chiefs. Ports, caravan nodes, and fertile agricultural districts were more directly controlled, while peripheral regions were held through tribute and military obedience.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要基于王权命令、习惯法、部族仲裁与财产惯例，商业纠纷尤其在港口和商路上由官员按惯例裁决。
legalSystem.en: Law was based chiefly on royal authority, customary law, tribal arbitration, and property practice, with commercial disputes—especially in ports and on trade routes—judged by officials according to established custom.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入来自农业贡赋、牲畜税、港口关税、商税、附庸进贡及战利品；实物征收与贵金属货币并行。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came from agrarian tribute, livestock taxes, port customs, commercial duties, tributary payments, and spoils of war; collection in kind operated alongside precious-metal coinage.

religion.zh: 220年的阿克苏姆仍以本土多神信仰为主，崇奉天神与地方神祇，并强调王权的神圣正当性；南阿拉伯宗教影响亦可见于铭文与王室象征。
religion.en: In 220 CE Aksum remained predominantly devoted to indigenous polytheistic religion, venerating heavenly and local deities while stressing the sacred legitimacy of kingship; South Arabian religious influence was also visible in inscriptions and royal symbolism.

philosophy.zh: 阿克苏姆没有形成如中原诸子般成体系哲学，核心观念体现为王权、祭祀、祖先传统、商业信誉与跨海交往中的实用政治智慧。
philosophy.en: Aksum did not produce a formal philosophical tradition comparable to the Chinese schools, but its core ideas centered on kingship, ritual, ancestral custom, mercantile trust, and practical political wisdom in overseas interaction.

writingSystem.zh: 使用早期吉兹文铭刻，同时在对外与钱币环境中受南阿拉伯文字传统影响；碑铭和王权纪念文是重要书写形式。
writingSystem.en: Early Ge'ez was used in inscriptions, while South Arabian script traditions influenced external and numismatic contexts; stelae inscriptions and royal commemorative texts were major written forms.

culturalAchievements.zh: 阿克苏姆以巨型石碑、王权纪念建筑、成熟的远洋贸易文化、跨红海外交网络及较早铸币传统著称，是东北非洲最具国际性的文明之一。
culturalAchievements.en: Aksum was noted for monumental stelae, royal commemorative architecture, a mature maritime trade culture, cross–Red Sea diplomatic networks, and an early coinage tradition, making it one of the most internationally connected civilizations in Northeast Africa.

languageFamily.zh: 闪含语系阿非罗-亚细亚语系中的南闪米特支，以吉兹语为王国核心语言之一，并与库施特语族和其他地方语言长期接触。
languageFamily.en: Afro-Asiatic, specifically South Semitic, with Ge'ez as one of the kingdom’s core languages, in long contact with Cushitic and other local languages.

gdpDescription.zh: 按古代标准，阿克苏姆经济高度繁荣。其财富并非仅来自高地产粮区，而是来自将非洲内陆象牙、奴隶、香料、龟甲、黄金与罗马、阿拉伯、印度洋世界之间的转运贸易结合起来的能力。
gdpDescription.en: By ancient standards, Aksum’s economy was highly prosperous. Its wealth came not only from productive highland agriculture but from its ability to combine inland African exports—ivory, slaves, aromatics, tortoiseshell, and gold—with transit trade linking the Roman, Arabian, and Indian Ocean worlds.

mainIndustries.zh: 高地农业、畜牧业、象牙与奢侈品贸易、红海航运、港口服务、金属加工、石材建筑与铸币。
mainIndustries.en: Highland agriculture, pastoralism, ivory and luxury trade, Red Sea shipping, port services, metalworking, stone construction, and minting.

tradeGoods.zh: 象牙、犀角、黄金、奴隶、乳香与没药、龟甲、皮革、牲畜、盐，以及进口的罗马酒、玻璃器、金属器、纺织品与印度洋奢侈品。
tradeGoods.en: Ivory, rhinoceros horn, gold, slaves, frankincense and myrrh, tortoiseshell, hides, livestock, and salt, alongside imports such as Roman wine, glassware, metal goods, textiles, and Indian Ocean luxuries.

name.zh: 阿克苏姆金币、银币与铜币
name.en: Aksumite gold, silver, and bronze coinage

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异极大。高地普通农户财富主要表现为土地使用权、谷物储备与牲畜；商人和贵族则积累贵金属货币、进口奢侈品、奴隶和驮畜。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied sharply. Ordinary highland farmers held wealth mainly in land use rights, grain stores, and livestock, while merchants and nobles accumulated precious-metal coin, imported luxuries, slaves, and pack animals.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业基础、王室再分配和长距离商业并存的混合经济。国家通过港口、关税和朝贡体系介入市场，但地方生产仍多依赖村社与贵族网络。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining an agrarian base, royal redistribution, and long-distance commerce. The state intervened through ports, customs, and tributary systems, while local production still relied heavily on village communities and aristocratic networks.

source.zh: 牲畜税、盐税及杂项征收
source.en: Livestock taxes, salt dues, and miscellaneous levies

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以贵金属、进口奢侈品、粮食储备和牲畜形式并存。由于阿克苏姆控制贸易节点，流动性优于许多纯农业国家，但财政亦受红海航道安全与区域战争影响。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury existed in a mix of precious metal, imported luxury goods, grain reserves, and livestock. Because Aksum controlled key trade nodes, its liquidity was stronger than that of many purely agrarian states, though finances were still sensitive to Red Sea security and regional warfare.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 王室倾向于维持港口开放、保障商路和强化贡赋体系，以贸易税补足农业征收；财政政策务实，重在稳定现金与实物双重收入来源。
fiscalPolicy.en: The crown aimed to keep ports open, secure trade routes, and strengthen tributary collection, using customs revenues to supplement agrarian dues; fiscal policy was pragmatic and focused on stabilizing both monetary and in-kind income.

description.zh: 由长矛、盾牌与近战武器装备的核心地面部队，承担宫廷保卫、边境行动和主要野战任务。
description.en: The core land force equipped with spears, shields, and close-combat weapons, responsible for court protection, frontier action, and major field operations.

description.zh: 由附属地区与高地社群按需征发，适合地区防御、护送和大规模远征时补充兵力。
description.en: Raised as needed from dependent regions and highland communities, suitable for local defense, escort duty, and reinforcement during larger expeditions.

description.zh: 规模不大，主要用于侦察、通信、护送商队和机动作战支持。
description.en: Limited in size, used mainly for reconnaissance, communications, caravan escort, and operational mobility support.

name.zh: 港口守备与海上护航力量
name.en: Port Garrisons and Maritime Escort Forces

description.zh: 驻守阿杜利斯等港口，保护仓储、船只与海上贸易航线，虽非大型海军，但具备护航与沿岸控制能力。
description.en: Stationed at ports such as Adulis to protect warehouses, shipping, and sea lanes; not a large navy, but capable of escort and coastal control.

technology.zh: 军备以铁制兵器、长矛、盾牌、弓箭和攻守城基础技术为主，配合红海航海经验和跨山地后勤能力，在东北非与南阿拉伯环境中具有较强适应性。
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron weapons, spears, shields, bows, and basic siege methods, combined with Red Sea seafaring experience and cross-highland logistics, giving strong adaptability in Northeast African and South Arabian conditions.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与麦罗埃库施的竞争、南阿拉伯政治介入、红海海路安全以及边疆部族的不稳定。阿克苏姆虽强盛，但必须持续维护贸易与军事平衡。
threats.en: Main threats came from rivalry with Meroitic Kush, political involvement in South Arabia, the security of Red Sea shipping, and instability among frontier peoples. Aksum was powerful but had to maintain a constant balance between trade and military force.

description.zh: 精锐训练标准，实战演习频繁
description.en: Elite standards, frequent live exercises

description.zh: 士气较高，部队凝聚力良好
description.en: Fairly high morale, good unit cohesion

description.zh: 复合弓或长弓，远射主力
description.en: Composite or longbow, primary ranged weapon

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 有顶棚防护的攻城冲车
description.en: Roofed battering ram carriage for siege warfare

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

description.zh: 单兵弩与床弩，中国军队核心远程武器
description.en: Infantry crossbow and mounted crossbow, core ranged weapon

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于厄立特里亚与埃塞俄比亚北部高地农业区，另有沿海港口居民、商人群体、牧民及附属边疆社群。作为区域强国，阿克苏姆人口在东北非属高水平。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated mainly in the highland agricultural zones of northern Ethiopia and Eritrea, with additional coastal port populations, merchant communities, pastoralists, and dependent frontier groups. As a regional great power, Aksum’s population was large by Northeast African standards.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与贵族、祭司与官员、军人、港口商人与长途商旅、手工业者、自由农民与牧民、依附民和奴隶构成主要社会阶层。
socialClasses.en: The main social strata were the royal family and nobles, priests and officials, soldiers, port merchants and long-distance traders, artisans, free peasants and pastoralists, dependents, and slaves.

allies.zh: 阿克苏姆在220年没有稳定意义上的正式盟友体系，但与部分南阿拉伯派系、红海商人网络以及某些附属地方首领维持合作关系。
allies.en: In 220 CE Aksum did not maintain a fixed alliance system in the formal sense, but it cooperated with certain South Arabian factions, Red Sea merchant networks, and some subordinate local rulers.

enemies.zh: 主要对手为麦罗埃库施，以及在南阿拉伯与红海贸易控制上与其竞争的希木叶尔和其他南阿拉伯势力。
enemies.en: Its principal opponents were Meroitic Kush, along with Himyar and other South Arabian powers competing for influence in Arabia and control of Red Sea trade.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以控制红海贸易、干预南阿拉伯政治、压制尼罗河上游竞争者并维持与罗马埃及商路畅通为核心。阿克苏姆倾向通过商业、威望与有限军事投送共同扩张影响力。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on controlling Red Sea trade, intervening in South Arabian politics, checking competitors in the Upper Nile sphere, and preserving open routes to Roman Egypt. Aksum tended to expand influence through a combination of commerce, prestige, and limited military projection.

era.zh: 古典时代晚期前段，约相当于东亚三国时代同时期
era.en: Early Late Classical Age, roughly contemporary with the Three Kingdoms era in East Asia

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的红海航运组织、区域性铸币体系、石碑与巨石建筑工程、高地农业整合、跨山地与海港物流网络，以及多区域贸易管理能力。
keyInnovations.en: Advanced Red Sea maritime organization, a regional coinage system, stelae and megalithic construction engineering, integrated highland agriculture, cross-highland and port logistics networks, and effective management of multi-regional trade.

architectureAndTrade.zh: 石碑建筑与国际贸易枢纽：阿克苏姆王国修建了世界上最高的独石石碑（方尖碑），高达33米，展示了先进的石材加工和工程技术。阿杜利斯港是红海最重要的贸易港口之一，连接罗马帝国和印度洋贸易网络。
architectureAndTrade.en: Stele architecture and international trade hub: the Kingdom of Aksum erected the world's tallest monolithic stelae (obelisks), up to 33m tall, demonstrating advanced stone-working and engineering. The port of Adulis is one of the Red Sea's most important trade ports, connecting the Roman Empire and Indian Ocean trade networks.

overallAssessment.zh: 公元220年的阿克苏姆王国是东非最先进的文明，石碑建筑工程和国际贸易网络展示了其技术和组织能力。阿杜利斯港使阿克苏姆成为连接地中海与印度洋世界的关键枢纽。阿克苏姆即将成为最早皈依基督教的非洲王国之一。
overallAssessment.en: The Kingdom of Aksum in 220 CE is East Africa's most advanced civilization; stele engineering and international trade networks demonstrate its technological and organizational capability. The port of Adulis makes Aksum a key hub connecting Mediterranean and Indian Ocean worlds. Aksum will soon become one of the earliest African kingdoms to adopt Christianity.

strengths.zh: 阿克苏姆拥有强大的贸易地位、稳定的高地农业基础、铸币能力、红海港口控制权及对南阿拉伯事务的干预能力，是220年前后东北非最强盛的国家之一。
strengths.en: Aksum possessed a powerful trade position, a stable highland agrarian base, minting capability, control of Red Sea ports, and the ability to intervene in South Arabian affairs, making it one of the strongest states in Northeast Africa around 220 CE.

weaknesses.zh: 王国高度依赖商路安全与区域转运，若红海航运受阻或南阿拉伯局势恶化，财政会迅速承压；同时地方统治仍需依赖附属首领，中央集权尚非绝对稳固。
weaknesses.en: The kingdom was highly dependent on secure trade routes and regional transit; if Red Sea shipping were disrupted or South Arabian politics worsened, finances would come under rapid strain. At the same time, local rule still depended on subordinate chiefs, so centralization was not yet fully secure.

outlook.zh: 220年的阿克苏姆前景总体乐观。只要继续掌控阿杜利斯和红海通道、压制库施并维持高地生产，它很可能进一步成长为跨非洲与阿拉伯海域的重要帝国。
outlook.en: Aksum’s outlook in 220 CE was broadly favorable. If it continued to control Adulis and the Red Sea corridor, contain Kush, and preserve highland production, it was likely to develop further into a major empire spanning African and Arabian spheres.

description.zh: 阿克苏姆控制红海贸易，是东北非最强大的国家之一。
description.en: Aksum dominated Red Sea trade and was one of the most powerful states in Northeast Africa.

governmentForm.zh: 以金官伽倻為首的鬆散部族—城邦邦聯，各國由世襲王族統治，透過盟約、婚姻與軍事協商維持共同利益
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal-city-state confederation led by Geumgwan Gaya, with constituent polities ruled by hereditary royal houses and bound together by alliance, marriage, and military consultation.

socialStructure.zh: 社會由王族、地方貴族、戰士、鐵匠與其他工匠、農民、漁民、商人及依附民構成，沿洛東江流域形成層級分明但地方性強的社會
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of royal houses, local aristocrats, warriors, ironworkers and other craftsmen, farmers, fishers, merchants, and dependent commoners, forming a clearly stratified yet strongly local order along the Nakdong basin.

rulingClass.zh: 各小國王族、邑落首長與掌握鐵器生產和貿易的貴族家門
rulingClass.en: The ruling class comprised the royal clans of the member polities, settlement chiefs, and aristocratic lineages controlling iron production and trade.

succession.zh: 以家族世襲為主，通常由王族直系或近支繼承；邦聯盟主地位則受實力、婚盟與各國承認影響
succession.en: Succession was primarily hereditary within ruling houses, usually passing to direct or collateral royal kin; the leading position within the confederacy depended additionally on power, marriage ties, and recognition by other member states.

structure.zh: 伽倻並無統一的中央官僚國家，而是由金官伽倻、阿羅伽倻、大伽倻前身諸勢力等組成的邦聯。盟主國處理對外關係、戰時協調與貢納分配，各成員國保留內政、祭祀與土地控制權。
structure.en: Gaya was not a unified centralized bureaucracy but a confederation of polities including Geumgwan Gaya, Ara Gaya, and early powers that would later be associated with Daegaya. The leading state managed foreign relations, wartime coordination, and tribute distribution, while member polities retained internal administration, ritual authority, and control over land.

function.zh: 由王族、貴族與主要邑落首長組成，討論盟約、戰事、婚姻外交與重大裁決
function.en: Composed of royal kin, nobles, and major settlement chiefs, it deliberated on alliances, warfare, marriage diplomacy, and major judgments.

function.zh: 徵收穀物、布帛、鐵料與勞役，並管理王室倉儲與分配
function.en: Collected grain, cloth, iron materials, and labor dues, and managed royal storage and redistribution.

function.zh: 監督冶鐵、鍛造、兵器與農具生產，並協調對外交易
function.en: Oversaw smelting, smithing, the production of weapons and agricultural tools, and coordination of external trade.

function.zh: 統籌戰士徵發、城寨防衛、河道渡口與邊境警戒
function.en: Coordinated warrior levies, fortification defense, river crossings, and frontier watch.

function.zh: 主持祖先祭祀、王室禮儀、墓葬工程與神聖合法性相關事務
function.en: Managed ancestral rites, royal ceremonies, tomb construction, and matters tied to sacred legitimacy.

localAdmin.zh: 地方以邑落、港口聚落與小型城寨為行政單位，由王族親屬、豪族或首長管理，負責徵收實物稅、動員勞役並維持地方秩序。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on settlements, port communities, and small fortified sites, managed by royal relatives, local magnates, or chiefs responsible for collecting taxes in kind, mobilizing labor, and maintaining order.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以習慣法、王命與貴族仲裁為主，重視血緣秩序、財產、婚姻與對王權的不敬；對盜竊、叛服與違反盟約者施以賠償、奴役或處決。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on customary law, royal commands, and aristocratic arbitration, emphasizing kin order, property, marriage, and respect for royal authority; theft, rebellion, and breaches of alliance could be punished by compensation, servitude, or execution.

taxationSystem.zh: 稅收主要以實物與勞役形式徵集，包括稻穀、粟、豆、布、魚鹽、鐵錠與成品鐵器；王室亦從礦產、工坊與貿易渡口取得收入。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was collected chiefly in kind and labor service, including rice, millet, beans, cloth, fish, salt, iron ingots, and finished iron goods; the royal houses also drew income from mineral extraction, workshops, and trade crossings.

religion.zh: 以薩滿性質的本地信仰、祖先崇拜與山川祭祀為主，王權與祭儀密切相連；佛教尚未在伽倻形成制度性影響。
religion.en: Religion was centered on local shamanic practice, ancestor worship, and rites to mountains and rivers, with kingship closely tied to ritual; Buddhism had not yet developed institutional influence in Gaya.

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成如中國諸子般的成文哲學傳統，政治倫理主要來自宗族秩序、祭祀觀念、勇武價值與對外來漢文化禮制元素的有限吸收。
philosophy.en: There was no developed written philosophical tradition comparable to the Chinese schools; political ethics derived mainly from clan hierarchy, ritual ideas, martial values, and limited absorption of Han cultural norms.

writingSystem.zh: 本地尚無成熟自有文字，正式記錄極少；對外交流可能零星使用或接觸漢字與漢文記錄方式，但行政運作仍以口傳和記憶為主。
writingSystem.en: There was no mature indigenous writing system in local use, and formal records were scarce; in external contacts Gaya may have encountered or occasionally used Chinese characters and literary Chinese methods, but administration remained primarily oral and memory-based.

culturalAchievements.zh: 伽倻以高水準冶鐵技術、精良甲胄兵器、土器、珠飾以及大型木槨與豐富副葬墓制聞名，展現洛東江流域高度活躍的工藝與交換文化。
culturalAchievements.en: Gaya was noted for advanced ironworking, quality armor and weapons, pottery, bead ornamentation, and large wooden-chamber tombs with rich grave goods, reflecting a highly active craft and exchange culture in the Nakdong basin.

languageFamily.zh: 一般被視為古代韓語系或半島南部扶餘—韓語群的一部分，與三韓諸語相近，但具地方差異。
languageFamily.en: Its language is generally treated as part of the ancient Koreanic or southern peninsular Puyŏ-Han linguistic continuum, related to the Samhan languages but with local variation.

unit.en: seok of grain equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 伽倻經濟屬於以農業為基礎、冶鐵為特色、沿河與海上交換為增益的區域性首領經濟。其財富不在大規模貨幣流通，而在糧食盈餘、鐵產量、工藝品質與對日交流能力。
gdpDescription.en: The Gaya economy was a regional chiefly economy based on agriculture, distinguished by iron production and strengthened by riverine and maritime exchange. Its wealth lay not in large-scale monetization but in food surplus, iron output, craft quality, and the ability to trade with the Japanese archipelago.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作與旱作農業、冶鐵與鍛造、兵器與農具製造、陶器、漁鹽、木工、珠飾加工與區域轉運貿易
mainIndustries.en: Wet-rice and dry-field agriculture, iron smelting and smithing, manufacture of weapons and farm tools, pottery, fishing and salt production, woodworking, bead making, and regional transit trade.

tradeGoods.zh: 鐵錠、鐵器、甲胄、武器、陶器、布帛、珠玉、鹽、魚乾、稻穀，以及自外部取得的奢侈品與原材料
tradeGoods.en: Iron ingots, iron tools, armor, weapons, pottery, cloth, beads, salt, dried fish, rice, and imported prestige goods and raw materials.

householdWealth.zh: 普通農戶財富主要體現在田地使用權、穀藏、家畜、農具與宗族網絡；精英財富則集中於鐵資源控制權、武器、馬具、奢侈品與墓葬動員能力。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was mainly held in land-use rights, stored grain, livestock, tools, and kinship ties; elite wealth was concentrated in control over iron resources, weapons, horse gear, prestige goods, and the ability to mobilize labor for burials.

unit.zh: 石糧等值/成年勞動者年
unit.en: seok of grain equivalent per adult worker per year

economicSystem.zh: 以宗族控制土地、實物租賦、首領再分配與工坊生產相結合的混合經濟，市場交換存在但未脫離貴族權力與禮物外交。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining kin-based control of land, taxes in kind, chiefly redistribution, and workshop production, with market exchange present but still embedded in aristocratic power and gift diplomacy.

source.en: Commuted labor dues and subordinate tribute

treasuryDescription.zh: 國庫並非單一金屬庫藏，而是分散於王室糧倉、鐵料庫、武器庫、布帛與禮品儲備中的綜合財富。其動員能力比名義存量更重要。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not a single metallic reserve but a composite store of wealth dispersed among royal granaries, iron depots, armories, cloth stocks, and gift reserves. Its ability to mobilize resources mattered more than nominal holdings.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 財政政策偏向穩健，維持小幅盈餘以備歉收與戰事，同時優先投入鐵業、軍備與對外贈答，以鞏固邦聯內部與對外地位。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was generally cautious, maintaining a modest surplus against bad harvests and warfare while prioritizing investment in iron production, military resources, and diplomatic gifting to reinforce internal cohesion and external standing.

description.zh: 由王族侍從與貴族戰士組成，裝備較佳，用於護衛王庭、指揮核心與突擊作戰。
description.en: Formed from royal retainers and aristocratic warriors, better equipped and used for court protection, command duties, and shock combat.

description.zh: 由各邑落徵發的步兵構成，使用矛、盾、弓與鐵製短兵器，是防守城寨與野戰的主要力量。
description.en: Made up of levied infantry from local settlements, armed with spears, shields, bows, and iron sidearms; they formed the principal force for fort defense and field operations.

description.zh: 數量不大但具機動性，多由貴族與富裕戰士擔任，用於偵察、追擊與展示威望。
description.en: Small in number but mobile, largely drawn from nobles and wealthy warriors, used for scouting, pursuit, and displays of prestige.

description.zh: 以小型舟船與港口武裝人員為主，負責洛東江下游巡防、運輸護衛與對海上交通線的控制。
description.en: Consisted mainly of small craft crews and armed port personnel responsible for patrols in the lower Nakdong, transport escort, and control of maritime routes.

commanderInChief.zh: 邦聯盟主王及諸國將帥共議
commanderInChief.en: The leading confederate king in consultation with the commanders of the member states

technology.zh: 軍事技術以優質鐵兵器、鐵甲片、長矛、弓箭、木盾、土木城寨和小型舟船為核心；在冶鐵品質上具區域優勢，但缺乏大帝國式攻城體系。
technology.en: Military technology centered on high-quality iron weapons, lamellar elements, spears, bows, wooden shields, earth-and-timber fortifications, and small vessels; Gaya enjoyed a regional advantage in iron quality but lacked an imperial-scale siege system.

threats.zh: 主要威脅來自新羅與百濟在半島南部的擴張競爭，以及邦聯內部各國利益不一導致的協同不足；北方強權高句麗雖距離較遠，仍影響整體半島權力平衡。
threats.en: The main threats came from the expansionist rivalry of Silla and Baekje in southern Korea, along with weak coordination caused by divergent interests among the member states; the northern power of Goguryeo, though more distant, still affected the peninsula-wide balance.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

description.zh: 可连发十矢的机械弩，守城利器
description.en: Mechanical crossbow firing ten bolts in succession, excellent for defense

description.zh: 重骑兵标志性武器，冲击力极强
description.en: Signature heavy cavalry weapon with tremendous charge impact

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中於洛東江中下游平原、河口地帶與沿海聚落，分布於數個伽倻小國之中。人口規模足以支撐穩定農業、工匠生產與有限對外軍事行動。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in the middle and lower Nakdong plains, estuarine zones, and coastal settlements, distributed across several Gaya polities. This scale was sufficient to sustain stable agriculture, craft production, and limited external military activity.

name.zh: 古寧伽倻地區中心聚落
name.en: Core settlement of the Goryeong Gaya region

socialClasses.zh: 上層為王族與貴族，其下為戰士與地方豪族，再下為自由農民、工匠與商旅，最底層為依附民、債役者與戰俘。社會地位常與血緣、冶鐵控制權和軍事服務相連。
socialClasses.en: At the top were royal and aristocratic lineages, followed by warriors and local magnates, then free peasants, craftsmen, and traders, with dependent laborers, debt bondsmen, and captives at the bottom. Social status was closely linked to kinship, control of iron production, and military service.

allies.zh: 無固定永久盟友；與倭地諸勢力保持活躍海上交流，並依局勢與百濟或新羅中的一方形成臨時合作或婚姻聯繫。
allies.en: There were no fixed permanent allies; Gaya maintained active maritime ties with Wa polities and, depending on circumstances, entered temporary cooperation or marriage connections with either Baekje or Silla.

enemies.zh: 無單一絕對宿敵，但與新羅在洛東江東部和南部通道上存在長期競爭，亦警惕百濟勢力南下及各伽倻小國間的內部摩擦。
enemies.en: There was no single absolute archenemy, but Gaya faced sustained rivalry with Silla over routes east of the Nakdong and in the south, while also watching Baekje’s southern influence and friction among the Gaya states themselves.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以平衡求存為核心：利用鐵器出口、婚盟、禮物外交與港口貿易，在百濟與新羅之間維持空間，同時經由海路加強與倭地往來，以避免被單一鄰國吞併。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on survival through balance: using iron exports, marriage alliances, gift diplomacy, and port trade to preserve room for maneuver between Baekje and Silla, while strengthening maritime ties with Wa to avoid absorption by any single neighbor.

era.zh: 三國時代早期的朝鮮半島鐵器文化階段
era.en: Early Three Kingdoms-era iron culture of the Korean Peninsula

keyInnovations.zh: 高效率冶鐵與鍛造、鐵製農具與武器量產、甲胄與馬具製作、河海聯運技術、木槨墓與工藝性隨葬品製作
keyInnovations.en: Efficient iron smelting and forging, large-scale production of iron agricultural tools and weapons, manufacture of armor and horse gear, integrated river-sea transport techniques, and the construction of wooden-chamber tombs with elaborate grave goods.

strengths.zh: 伽倻最大的優勢在於豐富鐵資源、優秀工藝、河海交通位置與對倭交流網絡。其多中心結構使地方活力強，能快速動員特定區域資源並以鐵器換取外部支持。
strengths.en: Gaya’s chief strengths were its rich iron resources, strong craftsmanship, strategic river-sea location, and exchange networks with Wa. Its multicentered structure gave it strong local dynamism and the ability to mobilize specific regional resources quickly, using iron goods to obtain outside support.

weaknesses.zh: 其根本弱點是邦聯鬆散、缺乏統一中央權威，面對新羅或百濟等較集中政權時不易持久協同；人口與農業腹地亦不足以支撐長期大規模戰爭。
weaknesses.en: Its fundamental weaknesses were confederal looseness and the absence of a unified central authority, making sustained coordination difficult against more centralized powers such as Silla or Baekje; its population and agrarian base were also insufficient for prolonged large-scale war.

outlook.zh: 220年時伽倻整體局勢穩定，並因鐵器貿易而具相當活力；短期內仍可維持區域自主與經濟重要性，但若無法形成更緊密的政治整合，長期前景將受新羅與百濟擴張嚴重擠壓。
outlook.en: In 220 CE Gaya was broadly stable and economically vigorous thanks to iron trade; in the short term it could preserve regional autonomy and importance, but without tighter political integration its long-term prospects would be heavily constrained by the expansion of Silla and Baekje.

description.zh: 伽倻是洛东江流域的铁资源联盟，介于百济与新罗之间。
description.en: Gaya was an iron-rich confederation in the Nakdong basin situated between Baekje and Silla.

ruler.zh: 不确定的麦罗埃君主（国王或坎达刻）

governmentForm.zh: 世袭君主制，结合王权、神庙权威与地方贵族统治
governmentForm.en: Hereditary monarchy combining royal authority, temple influence, and provincial noble rule

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、神庙祭司、贵族与地方总督、军人、工匠与商人、农民牧民以及奴婢与附属人口构成；尼罗河谷定居农业区与东部沙漠、草原地带的半游牧群体并存。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal family, temple priests, nobles and provincial governors, soldiers, artisans and merchants, farmers and pastoralists, and enslaved or dependent populations; settled Nile valley agricultural communities coexisted with semi-nomadic groups in the eastern desert and savanna zones.

rulingClass.zh: 麦罗埃王室、与王权结盟的祭司集团、地方贵族与军政官员
rulingClass.en: The Meroitic royal house, priestly elites allied to the crown, and provincial nobles and military-administrative officials

succession.zh: 王位通常由王室内部世袭继承，可能受母系血统与王族女性合法性影响；在政治衰弱时期，继承过程可能更依赖宫廷派系与地方精英支持。
succession.en: The throne was usually inherited within the royal house, likely influenced by matrilineal legitimacy and royal women; in periods of weakness, succession probably depended more heavily on court factions and support from provincial elites.

structure.zh: 中央朝廷设于麦罗埃，以君主为核心，通过宫廷官员、神庙机构与地方总督治理尼罗河沿岸各主要区域；对边远地区的控制较为间接，依赖附属首领、驻军与贡赋关系。
structure.en: The central court at Meroe revolved around the monarch and governed the main Nile regions through court officials, temple institutions, and provincial governors; control over peripheral areas was more indirect, relying on subordinate chiefs, garrisons, and tribute ties.

function.zh: 管理君主诏令、宫廷礼仪、王室财产与王家档案
function.en: Managed royal decrees, court ceremony, royal estates, and palace records

function.zh: 监督主要神庙、祭司组织、祭祀活动与神庙土地收入
function.en: Oversaw major temples, priestly organizations, rituals, and income from temple lands

function.zh: 征收粮食、牲畜、手工业品与商税，并管理王国仓储与再分配
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, craft goods, and trade dues, and managed royal storage and redistribution

function.zh: 协调诺姆式地区、边境驻地、地方首领与交通节点治理
function.en: Coordinated governance of district-like regions, frontier posts, local chiefs, and transport nodes

function.zh: 组织驻军、征发兵员、边防巡逻与军需供给
function.en: Organized garrisons, levies, frontier patrols, and military provisioning

function.zh: 管理铁冶炼、王室工坊、商路征税与对外交换
function.en: Managed iron production, royal workshops, caravan taxation, and external exchange

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托尼罗河沿岸城镇、神庙中心与地方贵族网络。核心地带由王室任命或认可的官员统治，边远地带则更依赖附属部族首领和驻军据点。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on Nile towns, temple centers, and networks of provincial nobles. Core regions were ruled by officials appointed or recognized by the crown, while peripheral zones depended more on subordinate tribal leaders and garrison posts.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以王命、习惯法、神庙权威和地方裁断相结合，涉及土地、赋税、婚姻、继承与贸易纠纷。成文法细节保存不多，但司法显然与王权和祭司权威紧密相连。
legalSystem.en: Law combined royal decree, customary practice, temple authority, and local adjudication, covering land, taxation, marriage, inheritance, and trade disputes. Few details of codified law survive, but justice was clearly tied closely to royal and priestly authority.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以实物为主，包括谷物、牲畜、黄金、铁器、手工业品与劳役；对尼罗河航运、商队贸易和边境交换征收关税与通行费。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was mainly in kind, including grain, livestock, gold, iron goods, craft products, and labor service; customs and transit dues were imposed on Nile transport, caravan trade, and frontier exchange.

religion.zh: 宗教以本土库施传统与埃及化神祇崇拜相结合，崇奉阿蒙、伊西斯、阿佩德马克等神灵；王权具有强烈神圣性，神庙仍是政治与经济生活的重要中心。
religion.en: Religion combined indigenous Kushite traditions with Egyptianized cults, venerating deities such as Amun, Isis, and Apedemak; kingship carried strong sacred meaning, and temples remained major centers of political and economic life.

philosophy.zh: 库施并无如希腊或中国式系统哲学传统，思想核心体现为王权神授、祖先崇拜、祭祀秩序、王国与自然丰饶之间的神圣联系。
philosophy.en: Kush did not possess a systematic philosophical tradition like Greece or China; its core worldview emphasized divinely sanctioned kingship, ancestor reverence, ritual order, and the sacred link between the kingdom and natural fertility.

writingSystem.zh: 使用麦罗埃文字，包括草书体与碑铭体，用于王室铭文、墓葬、宗教和行政记录；同时受晚期埃及书写传统影响。
writingSystem.en: The Meroitic script, in cursive and monumental forms, was used for royal inscriptions, funerary texts, religious use, and administrative records; it also reflected the influence of late Egyptian writing traditions.

culturalAchievements.zh: 代表性成就包括麦罗埃金字塔群、独特的王家陵墓建筑、阿佩德马克神庙艺术、铁冶炼传统、陶器制作与融合埃及、努比亚和非洲内陆元素的视觉文化。
culturalAchievements.en: Notable achievements included the pyramid fields of Meroe, distinctive royal tomb architecture, temple art devoted to Apedemak, iron-smelting traditions, fine pottery, and a visual culture blending Egyptian, Nubian, and inner African elements.

languageFamily.zh: 麦罗埃语可能属于东北非语言圈中的独立分支，学界多认为与东苏丹诸语言存在联系，但尚未完全释读。
languageFamily.en: The Meroitic language likely belonged to a distinct branch within the Northeast African linguistic sphere, often thought to be related to Eastern Sudanic languages, though it remains only partially deciphered.

gdpDescription.zh: 220年左右的麦罗埃库施经济明显衰退，但仍具区域性实力。其基础是尼罗河谷农业、牧业、铁冶炼、黄金与象牙贸易，以及作为东北非内陆转运地的地位。与罗马埃及、红海和非洲内陆的联系尚存，但已不如早期繁荣。
gdpDescription.en: Around 220 CE, the economy of Meroitic Kush was clearly in decline but still retained regional significance. Its base rested on Nile agriculture, pastoralism, iron production, gold and ivory trade, and its role as an inland transit zone in Northeast Africa. Links with Roman Egypt, the Red Sea, and inner Africa continued, though at a weaker level than in earlier centuries.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、牧业、铁冶炼、黄金采集、陶器、石工、神庙与王室工坊生产、尼罗河与沙漠商路贸易
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, iron smelting, gold extraction, pottery, stoneworking, temple and royal workshop production, and Nile and desert-route trade

tradeGoods.zh: 黄金、铁器、象牙、乌木、皮革、牲畜、谷物、陶器、奴隶、香料转运品，以及来自埃及和红海世界的奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Gold, iron goods, ivory, ebony, leather, livestock, grain, pottery, slaves, re-exported aromatics, and luxury goods arriving from Egypt and the Red Sea world

unitName.zh: 外来钱币 / 实物单位

householdWealth.zh: 大多数家庭财富主要体现在土地使用权、谷物储藏、牲畜、工具和亲属网络，而非大量铸币积累。贵族与神庙掌握较多剩余产品、金属器和奢侈消费品。
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth was held in land use rights, stored grain, livestock, tools, and kinship networks rather than large coin holdings. Nobles and temples controlled larger surpluses, metal goods, and luxury consumption.

unit.zh: 罗马第纳里银币等值/年

economicSystem.zh: 以农业和再分配为基础的王国经济，结合神庙地产、地方贡赋、劳役征发与有限市场交换；对外贸易重要但不足以扭转整体衰退。
economicSystem.en: A kingdom economy based on agriculture and redistribution, combining temple estates, provincial tribute, labor obligations, and limited market exchange; foreign trade remained important but was insufficient to reverse the broader decline.

source.en: Livestock, pastoral products, and provincial tribute

source.zh: 黄金、铁器与王室工坊收入
source.en: Gold, iron, and royal workshop income

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由金属储备、谷仓存粮、牲畜、手工业库存和可动员贡赋构成。由于边防压力与贸易衰退，财政缓冲能力有限，王室更依赖实物再分配而非稳定货币财政。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of metal reserves, granary stocks, livestock, workshop inventories, and mobilizable tribute. Because of frontier pressures and trade decline, fiscal cushioning was limited, and the crown relied more on in-kind redistribution than on a stable monetary system.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持宫廷、神庙和边防的基本运转，通过加重实物征收、加强商路控制和动员地方贡赋来应对衰退，但这也可能加剧地方离心。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on maintaining the basic functioning of court, temples, and frontier defense by increasing in-kind collections, tightening control of trade routes, and mobilizing provincial tribute, though this likely intensified local fragmentation.

unit.zh: 外来钱币 / 实物单位

description.zh: 以长矛、盾牌、弓箭和部分铁制武器装备的核心地面部队，负责驻守都城、河谷据点与主要神庙中心。
description.en: Core ground troops equipped with spears, shields, bows, and some iron weapons, responsible for guarding the capital, Nile strongpoints, and major temple centers.

description.zh: 继承努比亚射手传统，用于边境巡逻、机动袭扰与沙漠地带作战，是库施军队最具特色的兵种之一。
description.en: Heirs to the Nubian archer tradition, used for frontier patrols, mobile raiding, and desert warfare; they were among the most distinctive arms of Kushite forces.

description.zh: 规模不大，用于通信、侦察、护送商路与快速响应东部沙漠和南部边境威胁。
description.en: Small in number, they handled communication, reconnaissance, caravan protection, and rapid response to threats in the eastern desert and southern frontiers.

description.zh: 来自地方贵族和附属群体的临时兵力，在危机时期用于守备、运输和辅助作战，训练与忠诚度不一。
description.en: Temporary forces drawn from provincial nobles and subordinate groups, used in crises for garrison duty, transport, and auxiliary fighting; training and loyalty varied.

commanderInChief.zh: 麦罗埃君主亲掌最高军权，由王室将领与地方统帅辅佐
commanderInChief.en: The monarch of Meroe held supreme military authority, assisted by royal commanders and provincial war leaders

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁制兵器、复合或强化弓、长矛、盾牌、简易攻城与城防工事为主。库施虽有成熟铁冶炼传统，但在组织、后勤和重装能力上已落后于更强邻国。
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron weapons, composite or strengthened bows, spears, shields, and simple siege and fortification methods. Although Kush had a mature iron-working tradition, it lagged behind stronger neighbors in organization, logistics, and heavy equipment.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自东南方日益强盛的阿克苏姆、边疆部族脱离控制、商路不稳以及内部政治权威削弱。北方虽与罗马埃及直接大战较少，但其贸易与边境政策仍深刻影响库施生存。
threats.en: Major threats came from the rising kingdom of Aksum in the southeast, frontier peoples slipping from control, unstable trade routes, and weakening internal political authority. Although direct war with Roman Egypt was less pressing than before, Roman trade and border policy still strongly affected Kushite survival.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

populationDescription.zh: 王国人口约五十万，主要集中在尼罗河中游与支流绿洲带。人口总体较为分散，少数城市与宗教中心外，大多数居民生活于河谷村落、牧场和边缘地带聚落。
populationDescription.en: The kingdom’s population was about 500,000, concentrated mainly in the middle Nile and nearby oasis belts. Population was generally dispersed; outside a few cities and religious centers, most people lived in riverine villages, pastoral lands, and peripheral settlements.

name.zh: 穆萨瓦拉特·埃斯苏夫拉

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层大致包括王室与贵族、祭司、军政官员、地方首领、工匠和商人、农民与牧民，以及依附民和奴婢。阶层流动有限，但地方势力在衰退期相对上升。
socialClasses.en: Social strata broadly included the royal family and nobility, priests, military and administrative officials, local chiefs, artisans and merchants, farmers and herders, and dependent or enslaved people. Social mobility was limited, but local powerholders gained influence during the period of decline.

allies.zh: 无稳定强力盟友；可能与部分尼罗河上游、沙漠商路部族和地方首领保持松散朝贡或互利关系。
allies.en: No stable major allies; the kingdom may have maintained loose tributary or mutually beneficial relations with some upper Nile groups, desert-route tribes, and local chiefs.

enemies.zh: 阿克苏姆是最具战略威胁的竞争者；部分边疆部族和脱离控制的地方势力亦构成持续压力。
enemies.en: Aksum was the most significant strategic rival; some frontier tribes and increasingly autonomous local forces also posed continuing pressure.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以守成为主，试图维持与罗马埃及的贸易联系、保住尼罗河与沙漠商路、安抚边疆群体并遏制阿克苏姆扩张。相较早期，麦罗埃已更偏向防御和区域平衡，而非主动扩张。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was primarily defensive, aiming to preserve trade with Roman Egypt, retain control of Nile and desert routes, placate frontier groups, and contain Aksumite expansion. Compared with earlier eras, Meroe had shifted from active expansion to defense and regional balancing.

era.zh: 古典晚期前夜的东北非铁器王国时代
era.en: Northeast African Iron Age kingdom on the eve of Late Antiquity

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的铁冶炼与铁器生产、尼罗河农业管理、石建筑和陵墓工程、王室与神庙工坊体系、适应河谷与草原环境的混合农业牧业技术。
keyInnovations.en: Mature iron smelting and ironworking, Nile-based agricultural management, stone architecture and funerary engineering, royal and temple workshop systems, and mixed agro-pastoral techniques adapted to river valley and savanna environments.

strengths.zh: 麦罗埃库施仍拥有深厚王权传统、独特文化认同、铁冶炼能力、尼罗河农业基础和连接埃及、红海与非洲内陆的区位优势。
strengths.en: Meroitic Kush still possessed a deep tradition of kingship, a distinct cultural identity, iron-working capacity, a Nile agricultural base, and a strategic position linking Egypt, the Red Sea, and inner Africa.

weaknesses.zh: 王权衰弱、地方离心增强、贸易网络缩小、财政紧张、军事组织不及强邻，且文字和行政传统虽存在，却未能转化为更强的整合能力。
weaknesses.en: Royal authority was weakening, provincial fragmentation was growing, trade networks were shrinking, finances were strained, and military organization lagged behind stronger neighbors; existing literate and administrative traditions did not translate into stronger integration.

outlook.zh: 到220年时，麦罗埃库施虽未灭亡，但已处于明显衰落阶段。若无法恢复商路、重整边防与巩固中央权威，其在未来数十年内很可能被阿克苏姆压力、地方解体和经济转型所进一步削弱。
outlook.en: By 220 CE, Meroitic Kush had not yet fallen, but it was clearly in decline. Unless it could restore trade routes, reorganize frontier defense, and reinforce central authority, it was likely to be further weakened in the coming decades by Aksumite pressure, provincial disintegration, and economic transformation.

description.zh: 麦罗埃时期的库施仍统治努比亚，但受阿克苏姆与内陆变化冲击而衰弱。
description.en: Meroitic Kush still ruled Nubia but was weakening under pressure from Aksum and internal change.

name.zh: 萨巴与哈德拉毛诸王国

governmentForm.zh: 分立的君主制城邦与部族王国体系，萨巴、哈德拉毛等旧王国仍保有传统王权，但受希木叶尔扩张压迫
governmentForm.en: A fragmented system of monarchic city-states and tribal kingdoms, in which older polities such as Saba and Hadramawt retained traditional kingship but were increasingly pressured by Himyarite expansion

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、贵族氏族、祭司与神庙集团、灌溉农业民众、商旅阶层、工匠以及依附部族构成，奴隶与战俘亦存在
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of royal households, noble lineages, priestly and temple interests, irrigated agricultural populations, merchant caravanners, artisans, and dependent tribes, with slaves and war captives also present

rulingClass.zh: 王室家族、部族贵族、大型灌溉与神庙地产控制者
rulingClass.en: Royal families, tribal aristocracies, and controllers of major irrigation and temple estates

succession.zh: 通常为王族世袭，但常受部族支持、贵族协商与军事现实影响，继承并不总是稳定
succession.en: Usually dynastic and hereditary within royal houses, but often shaped by tribal backing, aristocratic negotiation, and military realities, making succession not always stable

structure.zh: 各王国中央政权规模有限，以王廷、王室书记机构、税赋与灌溉管理官员及地方部族首领共同治理；在绿洲、河谷与商道节点实行较强控制，在边缘高地和荒漠地带依赖盟附关系
structure.en: The central governments of the kingdoms were limited in scale, governed through royal courts, scribal administrations, tax and irrigation officials, and local tribal chiefs; control was stronger in oases, valleys, and trade nodes, while peripheral highlands and desert zones were managed through alliance and dependency

function.zh: 起草铭文、诏令、盟约与土地记录，维护王室档案并协调使节事务
function.en: Drafted inscriptions, decrees, treaties, and land records, maintained royal archives, and coordinated diplomatic missions

function.zh: 管理堤坝、渠道、分水制度与农田修复，尤其负责马里卜坝及相关灌溉体系的维护
function.en: Managed dams, canals, water allocation systems, and field repairs, especially the maintenance of the Marib Dam and related irrigation networks

function.zh: 征收地租、商税、市场税、道路与口岸关税，并监督附属部族贡纳
function.en: Collected land dues, trade taxes, market fees, road and port customs, and supervised tribute from dependent tribes

function.zh: 协调王权与地方神庙关系，管理祭祀供献、圣地产权与宗教合法性事务
function.en: Coordinated relations between kingship and local temples, managing offerings, sacred property, and matters of religious legitimacy

function.zh: 组织王室卫队、征召部族战士、护卫商队与边境据点
function.en: Organized royal guards, mobilized tribal warriors, and protected caravans and frontier strongpoints

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以城邑长官、绿洲管事、部族首领和神庙代理人并行，中央对核心灌溉区管控较强，对外缘地区多采取间接统治
localAdmin.en: Local administration combined town officials, oasis stewards, tribal leaders, and temple agents; central authority was stronger in core irrigated zones and more indirect in outer regions

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要基于习惯法、王令、部族仲裁与神庙誓约，产权、水权、婚姻、债务和商旅纠纷常由地方长老与王廷共同裁断
legalSystem.en: Law rested mainly on customary norms, royal decrees, tribal arbitration, and temple oaths; property, water rights, marriage, debt, and caravan disputes were often judged jointly by local elders and the royal court

taxationSystem.zh: 税制包括农产征收、灌溉地租、商队通行税、香料与乳香贸易关税、市场税以及附属部族贡赋，实物税与金属货币并用
taxationSystem.en: The taxation system included agricultural dues, irrigated land rents, caravan tolls, customs on aromatics and frankincense trade, market fees, and tribute from dependent tribes, paid in both kind and metal currency

religion.zh: 以南阿拉伯多神信仰为主，崇拜阿尔马克、阿萨塔尔、辛等地方神祇；同时到3世纪前后，犹太教与一神观念在部分地区开始传播
religion.en: Religion was predominantly South Arabian polytheism, with local deities such as Almaqah, Athtar, and Sin worshipped; by the 3rd century, Judaism and monotheistic tendencies were also beginning to spread in some areas

philosophy.zh: 思想传统以宗教伦理、王权正当性、祖先传统与部族荣誉观为核心，并不形成如希腊或中国式系统哲学
philosophy.en: The intellectual tradition centered on religious ethics, royal legitimacy, ancestral custom, and tribal honor, rather than developing a systematic philosophy in the Greek or Chinese sense

writingSystem.zh: 使用古南阿拉伯文字书写萨巴语、哈德拉毛语等铭文语言，常见于石刻、建筑题记与奉献铭文
writingSystem.en: Ancient South Arabian script was used to write inscriptional languages such as Sabaic and Hadramitic, commonly appearing on stone inscriptions, architectural texts, and dedicatory records

culturalAchievements.zh: 成就包括大型灌溉工程、香料贸易网络、石刻铭文传统、神庙建筑以及对阿拉伯南部沙漠与海岸贸易路线的长期经营
culturalAchievements.en: Achievements included major irrigation works, aromatic trade networks, a strong epigraphic tradition, temple architecture, and long-term management of desert and coastal trade routes in southern Arabia

languageFamily.zh: 闪米特语系南闪米特支
languageFamily.en: Semitic language family, South Semitic branch

gdpDescription.zh: 经济基础仍然稳固，依托灌溉农业、乳香与没药生产、内陆商队贸易及阿拉伯海海运，但由于传统陆上香料路线地位下降和希木叶尔崛起，整体较早期黄金时代明显衰退
gdpDescription.en: The economic base remained substantial, relying on irrigated agriculture, frankincense and myrrh production, inland caravan trade, and Arabian Sea shipping, but it had clearly declined from its earlier golden age because of the weakening overland incense routes and the rise of Himyar

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、乳香与没药采集、葡萄与椰枣种植、畜牧、石工建筑、商队运输、港口转运
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, frankincense and myrrh harvesting, grape and date cultivation, pastoralism, stone construction, caravan transport, and port transshipment

tradeGoods.zh: 乳香、没药、香膏、葡萄酒、椰枣、皮革、盐、纺织品、马匹、象牙转口品、印度洋输入的香料与奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Frankincense, myrrh, perfumes, wine, dates, leather, salt, textiles, horses, transshipped ivory, and Indian Ocean imports of spices and luxury goods

name.zh: 地方银币与外来钱币并用
name.en: Local silver issues and foreign coinage

householdWealth.zh: 财富分布极不均衡，王室、贵族与大香料商控制大部分剩余产品和贸易收益，多数农户依赖灌溉、部族保护与季节性市场交换维生
householdWealth.en: Wealth was highly unequal, with royal houses, nobles, and major incense merchants controlling much of the surplus and trade income, while most farming households depended on irrigation access, tribal protection, and seasonal market exchange

economicSystem.zh: 以王室与贵族控制的灌溉农业和香料贸易为核心的混合经济，兼具部族互惠、神庙地产、市场交换和跨海商业
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy centered on royal and aristocratic control of irrigated agriculture and aromatic trade, combined with tribal reciprocity, temple estates, market exchange, and overseas commerce

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库因贸易波动、地方分裂和军事压力而较脆弱，仍保有一定贵金属储备，但不足以长期支持大规模统一战争
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was strained by trade fluctuations, political fragmentation, and military pressure; it still held meaningful precious-metal reserves, but not enough to sustain prolonged large-scale unification wars

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持传统灌溉工程、保证商路收益与安抚部族盟友，在衰退背景下常通过提高关税、强化贡赋和动用王室储备弥补赤字
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized maintenance of traditional irrigation works, protection of commercial revenues, and appeasement of tribal allies; in decline, deficits were often met through higher customs, stronger tribute extraction, and drawing on royal reserves

description.zh: 由王廷直属守军、城镇步兵和矛盾兵组成，负责守卫都城、神庙和灌溉设施
description.en: Composed of royal guards, town infantry, and spearmen responsible for defending capitals, temples, and irrigation installations

description.zh: 由盟附部族在战时提供，机动性较强，但纪律与持续服役能力有限
description.en: Provided by allied and dependent tribes in wartime; relatively mobile, but limited in discipline and sustained service

description.zh: 用于沙漠巡逻、商路护卫、侦察与快速袭扰，是南阿拉伯作战的重要机动力量
description.en: Used for desert patrols, caravan protection, reconnaissance, and fast raiding, they were an important mobile arm of South Arabian warfare

description.zh: 驻守沿海据点与港埠，防范海盗并保护近海航运
description.en: Stationed at coastal strongpoints and ports to deter piracy and protect nearshore shipping

commanderInChief.zh: 各国国王及其任命的部族将领
commanderInChief.en: The kings of the respective kingdoms and their appointed tribal commanders

technology.zh: 武器以长矛、剑、匕首、弓箭、皮甲与少量金属甲为主，擅长在山地、沙漠与绿洲防御环境作战，缺乏当时大型帝国式攻城体系
technology.en: Weapons were mainly spears, swords, daggers, bows, leather armor, and limited metal armor; forces were well adapted to mountain, desert, and oasis defense but lacked the advanced siege systems of larger contemporary empires

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自希木叶尔的统一扩张、部族叛离、商路控制权争夺，以及红海贸易格局变化带来的财政压力
threats.en: Main threats came from Himyarite unification and expansion, tribal defections, struggles over trade-route control, and fiscal pressures caused by shifts in Red Sea commerce

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 复合弓或长弓，远射主力
description.en: Composite or longbow, primary ranged weapon

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于也门高地边缘、灌溉河谷、绿洲与沿海贸易点，其中马里卜和沙卜瓦周边仍是旧王国的核心聚居区
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly along the edges of the Yemeni highlands, in irrigated valleys, oases, and coastal trade points, with the regions around Marib and Shabwa remaining core settlement zones of the older kingdoms

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括王族与贵族、祭司与神庙管理者、富裕商人、自由农民、部族牧民、工匠、佃耕者及奴隶
socialClasses.en: Social classes included royal and noble families, priests and temple managers, wealthy merchants, free farmers, tribal pastoralists, artisans, tenant cultivators, and slaves

allies.zh: 无稳定统一盟友，各王国多依赖临时部族联盟，并在必要时与阿克苏姆、罗马埃及或印度洋商人维持务实商业关系
allies.en: There were no stable unified allies; the kingdoms relied mainly on temporary tribal coalitions and maintained pragmatic commercial relations when useful with Aksum, Roman Egypt, and Indian Ocean merchants

enemies.zh: 主要对手是希木叶尔；不同南阿拉伯旧王国之间也存在竞争、边界冲突与商路争夺
enemies.en: The chief rival was Himyar; there was also competition, border conflict, and trade-route rivalry among the older South Arabian kingdoms themselves

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以维持独立、保住香料产地与贸易节点、争取部族效忠并平衡红海和印度洋商业势力为主，但整体处于守势
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving independence, retaining aromatic-producing zones and trade nodes, securing tribal loyalty, and balancing Red Sea and Indian Ocean commercial powers, but overall it was defensive

era.zh: 古典时代晚期南阿拉伯区域文明
era.en: Late Classical South Arabian regional civilization

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括大型堤坝与渠道灌溉、旱地与绿洲农业调水、沙漠商队组织、石造纪念建筑，以及结合海岸与内陆的香料物流体系
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies included large-scale dam and canal irrigation, water management for dryland and oasis agriculture, organized desert caravans, stone monumental construction, and an incense logistics system linking coast and interior

strengths.zh: 优势在于悠久的灌溉文明、成熟的香料贸易传统、地处红海与印度洋之间的中介位置，以及对沙漠与绿洲环境的长期适应
strengths.en: Strengths included an old irrigation civilization, a mature incense-trade tradition, an intermediary position between the Red Sea and Indian Ocean, and long adaptation to desert and oasis environments

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于政治分裂、王权衰落、对商路收入依赖过深、灌溉设施维护成本高，并且难以抵御希木叶尔式更有整合力的竞争者
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses lay in political fragmentation, declining kingship, excessive dependence on trade-route income, the high cost of maintaining irrigation works, and difficulty resisting more integrative rivals such as Himyar

outlook.zh: 220年前后前景偏弱，若不能形成更强政治整合，萨巴与哈德拉毛等旧王国将继续被希木叶尔蚕食，最终失去主导地位
outlook.en: Around 220 CE the outlook was unfavorable; unless stronger political integration emerged, older kingdoms such as Saba and Hadramawt would continue to be eroded by Himyar and eventually lose their dominant position

description.zh: 萨巴与哈德拉毛等旧南阿拉伯王国在希木叶尔扩张下日益衰落。
description.en: Older South Arabian kingdoms such as Saba and Hadramawt were increasingly overshadowed by Himyarite expansion.

governmentForm.zh: 罗马帝国直属行省殖民行政体制，由皇帝通过埃及总督直接统治
governmentForm.en: Imperial provincial colonial administration governed directly by the Roman emperor through the prefect of Egypt

socialStructure.zh: 社会结构高度分层，由罗马官员与军人、希腊化城市精英、埃及祭司与地主、城镇工商业者、乡村农民以及奴隶与依附劳工组成；族群、法律身份与纳税义务彼此交织。
socialStructure.en: Society was highly stratified, consisting of Roman officials and soldiers, Hellenized urban elites, Egyptian priests and landholders, urban merchants and artisans, rural peasants, and slaves and dependent laborers; ethnicity, legal status, and tax obligations were closely intertwined.

rulingClass.zh: 罗马皇帝任命的埃及总督、驻军高级军官、亚历山大里亚与各大都市的希腊语精英，以及与国家财政和粮食征收合作的大地主阶层
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the emperor-appointed prefect of Egypt, senior military officers, Greek-speaking elites of Alexandria and the major cities, and large landholders cooperating with state fiscal and grain collection systems

succession.zh: 最高权力依罗马皇位继承而定，埃及地方行政长官由皇帝任命而非地方世袭
succession.en: Ultimate succession followed the Roman imperial succession, while Egypt’s provincial governor was appointed by the emperor rather than inherited locally

structure.zh: 埃及作为皇帝私人控制色彩极强的特殊行省，由骑士阶层出身的埃及总督统辖，下设财政、司法、档案、军务与粮运体系；地方以诺姆区、村社与城市议会为行政基础。
structure.en: Egypt functioned as a special imperial province under unusually close personal control of the emperor, administered by an equestrian prefect with subordinate fiscal, judicial, archival, military, and grain-transport offices; locally it rested on nomes, village communities, and urban councils.

function.zh: 统筹全省行政、司法、治安、税务监督与向皇帝报告
function.en: Coordinated provincial administration, justice, security, tax supervision, and reporting to the emperor

function.zh: 负责土地税、人头税、关税、专卖收入与账簿核验
function.en: Handled land tax, poll tax, customs dues, monopoly revenues, and audit of accounts

function.zh: 征集、储存并调运尼罗河流域粮食至亚历山大里亚与帝国其他地区
function.en: Collected, stored, and transported Nile grain to Alexandria and other parts of the empire

function.zh: 处理诉讼、文书登记、人口与土地档案以及法令发布
function.en: Managed litigation, document registration, population and land records, and promulgation of decrees

function.zh: 监督诺姆区官员、村长、治安队与公共劳役组织
function.en: Supervised nome officials, village headmen, police units, and corvée labor organization

function.zh: 管理亚历山大里亚、红海港口贸易、仓储与关税征收
function.en: Managed trade, warehousing, and customs collection at Alexandria and the Red Sea ports

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以传统诺姆区为核心，由战略官、书记官、税吏和村社长老执行；城市如亚历山大里亚拥有更复杂的希腊化市政机构，但总体仍受总督严格监督。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on the traditional nomes, carried out by strategoi, scribes, tax collectors, and village elders; cities such as Alexandria had more complex Hellenistic municipal institutions, but overall remained under close prefectural oversight.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系结合罗马法、希腊化城市法与部分埃及习惯实践；220年时卡拉卡拉的《安东尼努斯敕令》已大幅扩大帝国公民权，但税务、身份与地方法律差异依旧存在。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Roman law, Hellenistic civic law, and some Egyptian customary practice; by 220 the Constitutio Antoniniana of Caracalla had greatly expanded imperial citizenship, though differences in taxation, status, and local legal practice persisted.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以土地税、人头税、灌溉相关负担、商业关税、港口税、手工业杂税与国家专卖为主，并辅以实物征收和强制运输劳役。
taxationSystem.en: The tax system rested on land tax, poll tax, irrigation-related obligations, commercial customs, port dues, miscellaneous craft levies, and state monopolies, supplemented by payment in kind and compulsory transport labor.

religion.zh: 宗教生活极为多元，传统埃及宗教、希腊—埃及混合崇拜、罗马皇帝崇拜、犹太教与日益壮大的基督教并存；塞拉比斯、伊西斯和地方神祇仍具有广泛影响。
religion.en: Religious life was highly diverse, with traditional Egyptian religion, Greco-Egyptian syncretic cults, the Roman imperial cult, Judaism, and a growing Christianity coexisting; Serapis, Isis, and local deities remained widely influential.

philosophy.zh: 亚历山大里亚继续是希腊哲学、修辞学、医学与宗教思想交流中心，中期柏拉图主义、斯多亚思想以及早期基督教神学辩论并行发展。
philosophy.en: Alexandria remained a center of exchange for Greek philosophy, rhetoric, medicine, and religious thought, where Middle Platonism, Stoic ideas, and early Christian theological debate developed side by side.

writingSystem.zh: 行政与商业主要使用希腊文书写，拉丁文用于部分军政和帝国正式场合，埃及语则以通俗体和宗教性文字传统延续于地方社会与神庙环境。
writingSystem.en: Greek was the principal written language of administration and commerce, Latin appeared in some military and imperial formal contexts, and Egyptian continued in demotic and religious scripts within local society and temple environments.

culturalAchievements.zh: 亚历山大里亚在医学、天文学、地理学传统、纸草文书文化、玻璃制造与宗教思想汇聚方面保持重要地位，尽管其古典学术机构已不复托勒密时代巅峰。
culturalAchievements.en: Alexandria retained major importance in medicine, astronomy, geographical traditions, papyrus documentary culture, glassmaking, and the convergence of religious thought, though its classical scholarly institutions were no longer at their Ptolemaic height.

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言属于印欧语系的希腊语与拉丁语，以及亚非语系的埃及语和闪米特语族中的犹太社群语言
languageFamily.en: The main languages belonged to the Indo-European family—Greek and Latin—and the Afroasiatic family, including Egyptian and Semitic languages used by Jewish communities

gdpDescription.zh: 罗马埃及是帝国最富庶的农业与商业行省之一，经济核心为尼罗河谷高产农业、亚历山大里亚港口商业、红海—印度洋转口贸易以及高度发达的税收和仓储体系。
gdpDescription.en: Roman Egypt was one of the empire’s wealthiest agricultural and commercial provinces, with an economy centered on the high-yield agriculture of the Nile Valley, the port commerce of Alexandria, Red Sea–Indian Ocean transit trade, and a highly developed tax and storage system.

mainIndustries.zh: 粮食种植、亚麻与纺织、纸草加工、玻璃制造、航运、仓储、酿酒、油料加工和港口贸易服务
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, flax and textiles, papyrus processing, glassmaking, shipping, warehousing, wine production, oil processing, and port trade services

tradeGoods.zh: 小麦、大麦、纸草、亚麻布、玻璃器、葡萄酒、橄榄油、香料、象牙、宝石、阿拉伯与印度进口奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Wheat, barley, papyrus, linen, glassware, wine, olive oil, spices, ivory, gemstones, and luxury imports from Arabia and India

unitName.zh: 德拉克马与塞斯特斯并用
unitName.en: drachmae and sestertii in parallel use

householdWealth.zh: 财富分配极不均衡，亚历山大里亚商人、承包税吏和大地主积累可观财富，而大多数乡村家庭仅能维持基本生计，并常受税负、债务和歉收风险影响。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal: Alexandrian merchants, tax contractors, and major landholders accumulated considerable fortunes, while most rural households lived near subsistence and were vulnerable to taxation, debt, and poor harvests.

economicSystem.zh: 经济体系为罗马国家财政主导下的市场—税收混合模式，农业剩余通过官僚征收、仓储与运输网络转化为帝国粮食供给和财政收入，同时城市与远洋贸易保持活跃。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a market-tax hybrid under Roman state fiscal dominance, in which agricultural surplus was converted through bureaucratic collection, storage, and transport into imperial grain supply and revenue, while urban and long-distance trade remained vibrant.

source.zh: 土地税与农业实物折算
source.en: Land tax and monetized agricultural dues

category.zh: 神庙、城市修缮与应急储备
category.en: Temples, urban repairs, and contingency reserves

treasuryDescription.zh: 省库相对充实，得益于稳定农业产出、严格税务文书与高价值港口贸易；但其大量资源需上缴帝国财政或转化为对罗马与其他地区的粮食供给。
treasuryDescription.en: The provincial treasury was relatively well supplied thanks to stable agricultural output, strict fiscal documentation, and high-value port trade; however, much of its resources had to be remitted to the imperial fisc or converted into grain shipments for Rome and other regions.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调确保粮食输送、维持现金与实物并行征收、强化户籍与土地核查，并在社会稳定前提下尽量压榨省份剩余。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized securing grain deliveries, maintaining both cash and in-kind taxation, strengthening census and land verification, and extracting provincial surplus as fully as possible while preserving social stability.

unit.zh: 德拉克马与塞斯特斯并用
unit.en: drachmae and sestertii in parallel use

description.zh: 构成埃及防务核心，驻守尼罗河谷要地、亚历山大里亚周边和交通节点，用于镇压骚乱与守备边境。
description.en: Formed the core of Egypt’s defense, stationed at key Nile sites, around Alexandria, and at transport nodes for riot suppression and frontier garrison duty.

description.zh: 用于沙漠巡逻、迅速支援、通讯与对边缘地带部族活动的监控。
description.en: Used for desert patrols, rapid reinforcement, communications, and monitoring tribal activity on the margins.

name.zh: 尼罗河与港口舰船部队

description.zh: 负责河运护卫、港口警备、海岸巡逻与粮船调度安全。
description.en: Handled river convoy protection, port security, coastal patrols, and the safe movement of grain fleets.

description.zh: 包括城镇守备、村庄警吏与半军事化治安力量，负责征税护送、治安维护与协助行政控制。
description.en: Included town garrisons, village police, and semi-militarized security forces responsible for escorting tax collection, maintaining order, and assisting administrative control.

name.zh: 后备民役与运输征发人员
name.en: Reserve labor levies and transport mobilization personnel

description.zh: 战时或危机时为军队和粮运系统提供船工、车队、工事劳力与补充人员。
description.en: In wartime or crisis they provided boatmen, wagon teams, engineering labor, and replacement manpower for the army and grain transport system.

commanderInChief.zh: 埃及总督兼行省最高军事长官
commanderInChief.en: Prefect of Egypt as provincial commander-in-chief

technology.zh: 军备以罗马帝国标准装备为主，包括锁子甲、鳞甲、长盾、投枪、长矛、剑、复合弓、攻城器械及成熟的筑城与道路维护技术；在沙漠和河运环境中拥有丰富后勤经验。
technology.en: Military equipment followed standard Roman imperial patterns, including mail, scale armor, large shields, javelins, spears, swords, composite bows, siege equipment, and mature fortification and road-maintenance techniques; forces also possessed extensive logistical experience in desert and riverine environments.

threats.zh: 主要威胁并非大规模外敌入侵，而是南方努比亚方向的不稳定、东部沙漠与红海交通线安全、地方骚乱、城市暴动，以及帝国内战时的兵力调动压力。
threats.en: The main threats were not large-scale foreign invasion but instability toward Nubia in the south, security of the Eastern Desert and Red Sea routes, local unrest, urban riots, and the strain of troop redeployment during Roman civil conflicts.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

description.zh: 重骑兵标志性武器，冲击力极强
description.en: Signature heavy cavalry weapon with tremendous charge impact

description.zh: 可连发十矢的机械弩，守城利器
description.en: Mechanical crossbow firing ten bolts in succession, excellent for defense

populationDescription.zh: 人口稠密集中于尼罗河谷与三角洲，乡村农业人口占绝大多数；亚历山大里亚为全地中海最重要的大都会之一，同时拥有显著的希腊人、埃及人、犹太人及其他东方商旅社群。
populationDescription.en: The population was densely concentrated in the Nile Valley and Delta, with rural agricultural people forming the vast majority; Alexandria was one of the most important metropolises of the Mediterranean and contained notable Greek, Egyptian, Jewish, and other eastern merchant communities.

description.zh: 古代学术中心与地中海大港
description.en: Ancient center of learning and great Mediterranean port

description.zh: 古埃及首都，法老权力中心
description.en: Ancient Egyptian capital, center of pharaonic power

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括罗马官员与军官、希腊化城市上层、市民与商人、神庙与祭司群体、乡村地主、佃农、小农、工匠、码头劳工、奴隶与依附劳工；法律地位和税种常与职业、居住地和文化身份相连。
socialClasses.en: Social classes included Roman officials and officers, Hellenized urban upper strata, citizens and merchants, temple and priestly groups, rural landlords, tenant farmers, smallholders, artisans, dockworkers, slaves, and dependent laborers; legal status and tax category often correlated with occupation, residence, and cultural identity.

allies.zh: 作为罗马帝国行省，罗马埃及并无独立盟友，其安全依托帝国整体力量以及与东部附庸和友邦的体系性联系。
allies.en: As a province of the Roman Empire, Roman Egypt had no independent allies; its security depended on the wider power of the empire and its system of eastern client and friendly states.

enemies.zh: 直接敌对对象有限，潜在对手主要是帝国东部竞争势力、红海与沙漠地带的袭扰群体，以及南部尼罗河地区的不稳定政权或部族。
enemies.en: Direct enemies were limited; potential adversaries were chiefly eastern rivals of the empire, raiding groups in the Red Sea and desert zones, and unstable polities or tribes in the southern Nile region.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策服从罗马帝国东地中海战略，重点是确保亚历山大里亚粮运、维护红海—印度洋贸易通道、监控南部边疆并避免地方力量坐大。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy followed the Roman Empire’s eastern Mediterranean strategy, prioritizing the grain lifeline of Alexandria, maintenance of Red Sea–Indian Ocean trade routes, monitoring of the southern frontier, and prevention of excessive local power concentration.

era.zh: 罗马帝国盛期晚段与中国三国时代同期
era.en: Late high Roman imperial period, contemporaneous with the Three Kingdoms era in China

keyInnovations.zh: 大型灌溉与堤坝维护、成熟谷仓和港口物流、纸草文书行政、玻璃制造、地中海航运技术、红海远程贸易组织以及罗马军事工程体系
keyInnovations.en: Large-scale irrigation and dike maintenance, mature granary and port logistics, papyrus-based documentary administration, glass manufacturing, Mediterranean shipping technology, Red Sea long-distance trade organization, and the Roman military engineering system

strengths.zh: 尼罗河农业极其高产，亚历山大里亚是世界级港口与商业中心，税务与档案制度细密，连接地中海、红海和内陆非洲的贸易网络发达，且受罗马帝国军事体系保护。
strengths.en: Its strengths were the extraordinarily productive Nile agriculture, Alexandria as a world-class port and commercial hub, dense fiscal and documentary institutions, developed trade networks linking the Mediterranean, Red Sea, and inland Africa, and protection by the Roman imperial military system.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于对中央帝国权力高度依赖、税负沉重、族群与宗教矛盾复杂、城市骚乱传统深厚，并且经济高度依赖尼罗河水情、粮运秩序和对外航道安全。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in heavy dependence on central imperial power, a burdensome tax regime, complex ethnic and religious tensions, a deep tradition of urban unrest, and an economy highly dependent on Nile flood conditions, orderly grain transport, and secure overseas routes.

outlook.zh: 220年前后罗马埃及总体稳定且仍极为富庶，但若帝国中央财政恶化、军阀内战加剧或宗教社会冲突升级，其行政效率与地方忠诚都可能在未来数十年逐步受损。
outlook.en: Around 220 CE Roman Egypt was generally stable and still very prosperous, but if imperial finances deteriorated, military civil wars intensified, or religious and social conflict deepened, both administrative efficiency and local loyalty could gradually erode over the coming decades.

description.zh: 埃及为罗马最重要的谷仓与东地中海商业中心之一。
description.en: Egypt was one of Rome's most important grain-producing and commercial provinces in the eastern Mediterranean.

governmentForm.zh: 以绿洲贵族和王室为核心的世袭沙漠王国
governmentForm.en: A hereditary desert kingdom centered on the royal house and oasis aristocracy

socialStructure.zh: 社会以王室、地方贵族、战士与车兵、祭司和长老、绿洲自由民农户、工匠商旅，以及被役使的奴隶与依附人口构成。各大绿洲聚落在王权统摄下保持较强地方性。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal family, local nobles, warriors and chariot fighters, priests and elders, free oasis cultivators, artisans and caravan merchants, and enslaved or dependent laborers. Major oasis settlements retained strong local identities under royal authority.

rulingClass.zh: 王室、绿洲贵族家族、部族首领与掌握水源和商道的上层家族
rulingClass.en: The royal house, oasis noble lineages, tribal chiefs, and elite families controlling water sources and trade routes

succession.zh: 王位通常在王室内部世袭，可能以成年男性宗支继承为主，并需获得主要绿洲贵族与部族首领承认。
succession.en: The throne was generally hereditary within the royal house, likely favoring adult male dynasts, and required recognition by leading oasis nobles and tribal chiefs.

structure.zh: 王国政体较为简约，国王通过王廷、近臣、地方绿洲首领与商道监督者进行统治。中央直接控制加拉马及核心费赞绿洲，对边缘地区则依靠盟属和地方贵族间接治理。
structure.en: The kingdom had a relatively simple political structure. The king ruled through the royal court, close retainers, local oasis chiefs, and supervisors of caravan routes. The center directly controlled Garama and the core Fezzan oases, while peripheral zones were governed more indirectly through subordinate communities and local nobles.

function.zh: 管理王宫、使者接待、王命传达、贡品与宫廷仓储
function.en: Managed the palace, reception of envoys, royal communications, tribute, and court storage

function.zh: 监督地下引水道、井渠维护、农田分水与绿洲定居点协调
function.en: Oversaw foggaras, wells and channels, irrigation allocation, and coordination of oasis settlements

function.zh: 监管穿越撒哈拉的商旅、征收过路税、维持驿站与补给点
function.en: Regulated trans-Saharan caravans, collected transit duties, and maintained stations and supply points

function.zh: 组织王家卫队、战车兵、骑乘部队与绿洲守备力量
