function.en: Organized the royal guard, chariotry, mounted forces, and oasis garrisons

function.zh: 征收谷物、枣椰、牲畜、手工业品和附属部落贡纳
function.en: Collected grain, dates, livestock, craft goods, and tribute from subordinate groups

localAdmin.zh: 地方以绿洲聚落为行政核心，由王室任命或承认的首领、长老和贵族家族共同治理。偏远地带更多依赖部族关系、盟约和季节性巡察。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on oasis settlements governed jointly by chiefs, elders, and noble families appointed or recognized by the crown. More distant areas relied heavily on tribal ties, agreements, and seasonal royal inspection.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王命和贵族仲裁为主，强调水源使用、商旅安全、贡赋义务、血亲关系和赔偿。严重案件由国王或其代理人裁断。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on custom, royal decree, and noble arbitration, with emphasis on water rights, caravan security, tribute duties, kinship obligations, and compensation. Serious cases were judged by the king or his delegates.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要来自绿洲农业实物征收、商道关税、附属部族贡赋、牲畜征敛及对过境奢侈品贸易的抽成。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation came mainly from in-kind levies on oasis agriculture, customs duties on caravan traffic, tribute from subordinate tribes, livestock assessments, and shares of long-distance luxury trade.

religion.zh: 宗教以本地利比亚—撒哈拉多神信仰为主，崇拜祖先、地方神灵与自然力量，并吸收部分地中海世界宗教影响。
religion.en: Religion was primarily indigenous Libyan-Saharan polytheism, centered on ancestors, local deities, and natural powers, while also absorbing some influences from the Mediterranean world.

philosophy.zh: 缺乏成体系哲学传统，但政治与社会观念强调王权、血缘、对水源的控制、部族荣誉与商道秩序。
philosophy.en: There was no formal philosophical canon, but political and social ideas emphasized kingship, kinship, control of water, tribal honor, and the order of trade routes.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用利比科—柏柏尔文字及口述传统，在对外贸易和接触中也可能接触拉丁文与希腊文书写。
writingSystem.en: The kingdom primarily used Libyco-Berber script alongside strong oral traditions, and through external trade likely encountered Latin and Greek writing as well.

culturalAchievements.zh: 加拉曼特人的突出成就在于沙漠环境下的绿洲城市建设、地下引水道系统、长距离商旅组织、墓葬与岩画传统，以及对撒哈拉交通网络的整合。
culturalAchievements.en: Garamantian cultural achievements included oasis urbanism in a harsh desert environment, underground irrigation systems, organization of long-distance caravan trade, burial and rock-art traditions, and integration of Saharan transport networks.

languageFamily.zh: 柏柏尔语族，属阿非罗—亚细亚语系
languageFamily.en: Berber branch of the Afroasiatic language family

gdpDescription.zh: 王国经济规模在撒哈拉地区相当突出，但与罗马帝国或尼罗河流域相比仍属中小型。其财富主要建立在绿洲农业、商路控制和转运贸易，而非大面积高产农耕。
gdpDescription.en: The kingdom’s economy was impressive by Saharan standards but remained modest compared with the Roman Empire or the Nile valley. Its wealth rested on oasis agriculture, control of trade routes, and transit exchange rather than extensive high-yield farming.

mainIndustries.zh: 枣椰种植、谷物灌溉农业、畜牧、制革、陶器、盐与石材获取、商队运输和转口贸易
mainIndustries.en: Date cultivation, irrigated grain farming, pastoralism, leatherworking, pottery, extraction of salt and stone, caravan transport, and entrepôt trade

tradeGoods.zh: 枣椰、谷物、盐、牲畜、皮革、奴隶、象牙、乌木、羽毛、半宝石，以及来自地中海的酒、金属器、玻璃与陶器
tradeGoods.en: Dates, grain, salt, livestock, leather, slaves, ivory, ebony, feathers, semi-precious stones, and Mediterranean imports such as wine, metal goods, glass, and pottery

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在枣椰树、牲畜、水权、粮食储备和亲族网络。精英家庭则掌握井渠、奴隶劳力、驼畜与贸易收益。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured chiefly in date palms, livestock, water rights, grain stores, and kin networks. Elite households controlled irrigation works, enslaved labor, pack animals, and caravan income.

unit.zh: 罗马第纳里乌斯估值/年
罗马第纳里乌斯估值/年

economicSystem.zh: 以绿洲农业和商路控制为基础的王权—贵族混合经济，兼具实物征收、部族交换、奴隶劳动和跨区域市场贸易。
economicSystem.en: A royal-aristocratic mixed economy based on oasis agriculture and trade-route control, combining in-kind extraction, tribal exchange, slave labor, and interregional market trade.

unit.zh: 罗马第纳里乌斯估值/年
罗马第纳里乌斯估值/年
罗马第纳里乌斯估值/年

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以金属币、贵重物资、粮食储备、牲畜和可调动物资构成。由于沙漠贸易波动较大，王室倾向保有较高实物储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of coin, valuables, stored grain, livestock, and mobilizable supplies. Because desert trade could fluctuate sharply, the crown favored substantial material reserves.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重稳健与储备导向，优先维持商路安全、灌溉工程和核心绿洲稳定，避免对地方贵族与部族征敛过重。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was cautious and reserve-oriented, prioritizing route security, irrigation works, and stability in the core oases while avoiding excessive extraction from local nobles and tribes.

description.zh: 驻守加拉马并护卫国王与王廷的精锐部队，装备相对优良。
description.en: An elite force stationed at Garama to guard the king and court, comparatively well equipped.

description.zh: 分驻主要绿洲、井渠与仓储点，负责警备、巡逻和防御性作战。
description.en: Stationed in major oases, waterworks, and store sites, responsible for security, patrol, and defensive operations.

description.zh: 依靠马匹及其他沙漠驮乘能力进行快速侦察、追击和商道护送。
description.en: Used horses and other desert transport capacity for rapid scouting, pursuit, and caravan escort.

description.zh: 保留传统战车作战与贵族私人武装成分，在开阔地和象征性展示中仍有作用。
description.en: Retained traditional chariot warfare and noble private retinues, still useful in open terrain and as symbols of status.

technology.zh: 军事技术以轻装步兵、骑乘机动、战车传统、简单攻守工事和沙漠后勤经验为核心。其优势不在重装会战，而在长距离机动、熟悉水源和伏击能力。
technology.en: Military technology centered on light infantry, mounted mobility, traditional chariotry, simple field fortifications, and desert logistics. Its strengths lay not in heavy battlefield confrontation but in long-range movement, knowledge of water sources, and ambush capability.

unit.zh: 罗马第纳里乌斯估值/年

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自罗马北非边境压力、商道竞争、沙漠部族反叛、干旱导致的补给危机，以及对南北贸易节点控制权的争夺。
threats.en: Main threats came from pressure along the Roman North African frontier, competition over trade routes, rebellion among desert tribes, drought-driven supply crises, and struggles for control of north-south trade nodes.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

description.zh: 复合弓或长弓，远射主力
description.en: Composite or longbow, primary ranged weapon

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中在费赞地区的绿洲链和若干附属沙漠定居点，密度极不均衡。加拉马及周边灌溉区构成王国人口核心。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the Fezzan oasis chain and a number of dependent desert settlements, with highly uneven density. Garama and its surrounding irrigated zone formed the kingdom’s demographic core.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、贵族和控制水源商路的家族；中层为自由农户、商人、工匠和部族战士；下层为依附民、奴隶和战俘劳力。地方部族身份与阶层地位相互交织。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of the royal house, nobles, and families controlling water and trade routes; the middle strata included free cultivators, merchants, artisans, and tribal warriors; the lower strata included dependents, slaves, and captive laborers. Tribal identity and social rank were closely intertwined.

allies.zh: 无稳定意义上的远程正式盟友；更常见的是与撒哈拉部族、南方贸易伙伴及部分罗马边境商人维持务实关系。
allies.en: There were no stable long-distance formal allies in the strict sense; more typical were pragmatic relations with Saharan tribes, southern trade partners, and some merchants on the Roman frontier.

enemies.zh: 主要潜在敌手为试图限制其贸易和边境活动的罗马势力，以及与其争夺绿洲和商道的沙漠群体。
enemies.en: Its main potential enemies were Roman forces seeking to limit its trade and frontier activity, as well as desert groups competing for oases and caravan routes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制商路、维持绿洲缓冲带、在冲突与贸易之间灵活周旋为核心。王国通常避免与强大帝国正面长期战争，而通过中介贸易和边境谈判维护利益。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on controlling trade routes, maintaining buffers around the oases, and balancing conflict with commerce. The kingdom generally avoided prolonged direct war with stronger empires, instead preserving its interests through intermediary trade and frontier negotiation.

era.zh: 古典时代晚期沙漠绿洲文明阶段
era.en: Late Classical-era desert oasis civilization

keyInnovations.zh: 地下引水道系统、井渠灌溉、适应沙漠环境的商旅组织、绿洲城防布局，以及跨撒哈拉补给管理。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included underground irrigation channels, well-and-canal watering systems, caravan organization adapted to desert conditions, oasis defensive layouts, and trans-Saharan supply management.

strengths.zh: 王国最大的优势在于掌握撒哈拉关键绿洲和南北贸易通道，具备卓越的沙漠生存、水利组织和转运能力。其政治结构虽不复杂，却适合分散绿洲环境。
strengths.en: The kingdom’s greatest strengths were its control of critical Saharan oases and north-south trade corridors, along with outstanding desert survival, water management, and transit capabilities. Its political structure, though simple, suited a dispersed oasis environment.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于人口有限、农业基础脆弱、对水源和商路高度依赖，且难以与地中海大型国家进行正面军事对抗。若遭遇长期干旱或贸易改道，国力容易受损。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in limited population, a fragile agricultural base, heavy dependence on water and trade routes, and inability to confront large Mediterranean states in direct warfare. Prolonged drought or diversion of trade could quickly undermine its strength.

outlook.zh: 220年时加拉曼特王国总体稳定，仍是北非撒哈拉最重要的本土政权之一。只要核心井渠和商队网络得以维持，其统治可继续延续；但长期前景取决于气候、罗马边境局势和跨撒哈拉贸易流向。
outlook.en: In 220 CE the Garamantian kingdom was broadly stable and remained one of the most important indigenous states of the North African Sahara. So long as its core irrigation works and caravan networks were maintained, its rule could continue; however, its longer-term prospects depended on climate, the Roman frontier situation, and the direction of trans-Saharan trade.

description.zh: 加拉曼特控制撒哈拉绿洲与商道，是北非沙漠文明的代表之一。
description.en: The Garamantes controlled Saharan oases and trade routes, representing a major desert civilization in North Africa.

governmentForm.zh: 以南阿拉伯君主制为核心、通过部族联盟与地方附庸进行统治的集权王国
governmentForm.en: A centralized South Arabian monarchy ruling through tribal alliances and local subordinate polities

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、贵族与大部族首领、祭司与书吏、商人、灌溉农业居民、手工业者、牧民及奴仆构成；高地定居农业与商贸城镇是核心，部族关系仍深刻影响政治与军事组织。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal house, nobles and major tribal chiefs, priests and scribes, merchants, irrigated agricultural communities, artisans, pastoralists, and slaves or dependents; settled highland agriculture and trade towns formed the core, while tribal ties still strongly shaped politics and military organization.

rulingClass.zh: 希木叶尔王族、南阿拉伯贵族家族、受王权整合的大部族首领及宫廷高级官员
rulingClass.en: The Himyarite royal family, South Arabian noble lineages, major tribal leaders integrated into royal authority, and senior court officials

succession.zh: 王位通常由王族内部继承，并需获得贵族、部族精英与宫廷核心集团承认；在强势君主时期偏向父子相承，但仍受王族旁支与联盟部族影响。
succession.en: The throne was normally inherited within the royal house and required recognition by nobles, tribal elites, and the core court establishment; under strong kings it tended toward father-to-son succession, though collateral branches and allied tribes retained influence.

structure.zh: 政权以国王与宫廷为中枢，依托王室亲信、书吏机构、税收官、军政官和地方部族盟长治理高地、谷地与贸易通道；对新并入地区常采取保留地方首领而由王室征税与驻军监督的方式。
structure.en: The state was centered on the king and court, operating through royal confidants, scribal offices, tax collectors, military-administrative officials, and local tribal allies to govern the highlands, valleys, and trade routes; newly incorporated districts were often left under local chiefs but supervised by royal taxation and garrisons.

function.zh: 起草铭文、诏令、外交文书、土地与贡赋记录，维护宫廷档案
function.en: Drafted inscriptions, decrees, diplomatic documents, and records of land and tribute, and maintained court archives

function.zh: 征收地租、贡赋、商税与关税，管理粮仓、香料与王室储备
function.en: Collected land dues, tribute, market taxes, and customs duties, and managed granaries, aromatics, and royal reserves

function.zh: 负责梯田、蓄水、道路、山地通道、城墙及维修工程
function.en: Oversaw terraces, water storage, roads, mountain passes, walls, and maintenance works

function.zh: 组织王室卫队、征调部族兵、管理驻军与边地防务
function.en: Organized the royal guard, mobilized tribal levies, and managed garrisons and frontier defense

function.zh: 监管红海与亚丁湾港口贸易、内陆商队路线、安全与关卡
function.en: Supervised Red Sea and Gulf of Aden port trade, inland caravan routes, security, and checkpoints

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以高地中心区的王室直辖、谷地与边缘地区的间接统治相结合。各地区由王室任命官员、驻军指挥者或承认的部族首领管理，负责治安、税赋、灌溉协作与道路维护。
localAdmin.en: Local administration combined direct royal control in the highland core with indirect rule in valleys and peripheral areas. Districts were managed by royal appointees, garrison commanders, or recognized tribal chiefs responsible for order, taxation, irrigation coordination, and road upkeep.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以南阿拉伯习惯法、王命、部族仲裁和神庙誓约传统为基础。土地、水权、血亲责任、商债和贡赋纠纷多通过地方长老、官员和王室裁决处理。
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested on South Arabian customary law, royal commands, tribal arbitration, and temple oath traditions. Disputes over land, water rights, kin liability, commercial debts, and tribute were commonly settled by local elders, officials, and royal judgment.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自农业地租、灌溉地出产分成、香料与乳香贸易税、市场税、过境关税、附庸贡赋及战利品。税收多以实物、香料、牲畜、金属和部分货币支付。
taxationSystem.en: State revenue came chiefly from agricultural land dues, shares of irrigated produce, taxes on aromatics and frankincense trade, market taxes, transit customs, subordinate tribute, and war booty. Taxes were paid largely in kind, aromatics, livestock, metal, and some coin.

religion.zh: 220年时，希木叶尔仍以南阿拉伯多神崇拜为主，祭祀阿尔玛卡、阿塔尔、瓦德及地方神祇；同时受犹太教、基督教与阿拉伯半岛一神论观念的早期影响，但尚未完成宗教转型。
religion.en: In 220 CE Himyar remained primarily devoted to South Arabian polytheistic worship, including Almaqah, Athtar, Wadd, and local deities; it was also beginning to feel early influence from Judaism, Christianity, and Arabian monotheistic ideas, but had not yet undergone religious transformation.

philosophy.zh: 思想以王权神圣性、祖先传统、部族荣誉、契约誓言和对灌溉秩序与商业信誉的重视为核心，并非形成体系化哲学，而是体现于铭文、法律习俗与宗教实践中。
philosophy.en: Its intellectual outlook centered on sacred kingship, ancestral tradition, tribal honor, oath-bound agreements, and respect for irrigation order and commercial trust; it was not a formal philosophical system but was expressed through inscriptions, customary law, and religious practice.

writingSystem.zh: 使用古南阿拉伯文字书写萨巴文—希木叶尔行政与铭文传统，主要见于石刻、奉献铭文、王室纪功和简短行政记录；口语则逐渐显示更广泛的阿拉伯化趋势。
writingSystem.en: Old South Arabian script was used for the Sabaic-Himyarite administrative and inscriptional tradition, appearing mainly in stone texts, dedicatory inscriptions, royal commemorations, and brief administrative records; spoken language was gradually showing broader Arabizing tendencies.

culturalAchievements.zh: 希木叶尔文化成就体现在山地梯田农业、蓄水与灌溉工程、石刻铭文、要塞化聚落、香料贸易网络及王权纪念建筑上。其王国整合能力为后期南阿拉伯政治统一奠定了基础。
culturalAchievements.en: Himyarite cultural achievement was expressed in mountain terraced agriculture, water-storage and irrigation works, stone inscriptions, fortified settlements, aromatic trade networks, and royal commemorative building. Its capacity for political integration laid groundwork for later unification in South Arabia.

languageFamily.zh: 闪米特语系，主要为古南阿拉伯语支中的萨巴文书面传统，并伴随地方口语演变
languageFamily.en: Semitic language family, primarily the Old South Arabian branch in its Sabaic written tradition, alongside evolving local spoken forms

gdpDescription.zh: 希木叶尔在220年属于南阿拉伯最富庶且增长中的政权之一。其经济建立在高地灌溉农业、香料与芳香贸易、商道控制、附庸贡赋和区域转口之上。虽远不及罗马或汉地总量，但在阿拉伯南端属高强度商业化王国。
gdpDescription.en: In 220 CE Himyar was one of the wealthiest and most dynamic polities in South Arabia. Its economy rested on highland irrigated agriculture, the trade in incense and aromatics, control of caravan routes, subordinate tribute, and regional entrepôt exchange. Though far smaller than Rome or Han successor states in total output, it was a highly commercialized kingdom by southern Arabian standards.

mainIndustries.zh: 梯田农业、谷物与葡萄栽培、乳香与没药贸易、畜牧、陶器、石工、金属加工、商队运输、港口转运与王室工程
mainIndustries.en: Terraced agriculture, cultivation of grain and grapes, trade in frankincense and myrrh, pastoralism, pottery, stoneworking, metalworking, caravan transport, port transshipment, and royal construction

tradeGoods.zh: 乳香、没药、香膏、葡萄酒、皮革、蜂蜜、牲畜、盐、纺织品、金属器、进口玻璃器、罗马与印度商品及东非输入品
tradeGoods.en: Frankincense, myrrh, perfumes, wine, leather, honey, livestock, salt, textiles, metal goods, imported glassware, Roman and Indian merchandise, and goods brought from East Africa

name.zh: 南阿拉伯银铜币与外来金币并用
name.en: South Arabian silver-bronze coinage with foreign gold in circulation

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异很大。高地灌溉农户若拥有梯田、水权与牲畜，通常较为稳固；商人和控制商道的贵族积累显著财富；边缘牧民与依附劳工财富较低且更易受旱灾影响。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied greatly. Highland irrigated farming households with terraces, water rights, and livestock were often relatively secure; merchants and nobles controlling trade routes accumulated notable wealth; peripheral pastoralists and dependent laborers possessed much less and were more vulnerable to drought.

economicSystem.zh: 以王权监管下的农业—商贸混合经济为主，结合部族控制的土地与通道、宫廷征税、地方交换市场和跨海贸易网络。国家直接管理部分税源与战略节点，但日常生产仍以地方社群和商人组织为基础。
economicSystem.en: The economy was a mixed agrarian-commercial system under royal supervision, combining tribally controlled land and routes, court taxation, local exchange markets, and overseas trade networks. The state directly managed some revenue sources and strategic nodes, but everyday production remained rooted in local communities and merchant organization.

source.en: Agricultural land dues and irrigated produce

source.zh: 王室地产、矿产与杂项收入
source.en: Royal estates, mineral income, and miscellaneous receipts

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室财政状况稳健，得益于商路收益增长与对高地核心区的有效控制。国库同时以金属、香料、粮食、牲畜和贡品形式储存财富，而非完全以铸币计算。
treasuryDescription.en: The royal fiscal position was sound, supported by growing route revenues and effective control over the highland core. The treasury stored wealth not only in coin or bullion, but also in aromatics, grain, livestock, and tribute goods.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向审慎扩张：通过征服与整合扩大税基，同时投资于灌溉、道路、城防与贸易节点，以维持长期收益；在旱年则依赖粮仓与地方协作减轻冲击。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored cautious expansion: broadening the tax base through conquest and integration while investing in irrigation, roads, defenses, and trade nodes to sustain long-term returns; in dry years it relied on granary reserves and local coordination to soften shocks.

description.zh: 由王室直属士兵、宫廷护卫和核心地区驻军组成，负责保卫扎法尔、王室设施与关键山地通道。
description.en: Composed of royal troops, palace guards, and garrisons from the core regions, tasked with defending Zafar, royal facilities, and key mountain passes.

description.zh: 由附庸和联盟部族按需征发，适合山区作战、护卫商路与围攻地方据点，但忠诚度与持续作战能力取决于政治关系和供给。
description.en: Raised from subordinate and allied tribes as needed, these forces were suited to mountain warfare, route protection, and the siege of local strongholds, though loyalty and sustained campaigning depended on political relations and supply.

description.zh: 包括骑兵与骆驼队，主要用于侦察、突袭、护送商队和控制高地与沙漠边缘间的联络。
description.en: Including cavalry and camel corps, they were used mainly for reconnaissance, raids, caravan escort, and maintaining links between the highlands and desert margins.

description.zh: 驻守沿海据点与港口，保护装卸、征税与海上来往，不属于大型海军但具备近岸防卫功能。
description.en: Stationed in coastal strongholds and ports, these troops protected loading operations, tax collection, and maritime traffic; they were not a major navy but provided littoral defense.

technology.zh: 军备以矛、剑、匕首、弓箭、皮甲与金属护具、盾牌、攻城器具和山地防御工事为主，擅长利用高地堡垒、峡谷和补给线控制。其军事技术不如罗马与萨珊前夕伊朗世界复杂，但在南阿拉伯地形中十分有效。
technology.en: Military equipment centered on spears, swords, daggers, bows, leather and metal armor, shields, siege tools, and mountain fortifications, with strong emphasis on exploiting highland strongholds, valleys, and supply control. Its military technology was less complex than that of Rome or the Iranian world on the eve of the Sasanian rise, but highly effective in South Arabian terrain.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自仍未完全纳入的南阿拉伯竞争政权、反叛部族、对贸易节点的争夺，以及红海对岸阿克苏姆未来可能加强的干预。内部整合与商路安全比远距离大国入侵更现实。
threats.en: The chief threats came from South Arabian rival polities not yet fully absorbed, rebellious tribes, competition over trade nodes, and the possibility of stronger future intervention by Aksum across the Red Sea. Internal consolidation and route security were more immediate concerns than invasion by distant great powers.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 可连发十矢的机械弩，守城利器
description.en: Mechanical crossbow firing ten bolts in succession, excellent for defense

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

description.zh: 重骑兵标志性武器，冲击力极强
description.en: Signature heavy cavalry weapon with tremendous charge impact

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于也门高地、灌溉谷地和若干贸易城镇。高地农业承载了王国人口核心，沿海与商路节点则因贸易而聚集较多流动人口、商人和劳工。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in the Yemeni highlands, irrigated valleys, and a number of trade towns. Highland agriculture supported the demographic core, while coastal and caravan nodes gathered larger numbers of mobile people, merchants, and laborers because of trade.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、贵族、部族盟长和大商人；中层包括书吏、祭司、地方军官、富裕农户和手工业者；下层为普通农民、牧民、搬运工、依附劳工与奴仆。社会流动有限，但商业成功与王室服务可提升地位。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of the royal house, nobles, tribal allies, and major merchants; the middle ranks included scribes, priests, local officers, prosperous farmers, and artisans; the lower ranks comprised common cultivators, pastoralists, porters, dependent laborers, and slaves. Social mobility was limited, but commercial success and royal service could improve status.

allies.zh: 主要依靠南阿拉伯内部附庸、结盟部族及与红海、印度洋商人的务实合作关系；并无类似罗马式固定条约同盟体系。
allies.en: It relied mainly on subordinate South Arabian polities, allied tribes, and pragmatic cooperative ties with Red Sea and Indian Ocean merchants; it did not possess a fixed treaty alliance system comparable to Rome's.

enemies.zh: 敌对者主要是南阿拉伯未归附政权、地方反叛部族以及与其争夺贸易与宗主地位的地区势力；与阿克苏姆的竞争在220年已具潜在性但尚未达到后世规模。
enemies.en: Its enemies were primarily unsubmitted South Arabian polities, rebellious local tribes, and regional forces contesting trade and overlordship; rivalry with Aksum was already latent in 220 CE but had not yet reached the scale seen later.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以统一南阿拉伯、掌控高地与港口、保护乳香和转口贸易、通过婚盟与贡赋体系稳固周边为核心。对外更重视贸易通达与海峡安全，而非远征式帝国扩张。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on unifying South Arabia, controlling the highlands and ports, protecting incense and entrepôt trade, and stabilizing surrounding areas through marriage ties and tribute arrangements. Externally, it prioritized commercial access and maritime security more than expeditionary imperial conquest.

era.zh: 古典时代晚期的南阿拉伯高地—印度洋贸易技术体系
era.en: Late Classical South Arabian highland–Indian Ocean trade technological regime

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术在于山地梯田与水利维护、蓄水池与渠道、石砌堡垒、长距离商路组织、香料储运、骆驼运输和港口贸易管理。这些并非革命性新发明，却构成希木叶尔扩张与繁荣的核心能力。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies lay in mountain terracing and water management, cisterns and channels, stone fortifications, long-distance caravan organization, aromatic storage and transport, camel logistics, and port trade administration. These were not revolutionary inventions, but they formed the core capabilities behind Himyar's expansion and prosperity.

strengths.zh: 希木叶尔的优势在于高地农业基础稳固、香料与海陆商路收益丰厚、王权整合能力增强、地形防御条件优越，并能吸纳地方首领进入统治体系。
strengths.en: Himyar's strengths lay in its solid highland agrarian base, lucrative incense and overland-maritime trade, increasing royal integrative power, excellent defensive terrain, and ability to absorb local chiefs into its ruling system.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点包括对降雨与灌溉维护的依赖、地方部族忠诚不稳、行政整合仍不完全、沿海控制力有限，以及财政与军力都高度依赖贸易持续畅通。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included dependence on rainfall and irrigation maintenance, unstable tribal loyalties, incomplete administrative integration, limited coastal control, and heavy fiscal and military dependence on the continued smooth flow of trade.

outlook.zh: 220年的希木叶尔前景总体乐观。若王权继续压制竞争政权、巩固高地与港口并维持商路安全，其将进一步完成南阿拉伯统一，并在后续数世纪成为红海—阿拉伯贸易政治中的关键力量。
outlook.en: Himyar's outlook in 220 CE was broadly favorable. If royal authority continued to suppress rival polities, consolidate the highlands and ports, and keep trade routes secure, it was likely to complete the unification of South Arabia and become a major force in Red Sea–Arabian trade politics over the following centuries.

description.zh: 希木叶尔在也门高地扩张，逐步统一南阿拉伯的香料与商路诸国。
description.en: Himyar was expanding in the Yemeni highlands and steadily unifying the incense and trade polities of South Arabia.

governmentForm.zh: 以部落为基础的游牧联盟，由强势首领通过盟约、威望与军事追随关系维系
governmentForm.en: A tribal nomadic confederation held together by strong chiefs through alliance, prestige, and military followings

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、部落和部落联盟为核心，上层为贵族与首领家族，中层为自由牧民与战士，下层包括附庸部众、俘虏与少量奴婢
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on clans, tribes, and tribal confederations, with nobles and chiefly lineages at the top, free pastoralists and warriors in the middle, and dependent followers, captives, and a small number of slaves below

rulingClass.zh: 各部大人、贵族家族、亲兵首领与因战功而起的部众领袖
rulingClass.en: Tribal chieftains, noble lineages, retainer commanders, and follower leaders elevated through military merit

succession.zh: 继承并非严格嫡长制，通常由首领家族成员竞争，并需获得部众与附属首领支持
succession.en: Succession was not strictly primogeniture; it usually involved competition among members of the ruling lineage and required support from followers and subordinate chiefs

structure.zh: 政治结构分散而灵活，各部保持较强自治，在战争、迁徙和对外关系上由强势首领协调；不存在汉式中央官僚体系，但有随主首领行动的亲信幕僚与使者网络
structure.en: The political structure was decentralized and flexible. Individual tribes retained strong autonomy, while a dominant chieftain coordinated warfare, migration, and foreign relations; there was no Han-style centralized bureaucracy, but there were personal retainers, advisers, and messenger networks attached to leading chiefs

function.zh: 负责传令、结盟、议和、贡赐往来与对邻国交涉
function.en: Handled messages, alliances, truces, tribute exchanges, and dealings with neighboring states

function.zh: 协调部众分布、季节迁徙、牧场使用与牲畜征集
function.en: Coordinated the distribution of tribespeople, seasonal migration, pasture use, and livestock levies

function.zh: 组织亲兵、召集各部骑兵、管理战利品分配与警戒
function.en: Organized retainer troops, summoned tribal cavalry, and managed security and distribution of booty

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要依靠各部首领、氏族长和附属小帅，不设固定郡县；权力随部众迁徙而移动
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied mainly on tribal chiefs, clan elders, and subordinate headmen rather than fixed counties or commanderies; authority moved with the people

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法为主，强调部族传统、首领裁断、赔偿、血亲责任与战时纪律；涉及对外关系时常以盟誓与人质保障执行
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on customary law, emphasizing tribal tradition, chiefly judgment, compensation, kin responsibility, and wartime discipline; in external affairs, oaths and hostages often guaranteed enforcement

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一田赋税制，资源主要通过牲畜贡纳、战利品分配、附属部落进献、边境互市收益与对汉地贸易中介获取
taxationSystem.en: There was no uniform land-tax system; resources came chiefly from livestock tribute, distribution of booty, contributions from subordinate tribes, frontier trade gains, and brokerage in exchange with Chinese territories

religion.zh: 以萨满信仰、祖先崇拜、天神与自然灵信仰为主，重视祭天、占卜与战争前祈祷，也逐步接触汉地宗教观念
religion.en: Religion was primarily shamanic, centered on ancestor veneration, sky worship, and nature spirits, with rituals to Heaven, divination, and prayers before war; they were also gradually exposed to Chinese religious ideas

philosophy.zh: 鲜卑并无成体系书面哲学传统，其价值观强调勇武、忠诚、家族纽带、慷慨分配与首领威望，并在与汉接触中吸收部分儒家政治语言
philosophy.en: The Xianbei lacked a systematic written philosophical tradition; their values emphasized martial valor, loyalty, kinship, generous distribution, and chiefly prestige, while some Confucian political language entered through contact with the Chinese world

writingSystem.zh: 220年时无成熟本族文字，外交与贸易记录多依赖汉字及汉地文书人员
writingSystem.en: In 220 CE they had no mature native script; diplomatic and trade records often relied on Chinese characters and Chinese scribes

culturalAchievements.zh: 鲜卑文化在骑射、马具使用、草原音乐舞蹈、金属饰件、毛皮服饰和跨区域草原联盟组织方面表现突出；其骑兵传统深刻影响了北方军事文化
culturalAchievements.en: Xianbei culture excelled in mounted archery, horse gear, steppe music and dance, metal ornaments, fur clothing, and the organization of wide-ranging steppe alliances; their cavalry tradition strongly influenced northern military culture

languageFamily.zh: 鲜卑语，通常归入古代蒙古语族或与之密切相关的草原语言群
languageFamily.en: Xianbei language, usually classified within or closely related to the early Mongolic steppe language group

unit.en: Han wuzhu cash equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 鲜卑经济以游牧畜产为核心，辅以狩猎、边境贸易、掠夺、贡纳和少量附属农业生产。其总量不如定居帝国，但在北方草原中属于强盛且机动性极高的经济体
gdpDescription.en: The Xianbei economy was centered on pastoral livestock production, supplemented by hunting, frontier trade, raiding, tribute, and limited agriculture among dependent groups. Its total scale was smaller than that of settled empires, but it was a strong and highly mobile economy by steppe standards

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧业、马匹繁育、皮毛加工、乳制品、骨角器制作、金属工艺、边境贸易与军事掠夺
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, horse breeding, fur processing, dairy production, bone and horn craftwork, metalwork, frontier trade, and military raiding

tradeGoods.zh: 输出马匹、牛羊、皮毛、畜产品、奴隶与草原特产；输入铁器、丝织品、粮食、酒、漆器和奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: They exported horses, cattle and sheep, furs, livestock products, slaves, and steppe goods; they imported iron tools, silk textiles, grain, alcohol, lacquerware, and luxury items

name.en: Barter with Han cash in circulation

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要以马、牛、羊数量衡量，贵族拥有大群牲畜、俘虏和精良兵器，普通牧户财富波动受气候与战事影响极大
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured mainly in horses, cattle, and sheep; nobles owned large herds, captives, and fine weapons, while ordinary pastoral households saw sharp swings in wealth depending on climate and warfare

unit.en: Han wuzhu cash equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以游牧家庭生产为基础，结合首领再分配、部落贡纳、互市交换与战利品分享的混合型草原经济
economicSystem.en: A mixed steppe economy based on household pastoral production, combined with chiefly redistribution, tribal tribute, market exchange, and the sharing of war booty

source.zh: 牲畜征集与首领直属牧群

treasuryDescription.zh: 鲜卑没有固定国库建筑，财富以首领掌握的牲畜、缴获物资、丝帛、金属器和对附属部的索取能力体现；可迅速转化为军需与赏赐
treasuryDescription.en: The Xianbei had no fixed treasury building; wealth was embodied in the herds, captured goods, silk, metal objects, and extraction capacity controlled by chiefs, all of which could quickly be converted into military supply and rewards

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重在维持首领权威与军事追随网络，优先将资源用于赏赐、战争和联盟整合，而非长期公共工程
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on maintaining chiefly authority and military followings, prioritizing rewards, warfare, and alliance integration rather than long-term public works

description.zh: 鲜卑军力核心，擅长骑射、快速奔袭、包抄和远距离机动，是对曹魏北疆最具威胁的力量
description.en: The core of Xianbei military power, skilled in mounted archery, rapid raids, encirclement, and long-range mobility, making it the greatest threat on Wei's northern frontier

description.zh: 由首领直属勇士组成，装备较好，负责冲击、护卫和督战
description.en: Composed of elite warriors directly attached to chiefs, better equipped and used for shock action, protection, and battlefield control

description.zh: 包括看守营地、支援后勤和在不利地形作战的辅助人员，比例较低
description.en: Included camp guards, logistical support, and auxiliary fighters used in unfavorable terrain; they formed only a small share of the force

commanderInChief.zh: 各部联盟大人，如轲比能

technology.zh: 以复合弓、骑射战术、皮甲与部分铁制兵器为主，善用马匹耐力与地形情报；攻城技术较弱，通常依赖机动作战而非持久围攻
technology.en: Military technology centered on composite bows, mounted archery, leather armor, and some iron weapons, with strong use of horse endurance and terrain intelligence; siege capabilities were weak, so they preferred mobile operations over prolonged assaults

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自鲜卑内部各部竞争、与乌桓及其他北方部族的冲突，以及曹魏在北边的离间、招抚和军事打击
threats.en: Main threats came from rival Xianbei tribes, conflict with the Wuhuan and other northern peoples, and Wei's combination of divide-and-rule, appeasement, and military pressure on the frontier

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 覆盖全身的重装骑兵铁甲
description.en: Full-body heavy cavalry iron barding

description.zh: 单兵弩与床弩，中国军队核心远程武器
description.en: Infantry crossbow and mounted crossbow, core ranged weapon

populationDescription.zh: 人口由多个鲜卑部落及其附属群体组成，分布于辽西、塞北、漠南及更广阔草原地带，流动性强，难以按定居国家标准精确统计
populationDescription.en: The population consisted of multiple Xianbei tribes and their dependent groups spread across Liaoxi, the northern frontier, southern Mongolia, and the wider steppe; their mobility makes precise settled-state style counting difficult

socialClasses.zh: 社会层次包括首领家族与贵族、亲兵和有名望战士、普通牧民、附属部众、工匠、俘虏及奴婢；身份与财富高度依赖牲畜、战功和依附关系
socialClasses.en: Social strata included chiefly lineages and nobles, retainers and prestigious warriors, ordinary pastoralists, subordinate tribespeople, artisans, captives, and slaves; status and wealth depended heavily on herds, military merit, and patronage ties

allies.zh: 鲜卑诸部之间时有联盟，并与部分北方部族及边境势力保持临时合作；也可能接受曹魏封号与互市以换取利益
allies.en: Xianbei tribes sometimes allied with one another and maintained temporary cooperation with other northern peoples and frontier powers; they could also accept titles and trade arrangements from Wei when advantageous

enemies.zh: 主要敌对对象是北方边境上的曹魏势力，以及与其争夺草场、人口和贸易路线的其他草原部族
enemies.en: Their principal adversary was the Wei power on the northern frontier, along with other steppe groups competing for pasture, people, and trade routes

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交策略务实多变，兼用朝贡、互市、联姻、劫掠与分化手段，在中原政权分裂之际扩大自主空间并增强草原影响力
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was pragmatic and flexible, using tribute, trade, marriage alliances, raiding, and political division to expand autonomy and strengthen steppe influence during the fragmentation of the Chinese heartland

era.zh: 三国时代的北方草原军事文化阶段
era.en: Northern steppe military culture of the Three Kingdoms era

keyInnovations.zh: 精于复合弓骑射、机动集结、寒冷草原环境适应、马具改进与远距离情报传递；技术重点在军事与生计适应，而非城市工程
keyInnovations.en: Key strengths included composite-bow mounted archery, mobile mustering, adaptation to cold steppe environments, improved horse gear, and long-distance information relay; technological emphasis lay in warfare and subsistence adaptation rather than urban engineering

strengths.zh: 机动性极强，骑兵战力突出，政治组织虽分散却富于弹性，能够在汉末动乱后迅速扩张并掌控北方草原的重要贸易与军事通道
strengths.en: They possessed exceptional mobility, formidable cavalry power, and a politically flexible confederative structure, allowing rapid expansion after the late Han turmoil and control of important northern trade and military corridors

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏稳固中央制度与固定都城，内部部落竞争激烈，财政与权威依赖首领个人魅力、战利品和牲畜资源，攻城和长期行政能力有限
weaknesses.en: They lacked stable central institutions and a fixed capital, faced intense internal tribal rivalry, and depended on personal leadership, booty, and livestock resources for finance and authority; siege warfare and long-term administration were limited

outlook.zh: 220年前后鲜卑处于上升阶段，随着曹魏巩固北方，其与中原政权的关系将更趋复杂；若能维持部落整合与贸易通道控制，鲜卑将在未来数十年继续成为北亚最重要的力量之一
outlook.en: Around 220 CE the Xianbei were in an expansionary phase. As Wei consolidated the north, relations with Chinese states would become more complex; if they could preserve tribal cohesion and control trade corridors, the Xianbei would remain one of the most important powers in northern Asia for decades to come

description.zh: 鲜卑在后汉末年持续壮大，是魏国北境最重要的草原势力之一。
description.en: The Xianbei continued to grow after the late Han and were among the most important steppe powers on Wei's northern frontier.

name.zh: 萨尔马提亚人与阿兰人

governmentForm.zh: 由骑马贵族与氏族首领主导的游牧部族联盟，权力分散，按部众、牧场与战时追随关系组织
governmentForm.en: A nomadic tribal confederational order led by mounted aristocrats and clan chiefs, with decentralized authority organized around tribes, pasture rights, and wartime followings

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、家族与追随集团为核心，上层为武装贵族和首领家族，中层为自由牧民战士，下层包括依附者、俘虏与奴隶；妇女在贵族家族与骑射文化中地位相对显著
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on clans, extended families, and retinues, with an upper layer of armed aristocrats and chiefdom lineages, a middle layer of free pastoral warriors, and lower groups including dependents, captives, and slaves; women held comparatively visible roles in aristocratic families and mounted culture

rulingClass.zh: 萨尔马提亚与阿兰各部的骑马贵族、氏族长老与军事首领
rulingClass.en: Mounted aristocrats, clan elders, and military leaders of the various Sarmatian and Alan tribes

succession.zh: 首领地位通常在显贵家族内部继承，但须获得部众、贵族战士与同盟首领认可；分支部族常可分立
succession.en: Leadership usually passed within prominent families, but required acceptance by followers, noble warriors, and allied chiefs; subordinate tribes could readily break away

structure.zh: 不存在统一官僚国家；治理依靠部族大会、首领议事、家族纽带和战时盟约。较强大的阿兰或萨尔马提亚首领可在一定时期内统合多个部众，但统合多为个人性与军事性。
structure.en: There was no unified bureaucratic state; governance relied on tribal assemblies, councils of chiefs, kinship ties, and wartime compacts. Strong Alan or Sarmatian leaders could temporarily unite several groups, but such cohesion was mainly personal and military.

function.zh: 协调部族间争端、缔结盟约、决定迁徙方向与大型战争行动
function.en: Coordinated intertribal disputes, concluded alliances, and decided major migrations and campaigns

function.zh: 维持首领权威、传递命令、征集战士并与外部政权接触
function.en: Maintained chiefly authority, carried orders, summoned warriors, and handled contacts with outside states

function.zh: 分配季节性牧场、收取牲畜贡赋、管理战利品与对附庸部众的要求
function.en: Allocated seasonal pastures, collected livestock tribute, and managed booty and obligations from dependent groups

function.zh: 与罗马边防、博斯普鲁斯王国、高加索诸国及中亚商旅进行谈判与交换
function.en: Negotiated and exchanged with Roman frontier authorities, the Bosporan Kingdom, Caucasian polities, and Central Asian merchants

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以部族、氏族和营地为单位，由地方首领、长老与贵族家族主持；没有固定州县制度，权力随季节迁徙而移动
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through tribes, clans, and encampments under local chiefs, elders, and noble lineages; there was no fixed provincial system, and authority moved with seasonal migration

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要为习惯法，重视血亲关系、赔偿、誓约、荣誉与战利品分配；重大争端由首领和长老裁决，强制力依赖亲族与武装追随者
legalSystem.en: Law was predominantly customary, emphasizing kinship, compensation, oaths, honor, and the division of booty; major disputes were judged by chiefs and elders, while enforcement depended on kin groups and armed retainers

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一货币税制；首领主要通过牲畜贡赋、战利品分成、贸易抽成、附庸部族进献及受雇于外邦所得维持权力
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified monetary tax system; chiefs mainly sustained power through livestock tribute, shares of booty, trade dues, gifts from subordinate tribes, and payments earned in foreign service

religion.zh: 以伊朗语系草原宗教为主，崇拜天神、火、太阳、祖先与战神，重视誓约、墓葬仪式和护身符；同时与高加索、希腊—罗马与地方信仰发生交流
religion.en: Religion was primarily an Iranic steppe tradition venerating sky powers, fire, the sun, ancestors, and war deities, with strong emphasis on oaths, funerary rites, and amulets; it also interacted with Caucasian, Greco-Roman, and local beliefs

philosophy.zh: 没有成体系书面哲学，核心价值在于勇武、荣誉、亲族忠诚、首领慷慨、骑射技艺与对自由游牧生活的维护
philosophy.en: There was no formal written philosophy; core values centered on valor, honor, kin loyalty, chiefly generosity, mounted martial skill, and the preservation of a free pastoral way of life

writingSystem.zh: 本土社会基本无广泛使用的统一文字；对外交流时可能借用希腊文、拉丁文、阿拉米文或邻近定居文明的书记传统
writingSystem.en: There was essentially no widely used native unified script; for external dealings they could rely on Greek, Latin, Aramaic, or the scribal traditions of neighboring settled civilizations

culturalAchievements.zh: 以骑兵战争文化、精美金属饰物、动物纹装饰、复合弓与长枪骑战传统、华丽墓葬随葬品以及跨草原交流网络著称；阿兰人与萨尔马提亚人的重装骑兵传统对周边军事文化影响深远
culturalAchievements.en: They were noted for cavalry warfare culture, fine metal ornaments, animal-style decoration, traditions of composite bows and long-lance cavalry combat, rich grave goods, and broad trans-steppe exchange networks; Alan and Sarmatian heavy cavalry traditions had lasting influence on neighboring military cultures

languageFamily.zh: 东伊朗语支，包含多种萨尔马提亚与阿兰方言
languageFamily.en: Eastern Iranian, comprising various Sarmatian and Alan dialects

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以畜牧业为核心，财富主要体现为马、牛、羊、奴隶、战利品与贵金属器物，而非城市市场产出。总量不及大型农耕帝国，但机动性强，能通过贸易、雇佣兵服务和掠夺获得额外收入。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was centered on pastoralism, with wealth expressed chiefly in horses, cattle, sheep, slaves, booty, and precious metal objects rather than in urban market output. Its total scale was far below that of major agrarian empires, but it was highly mobile and could gain additional income through trade, mercenary service, and raiding.

mainIndustries.zh: 马匹与牲畜养殖、乳制品、皮革与毛毡制作、金属工艺、武器装备、奴隶贸易、边境交换与护送商旅
mainIndustries.en: Horse and livestock breeding, dairy production, leather and felt working, metalcraft, weapon equipment, slave trading, frontier exchange, and caravan escorting

tradeGoods.zh: 马匹、牛羊、皮革、毛皮、毛毡、奴隶、蜂蜡、畜产品，以及从罗马和高加索获得的酒、金属器、玻璃器与奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Horses, cattle, sheep, leather, furs, felt, slaves, wax, livestock products, and goods obtained from Rome and the Caucasus such as wine, metalware, glassware, and luxury items

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要由帐幕、牲畜、武器、衣物和运输车辆构成；贵族家庭拥有大群马匹、金饰、甲胄、奴隶与多重姻亲网络
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth consisted mainly of tents, herds, weapons, clothing, and transport wagons; noble households possessed large horse herds, gold ornaments, armor, slaves, and extensive marriage alliances

economicSystem.zh: 以游牧畜牧经济为主，辅以掠夺、贡纳、跨边境贸易和对定居国家的军事服务；市场交换存在，但主要依附于边境口岸、河谷和高加索通道
economicSystem.en: A predominantly pastoral-nomadic economy supplemented by raiding, tribute, cross-border trade, and military service to settled states; market exchange existed but was concentrated around frontier stations, river valleys, and Caucasian corridors

category.en: Gift distribution, alliances, and marriage diplomacy

treasuryDescription.zh: 并无统一国库，财富分散于各大首领家族，以牲畜、金银饰物、武器、俘虏和外来钱币形式保存；战时可迅速集中，但平时高度分散
treasuryDescription.en: There was no single central treasury; wealth was dispersed among major chiefly families and stored as herds, gold and silver ornaments, weapons, captives, and foreign coinage; it could be concentrated rapidly in war, but remained highly decentralized in peacetime

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重点在于通过馈赠与分配维持忠诚，而非建立稳定税务机构；强首领倾向积累战利品后再分赐，以稳固追随集团
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on maintaining loyalty through gift distribution rather than building stable tax institutions; strong chiefs tended to accumulate booty and then redistribute it to secure their followings

description.zh: 由贵族和其亲随组成，装备长枪、鳞甲或札甲，是萨尔马提亚—阿兰军事力量的核心突击兵种
description.en: Composed of nobles and their retainers, armed with long lances and scale or lamellar armor, forming the core shock arm of Sarmatian-Alan military power

description.zh: 机动侦察、袭扰和追击主力，擅长远程消耗与草原机动战
description.en: The main force for scouting, harassment, and pursuit, skilled in ranged attrition and steppe maneuver warfare

description.zh: 由自由牧民在战争时期集中组成，装备不如贵族精良，但数量可观
description.en: Raised from free pastoralists in wartime; less well equipped than nobles but available in significant numbers

description.zh: 负责营地警戒、辎重防护、攻守据点和辅助作战，在草原作战中地位次要
description.en: Handled camp security, baggage defense, position fighting, and auxiliary tasks, playing a secondary role in steppe warfare

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅，战时由最强部族首领或盟主统军
commanderInChief.en: No single permanent commander-in-chief; in war the strongest tribal chief or hegemonic leader commanded

technology.zh: 军事技术以草原骑兵体系为主，包括复合弓、长枪冲击、鳞甲、马甲、快速迁营与远程侦察；缺乏成熟攻城技术，攻坚多依赖盟友或雇佣工匠
technology.en: Military technology was based on the steppe cavalry system, including the composite bow, long-lance shock tactics, scale armor, horse armor, rapid camp movement, and long-range scouting; siegecraft was underdeveloped and usually relied on allies or hired specialists

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括罗马边境反制、高加索山口争夺、来自东部草原势力的压力、部族内讧及附庸脱离；同时与日耳曼诸部和黑海北岸势力关系复杂
threats.en: Major threats included Roman frontier retaliation, struggles over the Caucasian passes, pressure from eastern steppe powers, internal tribal rivalries, and defections by subordinate groups; relations with Germanic tribes and northern Black Sea powers were also complex

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

description.zh: 覆盖全身的重装骑兵铁甲
description.en: Full-body heavy cavalry iron barding

populationDescription.zh: 人口广泛分布于顿河、伏尔加河下游、库班地区、北高加索前沿与黑海—里海草原，以流动营地和季节性牧场为主，人口密度较低
populationDescription.en: The population was spread widely across the Don region, lower Volga, Kuban area, northern Caucasian forelands, and the Pontic-Caspian steppe, with mobile encampments and seasonal pastures resulting in low population density

name.zh: 博斯普鲁斯边境营贸聚落

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括部族贵族、首领亲随、自由牧民战士、手工业者与商旅中介、附庸部众、俘虏与奴隶；流动性较高，但财富和声望集中在骑马贵族手中
socialClasses.en: Social classes included tribal nobles, chiefly retainers, free pastoral warriors, craftsmen and trade intermediaries, dependent tribesmen, captives, and slaves; mobility existed, but wealth and prestige were concentrated in the mounted aristocracy

allies.zh: 无持久统一盟友体系；常与北高加索诸部、博斯普鲁斯王国部分势力、亚美尼亚或帕提亚边境集团进行临时合作
allies.en: There was no permanent unified alliance system; they often cooperated temporarily with North Caucasian tribes, elements of the Bosporan Kingdom, and border factions in Armenia or Parthia

enemies.zh: 潜在敌对者包括罗马帝国边防军、争夺草场的其他草原部族、部分高加索山地集团，以及在特定时期发生冲突的日耳曼或地方定居政权
enemies.en: Potential enemies included Roman frontier forces, rival steppe tribes competing for pasture, some Caucasian mountain groups, and at times Germanic or local settled polities

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以实用主义为主，通过掠袭、护商、联姻、质子交换、雇佣兵服务和控制草原通道来扩大影响；目标不是建立固定边界，而是维持对牧场、商道和附庸网络的主导
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was pragmatic, using raiding, caravan protection, marriage ties, hostage exchange, mercenary service, and control of steppe routes to expand influence; the goal was not fixed borders but dominance over pastures, trade corridors, and networks of dependents

era.zh: 铁器时代晚期草原骑兵时代
era.en: Late Iron Age steppe cavalry era

keyInnovations.zh: 复合弓骑射、长枪重骑兵战术、鳞甲与马具改进、草原远距离侦察和高机动营地组织；在定居建筑、文字行政和攻城工程方面较弱
keyInnovations.en: Composite-bow horse archery, long-lance heavy cavalry tactics, improvements in scale armor and horse gear, long-range steppe reconnaissance, and highly mobile camp organization; they were weaker in permanent architecture, written administration, and siege engineering

strengths.zh: 机动性极强、骑兵战力出众、社会适应草原环境、能灵活介入黑海—高加索—中亚之间的贸易与战争网络
strengths.en: They possessed exceptional mobility, formidable cavalry power, strong adaptation to steppe conditions, and the ability to intervene flexibly in the trade and war networks linking the Black Sea, Caucasus, and Central Asia

weaknesses.zh: 政治分散、缺乏稳定官僚与税收体系、城市和攻城能力薄弱、联盟容易因首领更替或战利品分配而瓦解
weaknesses.en: They were politically fragmented, lacked stable bureaucratic and tax institutions, had weak urban and siege capacity, and their alliances could break apart easily over succession or the distribution of booty

outlook.zh: 220年时整体局势相对稳定，阿兰人与萨尔马提亚诸部仍控制广大草原并保持对外影响力；但长期看，他们将面临来自更东部草原力量和欧亚迁徙链条的更大冲击，内部整合能力将成为决定未来命运的关键
outlook.en: In 220 CE the overall situation was relatively stable, and the Alans and Sarmatian groups still controlled broad stretches of steppe while retaining external influence; in the longer term, however, they would face greater pressure from more easterly steppe powers and wider Eurasian migration chains, making their limited capacity for internal consolidation a decisive factor in their future

description.zh: 黑海—里海草原由萨尔马提亚与阿兰游牧联盟主导，与罗马、高加索及中亚诸国互动频繁。
description.en: The Pontic-Caspian steppe was dominated by Sarmatian and Alan nomadic confederations interacting with Rome, the Caucasus, and Central Asia.

governmentForm.zh: 分散的部族联盟与酋邦政治，由贵族家族、战士随从与民众大会共同运作
governmentForm.en: Decentralized tribal confederations and chiefdoms, governed through noble families, warrior retinues, and popular assemblies

socialStructure.zh: 社会以自由民、贵族战士、随从、佃附者与奴隶构成，亲族、氏族和部落关系是政治与经济生活的核心
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of free farmers, noble warriors, retainers, dependents, and slaves, with kinship, clan, and tribal ties forming the core of political and economic life

rulingClass.zh: 部族贵族、战争首领与拥有追随者的显贵家族
rulingClass.en: Tribal nobles, war leaders, and prominent families commanding personal followings

succession.zh: 酋长职位通常在显贵家族内部继承，但须得到贵族支持与部族集会承认；战时统帅常凭个人威望推举
succession.en: Chiefship usually passed within leading families but required support from nobles and recognition by tribal assemblies; wartime commanders were often elevated by personal prestige

structure.zh: 220年时，日耳曼世界并非统一国家，而是由法兰克前身部落、阿勒曼尼形成中的部族集团、萨克森人、哥特人以及其他较小部族组成。政务依赖地方酋长、氏族长老与战士集会处理，跨部族协调多为临时性质，主要用于战争、迁徙或与罗马谈判。
structure.en: In 220 CE, the Germanic world was not a unified state but a mosaic of proto-Frankish tribes, emerging Alamannic groupings, Saxons, Goths, and many smaller peoples. Governance depended on local chiefs, clan elders, and warrior assemblies, while intertribal coordination was usually temporary and focused on war, migration, or negotiations with Rome.

function.zh: 管理酋长财物、宴飨、馈赠、随从与对外接待，是部族政治核心而非正式官署
function.en: Managed the chief's property, feasting, gift-giving, retainers, and diplomacy, serving as the political core rather than a formal bureau

function.zh: 由显贵家族长者与有威望战士组成，负责仲裁争端、辅佐酋长并商议战争、结盟和赔偿
function.en: Composed of elder nobles and respected warriors, it arbitrated disputes, advised chiefs, and discussed war, alliances, and compensation

function.zh: 自由民定期集会，认可重要决定、推举战时领袖、审议重大纠纷和传统法
function.en: Assemblies of free men ratified major decisions, chose wartime leaders, and judged serious disputes under customary law

function.zh: 作为酋长与贵族的私人武装和执行力量，承担护卫、征战、征收贡赋与威望展示
function.en: Served as the private armed force and executive arm of chiefs and nobles, handling protection, warfare, tribute collection, and displays of prestige

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以村落、氏族与小部落为基础，没有统一官僚体系。权力掌握在地方酋长、家族长老与战士领袖手中，司法和赋役多依习惯与面对面协商执行。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on villages, clans, and small tribes, without a unified bureaucracy. Power lay in the hands of local chiefs, family elders, and war leaders, while justice and obligations were enforced through custom and face-to-face negotiation.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要为口传习惯法，强调血亲责任、赔命金、誓言、见证人与复仇调解。严重冲突常通过赔偿、决斗或集会裁判解决，而非成文法典。
legalSystem.en: Law was chiefly oral customary law, emphasizing kin responsibility, wergild compensation, oaths, witnesses, and mediation of feuds. Serious conflicts were settled by compensation, combat, or assembly judgment rather than written codes.

taxationSystem.zh: 不存在类似帝国的系统税制。酋长收入主要来自贡礼、战利品分配、畜产供奉、宴飨馈赠义务，以及与罗马边境贸易或受雇服役所得。
taxationSystem.en: There was no systematic imperial-style taxation. Chiefs derived income mainly from tribute gifts, shares of booty, livestock contributions, obligations tied to feasting and gift exchange, and earnings from frontier trade or service with Rome.

religion.zh: 信仰多神教与自然崇拜，祭祀天空、战争、生育与丰产相关神灵，并重视圣林、沼泽、泉水和祖先传统。各部落神名与仪式不尽相同，尚未形成统一教义。
religion.en: Religion was polytheistic and nature-centered, venerating deities linked to sky, war, fertility, and abundance, while placing great importance on sacred groves, bogs, springs, and ancestral tradition. Divine names and rites varied by tribe, and there was no unified doctrine.

philosophy.zh: 缺乏成体系书面哲学，核心观念体现于英雄伦理、忠诚、荣誉、复仇、慷慨赠礼与氏族义务之中，价值观通过诗歌、传说与习俗传承。
philosophy.en: There was no systematic written philosophy; core ideas were expressed through heroic ethics, loyalty, honor, vengeance, generous gift-giving, and clan obligation, transmitted by poetry, legend, and custom.

writingSystem.zh: 220年时大多数日耳曼部族尚未普遍使用文字。少量符文传统可能已在北欧和北海周边出现，但在广大日耳曼地区行政与文化传播仍以口述为主。
writingSystem.en: By 220 CE, most Germanic tribes did not yet use writing widely. Early runic traditions may already have appeared in Scandinavia and the North Sea zone, but across most Germanic lands administration and cultural transmission remained primarily oral.

culturalAchievements.zh: 其文化成就在于高水平金属工艺、武器与饰物制作、木构建筑、口头史诗传统、船只制造以及跨莱茵—多瑙边境形成的混合物质文化。对罗马器物的吸收与再创造尤为显著。
culturalAchievements.en: Their cultural achievements included skilled metalworking, weapon and ornament production, timber construction, oral epic tradition, boatbuilding, and hybrid frontier material cultures formed across the Rhine and Danube zones. The adoption and reinterpretation of Roman goods was especially notable.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系日耳曼语族，包含西日耳曼、北日耳曼与东日耳曼诸方言群
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Germanic branch, including West Germanic, North Germanic, and East Germanic dialect groups

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业和畜牧业为主，市场交换有限但并非缺失。沿罗马边境的贸易、雇佣兵服役、赎金、贡礼和战利品对部分部族财富积累非常重要。总体产出低于同时代帝国，但边疆接触带局部较为活跃。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based chiefly on subsistence agriculture and pastoralism, with limited but real market exchange. Frontier trade with Rome, military service as mercenaries, ransom, gift tribute, and booty were highly important for wealth accumulation among some tribes. Overall output was lower than that of contemporary empires, but frontier contact zones were locally dynamic.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、畜牧、林业、狩猎、采集、铁器锻造、琥珀与毛皮贸易、船只制造、纺织与骨角工艺
mainIndustries.en: Cereal farming, livestock herding, forestry, hunting, gathering, ironworking, amber and fur trade, boatbuilding, textile production, and bone-horn crafts

tradeGoods.zh: 输出琥珀、毛皮、蜂蜡、奴隶、牲畜、皮革、猎物制品、铁器与战士服务；输入罗马酒类、陶器、玻璃器、金属器、硬币、奢侈品与武器
tradeGoods.en: Exports included amber, furs, beeswax, slaves, livestock, leather, animal products, iron goods, and warrior service; imports included Roman wine, pottery, glassware, metal goods, coin, luxury items, and weapons

name.zh: 以物易物与罗马硬币并用
name.en: Barter with Roman coin in circulation

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在土地使用权、牲畜、谷仓储备、工具、武器与亲族支持网络。贵族财富则体现在马匹、金银饰物、进口器物、奴隶与随从人数。
householdWealth.en: For ordinary households, wealth was chiefly measured in land use rights, livestock, stored grain, tools, weapons, and kin support networks. Noble wealth was expressed through horses, precious ornaments, imported goods, slaves, and the number of retainers maintained.

economicSystem.zh: 以家户生产、亲族互助、酋长再分配和边境交换为核心的部族经济体系，市场化程度有限，但在罗马边疆受到货币和奢侈品流入影响日益加深
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy centered on household production, kin-based reciprocity, chiefly redistribution, and frontier exchange, with limited marketization but increasing influence from the inflow of Roman money and prestige goods

treasuryDescription.zh: 不存在统一国库，财富分散储存在各酋长家廷、圣地、仓储点及贵族私人收藏中。可动员财富更多依赖个人忠诚网络，而非固定财政机构。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no unified state treasury; wealth was dispersed among chiefly households, sacred sites, storage centers, and private noble holdings. Mobilizable resources depended more on personal loyalty networks than on fixed fiscal institutions.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政运作偏向再分配模式：酋长通过收受贡礼、积累战利品与对外所得，再以宴飨、赏赐和护卫维系权威。稳定时期趋于节制储备，战时则迅速动员物资与人力。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice followed a redistributive model: chiefs gathered tribute gifts, booty, and external income, then maintained authority through feasting, rewards, and protection. In stable periods they conserved reserves modestly, while in wartime they mobilized resources and manpower rapidly.

description.zh: 由自由民和战士核心组成，是主要作战力量，装备长矛、盾牌、短剑、标枪与少量甲具，擅长近距离冲击与森林沼泽地形作战
description.en: Made up of free men and a warrior core, this was the principal fighting force, equipped with spears, shields, short swords, javelins, and limited armor, excelling in close combat and warfare in forests and marshlands

description.zh: 数量较少但机动性更强，多由贵族及其亲随组成，用于侦察、突击、追击和首领护卫，在东部与南部部族中更为常见
description.en: Smaller in number but more mobile, these units were composed largely of nobles and their followers and were used for scouting, shock action, pursuit, and elite protection, being more common among eastern and southern tribes

description.zh: 主要见于北海和波罗的海沿岸部族，使用小型木船进行沿岸运输、劫掠与跨河作战，尚未形成常备海军
description.en: Found mainly among North Sea and Baltic coastal tribes, they used small wooden boats for coastal transport, raiding, and river crossings, but did not constitute a permanent navy

description.zh: 在大战、复仇或保卫迁徙时临时动员，由各村落和氏族提供，训练与装备不如核心战士稳定
description.en: Temporarily mobilized during major wars, feuds, or migration defense, these levies were provided by villages and clans and were less consistently trained and equipped than the warrior core

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅，战时由最具威望的酋长或战争首领临时统领
commanderInChief.en: No unified commander-in-chief; in war, the most prestigious chief or war leader exercised temporary command

technology.zh: 军事技术总体处于铁器时代后期，使用铁矛、长盾、剑、斧、标枪、简易锁甲或皮甲，并善于构筑临时寨栅与利用地形伏击。部分精英装备受罗马影响明显。
technology.en: Military technology belonged to the late Iron Age, using iron spears, long shields, swords, axes, javelins, and simple mail or leather armor, with effective use of temporary fieldworks and terrain ambushes. Some elite equipment showed clear Roman influence.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自部族间竞争、与萨尔马提亚—阿兰草原势力的冲突，以及与罗马帝国莱茵、多瑙边境上的报复性战争、边境封锁和外交操控。迁徙压力和首领争位也会引发内部动荡。
threats.en: Major threats came from intertribal competition, clashes with Sarmatian-Alan steppe powers, and retaliatory warfare, frontier controls, and diplomatic manipulation by the Roman Empire along the Rhine and Danube. Migration pressures and rivalries among leaders also produced internal instability.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 单兵弩与床弩，中国军队核心远程武器
description.en: Infantry crossbow and mounted crossbow, core ranged weapon

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散居住于从莱茵河以东、北海沿岸到维斯瓦河与黑海北部接触带的广大区域，各部族规模差异很大。聚落多为村落和小型聚居点，少有真正意义上的大型城市。
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across a vast area from east of the Rhine and along the North Sea coast to the Vistula and contact zones north of the Black Sea, with great variation in tribal size. Settlements were mostly villages and small communities, with few true large cities.

name.zh: 无固定大型城市，以下为重要聚落中心：乌普兰诸集会地
name.en: No fixed major cities; notable centers included assembly sites in Uppland

name.zh: 哥特部族维斯瓦河口聚落群
name.en: Gothic settlement clusters near the Vistula mouth

name.zh: 下莱茵法兰克前身部落集会中心
name.en: Assembly centers of proto-Frankish tribes on the lower Rhine

name.zh: 易北河下游萨克森聚落中心
name.en: Saxon settlement centers on the lower Elbe

name.zh: 上多瑙阿勒曼尼形成区聚落
name.en: Settlements in the emerging Alamannic zone on the upper Danube

socialClasses.zh: 大致可分为部族贵族与显贵家族、自由战士与农民、依附者与佃附人口、奴隶或战俘。流动性部分存在，勇武、财富和酋长恩宠能够提升地位。
socialClasses.en: Society can be broadly divided into tribal nobles and leading families, free warriors and farmers, dependents and semi-free populations, and slaves or war captives. Some social mobility existed, as valor, wealth, and chiefly favor could elevate status.

allies.zh: 无持久统一盟友体系；各部族会与邻近日耳曼部落缔结临时同盟，并偶尔与萨尔马提亚或罗马边境将领达成互利安排
allies.en: There was no lasting unified alliance system; tribes formed temporary coalitions with neighboring Germanic peoples and occasionally reached mutually beneficial arrangements with Sarmatian groups or Roman frontier commanders

enemies.zh: 主要外部对手是罗马帝国边防军与敌对部族，局部地区也与萨尔马提亚人、阿兰人及相邻竞争部落发生冲突
enemies.en: The principal external opponents were Roman frontier forces and rival tribes; in some regions they also clashed with Sarmatians, Alans, and neighboring competitors

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以现实主义和机会主义为主：通过劫掠、贸易、结盟、婚姻、交换人质和为罗马服役获取利益。并不存在统一的对外战略，各部落通常根据边境压力和内部权力斗争调整立场。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was pragmatic and opportunistic, seeking advantage through raiding, trade, alliances, marriage, hostage exchange, and service with Rome. There was no single overarching strategy, and each tribe adjusted its stance according to frontier pressures and internal power struggles.

era.zh: 欧洲晚期铁器时代与罗马边疆接触时代
era.en: Late European Iron Age and the age of Roman frontier contact

keyInnovations.zh: 改良铁器锻造、长盾与矛战术、木船和河海航行、谷物储藏技术、手工业饰件制作、早期符文使用，以及对罗马金属器、玻璃器和军事装备的吸收
keyInnovations.en: Improved ironworking, long-shield and spear tactics, wooden boats and river-sea navigation, grain storage methods, decorative craft production, early rune use, and the adoption of Roman metalware, glassware, and military equipment

strengths.zh: 优势在于人口广布、部族战士传统强、地形适应力高、社会组织富有弹性，并能从罗马边境贸易和军事接触中吸收财富与技术。地方自主性强，使其在遭受打击后仍能迅速恢复。
strengths.en: Strengths included wide demographic spread, strong warrior traditions, high adaptability to varied terrain, and flexible social organization, together with the ability to absorb wealth and techniques from Roman frontier trade and military contact. Strong local autonomy allowed rapid recovery after setbacks.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点是政治极度分散、缺乏统一财政与常备官僚体系、内部竞争频繁、后勤组织有限，面对罗马这种制度化帝国时难以长期维持大规模联合行动。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses were extreme political fragmentation, the absence of unified finance and a permanent bureaucracy, frequent internal competition, and limited logistical organization, making it difficult to sustain large coordinated operations against a highly institutionalized empire like Rome.

outlook.zh: 220年前后总体稳定，但未来数代中，罗马边境危机、部族联盟扩大、向南和向西迁动，以及哥特、法兰克、阿勒曼尼、萨克森等更大政治共同体的形成，将显著提升日耳曼世界对欧洲政治格局的影响力。
outlook.en: Around 220 CE the situation was broadly stable, but over the coming generations Roman frontier crises, the enlargement of tribal confederations, southward and westward movements, and the emergence of larger political groupings such as the Goths, Franks, Alamanni, and Saxons would greatly increase the impact of the Germanic world on the political order of Europe.

description.zh: 法兰克、阿勒曼尼、撒克逊与哥特等部族分布于罗马北方边境外。
description.en: Franks, Alamanni, Saxons, Goths, and other tribes occupied lands beyond Rome's northern frontiers.

ruler.en: Various Holy Lords (Ajaws of individual city-states)

capital.zh: 蒂卡尔、卡拉克穆尔及其他城邦中心
capital.en: Tikal, Calakmul, and other city-state centers

governmentForm.zh: 分散的神权王权城邦体系，各城由世袭王室统治
governmentForm.en: A decentralized sacral kingship city-state system, with each polity ruled by a hereditary royal house

socialStructure.zh: 社会呈金字塔结构，上层为王室、贵族与祭司，其下为书记官、工匠、商人、战士与农民，底层包括劳役者与战俘奴隶
socialStructure.en: Society was pyramidal, with royal families, nobles, and priests at the top, followed by scribes, artisans, merchants, warriors, and farmers, with laborers and war captives as the lowest strata

rulingClass.zh: 王室贵族、祭司集团与高级书记官
rulingClass.en: Royal nobles, priestly elites, and high-ranking scribes

succession.zh: 通常为王室父系世袭，但在继承危机时也可能出现旁支继位、摄政或以战争重定合法性
succession.en: Usually patrilineal hereditary succession within royal houses, though collateral succession, regencies, or warfare could redefine legitimacy during succession crises

structure.zh: 玛雅并无统一帝国政体，而是由数十个大小不等的城邦构成；每一城邦以王宫为行政核心，统治周边聚落，并通过婚盟、朝贡、战争与附庸关系形成地区性政治网络
structure.en: The Maya did not form a single imperial state but consisted of dozens of city-states of varying size; each polity centered administration in the royal palace, governed surrounding settlements, and maintained regional political networks through marriage alliances, tribute, warfare, and vassalage

function.zh: 管理宫廷、王室地产、仪式筹备、使节接待与王族供养
function.en: Managed the court, royal estates, ritual preparation, reception of envoys, and maintenance of the royal family

function.zh: 征集粮食、可可、棉布、羽毛、盐与劳役，并储存与再分配物资
function.en: Collected food, cacao, cotton cloth, feathers, salt, and labor obligations, and stored and redistributed goods

function.zh: 主持祭祀、天文观测、历法推算、吉日选择与王权神圣化宣传
function.en: Conducted rituals, astronomical observations, calendar calculations, auspicious day selection, and sacral legitimation of kingship

function.zh: 记录王室谱系、战争、朝贡和纪年，并负责碑铭、壁画与官方文书
function.en: Recorded royal genealogies, wars, tribute, and dates, and produced inscriptions, murals, and official texts

function.zh: 组织贵族战队、协调盟邦与附庸、筹划袭击与俘虏征战
function.en: Organized noble war bands, coordinated allies and vassals, and planned raids and captive-taking campaigns

function.zh: 征调劳工修建金字塔、神庙、道路、水库、梯田与宫殿群
function.en: Mobilized labor to build pyramids, temples, causeways, reservoirs, terraces, and palace complexes

localAdmin.zh: 各大城邦直接控制核心城区与近郊村社，并通过地方领主、家族首领和附庸城镇间接治理外围地区；行政边界常随战争与婚盟变化
localAdmin.en: Major city-states directly controlled urban cores and nearby villages while governing outer territories indirectly through local lords, lineage heads, and subordinate towns; administrative boundaries often shifted with war and marriage alliances

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯、王命与宗教规范为基础，由统治者、贵族议事圈和祭司共同裁断；惩罚包括赔偿、劳役、降黜、处死与祭祀性处置，战俘则常用于献祭或强制劳动
legalSystem.en: Law was based on custom, royal command, and religious norms, adjudicated by rulers, noble councils, and priests; punishments included compensation, labor service, demotion, execution, and ritualized penalties, while captives were often used for sacrifice or forced labor

taxationSystem.zh: 以实物贡赋与劳役为主，而非统一货币税；农户与附庸需定期上缴玉米、豆类、棉布、蜂蜜、可可、盐、玉器、羽毛及建筑劳役和军事服务
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied mainly on tribute in kind and labor rather than uniform monetary taxes; households and subordinate communities regularly delivered maize, beans, cotton cloth, honey, cacao, salt, jade goods, feathers, construction labor, and military service

religion.zh: 多神信仰与祖先崇拜并行，重视天体循环、农业神灵、雨神、玉米神、羽蛇神及王室祖灵；祭祀常伴随献血、焚香、舞蹈与人祭
religion.en: Polytheism coexisted with ancestor worship, emphasizing celestial cycles, agricultural deities, the rain god, maize god, feathered serpent, and royal ancestors; rituals often involved bloodletting, incense burning, dance, and human sacrifice

philosophy.zh: 世界观强调时间循环、天地冥界三重宇宙、王权与神意相连，以及人与祖先、自然和历法秩序之间的平衡
philosophy.en: The Maya worldview emphasized cyclical time, a tripartite cosmos of sky, earth, and underworld, the linkage of kingship to divine will, and balance between humans, ancestors, nature, and calendrical order

writingSystem.zh: 玛雅象形文字为美洲最复杂的文字体系之一，兼具表意与音节成分，用于碑铭、陶器、抄本前身与宫廷记录
writingSystem.en: Maya hieroglyphic writing was one of the most complex writing systems in the Americas, combining logographic and syllabic elements for inscriptions, ceramics, proto-codical media, and court records

culturalAchievements.zh: 经典期早期的玛雅在金字塔神庙、石碑纪年、天文观测、长纪历、王室肖像艺术、灰泥雕塑与彩绘陶器方面成就卓著，蒂卡尔、乌阿克萨克通、科潘等地已形成成熟礼仪与建筑传统
culturalAchievements.en: In the Early Classic period, the Maya excelled in pyramid-temples, stela dating, astronomy, the Long Count calendar, royal portraiture, stucco sculpture, and polychrome ceramics; sites such as Tikal, Uaxactun, and Copán had already developed mature ritual and architectural traditions

languageFamily.zh: 玛雅语系，多种地方语言并存，如乔兰语群、尤卡坦语群及其他分支
languageFamily.en: Mayan language family, with multiple regional languages in use, including Ch'olan, Yucatecan, and other branches

gdpDescription.zh: 玛雅经济以农业剩余、宫廷再分配、工艺生产和区域交换为核心。总量可观，但分散于众多城邦，缺乏统一市场与财政核算，因此估值为现代学术中的粗略折算
gdpDescription.en: The Maya economy was centered on agricultural surplus, court redistribution, craft production, and regional exchange. Its aggregate size was substantial but dispersed among many city-states, lacking a unified market and fiscal accounting, so any estimate is necessarily a rough modern conversion

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米农业、豆类与南瓜种植、可可栽培、棉织、陶器、石器与黑曜石加工、玉器雕琢、羽饰制作、建筑工程与盐生产
mainIndustries.en: Maize agriculture, bean and squash cultivation, cacao growing, cotton weaving, pottery, lithic and obsidian working, jade carving, featherwork, construction, and salt production

tradeGoods.zh: 黑曜石、玉石、可可、盐、蜂蜜、棉布、羽毛、海贝、陶器、燧石器、颜料、贵重兽皮与礼仪器物
tradeGoods.en: Obsidian, jade, cacao, salt, honey, cotton cloth, feathers, marine shells, ceramics, flint tools, pigments, luxury hides, and ritual goods

name.zh: 无统一货币，以可可豆、棉布与贵重物作交换尺度
name.en: No unified currency; cacao beans, cloth, and valuables served as media of exchange

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要体现为住房、农具、玉米储备、家禽、织物与亲族劳动力；贵族则掌握土地、附庸、玉器、羽饰、祭仪用品与宫廷馈赠网络
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth lay mainly in dwellings, tools, maize stores, domestic fowl, textiles, and kin labor; nobles held land, dependents, jade, feather regalia, ritual objects, and court gift networks

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 宫廷再分配经济与区域市场交换并存，基础是村社农业和劳役征发；远距离贸易由商旅、贵族代理人和附庸网络推动，奢侈品流通对政治合法性尤为重要
economicSystem.en: A mixed system of court redistribution and regional market exchange operated atop village agriculture and labor levies; long-distance trade was driven by merchants, noble agents, and vassal networks, with luxury circulation especially important for political legitimacy

treasuryDescription.zh: 玛雅“国库”并非单一中央金库，而是分散在各城邦宫殿、仓储建筑与贵族家产中的粮食、织物、可可、玉器、贝饰和礼仪珍品
treasuryDescription.en: The Maya 'treasury' was not a single central vault but wealth dispersed across palace compounds, storehouses, and noble estates in the form of grain, textiles, cacao, jade, shell ornaments, and ritual valuables

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重在维持王室威望、祭祀秩序和大型建筑项目；盛期通常可保持小幅盈余，但若遭旱灾、战败或继承危机，储备会迅速消耗
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized royal prestige, ritual order, and monumental construction; in prosperous periods small surpluses were achievable, but drought, defeat, or succession crises could quickly consume reserves

description.zh: 由王室亲兵、贵族战士和精锐俘虏猎取者构成，负责宫廷防卫、仪式护卫与主力突击
description.en: Composed of royal retainers, noble warriors, and elite captive-takers responsible for palace defense, ceremonial protection, and main assault actions

description.zh: 由平民征召兵组成，使用矛、投枪、木盾、石刃武器和弓箭，战时随领主集结
description.en: Made up of commoner levies using spears, darts, wooden shields, stone-bladed weapons, and bows, assembled under local lords in wartime

description.zh: 来自附庸城镇和盟邦的支援兵力，常用于边境袭扰、增援和象征政治服从
description.en: Support forces from subordinate towns and allies, often used for frontier raiding, reinforcement, and symbolic displays of political obedience

commanderInChief.zh: 各城邦圣主亲自统军，辅以战首领与贵族将领
commanderInChief.en: Each city-state's Holy Lord personally commanded war, assisted by war captains and noble commanders

technology.zh: 军事技术以黑曜石刃器、燧石矛头、投枪、弓箭、木棉护甲、木盾和城防工事为主；无冶铁、骑兵与大型攻城机械，但善于丛林机动、伏击与俘虏战
technology.en: Military technology centered on obsidian-edged weapons, flint spearheads, darts, bows, cotton armor, wooden shields, and fortifications; there was no ironworking, cavalry, or large siege machinery, but the Maya were adept at jungle mobility, ambush, and captive-taking warfare

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自其他玛雅强邦间的争霸、附庸叛离、贸易路线争夺，以及中部低地与南部低地势力格局变化；同时需警惕来自特奥蒂瓦坎影响圈的间接政治压力
threats.en: The main threats came from rivalry among powerful Maya polities, vassal defections, and competition over trade routes, along with shifting balances in the central and southern lowlands; there was also indirect political pressure from the sphere of Teotihuacan influence

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 复合弓或长弓，远射主力
description.en: Composite or longbow, primary ranged weapon

description.zh: 覆盖全身的重装骑兵铁甲
description.en: Full-body heavy cavalry iron barding

description.zh: 可连发十矢的机械弩，守城利器
description.en: Mechanical crossbow firing ten bolts in succession, excellent for defense

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于尤卡坦半岛南部、佩滕低地、伯利兹及邻近高地边缘的村社与城邦腹地。220年时经典期发展加速，若干大型中心正在扩张，但人口仍以农村为主
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in village networks and city-state hinterlands across the southern Yucatán Peninsula, the Petén lowlands, Belize, and adjacent highland margins. By 220 CE, Early Classic growth was accelerating, with several large centers expanding, though the population remained overwhelmingly rural

name.zh: 卡拉克穆尔地区核心聚落
埃尔米拉多尔遗绪地区聚落群
name.en: Settlements of the El Mirador legacy zone

socialClasses.zh: 王室与贵族、祭司与书记官、武士与行政从属者、工匠与商人、自耕农与佃作者、劳役者与战俘奴隶
socialClasses.en: Royalty and nobility, priests and scribes, warriors and administrative dependents, artisans and merchants, peasant cultivators and tenants, and laborers and war-captive slaves

allies.zh: 无统一对外盟友；各玛雅城邦以婚盟、附庸关系和短期军事联盟为主，部分城邦与特奥蒂瓦坎商贸网络存在友好联系
allies.en: There were no unified external allies; Maya city-states relied on marriage alliances, vassal ties, and short-term military coalitions, while some polities maintained friendly links with Teotihuacan trade networks

enemies.zh: 主要敌对对象是彼此竞争的玛雅城邦与叛离附庸，局部还与邻近非玛雅群体发生边境冲突
enemies.en: Their principal enemies were rival Maya city-states and rebellious subordinates, with occasional frontier conflict against neighboring non-Maya groups

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以王室联姻、礼物交换、俘虏展示、碑铭宣传和贸易路线控制为核心，目标是扩大威望、维持附庸忠诚并争夺地区霸权，而非建立统一的广域帝国
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on royal marriage, gift exchange, captive display, inscriptional propaganda, and control of trade routes, aiming to expand prestige, maintain vassal loyalty, and compete for regional hegemony rather than build a single vast empire

era.zh: 玛雅早期经典期
era.en: Maya Early Classic Period

keyInnovations.zh: 长纪历与复杂历法并用、精密天文观测、成熟的象形文字碑铭体系、灰泥建筑装饰、水库与蓄水工程、抬高道路、精美彩陶与玉器工艺
keyInnovations.en: The use of the Long Count alongside complex calendrical systems, precise astronomical observation, a mature hieroglyphic inscription system, stucco architectural ornament, reservoirs and water-management works, raised causeways, and refined polychrome ceramics and jade craftsmanship

strengths.zh: 农业基础稳固，城邦网络密集，宗教与王权结合紧密，历法与文字高度发达，建筑与艺术成就突出，能够动员大量劳役与区域贸易资源
strengths.en: Its strengths included a solid agricultural base, dense city-state networks, a close fusion of religion and kingship, highly developed calendrics and writing, outstanding architecture and art, and the ability to mobilize large labor and regional trade resources

weaknesses.zh: 政治高度分裂，城邦间长期竞争消耗资源；财政和行政缺乏统一标准，对降雨、土壤条件和贸易通道依赖显著，继承危机与战争易使局势迅速恶化
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were intense political fragmentation, prolonged inter-polity competition that consumed resources, lack of unified fiscal and administrative standards, and strong dependence on rainfall, soil conditions, and trade corridors; succession crises and warfare could quickly destabilize conditions

outlook.zh: 220年时玛雅低地整体处于繁荣上升阶段，未来数世纪将迎来更多纪年碑铭、宫殿扩建与强邦争霸；但这种繁荣建立在脆弱的区域均衡之上，长期仍潜藏环境与政治风险
outlook.en: In 220 CE the Maya lowlands were broadly in a rising phase of prosperity; the coming centuries would bring more dated monuments, palace expansion, and contests among major powers. Yet this flourishing rested on a fragile regional balance, with enduring environmental and political risks beneath it

description.zh: 玛雅低地处于古典期兴盛阶段，多座城市国家在政治、历法与建筑上高度发达。
description.en: The Maya lowlands were flourishing in the Classic period, with numerous city-states advanced in politics, calendrics, and architecture.

governmentForm.zh: 以昆弥为首、诸部贵族共治的部落联盟君主制
governmentForm.en: A tribal confederation monarchy headed by the Kunmi and shared with aristocratic clan chiefs

socialStructure.zh: 社会以游牧部族为基础，分为王族、贵族部众、普通牧民、附属部落与奴婢。政治与军事组织高度重叠，氏族与部落首领掌握人口、牧群与武装。
socialStructure.en: Society was based on pastoral tribes and divided into the royal house, aristocratic followers, ordinary herders, subordinate tribes, and slaves or dependents. Political and military organization largely overlapped, with clan and tribal chiefs controlling people, herds, and armed retainers.

rulingClass.zh: 昆弥家族、左右大将与各部贵族首领
rulingClass.en: The Kunmi lineage, left and right military nobles, and the chiefs of major tribes

succession.zh: 以王族世袭为原则，但常受诸部贵族拥立、分封与外部强权干预影响，继承并不稳定。
succession.en: Succession was in principle hereditary within the royal line, but in practice depended on endorsement by tribal nobles, partition arrangements, and interference from stronger neighboring powers, making it unstable.

structure.zh: 220年时的乌孙政体已较盛时松散。昆弥名义上总领诸部，实际依赖左右翼贵族、部帅与家臣维系统治。中央机构较简，更多通过亲族关系、婚姻联盟、贡赋与军事追随来实现统治。
structure.en: By 220 CE the Wusun polity was looser than in its peak period. The Kunmi nominally ruled all tribes, but in practice relied on left and right wing nobles, tribal commanders, and retainers. The central apparatus was limited, and authority operated mainly through kinship ties, marriage alliances, tribute, and military followership.

function.zh: 处理王室决策、贵族会商、分配牧地与协调对外关系
function.en: Handled royal decisions, consultations with nobles, allocation of pasturelands, and coordination of external relations

function.zh: 统辖主要部众骑兵，负责征召、巡牧与边境防御
function.en: Controlled the main tribal cavalry forces and oversaw mobilization, pasture patrols, and frontier defense

function.zh: 统计牲畜贡纳、征收实物税并管理王庭牧群
function.en: Recorded livestock tribute, collected taxes in kind, and managed the royal herds

function.zh: 负责向周边政权遣使、接待来使并维持草原交通联络
function.en: Managed embassies to neighboring states, received foreign envoys, and maintained steppe communications

function.zh: 仲裁部族纠纷、处理盗牧、婚约与血亲赔偿案件
function.en: Arbitrated tribal disputes and dealt with stock theft, marriage arrangements, and kin-compensation cases

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以各部首领为核心，按部族、营帐和冬夏牧场分治。赤谷城周边较具定居行政色彩，但多数地区仍由部落自治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on tribal chiefs, organized by tribe, encampment, and seasonal pastures. The area around Chigu had somewhat more settled administration, but most regions remained under tribal self-rule.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要依习惯法而行，重视部族传统、首领裁断与赔偿原则。盗牲、叛附、伤杀与婚姻争议多以罚畜、赔偿或部族调停解决。
legalSystem.en: Law was based chiefly on customary practice, emphasizing tribal tradition, judgment by chiefs, and compensation. Livestock theft, defection, injury, homicide, and marital disputes were commonly settled through fines in animals, compensation, or tribal mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政征收以实物为主，包括马、牛、羊、皮革、乳制品及少量贸易税。附属部落向王庭进献牲畜与军役，定居绿洲人口则可能承担粮食与手工业贡纳。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was mainly in kind, including horses, cattle, sheep, hides, dairy products, and limited trade duties. Subject tribes rendered livestock and military service to the court, while settled oasis populations may also have provided grain and craft tribute.

religion.zh: 乌孙宗教以草原信仰为主，崇敬天、祖先、自然神灵与火祭传统，并带有萨满式仪式。与西域诸国交往下，也可能接触佛教与伊朗宗教因素，但未成为主体。
religion.en: Wusun religion remained primarily a steppe belief system centered on Heaven, ancestors, natural spirits, and fire rites, with shamanic elements. Through contact with the Western Regions they likely encountered Buddhism and Iranian religious influences, but these did not become dominant.

philosophy.zh: 无成体系书面哲学，价值观重勇武、忠于部族、长者权威、盟誓与牧业生活秩序。政治理念更多体现为草原贵族共议与血缘合法性。
philosophy.en: There was no fully systematized written philosophy. Core values emphasized martial courage, loyalty to tribe, elder authority, oath-keeping, and the order of pastoral life. Political ideas were expressed more through aristocratic consultation and kin-based legitimacy.

writingSystem.zh: 乌孙本身未见成熟自用文字系统，政务与外交往来可能依赖汉文书写、粟特或其他西域书写传统的外来书记人员。
writingSystem.en: The Wusun themselves do not appear to have possessed a fully developed indigenous writing system. Administration and diplomacy likely depended on foreign scribes using Chinese writing or other scripts of the Western Regions, including Iranian-related traditions.

culturalAchievements.zh: 其文化成就在于草原骑射传统、良马培育、游牧组织能力以及连接伊犁河谷与西域贸易通道的中介角色。物质文化包括毡帐、金属马具、皮毛制品与混合草原—绿洲风格器物。
culturalAchievements.en: Their cultural achievements lay in steppe mounted warfare, horse breeding, pastoral organization, and their intermediary role linking the Ili Valley with trade routes of the Western Regions. Material culture included felt tents, metal horse gear, fur products, and objects showing a mixed steppe-oasis style.

languageFamily.zh: 语言归属不明，现代学界多认为其可能与印欧语系中的伊朗语支或吐火罗相关群体有关，但证据不足。
languageFamily.en: The language affiliation is uncertain. Many modern scholars consider the Wusun possibly connected to Indo-European groups, perhaps Iranian or related to Tocharian-associated populations, though the evidence remains insufficient.

gdpDescription.zh: 乌孙经济已明显衰落，仍以畜牧为绝对核心，辅以伊犁河谷局部农业、狩猎、手工业与过境贸易收益。与大型定居帝国相比，其总量不高，但在区域草原社会中仍具一定资源动员能力。
gdpDescription.en: The Wusun economy was clearly in decline, but still rested overwhelmingly on pastoralism, supplemented by limited agriculture in the Ili Valley, hunting, craft production, and transit trade revenues. Compared with major settled empires its overall output was modest, though it still retained some ability to mobilize resources in the regional steppe world.

mainIndustries.zh: 马匹与牲畜饲养、皮革与毛毡制作、乳制品、少量谷物种植、金属器修造、边境贸易
