mainIndustries.en: Horse and livestock raising, leather and felt production, dairy products, limited grain cultivation, metalworking and repair, and frontier trade

tradeGoods.zh: 马、牛羊、皮毛、毡毯、乳制品，以及转运的丝绸、金属器、珠饰与西域杂货
tradeGoods.en: Horses, cattle and sheep, hides and furs, felt textiles, dairy goods, and re-exported silk, metal goods, beads, and miscellaneous products from the Western Regions

name.zh: 五铢钱与实物交换并用

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为牲畜数量、马群质量、毡帐与家臣。贵族拥有大量牧群和依附人口，普通牧户财富波动较大，易受严冬、疫病与战乱影响。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in the number of animals owned, the quality of horse herds, tents, and dependents. Nobles controlled large herds and attached followers, while ordinary pastoral households experienced sharp fluctuations due to harsh winters, disease, and warfare.

economicSystem.zh: 以游牧畜产经济为核心、辅以贡赋与边贸的混合型部落经济。生产与分配受氏族关系和贵族控制强烈影响，市场交换存在但不居主导地位。
economicSystem.en: A mixed tribal economy centered on pastoral production, supplemented by tribute and frontier trade. Production and distribution were strongly shaped by clan relations and aristocratic control, while market exchange existed but was not dominant.

source.zh: 附属部落贡赋与军役折值
source.en: Subject tribal tribute and commuted service
Royal herds and direct domain output

treasuryDescription.zh: 王庭储备并非完全以铸币形式存在，更多包含牲畜、皮毛、丝织品、金属器与可供馈赠的奢侈品。220年前后因长期衰落与外部压力，财政缓冲能力有限。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury did not exist wholly in coined form, but consisted largely of livestock, hides, textiles, metal goods, and prestige items suitable for gifts. Around 220 CE, after prolonged decline and external pressure, fiscal reserves were limited.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持王庭威望、安抚贵族和保证骑兵动员为主，缺乏定居帝国式精细预算。遇到危机时，往往通过加重贡赋、重新分配牧群或寻求对外援助来应对。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed chiefly at sustaining court prestige, satisfying nobles, and ensuring cavalry mobilization rather than maintaining a finely managed budget like that of settled empires. In crisis, the regime tended to respond by increasing tribute demands, redistributing herds, or seeking outside support.

description.zh: 直属昆弥与王族贵族的精锐骑兵，负责护卫王庭、快速突击与指挥核心作战。
description.en: Elite cavalry directly attached to the Kunmi and royal nobles, used for court protection, rapid strikes, and command-center operations.

description.zh: 乌孙军力主体，由各部征发的骑射手组成，擅长侦察、袭扰、追击与草原机动作战。
description.en: The main body of Wusun forces, composed of mounted archers levied from the tribes, skilled in scouting, raiding, pursuit, and mobile steppe warfare.

description.zh: 由附属部落、定居人口与护营人员组成，用于守卫营地、辎重和局部据点。
description.en: Drawn from subordinate tribes, settled populations, and camp personnel, these forces guarded encampments, baggage, and local strongpoints.

technology.zh: 军事技术以复合弓、骑射、长矛、短剑、鳞甲或皮甲、套马与快速换马为核心。攻城能力有限，更适合草原与河谷机动作战。
technology.en: Military technology centered on the composite bow, mounted archery, spears, short swords, scale or leather armor, lassos, and rapid remounting. Siege capability was limited, and the army was better suited to mobile warfare in the steppe and river valleys.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自鲜卑等草原新势力扩张、周边西域政治重组、部族离散以及自身王权衰弱。乌孙已难恢复前汉时期制衡匈奴与西域的地位。
threats.en: The main threats came from the expansion of rising steppe powers such as the Xianbei, political reconfiguration in the Western Regions, tribal fragmentation, and weakening royal authority. The Wusun could no longer regain the role they had once played in balancing the Xiongnu and the Western Regions in the Former Han era.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

description.zh: 单兵弩与床弩，中国军队核心远程武器
description.en: Infantry crossbow and mounted crossbow, core ranged weapon

populationDescription.zh: 220年时乌孙人口约三十万，较早期强盛阶段显著减少，分布于伊犁河谷及周边草原、山麓与部分绿洲地带。人口构成兼具游牧部众、附属部落与少量定居农业人口。
populationDescription.en: Around 220 CE the Wusun population was about 300,000, significantly reduced from their earlier period of strength. They were distributed across the Ili Valley and surrounding steppes, foothills, and some oasis zones. The population included pastoral tribesmen, subordinate groups, and a smaller settled agricultural element.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括昆弥王族、军事贵族、部族首领、自由牧民、附庸与依附民、定居农工人口及少量奴婢。阶层差异主要体现在牧群规模、武装追随者和政治影响力上。
socialClasses.en: Social classes included the Kunmi royal family, military aristocracy, tribal chiefs, free herders, vassals and dependents, settled farming and artisan populations, and a smaller slave stratum. Differences in status were reflected mainly in herd size, armed followers, and political influence.

allies.zh: 无稳固强盟；与部分西域邻近部族和绿洲政权维持临时性互市、婚盟或共御更强草原势力的合作。
allies.en: No firm great-power alliance; the Wusun maintained temporary trade arrangements, marriage ties, or limited cooperation with neighboring tribes and oasis polities of the Western Regions against stronger steppe powers.

enemies.zh: 鲜卑等新兴草原势力、争夺牧地的邻近部落，以及任何试图控制伊犁通道的外部力量。
enemies.en: Rising steppe powers such as the Xianbei, neighboring tribes competing for pasture, and any outside force seeking to control the Ili corridor.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以生存和均势为目标，避免卷入中原三国直接争霸，更多通过朝贡、通使、婚姻和边贸在强邻之间保持缓冲地位。其对魏等中原政权的关系较间接，已不复汉代时的重要战略地位。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed at survival and balance, avoiding direct involvement in the Three Kingdoms struggle of China proper. The Wusun relied instead on tribute, embassies, marriage, and frontier trade to preserve a buffer position between stronger neighbors. Their relationship with states such as Wei was more indirect and no longer carried the strategic weight it had under the Han.

era.zh: 三国时代的中亚草原—西域过渡阶段
era.en: A Central Asian steppe–Western Regions transitional phase in the Three Kingdoms era

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术并非220年的新发明，而是成熟运用草原骑射体系、复合弓、良马育种、毡帐生活技术、皮革与马具制作，以及对河谷牧农混合生计的适应。
keyInnovations.en: The key technologies were not novel inventions of 220 CE but mature applications of the steppe mounted-archery system, the composite bow, horse breeding, felt-tent technologies, leather and harness manufacture, and adaptation to a mixed pastoral-agricultural life in river valleys.

strengths.zh: 仍控制伊犁地区重要牧场与交通节点，具备骑兵机动优势，拥有悠久的草原政治传统，并能在东西贸易网络间充当中介。
strengths.en: They still controlled important pastures and transit points in the Ili region, retained the mobility advantages of cavalry, possessed a long-established steppe political tradition, and could still serve as intermediaries in east-west trade networks.

weaknesses.zh: 人口与军力下降，王权对诸部控制减弱，财政基础狭窄，缺乏稳定文字行政体系，易受鲜卑等更强势草原集团压迫与吸纳。
weaknesses.en: Their population and military power had declined, royal control over the tribes had weakened, the fiscal base was narrow, there was no stable indigenous written administrative system, and they were vulnerable to pressure and absorption by stronger steppe confederations such as the Xianbei.

outlook.zh: 220年前后的乌孙已处衰落末期，短期内尚能维持区域性部族政权，但长期趋势是继续分化、被邻近强权挤压，并逐渐淡出史籍中心。
outlook.en: By around 220 CE the Wusun were in a late phase of decline. In the short term they could still maintain a regional tribal polity, but the long-term trend pointed toward further fragmentation, pressure from neighboring powers, and gradual disappearance from the center of recorded history.

description.zh: 乌孙在伊犁河谷一带延续存在，但相较前代已明显衰弱。
description.en: The Wusun still existed in the Ili region, though they were much diminished from earlier centuries.

governmentForm.zh: 以蒙特阿尔班为核心的区域城邦国家，由世袭贵族家族统治并维系附属聚落网络
governmentForm.en: A regional city-state centered on Monte Albán, ruled by hereditary noble houses and sustained through a network of subordinate settlements

socialStructure.zh: 社会分层明显，上层为王族与贵族祭司，中层为武士、行政人员、工匠与商贩，下层为农民、劳役者与附属村社居民
socialStructure.en: Society was strongly stratified, with royal and noble priests at the top, warriors, administrators, artisans, and traders in the middle, and peasants, laborers, and dependent village communities below

rulingClass.zh: 蒙特阿尔班及各中心聚落的世袭贵族、祭司集团与军事首领
rulingClass.en: Hereditary nobles, priestly groups, and military leaders of Monte Albán and other major centers

succession.zh: 以家族世袭为主，通常在统治贵族内部传承，并受祭司认可与政治联盟影响
succession.en: Primarily hereditary within ruling noble families, usually transmitted inside elite lineages and shaped by priestly approval and political alliances

structure.zh: 蒙特阿尔班作为宗教、政治和礼仪中心，统辖瓦哈卡谷地若干次级中心与村落；权力由宫廷贵族、祭司和军事首领共同运作
structure.en: Monte Albán functioned as the religious, political, and ceremonial center overseeing secondary centers and villages across the Valley of Oaxaca; authority was exercised jointly by court nobles, priests, and military leaders

function.zh: 组织宫廷礼仪、祖先祭祀、公共仪式和精英居住区事务
function.en: Managed court ritual, ancestor worship, public ceremonies, and affairs of elite residential compounds

function.zh: 征集玉米、豆类、纺织品、陶器和劳役，并管理储藏设施与再分配
function.en: Collected maize, beans, textiles, pottery, and labor service, and managed storage facilities and redistribution

name.zh: 地方统治与附属聚落事务

function.zh: 监督谷地与周边地区的附属中心、地方领袖、道路联络和政治忠诚
function.en: Oversaw dependent centers, local lords, road connections, and political loyalty in the valleys and nearby regions

name.zh: 宗教历法与文字记录事务

function.zh: 维护历法、铭文、命名记录、祭祀时序和纪念碑文本
function.en: Maintained calendars, inscriptions, naming records, ritual timing, and monument texts

function.zh: 组织战士、守卫高地中心、护卫贸易路线并应对邻近竞争势力
function.en: Organized warriors, guarded hilltop centers, protected exchange routes, and responded to nearby rival powers

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托区域中心、村落首领和贵族代理人实施，附属聚落以贡赋、劳役和礼仪服从方式与蒙特阿尔班连接
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through regional centers, village headmen, and noble agents, with dependent settlements tied to Monte Albán through tribute, labor obligations, and ritual subordination

legalSystem.zh: 未见成文法典留存，秩序主要依靠习惯法、贵族裁决、宗教规范和对贡赋义务的强制执行
legalSystem.en: No surviving written law code is known; order was maintained mainly through customary law, noble judgment, religious norms, and enforcement of tribute obligations

taxationSystem.zh: 财政基础为实物贡赋与劳役，包括农产品、手工业品、建筑劳役和军事服务，而非统一货币税
taxationSystem.en: The fiscal base rested on tribute in kind and labor service, including agricultural produce, craft goods, construction labor, and military service rather than a uniform monetary tax

religion.zh: 多神信仰，重视祖先崇拜、山岳与雨水神灵、农业丰产仪式以及统治者的神圣合法性
religion.en: Polytheistic religion emphasizing ancestor worship, mountain and rain deities, agricultural fertility rites, and the sacred legitimacy of rulers

philosophy.zh: 思想体系以宇宙循环、历法秩序、祖先权威与统治—祭祀结合为核心，并非类似欧亚的抽象哲学传统
philosophy.en: Its worldview centered on cosmic cycles, calendrical order, ancestral authority, and the fusion of rule and ritual rather than an abstract philosophical tradition like those of Eurasia

writingSystem.zh: 使用中美洲早期文字与历法记号体系，在石碑、建筑和纪念物上记录人名、地名、征服与仪式信息
writingSystem.en: Used an early Mesoamerican writing and calendrical notation system, recording personal names, place names, conquests, and ritual information on stelae, buildings, and monuments

culturalAchievements.zh: 蒙特阿尔班大型礼仪建筑群、广场规划、石刻铭文、墓葬壁龛、陶器工艺和历法传统均代表其高度发展文化成就
culturalAchievements.en: The great ceremonial complex of Monte Albán, planned plazas, carved inscriptions, tomb chambers, ceramic craftsmanship, and calendrical traditions all represent major cultural achievements

languageFamily.zh: 奥托-曼格语系，主要为萨波特克诸语
languageFamily.en: Oto-Manguean language family, primarily Zapotec languages

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、贡赋再分配和地区性手工业为主，市场交换存在但未形成货币化国家经济；估值仅为跨文明比较的近似折算
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on agriculture, tributary redistribution, and regional craft production, with exchange present but not organized as a monetized state economy; the estimate is only a rough cross-civilizational conversion

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米农业、豆类与南瓜种植、陶器制造、纺织、石器与建筑工程、黑曜石与贝壳制品加工
mainIndustries.en: Maize farming, cultivation of beans and squash, pottery production, weaving, stoneworking and construction, and processing of obsidian and shell goods

tradeGoods.zh: 玉米、豆类、辣椒、纺织品、陶器、黑曜石、玉石、贝壳、羽毛和礼仪物品
tradeGoods.en: Maize, beans, chili peppers, textiles, pottery, obsidian, jade, shell, feathers, and ritual goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异显著，贵族掌握土地、劳力和礼器，普通农户主要拥有住房、农具、少量储粮和家用器物
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied sharply, with nobles controlling land, labor, and prestige goods, while ordinary farming households mainly possessed dwellings, tools, modest grain stores, and domestic goods

economicSystem.zh: 以农业村社生产、精英控制的贡赋再分配和长短程交换网络相结合的礼仪性再分配经济
economicSystem.en: A ceremonial redistributive economy combining village agricultural production, elite-controlled tribute redistribution, and short- and long-distance exchange networks

source.zh: 区域交换与附属中心供纳
source.en: Regional Exchange and Dependent Center Contributions

category.zh: 仓储、再分配与灾荒储备

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”主要体现为粮仓储备、纺织品、工艺品、礼器和可动员劳力，而非金属货币储藏
treasuryDescription.en: The “treasury” consisted chiefly of stored grain, textiles, crafted goods, prestige objects, and mobilizable labor rather than stockpiled metallic currency

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持礼仪中心、巩固附属地区忠诚、储备粮食并以再分配支持统治合法性
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on maintaining the ceremonial center, securing loyalty from dependent regions, storing food, and using redistribution to sustain political legitimacy

description.zh: 驻守蒙特阿尔班核心建筑群，保护统治者、祭司和主要仓储设施
description.en: Stationed in the core complexes of Monte Albán to protect rulers, priests, and major storage facilities

description.zh: 由各聚落和附属中心提供，承担边境冲突、威慑和征伐行动
description.en: Provided by settlements and dependent centers, they handled frontier conflict, deterrence, and punitive campaigns

description.zh: 在危机时用于守卫山地据点、运输补给和修筑防御设施
description.en: Used in times of crisis to defend hilltop positions, transport supplies, and build defensive works

commanderInChief.zh: 蒙特阿尔班诸领主与贵族军事首领共同统帅
commanderInChief.en: Jointly commanded by the lords of Monte Albán and noble military leaders

technology.zh: 军备以木矛、投枪器、石刃武器、盾牌和棉甲或厚织物防护为主，依赖山地据点、防御地形和集体作战
technology.en: Armament centered on wooden spears, atlatls, stone-bladed weapons, shields, and cotton armor or thick textile protection, relying on hilltop strongholds, defensive terrain, and coordinated group fighting

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自瓦哈卡地区内部竞争势力、与邻近米斯特克及其他高地或低地群体的冲突，以及区域影响力衰退风险；并不存在与欧亚列强的直接军事接触
threats.en: The main threats came from internal rivals in Oaxaca, conflict with neighboring Mixtec and other highland or lowland groups, and the risk of declining regional influence; there was no direct military contact with Eurasian great powers

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

description.zh: 复合弓或长弓，远射主力
description.en: Composite or longbow, primary ranged weapon

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于瓦哈卡谷地与周边高地村社，蒙特阿尔班仍为象征性与政治性中心，但部分人口和权力已向其他地方中心分散
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the Valley of Oaxaca and surrounding highland communities; Monte Albán remained the symbolic and political center, though some population and power had spread to other local centers

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王族、贵族、祭司与高阶武士；中层为工匠、商贩、书记与地方头人；下层为农民、劳役者与依附群体
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum included royalty, nobles, priests, and senior warriors; the middle stratum included artisans, traders, scribes, and local headmen; the lower stratum consisted of peasants, laborers, and dependent groups

allies.zh: 与瓦哈卡谷地部分附属中心及若干通过婚姻、贡赋和礼仪联系维系的地方集团保持合作
allies.en: Maintained cooperation with some dependent centers in the Valley of Oaxaca and local groups tied through marriage, tribute, and ritual relationships

enemies.zh: 主要敌对者为地区竞争城邦和不稳定边缘群体；与玛雅及特奥蒂瓦坎文明既可能竞争也可能间接交换
enemies.en: Its principal enemies were rival regional polities and unstable frontier groups; relations with the Maya and Teotihuacan spheres could involve both competition and indirect exchange

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持瓦哈卡地区主导地位、保障交换通道、通过婚姻与礼仪联盟巩固影响力为主
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed to preserve primacy in Oaxaca, secure exchange routes, and reinforce influence through marriage ties and ritual alliances

era.zh: 中美洲古典早期
era.en: Early Classic Mesoamerica

keyInnovations.zh: 山顶城市规划、纪念碑铭刻、历法记录、精细陶器烧制、石构平台建筑和区域道路联络体系
keyInnovations.en: Hilltop urban planning, monument inscription, calendrical recording, fine ceramic firing, stone platform construction, and regional route integration

strengths.zh: 蒙特阿尔班拥有悠久的政治威望、成熟的礼仪中心、稳固的农业基础和覆盖瓦哈卡的地区影响力
strengths.en: Monte Albán possessed long-standing political prestige, a mature ceremonial center, a stable agricultural base, and regional influence across Oaxaca

weaknesses.zh: 权力依赖贵族网络与附属聚落忠诚，政治整合有限，经济货币化程度低，且其区域霸权已较早期有所削弱
weaknesses.en: Power depended on noble networks and the loyalty of dependent settlements, political integration was limited, monetization was low, and its regional hegemony had weakened compared with earlier centuries

outlook.zh: 到220年仍属稳定且有影响力的区域文明，但长期趋势显示中心权威可能继续分散，地方中心将逐渐增强
outlook.en: By 220 CE it remained a stable and influential regional civilization, but long-term trends suggest that central authority would continue to fragment as local centers gradually strengthened

description.zh: 瓦哈卡地区的萨波特克文明仍以蒙特阿尔班为核心维持区域影响。
description.en: In Oaxaca, the Zapotec civilization remained centered on Monte Albán and retained strong regional influence.

governmentForm.zh: 以宗教仪式与地方贵族网络为核心的神权分治政体，各河谷由强势礼仪中心统辖。
governmentForm.en: A theocratic and regionally segmented polity centered on ritual authority and elite networks, with major valleys ruled from powerful ceremonial centers.

socialStructure.zh: 社会高度分层，包括祭司王、武士贵族、行政与工匠精英、农民、渔民、劳役者与俘虏；政治与宗教地位紧密相连。
socialStructure.en: Society was highly stratified, including priest-kings, warrior nobles, administrative and craft elites, farmers, fishers, laborers, and captives; political and religious status were closely linked.

rulingClass.zh: 主持祭祀的王族、武士贵族、神庙管理者与控制灌溉和劳役的地方领主。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of royal ritual lineages, warrior nobles, temple administrators, and regional lords who controlled irrigation and labor.

succession.zh: 继承方式并不完全固定，通常在地方精英家族内部世袭，并以宗教合法性、军事威望与贵族联盟加以确认。
succession.en: Succession was not fully standardized; leadership was usually hereditary within elite regional lineages, confirmed by ritual legitimacy, military prestige, and noble alliances.

structure.zh: 莫切并非统一帝国，而是由北秘鲁多个河谷政体组成；每个核心中心围绕神庙、宫殿与手工业区运作，由祭司王及其贵族随从统治。
structure.en: The Moche were not a single unified empire but a constellation of valley polities on Peru’s north coast; each core center revolved around temples, palatial compounds, and craft districts ruled by a priest-king and noble retainers.

function.zh: 组织献祭、历法祭典、神庙维护与宗教合法性展示。
function.en: Organized sacrifice, calendrical ceremonies, temple maintenance, and displays of religious legitimacy.

function.zh: 监督渠道修筑、农田分配、季节性劳役与粮食征收。
function.en: Oversaw canal construction, field allocation, seasonal labor obligations, and grain collection.

function.zh: 保管玉米、豆类、纺织品、金属制品和手工贡物，并为祭祀和战争调配资源。
function.en: Stored maize, beans, textiles, metal goods, and craft tribute, and allocated supplies for ritual and warfare.

function.zh: 组织战士团体、守卫礼仪中心、押送俘虏并镇压地方反抗。
function.en: Organized warrior groups, guarded ceremonial centers, escorted captives, and suppressed local resistance.

function.zh: 管理金属、陶器、纺织和贝壳作坊，保障精英消费与礼仪需求。
function.en: Managed metal, ceramic, textile, and shell workshops, ensuring elite consumption and ritual production.

function.zh: 维持河谷之间的使者往来、资源调运与政治联络。
function.en: Maintained messenger traffic, resource movement, and political communication between valleys.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以河谷为单位，由附属领主、神庙主管和灌溉监督者共同执行；村社首领负责动员劳役与上缴贡品。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated at the valley level through subordinate lords, temple stewards, and irrigation supervisors; village headmen mobilized labor and delivered tribute.

legalSystem.zh: 莫切没有已知成文法典；秩序依靠习惯法、精英裁断、宗教禁忌和公开惩罚维持，战俘处置与社会控制常具有仪式性。
legalSystem.en: The Moche had no known written law code; order was maintained through customary law, elite adjudication, religious taboo, and public punishment, while treatment of captives and social control often took ritual form.

taxationSystem.zh: 国家收入主要来自劳役征发与实物贡赋，而非货币税。农产品、纺织品、渔获、金属器与手工业品按地位和义务征收。
taxationSystem.en: State income came primarily from labor drafts and in-kind tribute rather than monetary taxes. Agricultural produce, textiles, fish, metal goods, and crafted items were collected according to status and obligation.

religion.zh: 莫切宗教为多神信仰，强调山海、祖灵、丰产与战争祭祀。神庙壁画和陶器反复表现持刃神祇、献血与俘虏仪式，显示统治与神圣权威合一。
religion.en: Moche religion was polytheistic and emphasized mountains, sea, ancestors, fertility, and war ritual. Temple murals and ceramics repeatedly depicted blade-bearing deities, blood offerings, and captive rites, showing the fusion of rulership and sacred authority.

philosophy.zh: 莫切缺乏成文哲学传统，但其世界观建立在宇宙循环、祭祀交换、祖先崇敬与统治者作为人神中介的观念之上。
philosophy.en: The Moche lacked a written philosophical tradition, but their worldview rested on cosmic cycles, ritual reciprocity, ancestor veneration, and the ruler’s role as mediator between humans and divine powers.

writingSystem.zh: 尚无确证的文字体系。政治与宗教信息主要通过图像叙事、建筑布局、服饰标志与口传传统传达。
writingSystem.en: No confirmed writing system is known. Political and religious information was conveyed mainly through pictorial narrative, architectural layout, costume symbolism, and oral tradition.

culturalAchievements.zh: 莫切以写实陶肖像、复杂叙事陶器、彩绘神庙壁画、金银铜合金工艺和大型灌溉工程著称，是安第斯早期最杰出的礼仪与工艺文化之一。
culturalAchievements.en: The Moche were renowned for realistic portrait vessels, complex narrative ceramics, painted temple murals, sophisticated gold-silver-copper metallurgy, and large irrigation works, making them one of the most accomplished early ritual and craft cultures of the Andes.

languageFamily.zh: 具体语言仍有争议，可能与北秘鲁沿海的前克丘亚地方语言有关；行政与宗教主要依赖口语与象征符号而非书写。
languageFamily.en: The exact language is uncertain, likely belonging to local pre-Quechuan languages of Peru’s north coast; administration and religion relied on speech and symbolic media rather than writing.

gdpDescription.zh: 莫切经济建立在高生产力灌溉农业、沿海渔业、专业手工业与精英再分配体系之上。由于缺乏货币和统一市场，其总量估算应理解为折算的实物与劳役价值。
gdpDescription.en: The Moche economy rested on productive irrigation agriculture, coastal fishing, specialized crafts, and elite redistribution. Because there was no coinage and only limited integrated markets, total output should be understood as a converted value of goods and labor.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、渔业、陶器制作、纺织、冶金、贝壳与宝石工艺、建筑劳役。
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, fishing, ceramic production, textiles, metallurgy, shell and gemstone crafts, and construction labor.

tradeGoods.zh: 玉米、豆类、辣椒、棉花、鱼干、纺织品、铜器、金银饰物、精制陶器、羊驼制品、海贝尤其是棘螺壳。
tradeGoods.en: Maize, beans, chili peppers, cotton, dried fish, textiles, copper goods, gold and silver ornaments, fine ceramics, camelid products, and marine shells especially Spondylus.

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在农具、住房、纺织品、储粮与亲属劳动力；精英财富则集中在金属器、装饰品、祭器、俘虏与对水利和土地的控制权。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth consisted mainly of tools, housing, textiles, stored food, and kin labor; elite wealth concentrated in metal objects, ornaments, ritual gear, captives, and control over water and land.

economicSystem.zh: 以神庙与贵族再分配为核心的混合经济。基层以家庭生产和地方交换为主，国家层面依靠贡赋、仓储和礼仪消费整合资源。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy centered on temple and elite redistribution. At the local level it relied on household production and regional exchange, while at the state level resources were integrated through tribute, storage, and ritual consumption.

category.zh: 精英供养与宫廷再分配

treasuryDescription.zh: 莫切“国库”并非铸币储备，而是分散在神庙仓库、精英居所和区域储藏设施中的粮食、纺织品、金属器与礼仪珍品。
treasuryDescription.en: The Moche ‘treasury’ was not a coin reserve but a dispersed stock of food, textiles, metal goods, and ritual valuables held in temple storehouses, elite residences, and regional storage facilities.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重点在于保障水利、祭祀与精英统治的再分配能力。丰年积储，灾年调仓赈济或稳固附属关系，是维系政权的关键。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on maintaining irrigation, ritual obligations, and the redistributive capacity of elite rule. Accumulation in good years and redistribution in difficult years were crucial to preserving authority.

description.zh: 直属祭司王和主要礼仪中心的精锐武士，负责护卫、俘虏押解与重要战役。
description.en: Elite warriors attached to priest-kings and major ceremonial centers, responsible for guard duty, escorting captives, and major engagements.

description.zh: 由各地村社和附属领主征发的主力步兵，装备棍棒、矛、投石器、盾牌和铜制或木制武具。
description.en: The main body of troops levied from communities and subordinate lords in each valley, equipped with clubs, spears, slings, shields, and copper or wooden gear.

description.zh: 用于修筑工事、运输补给、支援远征和危机时增援的后备力量。
description.en: Reserve manpower used for building fieldworks, transporting supplies, supporting expeditions, and reinforcing in emergencies.

commanderInChief.zh: 各地祭司王及其首席武士统帅
commanderInChief.en: Regional priest-kings and their chief warrior commanders

technology.zh: 莫切军事技术以步兵战为核心，使用投石器、矛、战棍、盾牌、铜制头饰与防具。战争兼具夺取俘虏、威慑附属社群和展示礼仪权威的功能。
technology.en: Moche military technology centered on infantry combat using slings, spears, clubs, shields, and copper headdresses and armor. Warfare served to capture prisoners, intimidate subordinate communities, and display ritual authority.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近河谷政体、内部精英竞争、灌溉失修引发的动荡，以及与纳斯卡等南部文化圈的边缘摩擦。
threats.en: The main threats came from neighboring valley polities, internal elite rivalry, instability caused by irrigation failure, and frontier friction with southern cultural spheres such as the Nasca.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

description.zh: 单兵弩与床弩，中国军队核心远程武器
description.en: Infantry crossbow and mounted crossbow, core ranged weapon

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于北秘鲁沿海各灌溉河谷，集中在农耕村落、礼仪中心周边与沿海渔村。环境承载力高度依赖人工水利。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the irrigated valleys of Peru’s north coast, in farming villages, around ceremonial centers, and in coastal fishing settlements. Carrying capacity depended heavily on artificial water control.

name.zh: 月亮神庙—太阳神庙地区
name.en: Huaca de la Luna–Huaca del Sol region

name.zh: 加里纳索河谷礼仪中心

socialClasses.zh: 上层为祭司王、贵族武士和神庙精英；中层包括工匠、管理者、熟练农民和地方首领；下层为普通农民、渔民、劳役者、侍从与战俘。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of priest-kings, noble warriors, and temple elites; the middle included artisans, administrators, skilled cultivators, and local headmen; the lower included common farmers, fishers, laborers, attendants, and war captives.

allies.zh: 莫切并无跨大陆正式盟友，现实中的合作主要是与若干附属或结盟河谷领主维持的礼仪—政治关系。
allies.en: The Moche had no formal intercontinental allies; practical alliances consisted mainly of ritual-political relations with subordinate or cooperative valley lords.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对对象是竞争性的邻近河谷政体、拒绝贡赋的地方社群，以及南方纳斯卡文化圈中的潜在对手。
enemies.en: Their main enemies were rival neighboring valley polities, local communities resisting tribute, and potential opponents within the southern Nasca cultural sphere.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制沿海河谷、保护灌溉网络、争夺劳动力和俘虏、维持长距离奢侈品流通为核心。政治影响力更多依赖礼仪威望和军事威慑，而非稳定的领土官僚统治。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on controlling coastal valleys, protecting irrigation networks, securing labor and captives, and sustaining long-distance luxury exchange. Political influence depended more on ritual prestige and military intimidation than on stable territorial bureaucracy.

era.zh: 安第斯早期中间时期
era.en: Andean Early Intermediate Period

keyInnovations.zh: 大型引水渠道、沙漠河谷灌溉、失蜡与锤揲金属工艺、复杂陶模生产、巨型土坯建筑和高水平壁画装饰构成其技术核心。
keyInnovations.en: Its key innovations included large diversion canals, desert-valley irrigation, lost-wax and hammered metalworking, complex mold-made ceramics, monumental adobe architecture, and advanced mural decoration.

strengths.zh: 莫切的优势在于高效灌溉农业、卓越工艺生产、强大的礼仪整合能力以及能将战争、宗教与再分配结合起来的统治结构。
strengths.en: Moche strengths lay in efficient irrigation agriculture, outstanding craft production, strong ritual integration, and a ruling structure that combined warfare, religion, and redistribution.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点是政治统一度有限、对局部生态和水利条件依赖极强、缺乏文字行政体系，并容易因厄尔尼诺类气候冲击和精英内斗而分裂。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were limited political unity, extreme dependence on local ecology and irrigation, the absence of a writing-based administrative system, and vulnerability to El Niño-type climatic shocks and elite conflict.

outlook.zh: 220年前后的莫切总体仍处于繁荣阶段，礼仪中心与工艺生产持续扩张。但从长期看，环境波动、区域竞争和分治结构将逐步削弱其稳定性。
outlook.en: Around 220 CE the Moche remained broadly prosperous, with ceremonial centers and craft production continuing to expand. In the longer term, however, environmental volatility, regional competition, and fragmented political structure would gradually undermine stability.

description.zh: 莫切诸政体活跃于秘鲁北海岸，以灌溉农业、金工与神权统治著称。
description.en: Moche polities flourished on Peru's north coast, known for irrigation agriculture, metallurgy, and sacral rule.

governmentForm.zh: 由贵族、祭司与军事首领共同主导的神权寡头城邦
governmentForm.en: A theocratic oligarchic city-state led collectively by nobles, priests, and military leaders

socialStructure.zh: 社会呈高度分层结构，顶层为统治贵族与祭司集团，其下为武士、行政人员、工匠、商人和大量农民、劳役者；城市内还居住着来自玛雅地区、瓦哈卡和海湾低地的外来社群。
socialStructure.en: Society was highly stratified, with a ruling nobility and priestly elite at the top, followed by warriors, administrators, artisans, merchants, and large numbers of farmers and laborers; the city also contained immigrant communities from the Maya region, Oaxaca, and the Gulf lowlands.

rulingClass.zh: 掌控宫殿复合体、神庙祭祀与贡赋网络的贵族祭司集团
rulingClass.en: A noble-priestly elite controlling palace compounds, temple ritual, and tribute networks

succession.zh: 具体继承规则不明，较可能是在若干显赫家族或统治团体内部协商更替，而非明确的单一世袭君主制。
succession.en: The exact rules of succession are unknown; power was likely transferred by negotiation within leading families or ruling corporate groups rather than through a clearly defined single hereditary monarchy.

structure.zh: 政权围绕宗教仪式中心、宫殿式行政复合体和掌控街区的精英家族组织展开。国家以城市核心区为政治中枢，通过驻外联系、贸易、威慑和象征权威影响周边地区。
structure.en: Government was organized around ritual centers, palace-like administrative compounds, and elite lineages controlling urban districts. The state used the urban core as its political hub and projected influence into surrounding regions through external ties, trade, intimidation, and symbolic authority.

function.zh: 主持大型宗教仪式、祭祀活动、历法安排与神庙管理，以维护统治合法性。
function.en: Oversaw major religious ceremonies, sacrificial rites, calendrical observance, and temple administration to sustain political legitimacy.

function.zh: 征收周边地区的实物贡赋，管理玉米、豆类、棉织品、黑曜石与其他物资的储存和再分配。
function.en: Collected in-kind tribute from surrounding areas and managed the storage and redistribution of maize, beans, cotton textiles, obsidian, and other goods.

name.zh: 工匠与黑曜石作坊管理

function.zh: 监督黑曜石采集与加工、陶器生产、壁画装饰和高等级工艺品制作。
function.en: Supervised obsidian extraction and working, pottery production, mural decoration, and manufacture of high-status craft goods.

function.zh: 组织武装力量、护卫商队、处理对周边城邦和远方地区的外交、威慑与干预事务。
function.en: Organized armed forces, protected trade caravans, and handled diplomacy, coercion, and intervention toward neighboring polities and distant regions.

name.zh: 街区与住宅复合体管理

function.zh: 管理城市街区、住宅复合体、劳役征发、供水排水和公共秩序。
function.en: Managed urban districts, apartment compounds, labor obligations, water drainage, and public order.

localAdmin.zh: 特奥蒂瓦坎本身按大道和街区划分，由地方精英掌握各住宅复合体与工坊区；外围影响区则通过附属中心、贸易据点和亲附统治者进行间接控制。
localAdmin.en: Teotihuacan itself was divided by its great avenue and neighborhood districts, with local elites overseeing apartment compounds and workshop zones; outer zones of influence were controlled indirectly through subordinate centers, trade enclaves, and allied rulers.

legalSystem.zh: 无成文法典留存，秩序主要依靠宗教权威、习惯规范、精英裁断和社区管理维系；对贡赋、劳役和公共秩序的违背可能受到惩罚性征发、财物没收或社会惩戒。
legalSystem.en: No written law code survives; order was maintained mainly through religious authority, customary norms, elite adjudication, and community administration. Violations involving tribute, labor duties, or public order likely brought punitive labor, confiscation, or social sanctions.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政以实物贡赋和劳役为主，包括玉米、豆类、辣椒、纺织品、陶器、黑曜石制品及公共工程劳作；对受影响地区还可能征收象征性朝贡与战利品。
taxationSystem.en: The fiscal system relied chiefly on in-kind tribute and labor service, including maize, beans, chili, textiles, pottery, obsidian goods, and work on public projects; zones under influence may also have rendered symbolic tribute and war booty.

religion.zh: 多神信仰，以雨水、农业、生育、羽蛇、风暴和山岳神灵崇拜为核心，宗教活动与国家权力紧密结合。大型金字塔、供奉平台和祭祀牺牲在公共生活中占据中心地位。
religion.en: Polytheistic religion centered on deities of rain, agriculture, fertility, the Feathered Serpent, storms, and sacred mountains, with ritual closely integrated into state power. Monumental pyramids, offering platforms, and sacrificial rites stood at the center of public life.

philosophy.zh: 特奥蒂瓦坎未留下类似欧亚文明的哲学文献，其世界观强调宇宙秩序、神圣方位、祖先与祭祀义务，以及城市布局与天体、山岳和神话空间的对应关系。
philosophy.en: Teotihuacan left no philosophical literature comparable to Eurasian traditions, but its worldview emphasized cosmic order, sacred directions, ancestors, ritual obligation, and correspondences between city layout, celestial cycles, mountains, and mythic space.

writingSystem.zh: 尚未发现成熟的完整音节或语音文字体系；存在象形符号、图像记号和礼仪性标识，用于壁画、陶器与公共象征表达。
writingSystem.en: No fully developed phonetic writing system has been identified; the city used glyphic signs, pictorial markers, and ritual symbols on murals, pottery, and public imagery.

culturalAchievements.zh: 城市规划成就突出，拥有美洲古代最宏大的规整轴线城市之一；太阳金字塔、月亮金字塔、羽蛇神庙、标准化公寓式住宅、精美壁画和大规模黑曜石工艺体现了其高度组织能力与审美传统。
culturalAchievements.en: Its outstanding achievements lay in urban planning, producing one of the grandest orthogonal cities of the ancient Americas; the Pyramid of the Sun, Pyramid of the Moon, Temple of the Feathered Serpent, standardized apartment compounds, rich mural painting, and large-scale obsidian craftsmanship demonstrated exceptional organizational capacity and artistic tradition.

languageFamily.zh: 统治阶层所用语言尚不确定，学界常推测与奥托-曼格语系、托托纳克语系或多语言环境有关；城市人口很可能使用多种中部美洲语言。
languageFamily.en: The language of the ruling elite remains uncertain; scholars often suggest links to Oto-Manguean, Totonacan, or a multilingual environment, and the urban population likely used several Mesoamerican languages.

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 作为公元3世纪初中部墨西哥最大的都会，特奥蒂瓦坎经济规模在前哥伦布时期属极高水平。估值只能以农业产出、手工业集中度、远距交换和城市人口规模进行粗略折算，实际货币化程度远低于欧亚帝国。
gdpDescription.en: As the largest metropolis in central Mexico in the early 3rd century CE, Teotihuacan possessed an exceptionally large economy by pre-Columbian standards. This estimate is only a rough conversion based on agricultural output, craft concentration, long-distance exchange, and urban scale; monetization was far lower than in Eurasian empires.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米农业、豆类和苋类种植、黑曜石开采与加工、陶器制造、建筑营造、纺织、羽毛与贝壳装饰品制作、祭祀用品生产及商旅运输。
mainIndustries.en: Maize agriculture, cultivation of beans and amaranth, obsidian extraction and processing, pottery production, construction, textiles, feather and shell ornament production, ritual goods manufacture, and merchant transport.

tradeGoods.zh: 黑曜石刀片、陶器、面具、饰品、纺织品、玉米、盐、可可、羽毛、贝壳、绿石、橡胶、颜料和来自远方的奢侈品。
tradeGoods.en: Obsidian blades, pottery, masks, ornaments, textiles, maize, salt, cacao, feathers, shells, greenstone, rubber, pigments, and exotic prestige goods from distant regions.

householdWealth.zh: 城市普通家庭通常拥有稳定但有限的住房、陶器、衣物和工具；工匠与商人家庭财富高于一般农户，而精英住宅复合体集中掌握进口奢侈品、仪式物资和劳动力。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary urban households usually possessed stable but modest housing, pottery, clothing, and tools; artisan and merchant households were wealthier than common farmers, while elite compounds concentrated imported luxuries, ritual supplies, and labor resources.

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以农业剩余、国家或精英再分配、专业化工坊生产和长途交换共同构成的混合经济。货币不发达，实物交换、贡赋和礼物网络比市场现金交易更重要。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining agricultural surplus, state or elite redistribution, specialized workshop production, and long-distance exchange. Currency was undeveloped; barter, tribute, and gift networks mattered more than cash markets.

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year

source.zh: 附属中心进贡与战利品
source.en: Tribute from subordinate centers and booty

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非以铸币金属储备为核心，而主要表现为仓储粮食、织物、黑曜石半成品、祭祀物资、奢侈品和可调配劳动力。这里以贵金属等值作现代换算。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not primarily a stock of coined metal but consisted mostly of stored grain, textiles, obsidian semifinished goods, ritual supplies, prestige items, and mobilizable labor. Precious-metal values here are modern equivalents.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调通过稳定征收贡赋和劳役支持大型仪式、建筑工程、城市供给和军事威慑，并以再分配维系精英联盟与外部附属关系。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized steady extraction of tribute and labor to support monumental ritual, construction, urban provisioning, and military deterrence, while redistribution sustained elite coalitions and external dependencies.

description.zh: 由精英和受训战士构成，执行城市防卫、威慑和对外远征任务，常与宗教象征和俘虏获取相联系。
description.en: Composed of elites and trained fighters, they handled urban defense, coercion, and external expeditions, often linked to religious symbolism and the taking of captives.

description.zh: 负责神庙、仓储区、主要大道、市场与统治复合体的守卫，也维持城市秩序。
description.en: Guarded temples, storage zones, the main avenue, markets, and ruling compounds, while also maintaining urban order.

description.zh: 在战时由周边附属中心和城市居民中征发，主要承担支援、运输、守备和人数补充。
description.en: Raised in wartime from subordinate centers and city residents, mainly for support, transport, garrison, and manpower reinforcement.

commanderInChief.zh: 统治精英集团与高级武士首领共同统辖
commanderInChief.en: Jointly directed by the ruling elite and senior war leaders

technology.zh: 武器以黑曜石刃武器、标枪、投矛器、矛、棍棒和盾牌为主，无冶铁武备、骑兵或轮战车。军队依赖纪律、近程杀伤力、地形熟悉和仪式化威慑。
technology.en: Weapons centered on obsidian-edged arms, darts, atlatls, spears, clubs, and shields, without iron weapons, cavalry, or wheeled chariots. Military effectiveness relied on discipline, strong close-range lethality, familiarity with terrain, and ritualized intimidation.

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自中部墨西哥和周边高地、海湾低地及玛雅地区的竞争城邦、贸易路线争夺、附属地区反抗以及内部权力斗争，而非跨洋外敌。
threats.en: The main threats came from rival polities in central Mexico and nearby highland, Gulf lowland, and Maya regions, competition over trade routes, resistance in subordinate areas, and internal power struggles rather than overseas enemies.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 水战拍竿，击毁敌船上层结构
description.en: Ship-mounted swinging beam to smash enemy superstructures

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

populationDescription.zh: 约10万人口使特奥蒂瓦坎成为当时世界上最大的城市之一，也是前哥伦布美洲最重要的大都会之一。人口由本地居民与大量外来移民组成，族群与职业高度多样。
populationDescription.en: With about 100,000 inhabitants, Teotihuacan was one of the largest cities in the world at the time and one of the most important metropolises in pre-Columbian America. Its population combined locals with large numbers of immigrants, producing high ethnic and occupational diversity.

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括统治贵族与祭司、军事首领、行政管理者、专门工匠、商人、普通农民与城市劳工，以及一定数量的外来社群和从属人口。
socialClasses.en: Major social strata included ruling nobles and priests, military leaders, administrators, specialized artisans, merchants, ordinary farmers and urban laborers, as well as immigrant communities and dependent populations.

allies.zh: 无固定意义上的现代盟国体系；其主要外部联系包括中部墨西哥附属中心、友好贸易伙伴，以及与部分玛雅城邦保持的精英交流与影响关系。
allies.en: There was no fixed alliance system in the modern sense; its main external ties included subordinate centers in central Mexico, friendly trade partners, and elite exchange or influence relationships with some Maya polities.

enemies.zh: 潜在敌对者包括挑战其贸易网络和象征霸权的中部墨西哥竞争中心，以及不愿服从其影响的周边地区势力。
enemies.en: Potential enemies included competing centers in central Mexico that challenged its trade networks and symbolic hegemony, as well as neighboring powers unwilling to accept its influence.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以宗教威望、贸易渗透、精英馈赠、移民社群布局和必要时的军事干预相结合，追求在不建立广域官僚帝国的情况下扩大影响圈。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy combined religious prestige, trade penetration, elite gifting, placement of migrant communities, and military intervention when necessary, aiming to expand its sphere of influence without building a vast bureaucratic empire.

era.zh: 中部美洲古典期早期
era.en: Early Classic Mesoamerica

keyInnovations.zh: 规整网格化城市设计、公寓式多户住宅、大规模石灰灰泥建筑、精密排水设施、黑曜石标准化生产、壁画工艺和远距交换组织是其代表性技术与制度创新。
keyInnovations.en: Orthogonal urban design, multi-family apartment compounds, large-scale lime-plaster construction, organized drainage works, standardized obsidian production, mural techniques, and systems for long-distance exchange were its key technological and institutional innovations.

strengths.zh: 拥有巨型城市人口、发达的宗教权威、强大的工艺生产能力、优越的黑曜石资源控制和遍及中部美洲的影响网络，是220年时最繁盛的美洲都会之一。
strengths.en: It possessed a massive urban population, powerful religious authority, strong craft production, advantageous control over obsidian resources, and an influence network extending across Mesoamerica, making it one of the most flourishing American metropolises in 220 CE.

weaknesses.zh: 统治结构不透明且可能依赖精英平衡，政治整合较多依靠象征威望与间接控制；经济对贡赋、劳役和地区网络稳定性敏感，也缺乏冶铁、畜力和成熟文字行政工具。
weaknesses.en: Its ruling structure was opaque and likely dependent on elite balance; political integration relied heavily on symbolic prestige and indirect control. The economy was sensitive to tribute flows, labor obligations, and regional network stability, and it lacked ironworking, draft animals, and mature writing-based administration.

outlook.zh: 短期内前景强劲，凭借城市规模、宗教中心地位和外部联系将继续繁荣并扩大影响；但若内部派系失衡、外部贸易受阻或附属地区脱离，其长期稳定性将面临严峻考验。
outlook.en: Its short-term outlook was strong, and its urban scale, ritual centrality, and external connections were likely to sustain further prosperity and influence; however, long-term stability would face severe strain if factional balance broke down, trade was disrupted, or dependent regions drifted away.

description.zh: 特奥蒂瓦坎是中部墨西哥最大的都市与宗教中心，对中美洲影响深远。
description.en: Teotihuacan was the largest metropolis and religious center in central Mexico, exerting wide influence across Mesoamerica.

governmentForm.zh: 以仪式中心和祭司权威为核心的区域性神权体制
governmentForm.en: Regional theocratic system centered on ceremonial authority and priestly control

socialStructure.zh: 社会由祭司贵族、地方首领、工匠、农民、渔民与劳役群体构成，宗教仪式与水利组织将各河谷社群整合在一起。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of priestly elites, local chiefs, artisans, farmers, fishers, and laboring groups, with ritual life and irrigation organization binding together communities across the valleys.

rulingClass.zh: 掌控祭祀、再分配与大型工程的祭司贵族及地方酋长家族
rulingClass.en: Priestly nobles and local chiefly families who controlled ritual, redistribution, and major construction

succession.zh: 并非严格世袭王位制，最高权威多由祭司家族与地方精英协商延续，兼具血缘、威望与宗教资历因素。
succession.en: There was no strictly hereditary kingship; supreme authority likely passed through priestly lineages and local elites by a mix of kinship, prestige, and religious qualification.

structure.zh: 纳斯卡并无成熟成文官僚国家，而是由卡瓦奇这一大型仪式中心统合多个河谷聚落；祭司阶层通过宗教节庆、贡赋征集、劳役动员和灌溉协调行使统治。
structure.en: Nazca did not possess a fully developed written bureaucracy; instead, the great ceremonial center of Cahuachi coordinated multiple valley communities. Priestly authorities ruled through ritual festivals, tribute collection, labor mobilization, and irrigation management.

function.zh: 主持祭典、祖灵与神灵崇拜、祭品分配及农业时序安排
function.en: Oversaw ceremonies, ancestor and deity worship, distribution of offerings, and the agricultural calendar

function.zh: 协调河谷引水、地下水道维护、耕地分配与灾害应对
function.en: Coordinated valley irrigation, maintenance of subterranean aqueducts, field allocation, and response to environmental stress

function.zh: 征收纺织品、玉米、豆类、棉花、鱼干及手工业品，并管理再分配仓储
function.en: Collected textiles, maize, beans, cotton, dried fish, and craft goods, and managed redistributive storage

function.zh: 动员劳役修筑平台、广场、道路、地画与宗教设施
function.en: Mobilized labor to build platforms, plazas, roads, geoglyphs, and religious facilities

function.zh: 联系各河谷首领、处理纠纷、监督贡赋与公共劳役履行
function.en: Liaised with valley chiefs, handled disputes, and supervised tribute and public labor obligations

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托各河谷聚落首领与祭司代理人展开，行政更接近宗教网络和酋邦联盟，而非分层郡县制。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on valley community leaders and priestly agents, functioning more like a ritual network and confederation of chiefdoms than a tiered territorial bureaucracy.

legalSystem.zh: 无法律成文传统可考，社会秩序主要依赖习惯法、宗教禁忌、首领仲裁和共同体协商维持。
legalSystem.en: No written legal code is known; social order was maintained chiefly through customary law, religious taboo, chiefly arbitration, and communal negotiation.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政基础主要为实物贡赋与公共劳役，而非货币税；各社群按季节上缴农产、纺织品、祭品及工程劳力。
taxationSystem.en: The fiscal base was primarily in-kind tribute and corvée labor rather than money taxation; communities rendered seasonal agricultural produce, textiles, offerings, and labor for works projects.

religion.zh: 纳斯卡宗教为多神与祖灵崇拜结合的仪式体系，重视与水、丰产、山地和自然力量相关的神圣存在，祭祀、献供、圣地巡礼和头骨战利品仪式具有重要地位。
religion.en: Nazca religion was a ritual system combining polytheistic belief and ancestor veneration, emphasizing sacred powers linked to water, fertility, highlands, and natural forces. Offerings, pilgrimages to sacred sites, and trophy-head rituals were important features.

philosophy.zh: 其思想核心并非抽象哲学，而是以宇宙秩序、农业周期、祖先联系与祭祀互惠为中心的宗教世界观。
philosophy.en: Its intellectual core was not abstract philosophy but a religious worldview centered on cosmic order, agricultural cycles, ties to ancestors, and ritual reciprocity.

writingSystem.zh: 无已知文字体系，信息传递依赖口述传统、图像符号、陶器纹饰、纺织图案与仪式表演。
writingSystem.en: There was no known writing system; communication of meaning relied on oral tradition, visual symbolism, ceramic motifs, textile designs, and ritual performance.

culturalAchievements.zh: 纳斯卡以大型地画、彩绘陶器、复杂纺织、仪式建筑和地下引水工程著称。其图像艺术具有鲜明宗教象征性，在安第斯早期文化中极具辨识度。
culturalAchievements.en: Nazca was famed for its vast geoglyphs, polychrome ceramics, sophisticated textiles, ceremonial architecture, and subterranean waterworks. Its visual art was highly symbolic and among the most distinctive of early Andean cultures.

languageFamily.zh: 语言未有确证，通常视为安第斯南海岸地方语言，具体语系不明。
languageFamily.en: Its language is unattested; it is generally understood as a local language of the south Andean coast, with no securely known family affiliation.

gdpDescription.zh: 纳斯卡经济以灌溉农业、沿海渔业和高水平手工业为基础，整体规模不如大型欧亚帝国，但在安第斯南海岸属于组织度较高、具有跨谷地整合能力的区域经济。
gdpDescription.en: The Nazca economy rested on irrigated agriculture, coastal fishing, and high-quality craft production. It was far smaller than the great Eurasian empires, but on the south Andean coast it represented a relatively well-organized regional economy with cross-valley integrative capacity.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、棉纺织、彩陶制作、渔业、骆驼科动物产品交换、建筑与宗教工程
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, cotton textile production, polychrome pottery, fishing, exchange in camelid products, and construction and ritual works

tradeGoods.zh: 玉米、豆类、南瓜、辣椒、棉花、纺织品、彩陶、黑曜石、贝壳、鱼干、骆驼毛制品与高地交换品
tradeGoods.en: Maize, beans, squash, chili peppers, cotton, textiles, polychrome ceramics, obsidian, shells, dried fish, camelid-fiber goods, and highland exchange products

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为土地使用权、灌溉条件、储粮、纺织工具、陶器、棉田产出和亲族网络，而非贵金属积累。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly through access to land, irrigation, stored food, weaving tools, ceramics, cotton output, and kin networks rather than precious-metal accumulation.

economicSystem.zh: 以家户生产、地方交换、祭司再分配和劳役动员相结合的区域性再分配经济
economicSystem.en: A regional redistributive economy combining household production, local exchange, priestly redistribution, and mobilized labor

treasuryDescription.zh: 所谓“国库”主要是分散在卡瓦奇及各河谷中心的粮食、纺织品、祭品、工具和可再分配物资储备，而非统一金属货币库藏。
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' consisted mainly of stores of food, textiles, offerings, tools, and redistributable goods dispersed between Cahuachi and valley centers rather than a centralized metallic cash reserve.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持仪式权威、水利设施和跨谷地再分配稳定，通过征调劳役与储备物资来缓冲干旱和局部歉收。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining ritual authority, irrigation works, and stable inter-valley redistribution, using labor levies and stored goods to cushion drought and local crop shortfalls.

description.zh: 驻守卡瓦奇及主要圣地，保护祭司、仓储和朝觐活动，也承担威慑与治安职能。
description.en: Stationed at Cahuachi and major sacred sites, protecting priests, storage facilities, and pilgrimage activity while serving as a deterrent and police force.

description.zh: 由各聚落在冲突或防御时临时动员，装备较轻，主要用于保卫农田、水源和村落。
description.en: Temporarily mobilized from local communities in times of conflict or defense, lightly equipped and used chiefly to protect fields, water sources, and settlements.

description.zh: 依附地方精英的战士群体，在区域竞争、护送贡物和惩戒行动中发挥作用。
description.en: Warrior groups attached to local elites, active in regional rivalry, escorting tribute, and punitive expeditions.

technology.zh: 军事技术以木石兵器、投石器、矛、棍棒、护具和简易防御工事为主，不存在欧亚式重装军队；其优势在于熟悉沙漠河谷环境和快速社区动员。
technology.en: Military technology centered on wooden and stone weapons, slings, spears, clubs, protective gear, and simple fortifications, with nothing like the heavy armies of Eurasia. Its strengths lay in knowledge of desert-valley terrain and rapid communal mobilization.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与邻近安第斯社群的资源竞争、河谷间冲突、对水源与耕地的争夺，以及环境压力引发的社会紧张，而非远程帝国入侵。
threats.en: Primary threats came from competition with neighboring Andean societies, inter-valley conflict, struggles over water and arable land, and social stress caused by environmental pressure rather than invasion by distant empires.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布在秘鲁南海岸若干河谷与沿海地带，聚落密度受灌溉条件严格限制。卡瓦奇更像季节性大型仪式中心，而非全年持续高密度居住的都市。
populationDescription.en: The population was distributed mainly across several valleys and coastal zones of southern Peru, with settlement density tightly constrained by irrigation. Cahuachi was more a large seasonal ceremonial center than a permanently dense year-round city.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为祭司贵族与地方首领，中层为熟练工匠、专业纺织者与社区组织者，下层为多数农民、渔民和承担劳役的普通社群成员；战俘与边缘群体地位最低。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood priestly nobles and local chiefs; beneath them were skilled artisans, specialist weavers, and community organizers; below them were the majority of farmers, fishers, and ordinary laboring members of society. Captives and marginal groups occupied the lowest status.

allies.zh: 无现代意义上的正式国际同盟；主要依靠与安第斯高地和沿海邻近社群的交换关系、通婚网络与宗教联系维持和平。
allies.en: There were no formal international alliances in the modern sense; peace was maintained mainly through exchange relations, marriage networks, and religious ties with neighboring highland and coastal communities.

enemies.zh: 无明确的长期外部宿敌，但与周边安第斯社群在水源、贸易路线和威望竞争上存在周期性冲突。
enemies.en: There was no clearly defined long-term external archenemy, but periodic conflicts existed with surrounding Andean communities over water, trade routes, and prestige.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以区域平衡、宗教吸纳、交换网络扩展和避免破坏灌溉体系的战争为核心，倾向通过仪式权威和再分配而非持续征服实现影响力。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on regional balance, ritual incorporation, expansion of exchange networks, and avoidance of wars that would damage irrigation systems, favoring influence through sacred authority and redistribution rather than sustained conquest.

era.zh: 纳斯卡文化盛期，属安第斯早期中间时期
era.en: Nazca florescence in the Andean Early Intermediate Period

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与知识包括地下水道与引水工程、干旱区灌溉管理、复杂多彩陶烧制、精细纺织、地画规划与大型仪式建筑施工。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies and bodies of knowledge included subterranean aqueducts and water management, arid-zone irrigation, complex polychrome ceramic firing, fine textile production, geoglyph planning, and large ceremonial construction.

strengths.zh: 其优势在于强大的宗教凝聚力、独特且高水平的工艺传统、跨河谷的仪式整合能力，以及在极端干旱环境下发展出的水利适应技术。
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in strong religious cohesion, highly distinctive craft traditions, the ability to integrate multiple valleys through ritual, and water-management adaptations suited to an extremely arid environment.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点是政治结构较松散、缺乏文字和成熟官僚、对脆弱生态与灌溉系统依赖极高，并易受气候波动与区域冲突影响。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were a relatively loose political structure, the absence of writing and a mature bureaucracy, very high dependence on a fragile ecology and irrigation system, and vulnerability to climate fluctuation and regional conflict.

outlook.zh: 220年前后的纳斯卡总体稳定，卡瓦奇仍具重要宗教与政治号召力；但若未来干旱加剧、河谷竞争上升或仪式中心权威衰退，其区域整合能力可能逐步削弱。
outlook.en: Around 220 CE Nazca was broadly stable, and Cahuachi still held major religious and political prestige. Yet if drought intensified, valley competition increased, or the authority of ceremonial centers declined, its regional cohesion could gradually weaken.

description.zh: 纳斯卡文化活跃于秘鲁南海岸，以宗教中心与地画传统闻名。
description.en: The Nazca culture thrived on Peru's south coast, famous for its ceremonial centers and geoglyph tradition.

governmentForm.zh: 分散的部族酋邦联盟与亲族首领统治
governmentForm.en: Decentralized tribal chiefdoms and kin-based leadership

socialStructure.zh: 社会以村落、氏族与亲族网络为基础，若干地区形成较稳定的酋邦；地位通常由战功、年长、祭仪权威、婚姻联盟与对劳作和交换网络的组织能力决定。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around villages, clans, and kinship networks, with some regions forming relatively stable chiefdoms; status was usually shaped by war leadership, age, ritual authority, marriage alliances, and the ability to organize labor and exchange.

rulingClass.zh: 地方酋长家族、战士首领、祭仪专家与有威望的长者
rulingClass.en: Local chiefly families, war leaders, ritual specialists, and prestigious elders

succession.zh: 继承方式因部族而异，多为家族内部推举、长子或近亲继承，并常需获得长者与战士支持。
succession.en: Succession varied by group, often involving selection within a chiefly family, inheritance by a son or close kinsman, and requiring the support of elders and warriors.

structure.zh: 不存在统一国家机构。治理在村落和区域层面进行，由酋长、长者议事会、战士首领和宗教仪式主持者共同维持秩序；大型河流沿线可出现若干村社受单一首领协调的区域酋邦。
structure.en: There was no single state apparatus. Governance operated at village and regional levels, where chiefs, councils of elders, war leaders, and ritual leaders maintained order; along major rivers, regional chiefdoms could emerge in which several settlements were coordinated by one leading chief.

function.zh: 协调婚姻、耕作、渔猎、争端调解与集体劳作
function.en: Coordinated marriage ties, cultivation, fishing and hunting, dispute mediation, and communal labor

function.zh: 组织贡赋、宴饮再分配、接待访客与跨村交换
function.en: Organized tribute, feast redistribution, reception of visitors, and inter-village exchange

function.zh: 主持祭典、治病、占兆、维系祖灵与自然灵相关仪式
function.en: Conducted ceremonies, healing, divination, and rites concerning ancestors and nature spirits

function.zh: 组织防御、报复性袭击、护送贸易与保障河道安全
function.en: Organized defense, retaliatory raids, trade escort, and river-route security

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以村落为核心，部分地区有从属聚落向较大中心定期贡献粮食、工艺品或劳役。行政并非文书化官僚，而是通过亲属义务、酋长威望和仪式性再分配运作。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on villages, and in some areas subordinate settlements periodically contributed food, craft goods, or labor to larger centers. Administration was not a literate bureaucracy but operated through kin obligations, chiefly prestige, and ritual redistribution.

legalSystem.zh: 无成文法。纠纷通常由长者、酋长或相关亲族调解，处理方式包括赔偿、和解、婚姻安排、宴饮修复关系，严重冲突则可能导致迁村或报复行动。
legalSystem.en: There was no written law. Disputes were usually settled by elders, chiefs, or involved kin groups, with compensation, reconciliation, marriage arrangements, and feasting used to restore relations; severe conflict could lead to village relocation or retaliatory action.

taxationSystem.zh: 不存在统一税制。较强酋邦常以贡赋和劳役形式获取资源，如木薯制品、鱼获、羽饰、陶器、独木舟制造与公共工程劳作。
taxationSystem.en: There was no uniform taxation system. Stronger chiefdoms obtained resources through tribute and labor service, including manioc products, fish, featherwork, pottery, canoe building, and work on communal projects.

religion.zh: 泛灵信仰、祖灵崇拜与萨满传统广泛存在；河流、森林、动物、天象和祖先被视为拥有灵性，不同族群各有神话体系与仪式规范。
religion.en: Animist belief, ancestor veneration, and shamanic traditions were widespread; rivers, forests, animals, celestial bodies, and ancestors were understood as spiritually potent, with each people maintaining its own mythic corpus and ritual norms.

philosophy.zh: 思想以口述宇宙观、生态知识、祖训与互惠原则为核心，强调人与森林、河流、祖先及敌友部族之间的平衡关系。
philosophy.en: Thought centered on oral cosmologies, ecological knowledge, ancestral teachings, and principles of reciprocity, emphasizing balanced relations among people, forest, rivers, ancestors, and neighboring friends or enemies.

writingSystem.zh: 无本地书写系统，知识主要通过口述传统、歌谣、仪式、身体装饰图案与工艺传承。
writingSystem.en: There was no indigenous writing system; knowledge was transmitted mainly through oral tradition, song, ritual, body designs, and craft practice.

culturalAchievements.zh: 成就包括高效的热带雨林生计体系、木薯加工技术、独木舟与河道航行、精美羽饰与编织、陶器制作、黑土改良传统，以及跨流域交换网络的维持。
culturalAchievements.en: Achievements included efficient tropical rainforest subsistence systems, manioc-processing technology, canoe building and river navigation, elaborate featherwork and weaving, pottery production, traditions of anthropogenic dark-earth improvement, and the maintenance of inter-basin exchange networks.

languageFamily.zh: 语言极为多样，包括早期的阿拉瓦克、图皮、加勒比及众多其他语系与孤立语言。
languageFamily.en: Languages were highly diverse, including early Arawakan, Tupian, Cariban, and many other families and isolates.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给性农业、渔猎采集和地方交换为主，市场化程度很低。用现代货币折算意义有限，数值仅为便于跨文明比较的粗略银值估计。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on subsistence agriculture, fishing, hunting, gathering, and local exchange, with very little marketization. Conversion into modern monetary terms is highly limited; the figure is only a rough silver-value estimate for cross-civilizational comparison.

mainIndustries.zh: 刀耕火种农业、木薯加工、渔业、狩猎、采集、陶器、编织、独木舟制造、羽饰工艺与木石骨器制作
mainIndustries.en: Slash-and-burn agriculture, manioc processing, fishing, hunting, gathering, pottery, weaving, canoe construction, featherwork, and wood-stone-bone tool production

tradeGoods.zh: 盐、羽毛、陶器、石斧、木制品、树脂、染料、药用植物、干鱼、独木舟与装饰品在区域网络中流通。
tradeGoods.en: Salt, feathers, pottery, stone axes, wooden goods, resins, dyes, medicinal plants, dried fish, canoes, and ornaments circulated through regional networks.

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为耕地使用权、木薯园、舟船、工具、陶器、装饰品、亲族关系与可动员劳力，而非贵金属储藏。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in access to fields, manioc gardens, canoes, tools, pottery, ornaments, kin ties, and mobilizable labor rather than precious-metal hoards.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产、村社互助、酋长再分配和区域性礼物交换为主的部族经济
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy based on household production, village mutual aid, chiefly redistribution, and regional gift exchange

category.zh: 独木舟、住房与公共工程

treasuryDescription.zh: 不存在集中统一国库。所谓“财政储备”主要是各地酋长掌握的余粮、陶器、舟船、工具、装饰品与可动员劳力的折算总值。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no centralized state treasury. This 'treasury' is an estimated combined value of surplus food, pottery, canoes, tools, ornaments, and mobilizable labor held by local chiefs.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政实践重在通过宴饮、馈赠和再分配巩固威望，而非积累货币。稳定时期偏向维持联盟与公共劳作，危机时则优先保障迁徙、防御和粮食储备。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on strengthening prestige through feasting, gifting, and redistribution rather than accumulating money. In stable times it emphasized alliance maintenance and communal works; in crisis it prioritized movement, defense, and food reserves.

description.zh: 由成年男性战士组成，平时从事生产，战时集结进行村寨防御、袭击与护卫。
description.en: Composed of adult male warriors who worked in normal times and mustered for village defense, raiding, and escort duties.

description.zh: 依托独木舟在支流与大河机动，擅长伏击、运输和快速突袭。
description.en: Using canoes on tributaries and major rivers, they specialized in ambush, transport, and rapid strikes.

description.zh: 较强酋长维持的小规模常备随从，负责首领安全、传令与象征性军事威慑。
description.en: Small permanent followings maintained by stronger chiefs for personal security, message carrying, and symbolic military deterrence.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一总司令，由各地酋长与战士首领分别指挥
commanderInChief.en: No single commander-in-chief; each region was led by its own chiefs and war leaders

technology.zh: 武器以弓箭、矛、棍棒、木盾和吹箭为主，配合毒素、地形熟悉度与河道机动；缺乏金属甲胄和大型攻城器械。
technology.en: Weapons centered on bows, spears, clubs, wooden shields, and blowguns, combined with poisons, terrain knowledge, and river mobility; metal armor and large siege equipment were absent.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部族竞争、资源争夺、报复性袭击、俘虏掠夺以及局部环境波动，而非大规模外来帝国入侵。
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring tribal rivalry, competition over resources, retaliatory raids, captive-taking, and localized environmental fluctuation rather than invasion by large external empires.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

populationDescription.zh: 人口广泛分布于亚马孙河及其支流流域、森林边缘和部分较肥沃高地河岸地带，密度整体较低，但沿大河与资源丰富区可形成较大的聚落带。
populationDescription.en: The population was widely distributed across the Amazon basin and its tributaries, forest margins, and some more fertile riverbank uplands; overall density was low, though larger settlement belts could form along major rivers and resource-rich zones.

name.zh: 中亚马孙河岸中心村落群

socialClasses.zh: 社会分层相对温和，但并非完全平等；常见层次包括酋长家族、长者与祭仪专家、战士、普通农渔家庭，以及在部分地区的俘虏或依附者。
socialClasses.en: Social stratification was moderate rather than fully egalitarian; common layers included chiefly families, elders and ritual specialists, warriors, ordinary farming-fishing households, and in some areas captives or dependents.

allies.zh: 无统一对外盟友体系，主要是邻近部族间通过婚姻、馈赠、联合防御和交换形成的临时联盟。
allies.en: There was no unified foreign alliance system; relations consisted mainly of temporary alliances among neighboring peoples formed through marriage, gifting, joint defense, and exchange.

enemies.zh: 敌对关系多为地方性，源于旧怨、俘虏、贸易竞争与领地争端；不存在与欧亚帝国直接接触形成的战略敌人。
enemies.en: Hostilities were mostly local, arising from vendettas, captive-taking, trade rivalry, and territorial disputes; there were no strategic enemies created by direct contact with Eurasian empires.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以维持河道交换、婚姻联系、报复平衡与地方自主为主。更强势的酋长力图吸纳周边聚落进入其礼物与贡赋网络，但统合程度有限。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations focused on sustaining riverine exchange, marriage connections, retaliatory balance, and local autonomy. Stronger chiefs sought to draw surrounding settlements into their gift and tribute networks, but integration remained limited.

era.zh: 亚马孙流域前哥伦布时期区域酋邦发展阶段
era.en: Pre-Columbian Amazonian regional chiefdom development stage

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括木薯脱毒加工、森林资源精细利用、独木舟航行、陶器烧制、编织与渔具制作、土壤改良和以河流为骨干的通信交换网络。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included detoxification and processing of manioc, fine-grained use of forest resources, canoe navigation, pottery firing, weaving and fishing gear, soil improvement, and river-based communication and exchange networks.

strengths.zh: 优势在于高度适应热带森林与河流环境、食物来源多样、社区韧性强、流动性高、交换网络广，并能通过亲族与礼物关系快速重建合作。
strengths.en: Strengths included strong adaptation to tropical forest and river environments, diversified food sources, resilient communities, high mobility, broad exchange networks, and the ability to rebuild cooperation quickly through kinship and gift relations.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点是政治分散、缺乏统一防御和文书管理、技术标准不一、难以长期集中大规模劳力，且地方冲突容易破坏稳定交换网络。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses were political fragmentation, the lack of unified defense and written administration, uneven technological standards, difficulty concentrating large-scale labor for long periods, and the ease with which local conflict could disrupt exchange networks.

outlook.zh: 公元220年前后总体稳定，许多地区将继续维持酋邦化与区域网络扩张趋势，但发展路径仍受河流生态、人口迁移、地方战争与气候波动深刻影响。
outlook.en: Around 220 CE the overall situation was stable, and many regions would likely continue along paths of chiefdom formation and regional network expansion, though future development remained strongly shaped by river ecology, migration, local warfare, and climatic fluctuation.

description.zh: 南美内陆广布农业与河流部族社会，形成众多地方性酋邦与交换网络。
description.en: Across inland South America, agricultural and riverine societies formed many local chiefdoms and exchange networks.

governmentForm.zh: 以氏族与村社为基础的分散酋邦体制，各地由亲族网络、长老会议与酋长共同治理。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized chiefdom system based on clans and village communities, with governance shared by kinship networks, councils of elders, and chiefs.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以家族、氏族、村落与地方酋邦为核心，农业家庭构成基层，长老、富户、铁匠与祭司具有较高威望，部分地区吸纳并融合较早的采猎居民。
socialStructure.en: Society centered on households, clans, villages, and local chiefdoms. Farming families formed the base, while elders, wealthy household heads, smiths, and ritual specialists held higher prestige; in some regions earlier foraging populations were absorbed and integrated.

rulingClass.zh: 地方酋长、氏族长老、祭祀领袖及控制牲畜、土地分配与铁器生产的家族。
rulingClass.en: Local chiefs, clan elders, ritual leaders, and families that controlled livestock, land allocation, and iron production.

succession.zh: 多依父系氏族或地方习惯继承，通常由酋长之子、兄弟或同宗近亲继位，并需获得长老支持。
succession.en: Succession generally followed patrilineal clan custom, with a chief succeeded by his son, brother, or close agnatic kinsman, subject to the approval of elders.

structure.zh: 不存在统一国家机器，治理层级通常为家户—村落—氏族—酋邦。重大事务由酋长主持，长老会议、宗族领袖与仪式人物参与决策。
structure.en: There was no unified state apparatus; governance usually operated through the levels of household, village, clan, and chiefdom. Major matters were led by chiefs, with councils of elders, lineage heads, and ritual figures participating in decisions.

function.zh: 调解纠纷、确认继承、主持土地与婚姻安排，并就战争与迁徙提出意见。
function.en: Mediated disputes, confirmed succession, oversaw land and marriage arrangements, and advised on war and migration.

function.zh: 传达命令、征集贡赋与劳役、组织宴会、协调邻近村社关系。
function.en: Carried orders, collected tribute and labor obligations, organized feasts, and coordinated relations with nearby communities.

function.zh: 主持祖灵祭祀、祈雨、治病与占卜，在政治合法性中具有重要作用。
function.en: Conducted ancestor rites, rainmaking, healing, and divination, playing an important role in political legitimacy.

function.zh: 管理耕地分配、储粮、地方治安与集体劳动。
function.en: Managed field allocation, grain storage, local order, and communal labor.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依村长、氏族长与酋长分层运作，边缘区域控制力较弱，权威更多建立在亲属关系、馈赠与个人威望之上。
localAdmin.en: Local administration functioned through village heads, clan leaders, and chiefs; control was weaker in frontier areas, and authority rested more on kinship, gift exchange, and personal prestige.

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法为主，围绕婚姻、牲畜、土地使用、血亲义务与赔偿展开；纠纷多以调解、赔付牲畜或公开和解解决，严重暴力冲突可引发复仇或驱逐。
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily customary, centered on marriage, livestock, land use, kin obligations, and compensation; disputes were usually settled through mediation, livestock payments, or public reconciliation, while severe violence could lead to feud or expulsion.

taxationSystem.zh: 无标准化货币税制，酋长主要通过贡赋、宴飨馈赠、婚姻交换、战利品分配与季节性劳役获取资源。
taxationSystem.en: There was no standardized monetary tax system; chiefs drew resources mainly through tribute, feast-gifts, marriage exchange, distribution of war booty, and seasonal labor obligations.

religion.zh: 以祖灵崇拜、自然灵与地方神圣力量信仰为主，祭祀、祈雨、治病与占卜深度嵌入社会生活。
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor veneration, nature spirits, and localized sacred forces, with ritual, rainmaking, healing, and divination deeply embedded in social life.

philosophy.zh: 无成体系文字哲学传统，但重视祖先规范、长幼秩序、互惠义务、口传智慧与共同体和解。
philosophy.en: There was no formal literate philosophical tradition, but strong emphasis was placed on ancestral norms, age hierarchy, reciprocal obligation, oral wisdom, and communal reconciliation.

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字，知识、谱系、法律习惯、迁徙记忆与史事主要依靠口述传承、歌谣与仪式保存。
writingSystem.en: There was no indigenous writing system; knowledge, genealogies, customary law, migration memory, and historical events were preserved mainly through oral transmission, song, and ritual.

culturalAchievements.zh: 在陶器制作、铁冶炼、农业扩张、口头叙事、音乐舞蹈、木器编织和村落组织方面成就显著，并推动班图语支在东非内陆与湖区传播。
culturalAchievements.en: Notable achievements included pottery making, iron smelting, agricultural expansion, oral narrative traditions, music and dance, woodworking and weaving, and the spread of Bantu languages through the East African interior and lake regions.

languageFamily.zh: 尼日尔-刚果语系班图语支，地区间方言差异明显，并与当地非班图族群语言长期接触。
languageFamily.en: Niger-Congo, specifically Bantu languages, with marked regional dialect variation and long-term contact with non-Bantu languages of local populations.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业为主，辅以畜牧、狩猎、采集、冶铁和短途交换。缺乏大规模城市市场与国家征收体系，人均产出较低但总体可维持稳定扩张。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based primarily on subsistence agriculture, supplemented by herding, hunting, gathering, ironworking, and short-distance exchange. The absence of large urban markets and state extraction kept per capita output low, though overall production was sufficient for stable expansion.

mainIndustries.zh: 粟类与杂粮农业、豆类种植、香蕉与芋类等园圃作物（部分地区逐步普及）、畜牧、铁冶炼、陶器、木工、编织与地方交换。
mainIndustries.en: Millet and mixed grain farming, pulse cultivation, banana and root-crop horticulture in some areas, livestock keeping, iron smelting, pottery, woodworking, weaving, and local exchange.

tradeGoods.zh: 铁器、陶器、盐、牲畜、谷物、珠饰、兽皮、蜂蜜、木材与少量区域性奢侈品。
tradeGoods.en: Iron tools, pottery, salt, livestock, grain, beads, hides, honey, timber, and small quantities of regional prestige goods.

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在耕地使用权、粮食储备、铁器、牲畜数量、婚姻关系网络与可动员亲属劳力上。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in access to cultivated land, grain stores, iron tools, livestock holdings, marriage alliances, and the labor of kin.

unit.zh: 银币第纳尔等值/劳动者/年

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产、亲属互助、酋长再分配和地方互市为核心的部族经济，市场交换存在但规模有限。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy centered on household production, kin-based mutual support, chiefly redistribution, and local barter markets, with exchange present but limited in scale.

category.zh: 酋长家户与宴飨再分配

category.en: Messengers, marriage alliances, and gift exchange

treasuryDescription.zh: 不存在集中统一国库，财富以牲畜、粮食、铁器、珠饰与礼物形式分散储存于各酋长和重要家族手中。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no centralized treasury; wealth was stored in decentralized form as livestock, grain, iron tools, beads, and gift goods in the hands of chiefs and major families.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政偏重维持酋长威望、应对饥荒和保障武装追随者，依靠再分配而非固定税收，整体审慎且适应季节波动。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on maintaining chiefly prestige, coping with famine, and supporting armed retainers, relying on redistribution rather than fixed taxation; it was generally cautious and adapted to seasonal fluctuation.

description.zh: 由酋长直属随从和精壮青年组成，承担护卫、突袭与威慑任务，装备长矛、盾牌和部分铁制刀具。
description.en: Composed of the chief's direct followers and selected young warriors, they served in guarding, raiding, and coercive roles, armed with spears, shields, and some iron blades.

description.zh: 战时由各村动员，以防御、报复性袭击和护卫牲畜为主，作战依赖地形熟悉与集体冲锋。
description.en: Mobilized from villages in wartime, they focused on defense, retaliatory raids, and livestock protection, relying on local terrain knowledge and collective charges.

description.zh: 活跃于森林、草原与湖区边缘，兼具狩猎与警戒功能，适合侦察、伏击和追踪。
description.en: Active on forest, grassland, and lakefront frontiers, these groups combined hunting and security roles and were suited to scouting, ambush, and tracking.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一统帅，由各地酋长分别指挥
commanderInChief.en: No single commander; each chief led his own forces

technology.zh: 以铁制矛头、刀具、木盾、弓箭和棍棒为主，无攻城机械与重甲，作战方式偏重小规模冲突、突袭与地形机动。
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron spearheads, blades, wooden shields, bows, and clubs, with no siege engines or heavy armor; warfare emphasized small-scale conflict, raiding, and terrain mobility.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近酋邦之间的土地、牲畜与水源争夺，以及边疆迁徙摩擦；尚未面临大型帝国直接军事压力。
threats.en: The main threats came from competition with neighboring chiefdoms over land, livestock, and water, as well as friction along migration frontiers; they did not yet face direct military pressure from large empires.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于东非内陆高地、大湖地区周边、河谷与稀树草原村落，整体呈分散聚居状态，并处于持续扩张与融合过程中。
populationDescription.en: The population was spread across the East African interior highlands, the margins of the Great Lakes, river valleys, and savanna villages, generally in dispersed settlements and still undergoing expansion and assimilation.

socialClasses.zh: 社会分层较为温和，包含酋长与其家族、氏族长老、祭司与铁匠、普通农牧家庭、附庸与新近并入人口；身份差异存在，但尚未形成高度制度化等级国家。
socialClasses.en: Social stratification was moderate, including chiefs and their families, clan elders, ritual specialists and smiths, ordinary farming and herding households, dependents, and recently incorporated populations; status differences existed, but no highly institutionalized class state had formed.

allies.zh: 无统一盟友体系，各酋邦主要通过婚盟、馈赠与共同防御形成临时同盟；与阿克苏姆、梅罗埃及沿海或尼罗河世界仅有极间接联系。
allies.en: There was no unified alliance system; chiefdoms formed temporary coalitions through marriage, gifts, and common defense, with only very indirect links to Aksum, Meroitic, coastal, or Nile-world polities.

enemies.zh: 无固定总体敌国，主要对手是邻近竞争性酋邦及抵制扩张的地方群体。
enemies.en: There were no fixed overarching enemy states; principal adversaries were neighboring rival chiefdoms and local groups resisting expansion.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以迁徙扩张、土地开垦、婚姻结盟、地方交换与有限冲突并行为特征，重视吸纳人口而非远程征服。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations were characterized by migration, land clearance, marriage alliance, local exchange, and limited conflict, with greater emphasis on absorbing populations than on long-range conquest.

era.zh: 东非铁器时代早期班图扩张阶段
era.en: Early Iron Age East African Bantu expansion phase

keyInnovations.zh: 掌握并传播铁冶炼与铁器制作技术，改进森林开垦和农业定居能力；陶器风格、储粮设施、牲畜管理和区域性作物组合不断发展。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included the use and spread of iron smelting and iron tool production, improving forest clearance and settled agriculture; pottery styles, storage methods, livestock management, and regional crop combinations were also developing.

strengths.zh: 人口增长稳健，农业与冶铁结合促进扩张；社会组织灵活，能够通过亲属网络快速迁徙、吸纳人口并在新环境中建立村社。
strengths.en: Population growth was steady, and the combination of farming and ironworking supported expansion; social organization was flexible, enabling rapid migration through kin networks, incorporation of new populations, and settlement in new environments.

weaknesses.zh: 政治高度分散，缺乏统一征税、常备军与文字行政；面对气候波动、疫病、地方冲突或长距离贸易竞争时协调能力有限。
weaknesses.en: Politics were highly decentralized, lacking unified taxation, a permanent army, and written administration; coordination remained limited when facing climatic fluctuation, disease, local conflict, or competition over long-distance trade.

outlook.zh: 至220年前后，东非班图诸社会总体稳定且继续扩展，未来数世纪有望在湖区和高地形成更复杂的酋邦与区域文化网络，但短期内仍将维持分散的部族政治格局。
outlook.en: Around 220 CE, the East African Bantu societies were broadly stable and continuing to expand. Over the coming centuries they were likely to develop more complex chiefdoms and regional cultural networks in the lake and highland zones, though in the near term they would remain politically fragmented.

description.zh: 东非内陆与湖区分布着不断扩展的班图农业社会与地方酋邦。
description.en: The East African interior and lake regions were occupied by expanding Bantu agricultural societies and local chiefdoms.

governmentForm.zh: 以氏族、村社与部族联盟为基础的分散酋邦与部落治理
governmentForm.en: Decentralized chieftaincies and tribal governance based on clans, village communities, and tribal confederations

socialStructure.zh: 社会以家族、宗族与年龄等级组织为核心，村落长老、祭司、铁匠与战士在各地拥有不同程度威望；多数人口为农民、牧民、渔民与手工业者。
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on households, lineages, and age-grade organization, with village elders, ritual specialists, smiths, and warriors holding varying prestige; most people were farmers, pastoralists, fishers, and craftspeople.

rulingClass.zh: 地方酋长家族、长老议事集团、祭祀权威与控制铁器和牲畜资源的显贵
rulingClass.en: Local chiefly families, councils of elders, ritual authorities, and elites controlling iron tools and livestock resources

succession.zh: 多依氏族传统、长幼秩序与长老认可而定，常见父系继承，也有部分地区重视母系亲属关系与家族协商
succession.en: Succession generally followed clan custom, seniority, and approval by elders, often patrilineal, though some areas gave weight to matrilineal ties and family negotiation

structure.zh: 不存在统一国家官僚体系，政治权力分布于村落、氏族与区域性酋长之中；重要事务由酋长、长老会、祭司及武装追随者共同处理。
structure.en: There was no unified state bureaucracy; political authority was distributed among villages, clans, and regional chiefs, with major affairs handled jointly by chiefs, councils of elders, ritual specialists, and armed retainers.

function.zh: 裁决土地、婚姻、血仇、继承与村际争端，并监督习俗执行
function.en: Adjudicated land, marriage, feud, inheritance, and inter-village disputes and oversaw the enforcement of custom

function.zh: 征集贡赋、组织劳役、传达命令并维持地方权威
function.en: Gathered tribute, organized labor obligations, conveyed orders, and maintained local authority

function.zh: 主持祭仪、誓约、祖灵崇拜与神判性质的纠纷仲裁
function.en: Conducted rituals, oaths, ancestor veneration, and ordeals or sacred arbitration in disputes

function.zh: 管理公共粮仓、季节性交换场所、物资储备与来往商队接待
function.en: Managed communal granaries, seasonal exchange sites, stored goods, and received visiting traders

localAdmin.zh: 基层治理主要依托村长、家族首领与长老会。较大聚落如杰内-杰诺周边形成更稳定的区域协调机制，处理灌溉、粮仓、防御和市场事务。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied chiefly on village heads, lineage leaders, and councils of elders. Larger settlements such as those around Jenne-Jeno developed more stable regional coordination mechanisms to handle water management, granaries, defense, and market affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法为主，强调赔偿、调解、血亲责任与公开誓言。严重案件可能诉诸神判、驱逐或报复性惩罚，但通常以恢复群体秩序为先。
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily customary, emphasizing compensation, mediation, kin responsibility, and public oaths. Serious cases could involve ordeals, exile, or retaliatory punishment, but restoring communal order was usually the priority.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有统一税制，主要通过谷物、牲畜、鱼干、铁器、布匹和劳役形式向酋长与公共仓储缴纳贡赋；集市交易和过境交换也可能收取象征性抽成。
taxationSystem.en: There was no uniform tax system; tribute to chiefs and communal storage was paid mainly in grain, livestock, dried fish, iron goods, cloth, and labor service, while markets and transit exchange could also incur symbolic levies.

religion.zh: 以祖灵崇拜、自然灵信仰与地方神祇祭祀为主，重视土地、水域、铁匠火焰与氏族守护灵；尚未形成跨区域统一宗教体制。
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor veneration, nature spirits, and local deities, with special reverence for land, waters, smithing fire, and clan guardian spirits; no unified supra-regional religious system existed.

philosophy.zh: 思想传统主要体现在口述智慧、长者箴言、亲族义务、互惠伦理与人与祖先、自然之间的平衡观念。
philosophy.en: Intellectual traditions were expressed chiefly through oral wisdom, elders’ sayings, kin obligations, reciprocal ethics, and ideas of balance between people, ancestors, and the natural world.

writingSystem.zh: 绝大多数社会尚无本土文字行政传统，知识依赖口述记忆、歌谣、仪式语言与工艺传承保存。
writingSystem.en: Most societies lacked an indigenous written administrative tradition; knowledge was preserved through oral memory, songs, ritual speech, and craft transmission.

culturalAchievements.zh: 尼日尔内三角洲与萨赫勒地带已形成稳定聚落群、铁器冶炼传统、陶器生产和区域交换网络；杰内-杰诺一类早期城市化中心体现出复杂社会组织与手工业分工。
