culturalAchievements.en: The Inland Niger Delta and Sahel had already developed stable settlement clusters, iron-smelting traditions, pottery production, and regional exchange networks; early urbanizing centers such as Jenne-Jeno reflected complex social organization and craft specialization.

languageFamily.zh: 主要属于尼日尔-刚果语系、尼罗-撒哈拉语系及阿非罗-亚细亚语系中的部分萨赫勒语言群
languageFamily.en: Primarily belonged to Niger-Congo, Nilo-Saharan, and some Sahelian branches of the Afroasiatic language family

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业为基础，辅以牧业、渔业、采集、铁器生产与短中程区域交换。总体财富水平不高，但粮食生产和手工业在若干核心地带较为稳固。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on subsistence agriculture, supplemented by herding, fishing, gathering, iron production, and short- to medium-distance regional exchange. Overall wealth levels were modest, but food production and craft activity were relatively stable in several core zones.

mainIndustries.zh: 粟、黍与高粱种植，畜牧，渔业，铁器冶炼与锻造，陶器，纺织，制革，采盐与区域贸易
mainIndustries.en: Cultivation of millet, sorghum, and related grains, pastoralism, fishing, iron smelting and smithing, pottery, weaving, leatherworking, salt extraction, and regional trade

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、鱼干、盐、铁器、铜饰品、珠饰、皮革、布匹、木材、坚果与地方奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, dried fish, salt, iron tools, copper ornaments, beads, leather, cloth, timber, nuts, and local prestige goods

unitName.en: bundles of grain and goods equivalent

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要以耕地使用权、粮仓储备、牲畜数量、金属工具、陶器与亲族网络衡量；少数首领家庭拥有更大粮仓、更多牲畜与外来饰物。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured chiefly in access to farmland, granary stores, livestock, metal tools, pottery, and kin networks; a minority of chiefly households possessed larger granaries, more animals, and imported ornaments.

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以村社农业和氏族互助为基础，结合贡赋、再分配、集市交换与有限长距离贸易的混合经济
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on village agriculture and clan mutual aid, combined with tribute, redistribution, market exchange, and limited long-distance trade

source.en: Corvée labor and communal storage equivalent

category.en: Chiefly retainers and local armed forces

treasuryDescription.zh: 所谓“国库”主要表现为分散储存的谷物、牲畜、金属工具、礼物与祭祀用品，而非集中铸币储备。较大聚落可维持季节性余粮与首领财物仓储。
treasuryDescription.en: The ‘treasury’ consisted mainly of dispersed stores of grain, livestock, metal tools, gift goods, and ritual items rather than centralized coin reserves. Larger settlements could maintain seasonal grain surpluses and chiefly storehouses.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持粮仓安全、支持首领威望、应对旱灾和巩固村际联盟，通常避免过重征敛以防人口流散。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored maintaining granary security, supporting chiefly prestige, coping with drought, and reinforcing inter-village alliances, generally avoiding excessive exactions that might drive people away.

unit.en: bundles of grain and goods equivalent

description.zh: 由酋长直属战士和随从组成，负责护卫聚落、执行征收与在冲突中担任核心兵力。
description.en: Composed of warriors and retainers directly attached to chiefs, serving as settlement guards, tribute enforcers, and the core force in conflict.

description.zh: 战时由农民、牧民和渔民临时征集，装备以矛、弓、木盾和铁制刀具为主。
description.en: Raised in wartime from farmers, pastoralists, and fishers, armed mainly with spears, bows, wooden shields, and iron blades.

description.zh: 活动于萨赫勒较干燥地带的机动武装，依赖畜群、熟悉长距离行进，适合袭扰和护送。
description.en: Mobile armed groups active in the drier Sahelian zones, relying on herds and familiarity with long-distance movement, suited to raiding and escort duties.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一总司令，由各地酋长分别统兵
commanderInChief.en: No single commander-in-chief; forces were led separately by local chiefs

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁制矛头、刀具、箭镞、木盾与简易护具为主，缺乏统一攻城器械和常备重骑兵体系。
technology.en: Military technology relied on iron spearheads, blades, arrowheads, wooden shields, and simple protective gear, without standardized siege equipment or a permanent heavy cavalry system.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自村社之间的资源冲突、牲畜劫掠、旱灾导致的人口迁徙压力，以及萨赫勒边缘地带的局部武装竞争。
threats.en: The main threats came from resource conflicts between communities, livestock raiding, migration pressures caused by drought, and localized armed competition on the Sahelian frontier.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 单兵弩与床弩，中国军队核心远程武器
description.en: Infantry crossbow and mounted crossbow, core ranged weapon

description.zh: 复合弓或长弓，远射主力
description.en: Composite or longbow, primary ranged weapon

populationDescription.zh: 人口广泛分布在西非萨赫勒、尼日尔河流域与森林边缘，多数居于村落和小型聚落，少数集中于杰内-杰诺等较大交换中心。
populationDescription.en: The population was widely distributed across the West African Sahel, the Niger basin, and forest margins, with most living in villages and small settlements and a minority concentrated in larger exchange centers such as Jenne-Jeno.

name.zh: 尼日尔内三角洲聚落群
达尔-廷加迪斯地区聚落

socialClasses.zh: 社会分层相对温和，但存在酋长家族、长老、祭司、铁匠、自由农牧民与依附者之间的差异；某些地区可能已出现地位较低的被俘者或奴役人口。
socialClasses.en: Social stratification was comparatively moderate, but differences existed among chiefly families, elders, ritual specialists, smiths, free farmers and herders, and dependents; in some regions, lower-status captives or enslaved persons may already have existed.

allies.zh: 无统一对外盟友体系，通常以邻近部族、婚姻联盟和区域交换伙伴构成松散合作网络；与部分撒哈拉南缘社群保持互通。
allies.en: There was no unified foreign alliance system; cooperation usually took the form of neighboring tribes, marriage alliances, and regional exchange partners, including contact with some communities on the southern Saharan fringe.

enemies.zh: 无持续性的统一外敌，敌对关系多为地方性，主要涉及争夺水源、牧场、耕地和贸易节点的邻近群体。
enemies.en: There was no permanent single foreign enemy; hostilities were mostly local, involving neighboring groups competing over water, pasture, farmland, and trade nodes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以维持贸易通道、避免长期流血冲突、通过婚姻和礼物交换稳固和平为主；较强势酋长会尝试控制区域市场与渡口。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations focused on maintaining trade routes, avoiding prolonged bloodshed, and securing peace through marriage and gift exchange; stronger chiefs sought to control regional markets and river crossings.

era.zh: 西非早期铁器时代
era.en: West African Early Iron Age

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的铁器冶炼与锻造、适应萨赫勒环境的谷物农业、渔业技术、陶器工艺、粮仓储藏体系以及多中心聚落网络的发展
keyInnovations.en: Mature iron smelting and forging, grain agriculture adapted to Sahelian conditions, fishing techniques, pottery craft, granary storage systems, and the growth of multicentered settlement networks

strengths.zh: 农业、渔业和牧业互补，铁器技术提升生产力，社会组织灵活，区域交换网络稳固，较大聚落具备抵御局部危机的能力。
strengths.en: Agriculture, fishing, and herding complemented one another; iron technology improved productivity; social organization was flexible; regional exchange networks were resilient; and larger settlements could better withstand localized crises.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏统一政权和书面行政，跨区域协调能力有限，易受旱灾、疫病、迁徙冲击和局部冲突影响，财政与军事动员都较分散。
weaknesses.en: The lack of a unified state and written administration limited cross-regional coordination, while drought, disease, migration shocks, and local conflict could be disruptive; fiscal and military mobilization remained highly decentralized.

outlook.zh: 到220年，这些社会总体稳定，并在尼日尔河与萨赫勒地带继续朝更复杂的聚落组织和贸易整合发展；若环境条件允许，未来数世纪有望孕育更强的区域首领权与早期国家形态。
outlook.en: By 220 CE these societies were broadly stable and continued to develop more complex settlement organization and trade integration in the Niger and Sahel zones; if environmental conditions remained favorable, the coming centuries could see stronger regional chieftaincy and early state formation.

description.zh: 西非萨赫勒与森林边缘存在众多农业聚落、铁器社会与区域交换网络。
description.en: Across the West African Sahel and forest margins existed many farming settlements, ironworking societies, and regional exchange networks.

governmentForm.zh: 以部族长老与酋领会议为核心的松散部落联盟，没有稳定统一王朝。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal confederation centered on elders and chieftain councils, without a stable unified dynasty.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、村落与山海分区部族为基础，亲属关系是组织生产、婚姻与复仇的重要纽带。普通成员从事渔猎、刀耕火种与采集，战时由各部抽调青壮年组成武装。部分祭司、巫者与长老因掌握祭祀与传统知识而享有较高威望。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around clans, villages, and coastal or upland tribal groupings, with kinship serving as the main bond for production, marriage, and feud settlement. Commoners engaged in fishing, shifting cultivation, and gathering, while able-bodied men were mobilized for war when needed. Ritual specialists, shamans, and elders held elevated prestige because they controlled ceremonies and customary knowledge.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要由各部酋领、长老与主持祭祀的巫者构成。其权威更多来自血缘、战功与协调能力，而非成文官职。
rulingClass.en: The ruling stratum consisted mainly of tribal chieftains, elders, and ritual shamans. Their authority derived more from lineage, martial reputation, and skill in mediation than from formal office.

succession.zh: 继承通常在部族显贵家族内部进行，多由长老会议认可最有威望者继位。若首领软弱，分支部族常会自行分裂或另立酋领。
succession.en: Succession usually took place within prominent clan lineages, with the elders recognizing the most respected candidate. If a chief proved weak, subordinate groups could split away or elevate their own leaders.

structure.zh: 海南部族并无成熟官僚国家，政治中心是部族议事会与祭祀首领。重大事项如迁徙、结盟、复仇与贡纳，由各聚落首领协商决定。对外与岭南汉政权接触时，往往由最强部族代表全岛部分地区发言。
structure.en: The Hainan peoples did not possess a developed bureaucratic state; political authority centered on tribal councils and ritual leadership. Major matters such as migration, alliance, feud, and tribute were settled through negotiation among settlement chiefs. In dealings with Han authorities in Lingnan, the strongest tribe often spoke on behalf of parts of the island.

function.zh: 裁决部族争端，协商对外关系，并认可首领与战时动员。
function.en: Settled tribal disputes, coordinated external relations, and approved chieftains and wartime mobilization.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、保管贡物，并安排与外来商人或汉官的交换。
function.en: Managed rituals, safeguarded tribute goods, and arranged exchanges with foreign traders or Han officials.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以聚落为单位，由村社长老或家族首领主持。山区与沿海聚落差异较大，统一行政控制很弱。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated at the settlement level under village elders or clan heads. Upland and coastal communities differed considerably, and unified control remained weak.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法为主，强调血亲赔偿、盟誓与调解。严重冲突可演变为部族械斗，缺乏成文刑律体系。
legalSystem.en: Law was based chiefly on custom, stressing kin compensation, oath-taking, and mediation. Serious disputes could escalate into inter-tribal violence, as no written penal code existed.

taxationSystem.zh: 内部没有固定税制，首领主要通过共享猎获、分配贡品与战利品维系权威。对外可能以珠贝、海产、香木等形式向岭南政权间歇性纳贡。
taxationSystem.en: There was no fixed internal tax system; chiefs maintained authority through redistribution of game, tribute items, and war booty. Externally, they may have offered intermittent tribute to Lingnan authorities in shells, marine products, and aromatic woods.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 海南部族擅长海岛环境下的舟船、渔猎与热带作物利用，并保存丰富的歌舞与祭仪传统。其服饰、纹饰与口传神话体现出明显的南方海岛文化特征。
culturalAchievements.en: The Hainan peoples were skilled in boat use, fishing, hunting, and exploitation of tropical crops in an island environment, and they preserved rich traditions of song, dance, and ritual. Their dress, ornament, and oral mythology displayed distinct southern island cultural traits.

languageFamily.zh: 可能属古代百越相关语言群，具体分类未明。
languageFamily.en: Likely belonged to ancient Baiyue-related language groups, though precise classification is uncertain.

philosophy.zh: 以万物有灵、祖灵崇拜与自然禁忌为核心的部族信仰传统。
philosophy.en: A tribal belief tradition centered on animism, ancestor spirits, and natural taboos.

writingSystem.zh: 无本土成熟文字，知识主要靠口述传统与仪式传承；与汉人接触时偶借汉字记录贡纳事务。
writingSystem.en: No mature indigenous writing system existed; knowledge was transmitted mainly through oral tradition and ritual, though Chinese characters were occasionally used in contacts with Han officials.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模较小，以自给性生产为主，市场交换仅在沿海聚落与外来商人接触点较活跃。财富多体现在粮食储备、舟船、贝饰与铁器拥有量，而非货币积累。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was small and largely subsistence-based, with market exchange becoming more active only at coastal settlements dealing with outside traders. Wealth was expressed more in stored food, boats, shell ornaments, and iron tools than in money.

mainIndustries.zh: 渔猎、刀耕火种农业、采集、林产、海产交换
mainIndustries.en: Fishing and hunting, shifting agriculture, gathering, forest products, and exchange of marine goods

tradeGoods.zh: 海产、珠贝、香木、藤竹、兽皮、热带果物
tradeGoods.en: Marine products, shells and pearls, aromatic woods, rattan and bamboo, animal skins, and tropical fruits

householdWealth.zh: 一般家庭财富有限，主要体现为耕地使用权、渔具、独木舟与家畜。沿海部族首领因掌握对外交换渠道而相对富裕。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was modest and consisted mainly of cultivation rights, fishing gear, dugout boats, and livestock. Coastal chiefs were relatively wealthier because they controlled channels of outside exchange.

economicSystem.zh: 以部族再分配和家庭自给为主体，辅以有限海上交换。
economicSystem.en: Predominantly a household subsistence and tribal redistributive economy, supplemented by limited maritime exchange.

treasuryDescription.zh: 部族并无集中金库，储备主要以贝饰、铁器、盐、布匹与粮食形式分散保管于首领与聚落之中。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no centralized treasury; reserves were dispersed among chiefs and settlements in the form of shell ornaments, iron goods, salt, cloth, and grain.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持部族团结和再分配为主，尽量避免过度索取以免引发离散。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on maintaining tribal cohesion and redistribution, avoiding excessive demands that might provoke fragmentation.

description.zh: 以矛、弓、木盾为主，擅长山林伏击与近距离袭扰。
description.en: Armed mainly with spears, bows, and wooden shields, skilled in ambush and close harassment in forests and hills.

description.zh: 以小舟巡海与近岸机动为主，可用于劫掠、侦察与岛际联络。
description.en: Used small craft for coastal movement, raiding, reconnaissance, and inter-settlement communication.

commanderInChief.en: Paramount chieftain chosen by the tribes

technology.zh: 军事技术较为简朴，使用木石兵器并辅以少量铁制刀矛，防具有限。熟悉热带地形与海岸航行是其主要优势。
technology.en: Military technology was simple, relying on wooden and stone weapons with some iron blades and spearheads, while armor was limited. Knowledge of tropical terrain and coastal navigation was their chief advantage.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自部族间冲突、海上劫掠，以及岭南政权可能的征讨或强制纳贡。
threats.en: Main threats came from inter-tribal conflict, seaborne raiding, and possible punitive expeditions or tribute demands from Lingnan authorities.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于沿海聚落与内陆山地村社，整体密度较低。多数居民为本地部族成员，少量外来汉人商旅或流民只在接触区出现。
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed among coastal settlements and inland hill villages, with overall low density. Most inhabitants belonged to local tribes, while only small numbers of Han traders or migrants appeared in contact zones.

socialClasses.zh: 酋领与长老、巫者与祭司、自由部族成员、依附小氏族与俘获人口。
socialClasses.en: Chieftains and elders, shamans and ritual specialists, free tribal members, and dependent minor clans or captives.

allies.zh: 无固定正式盟友，偶与邻近海岛或岭南商人维持互利关系。
allies.en: No fixed formal allies, though some groups maintained pragmatic ties with nearby island communities and Lingnan traders.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象多为岛内竞争部族，外部则对汉郡县的强制干预保持戒备。
enemies.en: Enemies were usually rival tribes on the island, while outside interference from Han commanderies was regarded with suspicion.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以保持自治、有限通贡与交换为主，尽量避免被纳入严密郡县控制。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed at preserving autonomy through limited tribute and exchange while avoiding incorporation into tight commandery administration.

era.zh: 南方海岛部族铁器初步使用时期
era.en: Southern island tribal age with limited use of iron

keyInnovations.zh: 独木舟航行、热带作物栽培、渔网与骨角器制作、有限铁器吸收。
keyInnovations.en: Dugout canoe navigation, tropical crop cultivation, net making and bone-horn tools, and limited adoption of iron implements.

strengths.zh: 熟悉岛屿环境，社会弹性强，生计方式多样，外来政权难以彻底控制。
strengths.en: They knew the island environment well, possessed social resilience, had diversified subsistence strategies, and were difficult for outside states to control completely.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏统一政权、财政与成文制度，军事规模小，难以抵御组织化远征。
weaknesses.en: They lacked unified government, finance, and written institutions; military forces were small and poorly suited to resisting organized expeditions.

outlook.zh: 短期内可维持部族自治，但随着大陆政权海上影响增强，未来更可能逐步被羁縻化与边缘整合。
outlook.en: In the short term they could preserve tribal autonomy, but as mainland maritime influence expanded they would likely face gradual indirect incorporation and frontier integration.

description.zh: 海南诸部是以氏族与聚落为基础、依靠渔猎与刀耕火种维生的海岛部族共同体。
description.en: The Hainan peoples were island tribal communities based on clans and settlements, living by fishing, hunting, and shifting cultivation.

governmentForm.zh: 由君主统治、以都城阿努拉德普勒为中心的南亚佛教王国。
governmentForm.en: A monarchic South Asian Buddhist kingdom centered on the capital of Anuradhapura.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以国王、贵族官僚、佛教僧团、村社农民与工匠商人构成，灌溉农业是秩序基础。寺院在教育、土地管理与社会救济中地位突出。边缘地区仍有地方首领与部族社群保持较强自治性。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the king, noble officials, the Buddhist sangha, village cultivators, and artisan-merchants, with irrigated agriculture forming the basis of order. Monasteries played a major role in education, land management, and social relief. Peripheral regions still contained local chiefs and tribal communities with notable autonomy.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由王室、宫廷贵族、军政官员及与国家关系密切的大寺院高僧组成。王权需要依赖贵族家族与僧团共同维系合法性。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class comprised the royal house, court nobility, military-administrative officials, and senior monks of major monasteries closely tied to the state. Royal authority depended on both aristocratic families and the sangha for legitimacy.

succession.zh: 王位通常在王族内部继承，但常因支系竞争、宫廷政变或军事拥立而发生变动。获得首都控制与僧团认可，是继位成败的关键。
succession.en: The throne usually passed within the royal family, but succession was often affected by collateral rivalry, palace coups, or military backing. Control of the capital and recognition by the sangha were crucial for securing the throne.

structure.zh: 王国政务由国王主持，宫廷官署负责财政、灌溉、军务与宗教施惠。都城周边控制较强，外省则依靠地方官与附属首领治理。王权与佛教寺院之间形成相互支持的治理网络。
structure.en: Government was directed by the king, with court offices overseeing finance, irrigation, military affairs, and religious patronage. Control was strongest around the capital, while outer provinces were governed through local officials and subordinate chiefs. Royal power and Buddhist monasteries formed a mutually supporting network of rule.

function.zh: 征收土地税、管理仓储、保管金银与调配王室支出。
function.en: Collected land taxes, managed granaries, safeguarded bullion, and allocated royal expenditure.

function.zh: 修筑与维护水库、堤坝、渠道和道路，是王国农业命脉所在。
function.en: Built and maintained tanks, embankments, canals, and roads, forming the backbone of the kingdom's agrarian system.

function.zh: 统筹王家卫队、地方兵役与边境防务。
function.en: Coordinated the royal guard, provincial levies, and frontier defense.

function.zh: 管理对僧团的赐地、供养与宗教工程，协调王权与寺院关系。
function.en: Managed land grants, donations, and religious works for the sangha, and coordinated relations between crown and monasteries.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托村社、灌溉区与区域长官展开，税粮和劳役由地方层层征解。偏远地区常由效忠王室的地方酋长或王族成员代为统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through villages, irrigation districts, and regional governors, with taxes and labor collected through layered local structures. Remote areas were often ruled by local chiefs or royal kinsmen loyal to the crown.

legalSystem.zh: 法律由王命、习惯法与佛教伦理共同构成。土地、灌溉与寺产纠纷尤受重视，审断常兼顾世俗秩序与宗教功德观念。
legalSystem.en: Law consisted of royal commands, customary practice, and Buddhist moral norms. Disputes over land, irrigation, and monastery property were especially important, and judgments balanced secular order with religious ideas of merit.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以土地税、灌溉农业产出、劳役与贸易关税为主。寺院获有减免与赐田，既减少部分税基，也增强了王权的宗教合法性。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied on land revenue, irrigated agricultural output, labor dues, and customs on trade. Monasteries enjoyed exemptions and endowed lands, reducing part of the tax base while strengthening royal religious legitimacy.

religion.zh: 上座部佛教
religion.en: Theravada Buddhism

culturalAchievements.zh: 阿努拉德普勒时期以大型佛塔、寺院建筑和发达水利工程著称，形成南亚古代最重要的佛教都城之一。王国还积累了丰富的碑铭与寺院传统，为后世僧伽罗史学奠定基础。
culturalAchievements.en: The Anuradhapura age was renowned for great stupas, monastic architecture, and advanced hydraulic works, making it one of the most important Buddhist capitals of ancient South Asia. The kingdom also accumulated a rich inscriptional and monastic tradition that laid foundations for later Sinhala historiography.

languageFamily.zh: 僧伽罗语属印度-雅利安语族；宗教语言以巴利语为重。
languageFamily.en: Sinhala belonged to the Indo-Aryan language family, while Pali held major religious importance.

philosophy.zh: 以上座部佛教为核心，同时保留王权仪礼与本地护国信仰。
philosophy.en: Centered on Theravada Buddhism, while retaining royal ritual traditions and local protective cults.

writingSystem.zh: 使用早期僧伽罗—婆罗米系文字书写铭文与宗教记录，并以巴利语和本地语言并行。
writingSystem.en: Used early Sinhala-Brahmi derived scripts for inscriptions and religious records, alongside Pali and local speech.

gdpDescription.zh: 王国经济以稻作灌溉农业为核心，兼有寺院地产、手工业与印度洋海贸收益。都城与港口的财富集中度高于一般乡村地区。
gdpDescription.en: The kingdom’s economy was centered on irrigated rice cultivation, supplemented by monastery estates, handicrafts, and Indian Ocean trade revenues. Wealth was more concentrated in the capital and ports than in ordinary rural districts.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉稻作、寺院地产经营、手工业、宝石采集、海上贸易
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated rice farming, monastic estate management, handicrafts, gem extraction, and maritime trade

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、宝石、象牙、珍珠、香料、肉桂、纺织品
tradeGoods.en: Rice, gems, ivory, pearls, spices, cinnamon, and textiles

name.en: Base-metal coinage with weighed silver and gold

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富取决于灌溉土地、水权与牲畜数量。寺院周边村社较稳定，商人与高僧则拥有更高水平的金属器、布帛与储粮。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth depended on access to irrigated land, water rights, and livestock. Villages attached to monasteries tended to be more stable, while merchants and senior monks held greater stocks of metal goods, textiles, and grain.

economicSystem.zh: 以王权与寺院土地体系支撑的农业经济，辅以港口贸易和再分配机制。
economicSystem.en: An agrarian economy supported by royal and monastic landholding, complemented by port trade and redistributive institutions.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由贵金属、谷仓储备、布匹与可征发劳力共同构成；其中大量财富也以寺院捐献和王室储粮形式存在。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of precious metals, granary reserves, textiles, and mobilizable labor; a significant portion of wealth also existed as monastic endowments and royal grain stocks.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重点在维持灌溉系统、王权威望与佛教布施，通过稳定农业产出支持长期统治。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized irrigation maintenance, royal prestige, and Buddhist patronage, using stable agricultural production to sustain long-term rule.

description.zh: 为军队主体，装备矛、刀、盾与弓，负责守城、野战与地方镇压。
description.en: The core of the army, equipped with spears, swords, shields, and bows for garrison duty, field combat, and local suppression.

description.zh: 用于冲击与王室威仪，常配属贵族和宫廷精锐。
description.en: Used for shock action and royal display, often attached to nobles and elite court troops.

description.zh: 负责近海巡逻、运兵与港口防护，规模不及陆军但对海贸安全重要。
description.en: Handled coastal patrols, troop transport, and port defense; smaller than the land forces but important for maritime trade security.

technology.zh: 军队掌握铁制兵器、石木城防、象兵运用和较成熟的攻守城经验。海军技术以近海航行和港口控制为主，远洋作战能力有限。
technology.en: The military employed iron weapons, masonry and timber fortifications, war elephants, and fairly developed siege and defense practice. Naval technology focused on coastal navigation and port control, with limited blue-water capability.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自南印度势力介入、内部王位争夺及地方叛乱。海贸通道受扰也会削弱财政与物资流通。
threats.en: Main threats came from intervention by South Indian powers, internal dynastic struggles, and local revolts. Disruption of maritime trade routes could also weaken fiscal revenue and supply.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

description.zh: 单兵弩与床弩，中国军队核心远程武器
description.en: Infantry crossbow and mounted crossbow, core ranged weapon

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于北中部灌溉农业区与都城周边，乡村人口占绝大多数。港口与寺院附近形成较稳定的手工业和商贸人口。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in the irrigated north-central agricultural zone and around the capital, with the vast majority living in villages. Ports and monastic centers supported more stable artisan and merchant populations.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与贵族、僧团、高级官员、自由农民、工匠商人、依附劳役人口与边缘部族。
socialClasses.en: Royal family and nobility, the sangha, senior officials, free cultivators, artisans and merchants, dependent laborers, and peripheral tribal groups.

allies.zh: 与印度洋商人网络、部分南印度港口势力及佛教僧团往来密切，但正式军事同盟有限。
allies.en: It maintained close ties with Indian Ocean merchant networks, some South Indian ports, and Buddhist monastic circles, though formal military alliances were limited.

enemies.zh: 潜在敌对者主要为觊觎兰卡财富的南印度政治势力，以及国内争位集团。
enemies.en: Its main potential enemies were South Indian political forces seeking Lanka’s wealth, as well as rival internal claimants.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外重视海贸安全、宗教声望与维持对岛内的王权统合，必要时通过馈赠与婚盟缓和外部压力。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy emphasized maritime trade security, religious prestige, and royal integration of the island, while gifts and marriage ties were used when necessary to reduce outside pressure.

era.zh: 南亚古典水利王国时期
era.en: Classical South Asian hydraulic kingdom era

keyInnovations.zh: 大型蓄水池与渠道系统、砖石佛塔建筑、碑铭记录、战象与灌溉管理技术。
keyInnovations.en: Large tank-and-canal systems, brick and stone stupa construction, inscriptional record-keeping, and techniques of war-elephant use and irrigation management.

strengths.zh: 拥有成熟灌溉农业、佛教合法性、都城传统与印度洋贸易联系，国家组织能力在区域内较强。
strengths.en: It possessed mature irrigated agriculture, Buddhist legitimacy, an established capital tradition, and Indian Ocean trade connections, giving it strong regional state capacity.

weaknesses.zh: 王位继承不稳，地方控制在边缘区有限，对海贸和水利维护依赖较深。
weaknesses.en: Succession could be unstable, control over peripheral regions was limited, and the kingdom relied heavily on maritime trade and irrigation upkeep.

outlook.zh: 短期内仍可保持稳定与富庶，但若遭遇南印度干涉或内部夺位，政治平衡可能迅速恶化。
outlook.en: In the short term it could remain stable and prosperous, but South Indian intervention or internal succession conflict could quickly destabilize the political balance.

description.zh: 僧伽罗阿努拉德普勒王国是以佛教、灌溉农业与海上贸易支撑的南亚古典王国。
description.en: Simhala’s Anuradhapura kingdom was a classical South Asian state sustained by Buddhism, irrigated agriculture, and maritime trade.

dynasty.zh: 提比略—尤利乌斯王朝晚期博斯普鲁斯王国

governmentForm.zh: 受罗马影响深刻的希腊化世袭王国，依托黑海北岸城邦与王室统治。
governmentForm.en: A heavily Roman-influenced Hellenistic hereditary kingdom based on Black Sea littoral cities and royal authority.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由希腊化城市居民、地方贵族、王室附庸、乡村农民与草原背景人口共同构成。沿海城邦保留较强希腊传统，内陆则更多依赖与萨尔马提亚和其他草原群体的关系。奴隶、自由民与混合血统军人商人构成重要的社会中层与下层。
socialStructure.en: Society combined Hellenized urban residents, local aristocrats, royal clients, rural cultivators, and peoples of steppe background. Coastal cities retained strong Greek traditions, while the interior depended more on ties with Sarmatian and other steppe groups. Slaves, freedmen, and mixed-background soldier-merchants formed an important middle and lower social layer.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由王室、希腊城邦精英、骑兵贵族及与罗马保持关系的宫廷集团组成。王权须在城邦自治传统、草原军事需求与罗马宗主权之间维持平衡。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the royal house, Greek civic elites, cavalry aristocrats, and a court faction tied to Rome. Royal authority had to balance civic autonomy, steppe military needs, and Roman suzerainty.

succession.zh: 王位由王朝内部世袭，但常借罗马认可强化合法性。若王室衰弱，宫廷争斗、外族介入或城邦离心都会影响继承稳定。
succession.en: The throne was hereditary within the dynasty, though Roman recognition often reinforced legitimacy. If the royal house weakened, court conflict, foreign intervention, or civic separatism could disturb succession.

structure.zh: 博斯普鲁斯王国兼具希腊城邦制度与君主制特色，王室控制外交、军队、税务与铸币。主要城市保有地方议会与市政传统，但整体受王权与罗马影响制约。由于国势衰落，中央对边疆与腹地的控制日益依赖地方豪强与盟附部族。
structure.en: The Bosporan Kingdom combined Greek civic institutions with monarchy, as the crown controlled diplomacy, the army, taxation, and coinage. Major cities retained councils and civic traditions, but overall remained constrained by royal authority and Roman influence. As the state declined, central control over frontier and interior zones increasingly relied on local magnates and allied tribes.

function.zh: 发布王令、保管外交文书并协调城邦与王室之间的行政联络。
function.en: Issued royal decrees, kept diplomatic documents, and coordinated administration between the crown and the cities.

function.zh: 征收港税、管理王室收入并主持货币铸造与金属储备。
function.en: Collected port dues, managed royal income, and supervised coinage and metal reserves.

function.zh: 组织王家部队、雇佣骑兵并处理与草原部族的防务关系。
function.en: Organized royal forces, hired cavalry, and managed defense relations with steppe tribes.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托潘提卡派翁、法纳戈里亚等城邦及其周边领地进行。内陆与边境地区常由王室任命代理人或附庸首领间接管理。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on cities such as Panticapaeum and Phanagoria and their surrounding territories. Inland and frontier areas were often governed indirectly through royal agents or client chieftains.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系混合希腊城邦法、王室敕令与罗马法影响。城市商业、契约与财产关系较制度化，而边疆事务更多按军政裁断处理。
legalSystem.en: The legal system mixed Greek civic law, royal decrees, and Roman legal influence. Urban commerce, contracts, and property were relatively formalized, whereas frontier affairs were handled more by military-administrative judgment.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要来自港口关税、农业地产、渔业盐业与王室贡赋。衰退时期税基缩小，王室更依赖铸币与对贸易节点的控制。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came mainly from port customs, agricultural estates, fisheries and salt, and royal dues. In decline, the tax base narrowed and the crown depended more on coinage and control of trade nodes.

religion.zh: 希腊多神教
religion.en: Greek polytheism

culturalAchievements.zh: 博斯普鲁斯王国长期维持黑海北岸希腊城市文化、铸币传统与跨草原贸易中介地位。其墓葬艺术、铭文与钱币为研究晚期希腊化边疆世界提供重要材料。
culturalAchievements.en: The Bosporan Kingdom long preserved Greek urban culture, coinage traditions, and an intermediary role in trans-steppe trade on the northern Black Sea. Its funerary art, inscriptions, and coins are major evidence for the late Hellenistic frontier world.

languageFamily.zh: 官方与城市文化语言为希腊语，另有伊朗语系草原语言与地方混合语。
languageFamily.en: Greek was the official and urban cultural language, alongside Iranian steppe languages and local mixed vernaculars.

philosophy.zh: 以希腊多神教传统为主，混合地方神祇、王室崇拜与部分罗马文化影响。
philosophy.en: Primarily grounded in Greek polytheistic tradition, blended with local deities, royal cult, and some Roman cultural influence.

writingSystem.zh: 使用希腊字母书写希腊语，官方铭文与货币亦常体现王名与罗马联系。
writingSystem.en: Greek was written in the Greek alphabet, and official inscriptions and coinage often displayed royal names and ties to Rome.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以黑海贸易、谷物、渔业和港口服务为主，但受草原局势与罗马市场变化影响明显。尽管技术与城市传统仍在，整体已较盛期衰退。
gdpDescription.en: The economy centered on Black Sea trade, grain, fisheries, and port services, but was strongly affected by steppe conditions and changes in Roman markets. Although technology and urban traditions endured, the kingdom had clearly declined from its peak.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物生产、渔业、盐业、葡萄种植、港口贸易、铸币
mainIndustries.en: Grain production, fisheries, salt, viticulture, port trade, and minting

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、鱼制品、盐、葡萄酒、奴隶、皮革、金属制品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, fish products, salt, wine, slaves, leather, and metal goods

householdWealth.zh: 城市家庭拥有房产、仓储和贸易收益的机会较多，乡村居民则更多依赖农业与渔业。贵族和大商人掌握港口、土地与骑兵资源，贫富差距明显。
householdWealth.en: Urban households had more access to property, storage, and trade profits, while rural people depended more on farming and fishing. Aristocrats and major merchants controlled ports, land, and cavalry resources, producing marked inequality.

economicSystem.zh: 希腊化城市市场经济与王室控制贸易节点、贡赋和地产收益并存。
economicSystem.en: A Hellenistic urban market economy coexisted with royal control over trade nodes, tribute, and estate revenues.

category.en: Gifts to Rome and frontier tribes

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库仍保有一定金属储备与税谷，但财政紧张，需依赖铸币、借调城市资源及延迟支付军饷维持运转。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury still held some metal reserves and tax grain, but finances were strained, requiring reliance on coinage, extraction from cities, and delayed military payments.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持港口与军队，短期内通过加强税收和铸币弥补下滑收入，但长期损害经济活力。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized maintaining ports and the army; in the short run the crown compensated for falling income by heavier taxation and minting, but this undermined long-term economic vitality.

description.zh: 驻守城防与港口，装备矛、盾、短剑和部分弓弩，战斗力中等。
description.en: Garrisoned cities and ports, armed with spears, shields, short swords, and some missile weapons, with moderate combat value.

description.zh: 包含王室骑兵与雇佣萨尔马提亚骑兵，是边疆机动作战主力。
description.en: Included royal horsemen and hired Sarmatian cavalry, forming the main mobile force on the frontier.

description.zh: 承担海峡巡逻、护航与港湾防御，规模较小但对贸易命脉重要。
description.en: Handled strait patrols, convoy protection, and harbor defense; small in size but vital to trade routes.

technology.zh: 军事技术融合希腊化城防、罗马装备影响与草原骑射传统。城防和铸币技术仍较先进，但整体兵员与补给能力下降。
technology.en: Military technology blended Hellenistic fortification, Roman-influenced equipment, and steppe cavalry traditions. Fortifications and minting remained relatively advanced, but manpower and logistical capacity had declined.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自萨尔马提亚及其他草原部族压力、内部财政恶化导致的军队不稳，以及罗马支持减弱后的地缘孤立。
threats.en: Main threats came from pressure by Sarmatian and other steppe peoples, military instability caused by fiscal deterioration, and geopolitical isolation if Roman support weakened.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中于沿海城镇与其腹地农渔区，城市化程度高于多数草原邻邦。族群成分复杂，包括希腊化居民、地方土著、萨尔马提亚成分与奴隶人口。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in coastal towns and their agrarian-fishing hinterlands, with urbanization higher than in most neighboring steppe societies. Ethnic composition was mixed, including Hellenized residents, local natives, Sarmatian elements, and slaves.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与贵族、城邦议员与商人、士兵与自由民、农渔民、奴隶与依附部族。
socialClasses.en: Royal family and nobles, civic councillors and merchants, soldiers and freedmen, farmers and fishers, slaves, and dependent tribes.

allies.zh: 名义上受罗马庇护，并与部分草原部族维持临时盟约或雇佣关系。
allies.en: Nominally under Roman protection, it also maintained temporary alliances or mercenary ties with certain steppe tribes.

enemies.zh: 潜在敌人包括敌对萨尔马提亚集团、黑海海盗与觊觎港口控制权的周边势力。
enemies.en: Potential enemies included hostile Sarmatian groups, Black Sea raiders, and neighboring powers seeking control of the ports.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持罗马承认、保全海峡贸易并通过馈赠与婚盟安抚草原邻族为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving Roman recognition, safeguarding strait commerce, and pacifying steppe neighbors through gifts and marriage diplomacy.

era.zh: 晚期希腊化—罗马边疆王国时期
era.en: Late Hellenistic to Roman frontier kingdom era

keyInnovations.zh: 城墙与港口设施、区域铸币体系、黑海航运经验、步骑混合作战模式。
keyInnovations.en: Fortified walls and port infrastructure, a regional coinage system, Black Sea seafaring experience, and mixed infantry-cavalry warfare.

strengths.zh: 拥有港口贸易传统、较高城市化、成熟铸币与城防体系，并能连接罗马世界与草原经济。
strengths.en: It retained port-trade traditions, relatively high urbanization, mature coinage and fortifications, and the ability to link the Roman world with the steppe economy.

weaknesses.zh: 人口与财政基础有限，严重依赖外贸与外部支持，边疆防务成本高且王权已显衰弱。
weaknesses.en: Its demographic and fiscal base was limited, it depended heavily on trade and external support, frontier defense was costly, and royal authority was visibly weakening.

outlook.zh: 若无强力罗马援助，王国将继续衰退并更易受草原势力摆布，但短期内仍可凭城市与海峡优势维持存在。
outlook.en: Without strong Roman backing the kingdom would continue to decline and become more vulnerable to steppe manipulation, though in the short term it could still survive through its cities and strait position.

description.zh: 博斯普鲁斯王国是黑海北岸一个受罗马影响的晚期希腊化港口王国，虽仍具城市与贸易优势，但已步入衰落。
description.en: The Bosporan Kingdom was a Roman-influenced late Hellenistic port monarchy on the northern Black Sea, still advantaged by cities and trade but clearly in decline.

governmentForm.zh: 以礼仪中心和远距交换网络为纽带的部落酋邦联盟，由若干地方酋长在宗教与馈赠关系中维系统合。
governmentForm.en: A tribal chiefdom confederacy tied together by ceremonial centers and long-distance exchange networks, integrated through religious authority and gift-based relations among local chiefs.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以村落社群、氏族与主持礼仪的精英家庭为核心，上层人物在土丘营建、祭祀与交换活动中具有较高威望。普通成员多从事园圃农业、采集、渔猎与季节性劳作。不同聚落之间虽保有自主性，但在大型仪式和葬仪活动中会形成较强协作。
socialStructure.en: Society centered on village communities, clans, and elite households that oversaw ceremonies, with higher-status figures gaining prestige through mound building, ritual, and exchange. Most people engaged in horticulture, gathering, fishing, hunting, and seasonal labor. Settlements remained locally autonomous, but cooperation intensified during major rituals and mortuary events.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要由主持祭祀、调配贡礼与组织劳役的酋长家族构成。其权力更多建立在宗教威望、馈赠能力与人际网络，而非固定官僚机构之上。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted mainly of chiefly families who directed ritual, managed tribute gifts, and organized labor. Their authority rested more on religious prestige, generosity, and personal networks than on a fixed bureaucracy.

succession.zh: 继承通常在显赫家族内部进行，可能兼顾血缘、长幼与个人威望。若继承人无法维持交换网络和祭仪号召力，其他支系首领亦可能取而代之。
succession.en: Succession generally remained within prominent lineages, balancing kinship, seniority, and personal prestige. If an heir could not sustain exchange networks and ritual authority, rival leaders from related lines could replace them.

structure.zh: 霍普韦尔文化并无统一国家政府，而是由多个区域性酋邦和礼仪中心组成。重大事务通常通过酋长、长老与祭仪主持者协商决定，权力集中度有限。劳动力的组织主要围绕土丘建造、葬礼、季节性集会与交换活动展开。
structure.en: The Hopewell world had no single state government, but rather multiple regional chiefdoms and ceremonial centers. Major decisions were usually made through consultation among chiefs, elders, and ritual leaders, with limited centralization. Labor was mobilized mainly for mound construction, funerals, seasonal gatherings, and exchange.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、葬仪、历法性集会及大型土丘与围 enclosure 工程的组织。
function.en: Oversaw ceremonies, mortuary rites, calendrical gatherings, and the organization of major mound and enclosure works.

function.zh: 维持与周边及远方社群的礼物交换、特产流通和盟谊联络。
function.en: Maintained gift exchange, prestige-goods circulation, and alliance contacts with neighboring and distant communities.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托村落首领、氏族长老和礼仪权威人物，而非常设行政区。各聚落日常事务自行处理，但在大型仪式期间会接受中心聚落协调。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on village headmen, clan elders, and ritual authorities rather than permanent districts. Settlements handled daily affairs themselves, but during major ceremonies they accepted coordination from central sites.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要体现为习惯法、宗教禁忌与氏族调解。纠纷通常通过赔礼、馈赠、婚姻安排或长老仲裁解决。
legalSystem.en: Law took the form of customary norms, religious taboos, and clan mediation. Disputes were generally settled through compensation, gift exchange, marriage arrangements, or elder arbitration.

taxationSystem.zh: 并无制度化税收，酋长主要通过贡礼、劳役动员和仪式分配获取资源。重要工程和集会时，各聚落需贡献食物、手工品与人力。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; chiefs obtained resources through tribute gifts, labor mobilization, and ceremonial redistribution. For major works and gatherings, settlements contributed food, crafts, and manpower.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 霍普韦尔人以复杂土丘群、精致石管、铜器、云母制品和长距离交换网络著称。其几何围场与葬仪艺术体现出高度组织能力与象征思维。
culturalAchievements.en: The Hopewell are known for complex mound groups, finely made stone pipes, copperwork, mica objects, and extensive long-distance exchange networks. Their geometric earthworks and mortuary art reveal high organizational capacity and sophisticated symbolic thought.

languageFamily.zh: 语言未有定论，可能涉及北美东部多种语系前身；整体为无统一书写的部族语言环境。
languageFamily.en: Linguistic affiliation is uncertain, likely involving predecessors of several eastern North American language groups; overall it was a tribal multilingual environment without writing.

philosophy.zh: 以万物有灵、祖灵崇拜与礼仪宇宙观为核心，重视土丘、方圆空间布局与葬仪象征。
philosophy.en: Centered on animism, ancestor veneration, and a ritual cosmology that emphasized mounds, geometric sacred spaces, and mortuary symbolism.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，依赖口传传统、仪式图像与器物符号传递记忆与权威。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; memory and authority were transmitted through oral tradition, ritual imagery, and symbolic objects.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以玉米、藜科及其他园圃作物种植，辅以渔猎采集和手工业为主。总体剩余不高，但通过礼仪中心汇聚后可支持大型工程与远距交换。
gdpDescription.en: The economy relied on maize and other horticultural crops, supplemented by fishing, hunting, gathering, and craft production. Overall surplus was modest, but when concentrated at ceremonial centers it could support large projects and long-distance exchange.

mainIndustries.zh: 园圃农业、渔猎采集、土丘营建、石器与铜器制作、礼仪手工业
mainIndustries.en: Horticulture, fishing and foraging, mound construction, stone and copper craftwork, ceremonial artisanal production

tradeGoods.zh: 铜、云母、黑曜石、海贝、石管、皮毛、盐、优质石料
tradeGoods.en: Copper, mica, obsidian, marine shell, stone pipes, furs, salt, and fine stone materials

name.zh: 无铸币，以贡礼交换为主

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富多表现为粮食储备、工具、饰品与亲族关系，而非可累积货币。精英家庭拥有更多礼器、异地珍品与动员劳力的能力。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in stored food, tools, ornaments, and kin ties rather than accumulable money. Elite households possessed more ritual goods, exotic valuables, and the ability to mobilize labor.

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产、氏族互助、礼物交换和酋长再分配为基础的部落经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy based on household production, clan reciprocity, gift exchange, and chiefly redistribution.

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”主要体现为仓储粮食、仪式珍品、铜器和可调配的人情债，而非集中铸币储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' consisted mainly of stored food, ritual valuables, copper objects, and callable social obligations rather than centralized coin reserves.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重在维持礼仪声望与再分配能力，避免对附属聚落施加过重征取。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized maintaining ritual prestige and redistributive capacity while avoiding excessive extraction from subordinate settlements.

description.zh: 由酋长与显贵维持的常备护卫，负责护送、警戒与仪式安全。
description.en: A small standing body maintained by chiefs and elites, responsible for escort, security, and ceremonial protection.

description.zh: 由各聚落成年男子在冲突时临时集结，装备以矛、棍棒、石刃与弓箭为主。
description.en: Adult men from local settlements mobilized in times of conflict, armed mainly with spears, clubs, stone blades, and bows.

technology.zh: 军事技术较为基础，依赖木石骨角兵器、弓箭与防御性聚落布局，缺乏金属武器体系。
technology.en: Military technology was basic, relying on wood, stone, bone, and antler weapons, bows, and defensive settlement layouts, without a developed metal weapons system.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部落冲突、交换网络中断与内部酋邦竞争，而非大规模征服战争。
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring tribal conflicts, disruption of exchange networks, and rivalry among chiefdoms rather than large-scale conquest warfare.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 复合弓或长弓，远射主力
description.en: Composite or longbow, primary ranged weapon

description.zh: 单兵弩与床弩，中国军队核心远程武器
description.en: Infantry crossbow and mounted crossbow, core ranged weapon

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布在河谷与林地边缘的小型村落及礼仪中心周边，密度较低。季节性聚会会短暂提高中心聚落人口规模。
populationDescription.en: The population was spread across small villages in river valleys and woodland margins around ceremonial centers, with generally low density. Seasonal gatherings could temporarily swell the population of central sites.

socialClasses.zh: 大体可分为酋长与祭司性精英、熟练工匠与交换中介者、普通村民、附属亲族与战士群体。
socialClasses.en: Society can broadly be divided into chiefs and ritual elites, skilled artisans and exchange brokers, ordinary villagers, dependent kin groups, and warrior bands.

allies.zh: 与密西西比河流域、五大湖周边及阿巴拉契亚方向若干社群保持礼物交换与仪式性友好关系。
allies.en: Maintained gift-exchange and ceremonial friendly relations with communities in the Mississippi Valley, around the Great Lakes, and toward the Appalachians.

enemies.zh: 无固定国家级宿敌，冲突对象多为争夺狩猎地、通道或声望的邻近部落。
enemies.en: Had no fixed state-level enemies; conflicts were usually with neighboring tribes competing over hunting grounds, routes, or prestige.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策重在维持交换网络、扩展礼仪影响力并避免长期消耗性战争。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving exchange networks, extending ritual influence, and avoiding prolonged costly warfare.

era.zh: 北美东部中期林地时代晚段
era.en: Late Middle Woodland period of Eastern North America

keyInnovations.zh: 大型几何土木工程、区域性礼仪中心、精细石管与铜器加工、远距离珍品交换。
keyInnovations.en: Large geometric earthworks, regional ceremonial centers, fine stone-pipe and copper working, and long-distance prestige exchange.

strengths.zh: 礼仪整合力强，远距交换网络广泛，能够动员劳力修建大型工程并维持跨区域联系。
strengths.en: Strong ritual integration, broad long-distance exchange networks, and the ability to mobilize labor for major works and interregional ties.

weaknesses.zh: 政治集中度低，军事能力有限，对交换中断与地方离心较为敏感。
weaknesses.en: Low political centralization, limited military capacity, and high sensitivity to exchange disruption and local fragmentation.

outlook.zh: 若礼仪网络与精英互惠持续，文化中心仍可繁荣；但一旦区域联系减弱，整体体系易分散为更地方化的社群。
outlook.en: If ritual networks and elite reciprocity endure, the ceremonial centers can continue to thrive; if regional ties weaken, the system may fragment into more localized communities.

description.zh: 霍普韦尔文化是以礼仪土丘、远距交换和分散酋邦网络为特征的北美林地时代繁荣部落体系。
description.en: The Hopewell Culture was a flourishing Woodland-era tribal system marked by ceremonial mounds, long-distance exchange, and decentralized chiefdom networks.

governmentForm.zh: 以氏族、营地和长老协商为基础的分散部落组织，缺乏统一王权和固定都城。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal order based on clans, camps, and elder consultation, lacking unified kingship or a fixed capital.

socialStructure.zh: 社会结构通常较为平缓，以亲族关系、年龄资历与狩猎采集或牧养技能决定个人威望。部分科伊人群已有畜群财富差异，而桑人群体整体更强调共享与协商。迁徙性较强的小营地是基本社会单位。
socialStructure.en: Social structure was generally relatively egalitarian, with prestige shaped by kinship, age, and skill in hunting, gathering, or herding. Some Khoikhoi groups already showed wealth differences based on livestock, while many San groups emphasized sharing and consensus. The small mobile camp was the basic social unit.

rulingClass.zh: 严格意义上的统治阶层较弱，多由长老、优秀猎手、牧群拥有者或具灵力声望的祭仪人物取得领导地位。其权威主要依赖说服、亲族支持和资源调配能力。
rulingClass.en: A formal ruling class was weak; leadership was held by elders, accomplished hunters, herd owners, or ritual specialists with spiritual prestige. Their authority depended mainly on persuasion, kin support, and control over resources.

succession.zh: 领导权通常在家族或营地内部延续，但并非严格世袭。若继承者无法维持共识，营地成员可转而追随其他长老或分出新营地。
succession.en: Leadership often continued within families or camps, but was not strictly hereditary. If an heir failed to maintain consensus, camp members could shift allegiance to another elder or split off into a new camp.

structure.zh: 科伊桑诸部在公元220年前后并无统一政体，治理依靠营地会议、长老裁断与亲族网络。较大的牧民群体会在水源、放牧地和婚盟问题上形成临时性首领协调。整体行政极简，社会控制主要依靠习惯和舆论。
structure.en: Around 220 CE the Khoisan had no unified polity; governance relied on camp meetings, elder judgments, and kinship networks. Larger herding groups could form temporary leadership for water access, pasture, and marriage alliances. Administration was extremely light, with social control based mainly on custom and public opinion.

function.zh: 协调营地迁徙、取水次序、放牧范围与季节性资源使用。
function.en: Coordinated camp movement, access to water, grazing zones, and seasonal resource use.

function.zh: 处理婚姻、赔偿、争端调解及治疗和通灵仪式的组织。
function.en: Handled marriage matters, compensation, dispute mediation, and the organization of healing and trance rituals.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理即营地自理，由长老与亲族代表共同决定迁徙、狩猎与牧养安排。不存在成文行政层级，聚落之间联系较为松散。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was camp self-management, with elders and kin representatives deciding movement, hunting, and herding arrangements. There was no written administrative hierarchy, and links between settlements were loose.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法为主，强调共享规范、婚姻义务、赔偿与和解。严重冲突可能导致分营、驱逐或报复性争斗。
legalSystem.en: Law was customary, emphasizing norms of sharing, marital obligations, compensation, and reconciliation. Serious conflicts could lead to camp fission, expulsion, or retaliatory fighting.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，领导者依靠亲族馈赠、牲畜支持、猎获分配与共同劳作获得资源。公共事务由营地成员按需要分担。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; leaders relied on kin gifts, livestock support, division of game, and communal labor. Public obligations were shared among camp members as needed.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 科伊桑诸族创造了南部非洲最重要的岩画传统之一，并发展出高度适应干旱环境的狩猎、采集和牧养知识。其口传神话与治疗舞蹈体现出深厚的精神文化。
culturalAchievements.en: Khoisan peoples produced one of southern Africa's most significant rock-art traditions and developed highly adapted knowledge of hunting, foraging, and herding in arid environments. Their oral mythology and healing dances reflect a rich spiritual culture.

languageFamily.zh: 科伊桑诸语言群，包含多种带搭嘴音的语言；并非单一统一语系。
languageFamily.en: Khoisan language groups, including several click languages; not a single unified language family.

philosophy.zh: 信仰核心是万物有灵、祖灵或自然灵存在，以及通过舞蹈、 trance 与治疗仪式维系人与环境平衡。
philosophy.en: Core beliefs centered on animism, the presence of ancestral or natural spirits, and maintaining balance between people and environment through dance, trance, and healing ritual.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，以口述传统、歌谣、岩画和记忆地景传承知识。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, rock art, and remembered landscapes.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以采集、狩猎和局部牧养为主，生产剩余有限，但对生态环境适应性强。财富更多表现为牲畜、工具、婚姻网络和对水源的使用权。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on foraging, hunting, and localized herding, with limited surplus but strong ecological adaptability. Wealth was expressed more through livestock, tools, marriage ties, and access to water than through accumulated stores.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎采集、畜牧、皮革加工、骨木器制作、岩画与仪式活动
mainIndustries.en: Hunting and gathering, pastoralism, leather working, bone and wooden crafts, rock art and ritual activity

tradeGoods.zh: 兽皮、鸵鸟蛋壳珠、肉干、骨器、药材、牲畜、贝饰
tradeGoods.en: Hides, ostrich eggshell beads, dried meat, bone tools, medicinal materials, livestock, shell ornaments

name.zh: 无铸币，以牲畜与物物交换为主
name.en: No coinage; livestock and barter predominated

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异总体不大，但拥有较多牲畜、水源联系和珠饰的人家更具影响力。狩猎采集群体则更强调流动性和分享义务。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth disparities were generally modest, though families with more livestock, water connections, and bead valuables held greater influence. Hunting-foraging groups placed greater emphasis on mobility and obligations of sharing.

economicSystem.zh: 以营地生产、亲族共享、牲畜财富和小规模互市为基础的部落经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy based on camp production, kin sharing, livestock wealth, and small-scale exchange.

treasuryDescription.zh: 并无集中财政库藏，储备主要体现为牲畜、皮革、珠饰、干肉和亲族互助义务。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no centralized treasury; reserves were mainly held in livestock, hides, beads, dried meat, and kin-based reciprocal obligations.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 资源分配以维持营地生存、亲族平衡和季节迁徙弹性为优先，避免形成沉重常设负担。
fiscalPolicy.en: Resource allocation prioritized camp survival, kin balance, and seasonal mobility, avoiding heavy permanent obligations.

description.zh: 由经验丰富的猎手和青年战士组成，负责防卫营地与牲畜。
description.en: Made up of experienced hunters and younger warriors, tasked with defending camps and livestock.

description.zh: 冲突发生时临时召集，以弓箭、投枪、棍棒和少量盾具作战。
description.en: Temporarily mobilized in times of conflict, fighting with bows, throwing spears, clubs, and limited shields.

technology.zh: 以短弓、毒箭、投枪、木棍和简易皮革装备为主，擅长小规模机动与伏击。
technology.en: Used short bows, poison arrows, throwing spears, clubs, and simple leather equipment, excelling in small-scale mobility and ambush.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自争夺水源、牧场与猎场的邻近群体冲突，以及环境波动导致的资源紧张。
threats.en: Main threats arose from neighboring conflicts over water, pasture, and hunting grounds, as well as environmental fluctuation causing resource stress.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 复合弓或长弓，远射主力
description.en: Composite or longbow, primary ranged weapon

populationDescription.zh: 人口稀疏分布于南部非洲干旱与半干旱地带，多为流动性营地和小型定居点。聚落规模通常较小，人口季节波动明显。
populationDescription.en: The population was thinly spread across arid and semi-arid southern Africa in mobile camps and small settlements. Settlement size was usually small, with marked seasonal fluctuations.

socialClasses.zh: 总体阶层分化有限，可区分为长老与首领、祭仪治疗者、富畜家庭、普通营地成员与依附亲族。
socialClasses.en: Social stratification was limited overall, but one may distinguish elders and headmen, ritual healers, livestock-rich households, ordinary camp members, and dependent kin.

allies.zh: 盟友多为通婚与互访的邻近营地、牧群伙伴及共享水源的氏族群体。
allies.en: Allies were mainly neighboring camps linked by marriage, visiting partnerships, and shared access to water.

enemies.zh: 无持久统一宿敌，但与争夺草场、水井和狩猎路线的邻近群体常有摩擦。
enemies.en: There were no enduring unified enemies, but frictions were common with nearby groups competing for pasture, wells, and hunting routes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以通婚、互赠、迁徙回避与有限报复相结合，倾向避免长期大规模冲突。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations combined marriage ties, reciprocal exchange, migratory avoidance, and limited retaliation, generally favoring the avoidance of prolonged large-scale conflict.

era.zh: 南部非洲后石器时代至早期牧养过渡阶段
era.en: Southern African Later Stone Age to early pastoral transition

keyInnovations.zh: 毒箭狩猎技术、旱地生存知识、鸵鸟蛋壳珠制作、岩画传统与早期畜牧管理。
keyInnovations.en: Poison-arrow hunting, arid-land survival knowledge, ostrich-eggshell bead making, rock-art traditions, and early livestock management.

strengths.zh: 环境适应力强，机动性高，社会弹性大，冲突成本相对较低。
strengths.en: Strong environmental adaptation, high mobility, considerable social flexibility, and relatively low conflict costs.

weaknesses.zh: 人口分散、集中组织能力有限，难以支撑大型工程、常备军或广域政治整合。
weaknesses.en: Dispersed population and limited centralized organization made it difficult to sustain large works, standing forces, or broad political integration.

outlook.zh: 在生态条件稳定时可长期延续，但若外来扩张人口或气候压力增强，地方群体将更易迁移、分裂或被边缘化。
outlook.en: The system could endure for long periods under stable ecological conditions, but stronger external expansion or climatic stress would make local groups more prone to migration, fragmentation, or marginalization.

description.zh: 科伊桑诸部是在南部非洲以狩猎采集、早期牧养和长老协商治理为特征的分散部落社会。
description.en: The Khoisan peoples were a decentralized tribal society in southern Africa characterized by hunting-gathering, early pastoralism, and elder-based consensus governance.

governmentForm.zh: 以地方氏族和临时部落首领为核心的松散部落组织，依靠亲族、战斗威望与集体协商维系。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal organization centered on local clans and temporary tribal headmen, sustained by kinship, martial prestige, and collective consultation.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以小型聚落和流动性营地构成，亲族纽带在资源分配和婚姻安排中至关重要。长老与勇武者拥有较高影响力，但整体分层不深。人群主要从事采集、渔猎和局部园圃耕作，沿河地区定居性略强。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of small settlements and mobile camps, with kin ties crucial in resource allocation and marriage arrangements. Elders and successful warriors held influence, but stratification was shallow overall. People relied mainly on gathering, fishing, hunting, and localized horticulture, with somewhat more sedentary life along rivers.

rulingClass.zh: 并无稳定世袭贵族，首领多由战斗能力、调解才能和亲族支持推举。某些富有威望的家族可在数代内保持领导地位。
rulingClass.en: There was no stable hereditary nobility; leaders were elevated through fighting ability, mediation skills, and kin backing. Some prestigious families could maintain leadership across several generations.

succession.zh: 继承通常在同一家族或亲近支系中选择，但需得到长老和战士认可。若新首领缺乏号召力，部落可能分裂为多个独立群体。
succession.en: Succession usually drew from the same family or a close related branch, but required approval from elders and warriors. If a new headman lacked authority, the tribe could split into several autonomous groups.

structure.zh: 潘帕斯地区在220年前后尚未形成国家政权，治理主要依赖部落会议、长老共识和战时首领。河谷与草原地带的群体在狩猎区、采集地和迁徙路线方面进行松散协调。中央权力极弱，公共事务多为临时动员。
structure.en: Around 220 CE, the Pampas had not formed a state polity; governance relied on tribal councils, elder consensus, and wartime leaders. Groups in river valleys and grassland zones coordinated loosely over hunting grounds, gathering areas, and movement routes. Central power was very weak, and public affairs were handled through temporary mobilization.

function.zh: 安排狩猎区域、季节迁徙、食物分配与营地安全。
function.en: Organized hunting zones, seasonal movement, food distribution, and camp security.

function.zh: 处理婚姻、纠纷和部落仪式，维持群体内部和睦。
function.en: Handled marriage, disputes, and tribal rites, preserving internal harmony.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由各聚落长老和首领自行处理，常依河岸、泻湖或草场划分活动范围。各群体联系松散，更多依靠亲族网络而非正式行政。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was handled by elders and headmen within each settlement, often organized around riverbanks, lagoons, or grassland zones. Groups were loosely linked and depended more on kinship networks than formal administration.

legalSystem.zh: 法律为习惯法，强调亲族赔偿、婚盟责任和长老调解。严重冲突可能以决斗、报复或分群迁徙告终。
legalSystem.en: Law was customary, emphasizing kin compensation, marriage obligations, and elder mediation. Serious disputes could end in duels, retaliation, or fission and migration.

taxationSystem.zh: 无制度化税收，首领依靠猎获分配、馈赠和共同劳动建立权威。大型集会时，各群体会贡献食物、皮革和工具。
taxationSystem.en: There was no institutionalized taxation; leaders built authority through the distribution of game, gifts, and shared labor. During large gatherings, groups contributed food, hides, and tools.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 潘帕斯诸文化擅长因地制宜的草原生计、皮革加工、编织和沿河资源利用。其口传传统和祭仪活动对维系分散群体认同十分重要。
culturalAchievements.en: Pampas cultures were skilled in grassland subsistence, hide processing, weaving, and exploitation of riverine resources. Their oral traditions and rituals were important in maintaining identity among dispersed groups.

languageFamily.zh: 语言构成复杂，可能涉及后世潘帕斯与周边地区多种土著语言前身，未形成统一书面语。
languageFamily.en: Linguistic composition was complex, likely involving predecessors of several later Pampas and neighboring indigenous languages, without a unified written form.

philosophy.zh: 以万物有灵、祖先记忆和自然精灵观念为核心，重视狩猎、季节循环和土地神圣性。
philosophy.en: Centered on animism, ancestral memory, and beliefs in natural spirits, with strong emphasis on hunting, seasonal cycles, and the sacred quality of land.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，以口述史诗、歌谣、图纹装饰和仪式传承群体记忆。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; group memory was preserved through oral narratives, songs, decorative motifs, and ritual.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以狩猎、采集、捕鱼和局部园圃耕作为主，剩余水平较低。河流和湿地区域可提供较稳定食物来源，但整体仍以自给生计为核心。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on hunting, gathering, fishing, and localized horticulture, with low levels of surplus. Rivers and wetlands provided relatively stable food sources, but subsistence remained largely self-sufficient.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎采集、捕鱼、皮革加工、骨石器制作、编织、局部园圃农业
mainIndustries.en: Hunting and gathering, fishing, hide processing, bone and stone tool making, weaving, localized horticulture

tradeGoods.zh: 兽皮、鱼干、石器、骨器、编织品、羽饰、盐和染料
tradeGoods.en: Hides, dried fish, stone tools, bone tools, woven goods, feather ornaments, salt, and pigments

name.zh: 无铸币，以物物交换为主

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为食物储备、皮革、工具、渔猎技能和亲族联系。资源可移动性较高，不利于形成大规模积累。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth consisted mainly of food stores, hides, tools, fishing and hunting skill, and kin connections. The mobility of resources made large-scale accumulation difficult.

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭自给、亲族互助、季节性集会交换和首领再分配为特点的草原部落经济。
economicSystem.en: A grassland tribal economy characterized by household subsistence, kin reciprocity, seasonal gathering exchange, and headman redistribution.

treasuryDescription.zh: 储备主要表现为干鱼、皮革、石骨器和首领可调动的馈赠物资，而非集中贵金属库藏。
treasuryDescription.en: Reserves took the form of dried fish, hides, stone and bone tools, and gift goods that headmen could mobilize, not centralized precious-metal stores.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政取向偏向维持基本生存、安全与群体关系，避免过度集中资源于单一首领。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice aimed to maintain subsistence, security, and group relations while avoiding excessive concentration of resources in any single leader.

description.zh: 由亲族青年与善战者组成，负责护卫首领和营地核心区域。
description.en: Composed of kinsmen and capable fighters, protecting the headman and the camp core.

description.zh: 在部落冲突中临时集结，主要使用投枪、木矛、棍棒和石制武器。
description.en: Temporarily raised in tribal conflicts, using throwing spears, wooden lances, clubs, and stone weapons.

technology.zh: 军事技术基础，依赖步战、投射武器和地形熟悉，缺少金属兵器与正规工事。
technology.en: Military technology was basic, relying on infantry fighting, projectile weapons, and knowledge of terrain, with no metal arms system or formal fortifications.

threats.zh: 主要威胁为邻近部落争夺渔猎资源、河谷通道和季节性营地，以及偶发的报复性冲突。
threats.en: Main threats were neighboring tribal competition over fishing and hunting resources, river corridors, and seasonal camps, along with episodic retaliatory violence.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于潘帕斯草原、河谷和湿地边缘，以小型聚落与季节性营地为主。沿水域聚落略大，内陆群体流动性更高。
populationDescription.en: The population was distributed across Pampas grasslands, river valleys, and wetland margins, mainly in small settlements and seasonal camps. Waterside settlements were somewhat larger, while inland groups were more mobile.

socialClasses.zh: 可粗分为首领与长老、勇士与猎手、工艺与祭仪人物、普通家庭成员及依附亲族。
socialClasses.en: They may be broadly divided into headmen and elders, warriors and hunters, craft and ritual specialists, ordinary family members, and dependent kin.

allies.zh: 盟友多为邻近河谷和草原部落，通过通婚、共同狩猎与祭仪集会建立关系。
allies.en: Allies were mainly neighboring riverine and grassland tribes connected through marriage, joint hunts, and ritual gatherings.

enemies.zh: 无统一国家级敌人，敌对关系主要发生于争夺资源、边界和报复行动的部落之间。
enemies.en: There were no unified state-level enemies; hostility arose mainly among tribes competing over resources, boundaries, and revenge.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策重在维持邻近平衡、保障季节迁徙与资源获取，并通过婚盟减少长期纷争。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on maintaining local balance, securing seasonal movement and access to resources, and reducing prolonged conflict through marriage alliances.

era.zh: 南美南锥地区前陶或早期陶器并存的部落阶段
era.en: A tribal stage in the Southern Cone with pre-ceramic and early ceramic traditions coexisting

keyInnovations.zh: 草原与湿地复合生计、皮革处理、骨石器制作、简易编织与沿河定居模式。
keyInnovations.en: Mixed grassland-wetland subsistence, hide processing, bone and stone tool making, simple weaving, and river-oriented settlement patterns.

strengths.zh: 机动性强，能灵活适应草原和湿地环境，亲族网络有助于危机时互助。
strengths.en: They were highly mobile, adaptable to both grassland and wetland environments, and supported by kin networks useful in crisis.

weaknesses.zh: 人口规模小、政治分散、经济剩余有限，难以形成稳固的大型联盟或复杂政体。
weaknesses.en: Small population, political fragmentation, and limited economic surplus hindered the development of durable large alliances or complex polities.

outlook.zh: 在生态和资源条件平稳时可维持延续，但若遭遇持续资源压力或外来强势群体，其分散结构将使其更易重组或退避。
outlook.en: Under stable ecological and resource conditions the system could persist, but sustained pressure or stronger outside groups would make its dispersed structure more prone to reorganization or retreat.

description.zh: 潘帕斯文化是分布于南美草原与河谷地带、以渔猎采集和松散部落组织为特征的部落社会。
description.en: Pampas cultures were tribal societies of the South American grasslands and river valleys, characterized by fishing-hunting-gathering and loose tribal organization.

governmentForm.zh: 以村社与亲族为基础、由长老和酋长共同议事的松散部落治理体制。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal system based on villages and kin groups, governed through councils of elders and local chiefs.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以亲族、村落和年龄层级为核心，成员多从事渔猎、采集和小规模园圃种植。部落内部通常分为长老、战士、熟练工匠与普通家庭成员，各群体之间的界限较为流动。宗教仪式主持者在调解纠纷、解释自然现象和组织祭典方面具有重要地位。
socialStructure.en: Society centered on kinship, village life, and age hierarchy, with most people engaged in fishing, foraging, and small-scale horticulture. Within each tribe, elders, warriors, skilled artisans, and ordinary households formed broad social layers, though boundaries remained relatively fluid. Ritual specialists held influence in dispute mediation, explaining natural events, and organizing ceremonies.

rulingClass.zh: 统治层主要由部落长老、村社酋长与宗教祭仪主持者构成。其权威来自资历、声望、馈赠能力与战时领导，而非固定官僚职位。
rulingClass.en: The ruling stratum consisted mainly of tribal elders, village chiefs, and ritual leaders. Their authority rested on seniority, prestige, gift distribution, and wartime leadership rather than fixed bureaucratic office.

succession.zh: 继承通常依部落习俗在显赫家族或母系、父系亲族网络内推举产生。若首领能力不足，长老会议可支持更具威望的亲属或战士接替。
succession.en: Succession usually followed tribal custom within prominent families or maternal and paternal kin networks. If a leader proved ineffective, the council of elders could back a more respected relative or warrior.

structure.zh: 政治组织以分散村社为主，重大事项通过长老会议协商决定。战时或跨村社行动时，个别酋长可暂时取得更高号召力，但平时权力有限。
structure.en: Political organization was centered on dispersed villages, with major decisions made through councils of elders. In war or inter-village ventures, certain chiefs could gain temporary wider authority, but their normal power remained limited.

function.zh: 处理部落决策、纠纷调解、迁徙安排与对外交往。
function.en: Handled tribal decisions, dispute mediation, migration arrangements, and external relations.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、管理公共储藏、组织馈赠和季节性劳作。
function.en: Oversaw rituals, managed communal stores, and organized gift exchange and seasonal labor.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由各聚落首领和家族长者负责，没有严格层级官署。较大的岛屿上可能形成若干互相合作或竞争的村社网络。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was handled by settlement heads and clan elders, without a rigid hierarchy of offices. On larger islands, several village networks might cooperate or compete with one another.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要依口传习俗、赔偿原则和长老裁断执行。严重冲突常以馈赠、婚姻联盟、流放或报复平衡来解决。
legalSystem.en: Law operated mainly through oral custom, compensation, and judgments by elders. Serious disputes were often resolved through gift payments, marriage alliances, exile, or balanced retaliation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，公共资源来自家庭贡纳、共同劳动和战利品分配。首领的再分配能力是维持权威的重要基础。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; communal resources came from household contributions, shared labor, and division of spoils. A chief's ability to redistribute goods was a key basis of authority.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 擅长独木舟航行、岛际往来、渔猎技术和编织陶器等实用工艺。口头史诗、舞蹈和身体装饰在维系群体身份方面具有重要作用。
culturalAchievements.en: They were skilled in canoe navigation, inter-island travel, fishing, and practical crafts such as weaving and pottery. Oral epics, dance, and body ornamentation played major roles in sustaining group identity.

languageFamily.zh: 阿拉瓦克语系与加勒比语群并存
languageFamily.en: Arawakan and Cariban language groups

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇拜并重，强调人与海洋、森林、风暴及祖先之间的互惠关系。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor veneration predominated, emphasizing reciprocal relations between people, the sea, forests, storms, and ancestral spirits.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字系统，以口述传统、歌谣、纹饰与仪式记忆传承知识。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, designs, and ritual memory.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给性渔猎、采集和小规模木薯园圃种植为核心，市场交换程度有限。财富更多体现为食物储备、独木舟、工具、装饰物和亲族互助网络，而非货币积累。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was centered on subsistence fishing, foraging, and small cassava gardens, with only limited market exchange. Wealth was expressed more in food stores, canoes, tools, ornaments, and kin support networks than in monetary accumulation.

mainIndustries.zh: 渔业、采集、园圃农业、木器制作、陶器、编织
mainIndustries.en: Fishing, foraging, horticulture, woodworking, pottery, weaving

tradeGoods.zh: 鱼干、贝壳饰品、棉织物、木薯制品、羽毛、石器和独木舟
tradeGoods.en: Dried fish, shell ornaments, cotton textiles, cassava products, feathers, stone tools, and canoes

name.zh: 无铸币，以物易物与馈赠为主
name.en: No coined money; barter and gift exchange

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富普遍较低，但基本生计相对稳定。拥有大独木舟、储粮能力和广泛亲族联系的家庭更为富足。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was generally low, though basic subsistence was relatively stable. Families with large canoes, food storage capacity, and wide kin ties were better off.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产、公共分享和首领再分配为特征的部落互惠经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal reciprocal economy characterized by household production, communal sharing, and chiefly redistribution.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”主要表现为公共储粮、渔获储备、工具、装饰品和可用于馈赠的贵重物，而非集中金属货币。
treasuryDescription.en: The “treasury” mainly consisted of communal food stores, preserved fish, tools, ornaments, and prestige goods for redistribution rather than concentrated metallic money.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政强调维持公共储备、礼物交换和平衡各聚落关系，避免过度索取家庭资源。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice emphasized maintaining communal reserves, gift exchange, and balancing relations among settlements while avoiding excessive demands on households.

description.zh: 以矛、弓、木棒和盾牌作战，主要负责部落冲突与突袭。
description.en: Fought with spears, bows, clubs, and shields, mainly handling tribal conflict and raids.

description.zh: 利用独木舟进行岛际袭击、侦察和沿海机动作战。
description.en: Used canoes for inter-island raiding, reconnaissance, and coastal maneuver warfare.

commanderInChief.en: War chief chosen by the elders

technology.zh: 军备技术较低，以木石骨器和简易弓矛为主，但熟悉海上航行和热带环境作战。
technology.en: Military technology was low, relying on wood, stone, and bone weapons with simple bows and spears, though they were adept at seafaring and fighting in tropical environments.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部落竞争、资源短缺引发的冲突以及飓风和海况变化对聚落安全的间接影响。
threats.en: Main threats came from rival neighboring groups, conflicts driven by resource shortages, and the indirect impact of hurricanes and changing sea conditions on settlement security.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 单兵弩与床弩，中国军队核心远程武器
description.en: Infantry crossbow and mounted crossbow, core ranged weapon

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散居住于多个岛屿和沿海聚落，密度较低。多数居民生活在几十至数百人的村社中，季节性迁移较常见。
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across multiple islands and coastal settlements at low density. Most people lived in villages of dozens to a few hundred inhabitants, and seasonal movement was common.

name.zh: 小安的列斯独木舟港聚落

socialClasses.zh: 长老与酋长、祭仪主持者、战士、熟练工匠、普通家庭成员与俘获者或边缘附属人口。
socialClasses.en: Elders and chiefs, ritual specialists, warriors, skilled artisans, ordinary households, and captives or marginal dependent people.

allies.zh: 以邻近姻亲部落和临时海上交换伙伴为主，没有持久的超区域同盟。
allies.en: Allies were mainly nearby kin-linked tribes and temporary maritime exchange partners, without durable supra-regional alliances.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象通常是争夺渔场、耕地或航道的邻近部落。
enemies.en: Enemies were usually neighboring groups competing over fishing grounds, arable plots, or travel routes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持交换网络、避免长期消耗战并在必要时发动报复性突袭为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving exchange networks, avoiding prolonged attritional conflict, and launching retaliatory raids when necessary.

era.zh: 新石器至早期园圃农业阶段
era.en: Neolithic to early horticultural stage

keyInnovations.zh: 独木舟航海、木薯加工、渔网鱼叉、棉纺织、陶器烧制与热带资源利用知识。
keyInnovations.en: Canoe seafaring, cassava processing, nets and harpoons, cotton weaving, pottery firing, and detailed knowledge of tropical resource use.

strengths.zh: 熟悉海岛环境、机动性强、社会弹性较高，并具备稳定的口传传统与互助网络。
strengths.en: They knew the island environment well, were highly mobile, and possessed resilient social structures with stable oral traditions and mutual support networks.

weaknesses.zh: 人口规模小、缺乏金属器和大型组织能力，易受自然灾害与资源波动影响。
weaknesses.en: Their small population, lack of metal tools, and limited large-scale organization made them vulnerable to natural disasters and resource fluctuations.

outlook.zh: 短期内可维持分散稳定，但难形成统一政体，未来发展仍取决于岛际交流与生态条件。
outlook.en: In the short term they could remain stably dispersed, but they were unlikely to form a unified polity; future development depended on inter-island exchange and ecological conditions.

description.zh: 加勒比诸部落是以渔猎、采集和园圃农业为基础、依靠独木舟联结岛屿世界的分散部族社会。
description.en: The Caribbean peoples were dispersed tribal societies based on fishing, foraging, and horticulture, linked across the islands by canoe travel.

governmentForm.zh: 以氏族、部族和仪式网络为核心、由长老主导共识决策的分散治理体制。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized system centered on clans, tribes, and ritual networks, with elders leading through consensus.

socialStructure.zh: 社会组织以氏族、图腾分群、婚姻规则和仪式义务为基础，不同地区结构差异很大。长老、知识持有者和仪式主持者拥有较高威望，成年战士与狩猎者承担日常保护和资源获取职责。社会身份更多由亲属关系、圣地联系和梦时传统决定，而非固定财产等级。
socialStructure.en: Social organization was based on clans, totemic divisions, marriage rules, and ritual obligations, with considerable regional variation. Elders, custodians of knowledge, and ritual leaders held high prestige, while adult warriors and hunters managed protection and resource gathering. Social identity was shaped more by kinship, sacred-country ties, and Dreaming traditions than by fixed property classes.

rulingClass.zh: 并无统一的世袭贵族，领导层通常由长老、仪式知识持有者和有声望的狩猎或战斗首领组成。其权威依赖口传法、圣地知识、调停能力与慷慨分配。
rulingClass.en: There was no unified hereditary nobility; leadership generally consisted of elders, ritual knowledge holders, and respected hunting or war leaders. Their authority depended on command of customary law, sacred knowledge, mediation skill, and generous redistribution.

succession.zh: 继承通常不是单线固定传承，而是在合适亲属和长老认可下逐步取得地位。仪式知识和地权责任常由特定亲族群体传递。
succession.en: Succession was not usually a rigid single-line inheritance but a gradual assumption of status with the approval of kin and elders. Ritual knowledge and custodianship of land often passed within particular kin groups.

structure.zh: 政务主要由地方氏族与部族长老会议处理，各群体之间通过婚姻、仪式集会和圣地网络维持秩序。没有全国性中央机构，但在大型仪式会盟时可形成跨地区协调。
structure.en: Governance was handled mainly by local clan and tribal councils of elders, while marriage ties, ceremonial gatherings, and sacred-site networks maintained order between groups. There was no continent-wide central authority, though large ritual assemblies could create regional coordination.

function.zh: 裁决习俗争端、安排迁徙和狩猎区域、协调婚姻与部族关系。
function.en: Resolved customary disputes, arranged movements and hunting territories, and coordinated marriages and inter-group relations.

function.zh: 主持成年礼、歌线传承、圣地维护和季节性集会。
function.en: Oversaw initiation rites, songline transmission, sacred-site care, and seasonal gatherings.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政由各地氏族长者和土地守护者承担，决策通常通过共识达成。环境差异极大，因此海岸、沙漠、林地和河谷地区治理方式各不相同。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was carried out by clan elders and custodians of country, with decisions generally reached by consensus. Because environments varied greatly, governance differed across coasts, deserts, woodlands, and river valleys.

legalSystem.zh: 法律建立在口传习俗法和梦时秩序之上，强调对土地、亲属和仪式义务的遵守。处罚方式包括赔偿、公开仲裁、仪式性惩戒和关系修复。
legalSystem.en: Law rested on oral customary law and Dreaming order, emphasizing obligations to land, kin, and ritual practice. Punishments included compensation, public arbitration, ritual sanction, and restoration of social relations.

taxationSystem.zh: 无固定税收，公共资源依靠共同狩猎采集、礼物交换和仪式期间的食物集中。首领不能长期强制征敛，只能依靠威望组织资源。
taxationSystem.en: There was no fixed taxation; communal resources came from shared hunting and gathering, gift exchange, and concentration of food during ceremonies. Leaders could not permanently compel levies and depended instead on prestige to mobilize resources.

religion.zh: 梦时代信仰
religion.en: Dreamtime

culturalAchievements.zh: 在天文观察、地景记忆、火管理、复杂亲属制度和艺术表现方面极为发达。岩画、歌线和仪式舞蹈构成跨世代保存历史与地理知识的重要体系。
culturalAchievements.en: They were highly developed in astronomy, landscape memory, fire management, complex kinship systems, and artistic expression. Rock art, songlines, and ceremonial dance formed crucial systems for preserving history and geography across generations.

languageFamily.zh: 澳大利亚原住民诸语系，以帕马-纽恩甘语系最广
languageFamily.en: Australian Aboriginal language families, with Pama–Nyungan the most widespread

philosophy.zh: 以梦时传统为核心，认为祖灵在开创世界时确立了地貌、律法、亲属秩序与仪式路径。
philosophy.en: Dreaming traditions lay at the core of worldview, holding that ancestral beings shaped the landscape, law, kinship order, and ceremonial pathways.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过歌线、岩画、身体彩绘、雕刻与口述仪式系统传承。
writingSystem.en: There was no developed writing; knowledge was transmitted through songlines, rock art, body painting, carving, and oral ceremonial systems.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以狩猎、采集、渔捞和区域性资源管理为主，某些地区结合了半定居营地和水产利用。财富体现为对水源、圣地、工具石料、食物储备和交换网络的控制，而非货币收入。
gdpDescription.en: The economy relied on hunting, gathering, fishing, and regional resource management, with some areas featuring semi-settled camps and intensive aquatic use. Wealth was expressed through control of water sources, sacred places, tool stone, food reserves, and exchange networks rather than money income.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、采集、渔业、石器加工、木器制作、火管理
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, gathering, fishing, stoneworking, woodworking, fire management

tradeGoods.zh: 石斧胚料、赭石、贝饰、木矛、树脂、食物、羽毛与仪式物品
tradeGoods.en: Stone axe blanks, ochre, shell ornaments, wooden spears, resin, foodstuffs, feathers, and ritual items

name.zh: 无铸币，以礼物交换为主
name.en: No coined money; gift exchange predominated

householdWealth.zh: 家庭物质财富不高，但依托土地知识和亲属网络可维持较强生存能力。掌握优质水源、石料产地或重要歌线节点的群体更具优势。
householdWealth.en: Material household wealth was modest, but deep knowledge of country and kin networks provided strong survival capacity. Groups controlling good water sources, stone quarries, or important songline nodes enjoyed greater advantage.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以亲属共享、土地守护和长距离礼物交换构成的部落互惠经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal reciprocal economy structured by kin sharing, custodianship of country, and long-distance gift exchange.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 公共财富主要由食物储备、赭石、石器胚料、仪式用品及可再分配物资构成，并非金属库藏。
treasuryDescription.en: Public wealth consisted mainly of food reserves, ochre, stone blanks, ceremonial items, and redistributable goods rather than metal hoards.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政运作以维持仪式秩序、支持跨部族关系和保障旱季生存为重点，征集方式较为柔性。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on maintaining ceremonial order, supporting intertribal relations, and securing survival in dry seasons, with relatively flexible resource collection.

description.zh: 以矛、投矛器、棍棒和盾牌为主，擅长伏击、追踪和小规模冲突。
