description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

populationDescription.zh: 分布于南美东部从亚马逊到拉普拉塔的广阔区域。
populationDescription.en: Distributed across eastern South America from the Amazon to the Río de la Plata.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段
era.en: Neolithic to early tribal stage

keyInnovations.zh: 热带园圃农业、长屋建造、独木舟。
keyInnovations.en: Tropical horticulture, longhouse construction, canoes.

strengths.zh: 人口众多，分布广泛，农业稳定。
strengths.en: Large population, wide distribution, stable agriculture.

weaknesses.zh: 政治极度分散。
weaknesses.en: Extremely fragmented politically.

outlook.zh: 将持续扩展至更广大的区域。
outlook.en: Would continue expanding across a larger area.

description.zh: 瓜拉尼-图皮诸部是南美东部最广泛分布的语系部族，以木薯农业和长屋文化闻名。
description.en: The Guaraní-Tupí Peoples were the most widely distributed language-family group in eastern South America, known for manioc agriculture and longhouse culture.

governmentForm.zh: 以氏族为基本单位的分散部族社会，由长老与萨满共同主持事务，无集中政权。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal society with the clan as the basic unit, led jointly by elders and shamans, with no centralized authority.

socialStructure.zh: 社会高度平等，以血缘氏族为核心。个人地位取决于狩猎技能、年龄和萨满灵力。营地规模通常为二十至五十人的游动小群，随季节迁徙追逐驯鹿群和渔场。
socialStructure.en: Society was highly egalitarian, centered on blood-kin clans. Status depended on hunting skill, age, and shamanic power. Camps typically comprised mobile bands of twenty to fifty people, migrating seasonally to follow reindeer herds and fishing grounds.

rulingClass.zh: 无严格统治阶层，长老和萨满凭借经验与精神权威获得尊崇，其领导力基于共识而非强制。
rulingClass.en: No strict ruling class; elders and shamans gained respect through experience and spiritual authority, leading by consensus rather than coercion.

succession.zh: 领导权由氏族内部协商产生，通常传于有声望的年长猎人或萨满，不存在世袭制度。
succession.en: Leadership was chosen by clan consensus, typically passing to a prestigious senior hunter or shaman; no hereditary system existed.

structure.zh: 古西伯利亚诸部在公元220年前后分散于从叶尼塞河到楚科奇半岛的广阔区域，包括尤卡吉尔人、楚科奇先民、科里亚克先民、凯特人等多个语言不相通的群体。治理完全依靠氏族议事、长老裁断和萨满仪式指导，无统一政治组织。
structure.en: Around 220 CE the Paleo-Siberian peoples were scattered across the vast territory from the Yenisei River to the Chukchi Peninsula, comprising linguistically diverse groups including proto-Yukaghir, proto-Chukchi, proto-Koryak, and Ket peoples. Governance relied entirely on clan councils, elder judgments, and shamanic ritual guidance, with no unified political organization.

function.zh: 协调营地迁移、狩猎分工、氏族间纠纷调解和季节性集会安排。
function.en: Coordinated camp movements, hunting division of labor, inter-clan dispute resolution, and seasonal gatherings.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、占卜、治病仪式，引导迁徙方向和狩猎时机。
function.en: Conducted sacrifices, divination, and healing ceremonies, guiding migration routes and hunting timing.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理即各氏族营地自治，由长老与猎长协调日常事务。各营地之间联系松散，仅在特定季节的集会中交流信息和缔结婚姻。
localAdmin.en: Local administration meant clan-camp self-governance, with elders and hunt leaders coordinating daily affairs. Inter-camp links were loose, with information exchange and marriage alliances only at seasonal gatherings.

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法和血亲复仇为基础，纠纷主要通过长老调解解决。严重违规者可被驱逐出营。
legalSystem.en: Based on customary law and blood-kinship vengeance; disputes were mainly resolved through elder mediation. Severe offenders could be expelled from camp.

taxationSystem.zh: 无税制。猎获物按习惯分配给全营成员，首领依靠声望获取馈赠而非强制征收。
taxationSystem.en: No taxation. Game was shared among all camp members by custom; leaders relied on prestige gifts rather than compulsory levies.

religion.zh: 萨满教与万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Shamanism and animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 古西伯利亚诸部发展出高度适应极寒环境的生存技术，包括驯鹿追猎法、冰下捕鱼术、兽皮缝制御寒服饰和骨角雕刻艺术。其萨满教传统是东北亚最古老的精神体系之一，通过鼓乐、舞蹈和迷幻仪式沟通灵界。
culturalAchievements.en: Paleo-Siberian peoples developed highly adapted survival skills for extreme cold, including reindeer stalking, under-ice fishing, sewn hide clothing, and bone-antler carving arts. Their shamanic tradition was among the oldest spiritual systems in Northeast Asia, communicating with the spirit world through drumming, dance, and trance rituals.

languageFamily.zh: 古西伯利亚诸语系（尤卡吉尔语、楚科奇-堪察加语、叶尼塞语等，彼此无亲属关系）
languageFamily.en: Paleo-Siberian language families (Yukaghir, Chukotko-Kamchatkan, Yeniseian, etc.; mutually unrelated)

philosophy.zh: 核心世界观认为山川、河流、动物和风暴均有灵魂，人类须通过萨满沟通自然灵力以维持世界平衡。驯鹿被视为神圣的生存伙伴。
philosophy.en: The core worldview held that mountains, rivers, animals, and storms all possessed spirits, and humans needed shamans to communicate with natural forces to maintain cosmic balance. Reindeer were regarded as sacred partners of survival.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字体系，知识通过口传神话、歌谣和岩画传承。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral mythology, songs, and rock art.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济完全依赖狩猎（驯鹿、海豹、熊）、淡水与海洋捕鱼、采集浆果与根茎。各氏族在广袤的冻土带和泰加林间季节性迁徙，积累极为有限，生存本身即是首要经济活动。
gdpDescription.en: The economy depended entirely on hunting (reindeer, seals, bears), freshwater and marine fishing, and gathering berries and roots. Clans migrated seasonally across the vast tundra and taiga, with extremely limited accumulation; survival itself was the primary economic activity.

mainIndustries.zh: 驯鹿狩猎、捕鱼、采集、皮革加工、骨角器制作
mainIndustries.en: Reindeer hunting, fishing, gathering, leather working, bone and antler crafts

tradeGoods.zh: 毛皮、海象牙、驯鹿角、干鱼、兽骨工具
tradeGoods.en: Furs, walrus ivory, reindeer antlers, dried fish, bone tools

name.zh: 无铸币，以毛皮和实物交换为主
name.en: No coinage; furs and barter predominated

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富以驯鹿皮帐篷、雪橇、狩猎工具和毛皮储备为主。财富差异极小，强调共享分配。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth consisted mainly of reindeer-hide tents, sleds, hunting tools, and fur stores. Wealth disparity was minimal, with emphasis on communal sharing.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以氏族共享、猎获平均分配和小规模氏族间物物交换为基础的狩猎采集经济。
economicSystem.en: A hunting-gathering economy based on clan sharing, equal distribution of game, and small-scale inter-clan barter.

grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: "储备"主要为氏族共有的干肉、干鱼、毛皮存量和骨角器工具，由长老分配管理。
treasuryDescription.en: "Reserves" consisted mainly of communal stores of dried meat, dried fish, furs, and bone-antler tools, managed and distributed by elders.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以维持过冬储备和保障全营生存为核心的分配制度。
fiscalPolicy.en: A distribution system centered on maintaining winter reserves and ensuring the survival of the entire camp.

description.zh: 由成年男性猎手组成，平时狩猎、战时为战士，使用弓箭、骨矛和石刀。
description.en: Composed of adult male hunters who served as warriors in wartime, using bows, bone spears, and stone knives.

technology.zh: 以弓箭、骨矛、石刀和陷阱为主要武器，无金属冶炼。部分群体使用驯鹿骨甲片制作简易护甲。
technology.en: Bows, bone spears, stone knives, and traps were the main weapons; no metal smelting. Some groups crafted simple armor from reindeer bone plates.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 匈奴余部和鲜卑骑兵的北压、相邻氏族间的猎场争夺，以及极端气候威胁。
threats.en: Pressure from Xiongnu remnants and Xianbei cavalry to the south, hunting-ground disputes with neighboring clans, and extreme climate threats.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 复合弓或长弓，远射主力
description.en: Composite or longbow, primary ranged weapon

populationDescription.zh: 分布于从叶尼塞河流域到楚科奇半岛、堪察加半岛的广阔西伯利亚冻土和泰加林地区，人口密度极低，每百平方公里不足一人。
populationDescription.en: Distributed across the vast Siberian tundra and taiga from the Yenisei River basin to the Chukchi and Kamchatka Peninsulas, with extremely low population density of less than one person per hundred square kilometers.

socialClasses.zh: 长老、萨满、猎手、普通成员。社会等级极为扁平。
socialClasses.en: Elders, shamans, hunters, common members. Social hierarchy was extremely flat.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，各氏族依靠婚姻联盟和季节性集会维持松散联系。
allies.en: No fixed allies; clans maintained loose ties through marriage alliances and seasonal gatherings.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但南方的鲜卑、丁零等游牧民族有时向北侵扰猎场。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though Xianbei, Dingling, and other nomadic peoples to the south sometimes encroached on northern hunting grounds.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以回避冲突、保全猎场和维持氏族间互利交换为基本策略。
foreignPolicy.en: Basic strategy was conflict avoidance, preservation of hunting grounds, and maintaining mutually beneficial inter-clan exchange.

era.zh: 石器时代晚期至骨角器时代
era.en: Late Stone Age to Bone-Antler Age

keyInnovations.zh: 驯鹿雪橇、冰下捕鱼技术、兽皮缝制技术、骨角雕刻、萨满鼓。
keyInnovations.en: Reindeer sleds, under-ice fishing techniques, sewn hide clothing, bone-antler carving, shamanic drums.

strengths.zh: 对极寒环境的高度适应力，广阔的活动范围使其难以被征服，毛皮资源丰富。
strengths.en: Highly adapted to extreme cold; vast range made them difficult to conquer; rich fur resources.

weaknesses.zh: 人口极度稀疏，技术水平低，无金属冶炼，政治极度分散，无法形成有效的联合防御。
weaknesses.en: Extremely sparse population, low technology, no metalworking, highly fragmented politically, unable to form effective united defense.

outlook.zh: 将在数百年内继续保持分散的狩猎采集生活方式，直到突厥系和蒙古系民族逐步渗透西伯利亚。
outlook.en: Would continue their dispersed hunting-gathering lifestyle for centuries until Turkic and Mongolic peoples gradually penetrated Siberia.

description.zh: 古西伯利亚诸部是公元220年广泛分布于西伯利亚冻土和泰加林地带的多个语言互不相通的狩猎采集民族总称，包括尤卡吉尔人、楚科奇先民、科里亚克先民、凯特人等。他们以驯鹿追猎、渔业和采集为生，发展出高度适应极寒环境的生存技术和深厚的萨满教传统。这些分散的氏族群体占据了从叶尼塞河到太平洋沿岸的广阔区域，人口密度极低但文化独特，是东北亚最古老的原住民群体之一。
description.en: The Paleo-Siberian Peoples was a collective term for multiple linguistically unrelated hunting-gathering peoples widely distributed across the Siberian tundra and taiga around 220 CE, including the Yukaghir, proto-Chukchi, proto-Koryak, and Ket peoples. They subsisted on reindeer stalking, fishing, and foraging, developing highly adapted survival skills for extreme cold and a deep shamanic tradition. These scattered clan groups occupied a vast territory from the Yenisei River to the Pacific coast, with extremely low population density but distinctive cultures, constituting one of the oldest indigenous groups of Northeast Asia.

governmentForm.zh: 以部族和氏族为基本单位的半游牧社会，由首领与长老协商治理，无统一政权。
governmentForm.en: A semi-nomadic society with tribes and clans as basic units, governed by chiefs and elders through consultation, with no unified polity.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以父系氏族为核心，数个氏族组成部族。首领权威来自狩猎能力、牲畜财富和军事声望。养驯鹿的氏族地位较高，纯靠渔猎的氏族地位稍低。社会等级较为扁平但已有初步分化。
socialStructure.en: Society centered on patrilineal clans, with several clans forming a tribe. Chiefly authority derived from hunting prowess, livestock wealth, and military prestige. Reindeer-herding clans held higher status than purely fishing-hunting clans. Social hierarchy was relatively flat but showing early differentiation.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老，其权力基于共识和个人威望。
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders, whose authority rested on consensus and personal prestige.

succession.zh: 首领地位通常在特定家族内传承，但须获得部族长老的认可。军事才能和驯鹿群的规模是重要考量因素。
succession.en: Chieftainship typically passed within certain families but required endorsement from tribal elders. Military talent and size of reindeer herds were important considerations.

structure.zh: 通古斯诸部在公元220年前后散布于外满洲、黑龙江流域至外兴安岭和贝加尔湖以东地区。中国史书称之为"挹娄"或"肃慎"后裔的北方林中之民。各部族独立自治，通过季节性集会和氏族间婚姻维持松散联系。
structure.en: Around 220 CE the Tungusic peoples were scattered across Outer Manchuria, the Amur River basin, the Stanovoy Range, and areas east of Lake Baikal. Chinese historical records referred to them as northern forest-dwellers descended from the Yilou or Sushen. Each tribe was self-governing, maintaining loose connections through seasonal gatherings and inter-clan marriages.

function.zh: 协调迁徙路线、猎区分配、氏族间纠纷调解和对外事务。
function.en: Coordinated migration routes, hunting-ground allocation, inter-clan dispute resolution, and external affairs.

function.zh: 主持驯鹿祭祀、祖灵祭典、占卜和治病仪式。
function.en: Presided over reindeer sacrifices, ancestral rites, divination, and healing ceremonies.

localAdmin.zh: 各氏族营地由长老和猎长自行管理，部族首领对跨氏族事务拥有协调权但缺乏强制力。
localAdmin.en: Each clan camp was self-managed by elders and hunt leaders; the tribal chief had coordinating authority over inter-clan matters but lacked coercive power.

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法为主，重视氏族荣誉与血亲复仇。严重纠纷由部族首领和长老会仲裁。
legalSystem.en: Primarily customary law, emphasizing clan honor and blood-kin vengeance. Serious disputes were arbitrated by the tribal chief and council of elders.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，首领通过猎获分享、馈赠互惠和对部族成员提供庇护换取忠诚和劳力支持。
taxationSystem.en: No formal taxation; chiefs obtained loyalty and labor support through sharing game, reciprocal gifts, and offering protection to tribal members.

religion.zh: 萨满教
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 通古斯诸部发展出成熟的驯鹿驯养技术和高度适应东北亚针叶林环境的生活方式。他们的萨满教体系极为发达，通古斯语中的"萨满"（šaman）一词后来成为全世界对这类宗教现象的通用称谓。铁器已通过与南方挹娄和扶余的交换进入部分群体。
culturalAchievements.en: Tungusic peoples developed sophisticated reindeer domestication and a lifestyle highly adapted to the Northeast Asian boreal forest. Their shamanic system was extremely well-developed; the Tungusic word 'šaman' later became the universal term for this religious phenomenon worldwide. Iron implements had reached some groups through exchange with the Yilou and Buyeo to the south.

languageFamily.zh: 通古斯语族（阿尔泰语系假说成员，与满语、女真语同源）
languageFamily.en: Tungusic language family (hypothesized member of the Altaic group; cognate with Manchu and Jurchen)

philosophy.zh: 相信万物皆有灵魂，尤其尊崇熊灵和驯鹿灵。萨满通过击鼓、舞蹈和入迷状态沟通灵界，充当人与自然之间的中介。祖先崇拜是精神生活的重要组成部分。
philosophy.en: Believed all things possessed souls, with special reverence for bear and reindeer spirits. Shamans communicated with the spirit world through drumming, dance, and trance states, serving as intermediaries between humans and nature. Ancestor worship was an important part of spiritual life.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，依靠口传神话、歌谣和萨满经文（口诵）传承知识。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral myths, songs, and shamanic chants.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以驯鹿牧养、狩猎（鹿、熊、貂）和河流捕鱼为支柱。部分南方群体已从挹娄和扶余获取铁器并开始小规模粟种植。毛皮是与南方政权交换的主要商品。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on reindeer herding, hunting (deer, bears, sable), and river fishing. Some southern groups had obtained iron implements from the Yilou and Buyeo and begun small-scale millet cultivation. Furs were the main commodity traded with southern polities.

mainIndustries.zh: 驯鹿牧养、狩猎、渔业、毛皮加工、骨角器制作
mainIndustries.en: Reindeer herding, hunting, fishing, fur processing, bone-antler crafts

tradeGoods.zh: 貂皮、鹿茸、驯鹿皮、干鱼、蜂蜜
tradeGoods.en: Sable furs, deer antler velvet, reindeer hides, dried fish, honey

name.zh: 无铸币，以毛皮和铁器为交换媒介
name.en: No coinage; furs and iron implements served as exchange media

householdWealth.zh: 财富以驯鹿群规模、毛皮储备和铁器数量衡量。拥有较大驯鹿群的家庭享有更高社会地位。
householdWealth.en: Wealth was measured by reindeer herd size, fur stores, and number of iron implements. Families with larger herds enjoyed higher social standing.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以驯鹿牧养和狩猎采集为基础的部族经济，辅以与南方政权的毛皮交换。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy based on reindeer herding and hunting-gathering, supplemented by fur trade with southern polities.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: "储备"主要为首领控制的毛皮、铁器和干粮存量。
treasuryDescription.en: "Reserves" consisted mainly of chiefly controlled stores of furs, iron implements, and dried provisions.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以维持冬季储备、保障驯鹿群和获取南方铁器为核心。
fiscalPolicy.en: Centered on maintaining winter reserves, safeguarding reindeer herds, and acquiring iron from the south.

description.zh: 以成年男性为主，使用弓箭、铁刃矛和骨刀作战。部分精锐战士拥有南方换来的铁甲片。善于利用森林地形伏击。
description.en: Primarily adult males armed with bows, iron-tipped spears, and bone knives. Some elite warriors possessed iron armor plates acquired from the south. Skilled in forest-terrain ambushes.

technology.zh: 以弓箭和铁刃矛为主，部分群体已使用少量铁制甲片。擅长密林伏击和冬季雪地追击。
technology.en: Primarily bows and iron-tipped spears; some groups used limited iron armor plates. Excelled at dense-forest ambushes and winter snow pursuits.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 南方扶余国和高句丽的扩张压力，西方鲜卑骑兵的侵扰，以及部族间的驯鹿群和猎场争夺。
threats.en: Expansion pressure from Buyeo and Goguryeo to the south, Xianbei cavalry raids from the west, and inter-tribal competition over reindeer herds and hunting grounds.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 复合弓或长弓，远射主力
description.en: Composite or longbow, primary ranged weapon

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

populationDescription.zh: 分布于外满洲、黑龙江中上游、外兴安岭至贝加尔湖以东的广阔针叶林和河谷地带。人口密度低，以小型部族营地为聚居形式。
populationDescription.en: Distributed across Outer Manchuria, the middle and upper Amur, the Stanovoy Range, and areas east of Lake Baikal in vast boreal forests and river valleys. Low population density, settled in small tribal camps.

socialClasses.zh: 部族首领家族、长老、萨满、自由牧猎者、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, shamans, free herder-hunters, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，部族间通过婚姻和毛皮交换维持合作关系。
allies.en: No fixed allies; inter-tribal cooperation maintained through marriages and fur exchange.

enemies.zh: 与南方扶余国和高句丽存在间歇性冲突，西方鲜卑部族亦偶有侵扰。
enemies.en: Intermittent conflicts with Buyeo and Goguryeo to the south; Xianbei tribes to the west also occasionally raided.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以回避南方强权、维持猎区安全和通过毛皮交换获取铁器为基本策略。
foreignPolicy.en: Basic strategy was to avoid southern powers, maintain hunting-ground security, and acquire iron through fur exchange.

era.zh: 早期铁器渗透阶段
era.en: Early iron-penetration stage

keyInnovations.zh: 驯鹿驯养术、毛皮鞣制技术、桦树皮舟、铁器使用（来自南方交换）、萨满鼓。
keyInnovations.en: Reindeer domestication, fur tanning, birch-bark canoes, iron tool use (from southern exchange), shamanic drums.

strengths.zh: 驯鹿牧养和森林狩猎技术成熟，对东北亚针叶林环境高度适应，毛皮资源丰富，善于森林游击战。
strengths.en: Mature reindeer herding and forest hunting skills, highly adapted to Northeast Asian boreal forests, rich fur resources, skilled in forest guerrilla warfare.

weaknesses.zh: 人口稀少，缺乏自主冶铁能力，政治分散无法形成统一抵御南方强权的力量。
weaknesses.en: Sparse population, lacking indigenous iron smelting, politically fragmented and unable to form unified resistance against southern powers.

outlook.zh: 将在未来数百年间逐步发展壮大，其后裔靺鞨和女真最终建立渤海国和金朝，直至满族建立清朝。
outlook.en: Would gradually develop over the following centuries; their descendants the Mohe and Jurchen would eventually establish the Balhae kingdom and Jin dynasty, culminating in the Manchu founding of the Qing dynasty.

description.zh: 通古斯诸部是公元220年分布于外满洲和东西伯利亚针叶林地带的半游牧民族群体，中国史书中与"挹娄""肃慎"等记载相关联。他们以驯鹿牧养、狩猎和渔业为生，已通过与南方扶余和挹娄的交换获得少量铁器。通古斯萨满教体系极为发达，其"萨满"一词后来成为全球通用术语。这些分散的部族虽在当时默默无闻，却是后世女真、满洲等改变东亚格局的民族的直接祖先。
description.en: The Tungusic Peoples were semi-nomadic groups distributed across Outer Manchuria and the East Siberian boreal forests around 220 CE, associated with the 'Yilou' and 'Sushen' in Chinese historical records. They subsisted on reindeer herding, hunting, and fishing, having obtained limited iron implements through exchange with the Buyeo and Yilou to the south. The Tungusic shamanic system was highly sophisticated, and their word 'šaman' later became the universal global term. Though obscure at this time, these scattered tribes were the direct ancestors of the Jurchen and Manchu peoples who would later reshape East Asian history.

name.zh: 南岛海洋酋邦（努山塔拉）

dynasty.zh: 无统一王朝，各酋邦独立

capital.zh: 无统一都城（苏门答腊、爪哇和加里曼丹沿海分布多个酋邦中心）
capital.en: No unified capital (multiple chiefdom centers along the coasts of Sumatra, Java, and Borneo)

governmentForm.zh: 以港口聚落为中心的海洋酋邦体制，由达图（酋长）统治，各酋邦独立但通过贸易和婚姻联盟保持联系。
governmentForm.en: A maritime chiefdom system centered on port settlements, ruled by datus (chiefs), with independent chiefdoms linked through trade and marriage alliances.

socialStructure.zh: 社会分为达图（酋长）家族、自由民和依附者三个层级。达图家族垄断对外贸易和宗教仪式，自由民从事农耕、捕鱼和手工业，依附者为债务或战俘转化的劳动者。航海能力和贸易网络是社会威望的重要来源。
socialStructure.en: Society was stratified into datu (chief) families, freemen, and dependents. Datu families monopolized external trade and religious rituals; freemen engaged in farming, fishing, and crafts; dependents were laborers derived from debt or war captives. Seafaring ability and trade networks were major sources of social prestige.

rulingClass.zh: 达图家族与海洋贸易精英，其权威源于控制港口贸易、宗教仪式权和与印度、中国商人的外交关系。
rulingClass.en: Datu families and maritime trade elites, whose authority stemmed from control of port trade, religious ritual prerogatives, and diplomatic ties with Indian and Chinese merchants.

succession.zh: 酋长地位通常在达图家族内世袭，但须获得长老和贸易精英的支持。能力不足的继承人可被取代。
succession.en: Chieftainship was usually hereditary within the datu family but required endorsement from elders and trade elites. Incapable heirs could be replaced.

structure.zh: 公元220年的努山塔拉（今印度尼西亚群岛）由众多独立酋邦组成，分布于苏门答腊东岸、爪哇北岸和加里曼丹沿海。各酋邦控制一个或数个港口聚落及其腹地，通过海上贸易与印度商人和汉末中国商人保持联系。此时尚无统一的国家形态，但部分较大酋邦已显示出向早期国家演化的迹象——这些酋邦在两三百年后将催生室利佛逝等海洋帝国。
structure.en: The Nusantara (present-day Indonesian archipelago) around 220 CE comprised numerous independent chiefdoms along the eastern coast of Sumatra, northern coast of Java, and coastal Borneo. Each chiefdom controlled one or several port settlements and their hinterlands, maintaining contacts with Indian merchants and late-Han Chinese traders through maritime trade. No unified state form existed yet, but some larger chiefdoms were already showing signs of evolution toward early statehood — these chiefdoms would give rise to maritime empires like Srivijaya within two to three centuries.

function.zh: 管理港口税收、外来商船停泊、货物仓储和贸易纠纷裁决。
function.en: Managed port levies, foreign vessel mooring, cargo warehousing, and trade dispute adjudication.

function.zh: 主持祖灵祭祀、航海祈福仪式，以及与印度和中国商人的外交接待。
function.en: Presided over ancestral rites, seafaring benediction ceremonies, and diplomatic reception of Indian and Chinese merchants.

localAdmin.zh: 各港口聚落由达图直接管理，内陆农业村落由村长管理并向达图纳贡。治理范围通常不超出港口周边数十公里。
localAdmin.en: Port settlements were directly managed by datus; inland agricultural villages were managed by village heads who paid tribute to datus. Governance typically extended no more than a few dozen kilometers around the port.

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法（阿达特法）为基础，由达图和长老裁决纠纷。贸易纠纷有专门仲裁机制。
legalSystem.en: Based on customary law (adat); disputes were adjudicated by datus and elders. Trade disputes had specialized arbitration mechanisms.

taxationSystem.zh: 通过港口贸易税、过境费、内陆村落实物贡赋和劳役征发获取收入。外国商船须缴纳停泊费和货物税。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue from port trade taxes, transit fees, in-kind tribute from inland villages, and corvée labor levies. Foreign vessels were required to pay mooring fees and cargo duties.

religion.zh: 南岛原始信仰（万物有灵与祖灵崇拜），印度教和佛教元素开始传入
religion.en: Austronesian indigenous beliefs (animism and ancestor worship), with Hindu and Buddhist elements beginning to arrive

culturalAchievements.zh: 南岛酋邦发展出当时世界最先进的航海技术之一，能够建造可承载数十人的大型双体船和外撑架船，凭借星象和洋流知识在广阔的印度洋-太平洋水域航行。他们是连接印度与中国之间"海上丝绸之路"的关键中介。此时印度文化要素——梵文、印度教神话、佛教概念——正通过贸易渠道开始渗透，为后来的印度化时期奠定基础。铜鼓文化（东山文化影响）在群岛中广泛传播。
culturalAchievements.en: Austronesian chiefdoms developed some of the most advanced seafaring technology in the contemporary world, capable of building large double-hulled and outrigger canoes carrying dozens of people, navigating the vast Indian-Pacific waters using celestial and current knowledge. They were key intermediaries on the 'Maritime Silk Road' between India and China. Indian cultural elements — Sanskrit, Hindu mythology, Buddhist concepts — were beginning to penetrate through trade channels, laying the groundwork for the later Indianization period. Bronze drum culture (Dong Son influence) was widely distributed across the archipelago.

languageFamily.zh: 南岛语系（马来-波利尼西亚语族）
languageFamily.en: Austronesian language family (Malayo-Polynesian branch)

philosophy.zh: 核心信仰以祖灵崇拜和万物有灵为主，认为山川、海洋和风暴均有灵力。航海前须举行祭祀仪式以求庇佑。印度教和佛教的宇宙观正在通过印度商人的传播开始被精英阶层接纳。
philosophy.en: Core beliefs centered on ancestor worship and animism, holding that mountains, seas, and storms all possessed spiritual power. Sacrificial rites were performed before voyages to seek divine protection. Hindu and Buddhist cosmological concepts were beginning to be adopted by elites through dissemination by Indian merchants.

writingSystem.zh: 尚无本土成熟文字，但印度帕拉瓦文字已随商人传入沿海酋邦精英圈，为后来的古爪哇文和古马来文奠定基础。
writingSystem.en: No mature indigenous script yet, but Indian Pallava script had already reached coastal chiefdom elites via merchants, laying the foundation for later Old Javanese and Old Malay scripts.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以海上贸易为核心驱动力，辅以水稻种植、渔业和林产品采集。苏门答腊和加里曼丹出产的樟脑、沉香、丁香和其他香料是国际贸易中的高价值商品，通过马六甲海峡和巽他海峡出口至印度和中国。沿海酋邦通过控制海峡贸易通道获取巨大利润。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was driven by maritime trade, supplemented by wet-rice cultivation, fishing, and forest-product collection. Camphor, agarwood, cloves, and other spices from Sumatra and Borneo were high-value commodities in international trade, exported through the Strait of Malacca and Sunda Strait to India and China. Coastal chiefdoms derived enormous profits from controlling these strait trade routes.

mainIndustries.zh: 海上贸易、水稻种植、渔业、香料采集、樟脑生产、造船业
mainIndustries.en: Maritime trade, wet-rice cultivation, fishing, spice collection, camphor production, shipbuilding

tradeGoods.zh: 樟脑、沉香、丁香、肉豆蔻、檀香木、玳瑁、珍珠、金砂、锡
tradeGoods.en: Camphor, agarwood, cloves, nutmeg, sandalwood, tortoiseshell, pearls, gold dust, tin

name.zh: 以物物交换为主，金砂和珠饰作为价值衡量
name.en: Primarily barter, with gold dust and bead ornaments as value measures

householdWealth.zh: 达图家族拥有船队、港口设施和大量贸易品；自由民拥有水田、渔船和手工工具；依附者仅有少量个人财物。
householdWealth.en: Datu families owned fleets, port facilities, and large trade stocks; freemen owned rice paddies, fishing boats, and craft tools; dependents held only minimal personal possessions.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以港口贸易为核心、水稻农业和渔业为基础的海洋酋邦经济。达图家族垄断对外贸易，内陆村落提供农业剩余和劳役。
economicSystem.en: A maritime chiefdom economy centered on port trade, with wet-rice agriculture and fishing as the base. Datu families monopolized external trade; inland villages supplied agricultural surplus and labor.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

source.en: Port trade taxes and mooring fees

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 各酋邦的"国库"主要为达图家族控制的金砂、珠饰、贸易品储备和船只资产。
treasuryDescription.en: Each chiefdom's "treasury" consisted mainly of gold dust, bead valuables, trade-good reserves, and vessel assets controlled by the datu family.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以扩大港口贸易规模、吸引外国商船和积累贸易品储备为核心财政目标。
fiscalPolicy.en: Core fiscal goals were expanding port trade volume, attracting foreign vessels, and accumulating trade-good reserves.

description.zh: 驾驶战船保护贸易航线，打击海盗，必要时对敌对酋邦发动海上突袭。装备铁矛、短剑和盾牌。
description.en: Sailed warships to protect trade routes, combat piracy, and launch maritime raids against hostile chiefdoms when necessary. Armed with iron spears, short swords, and shields.

description.zh: 守卫港口聚落和仓库，维持内陆秩序，征召自农业村落。
description.en: Guarded port settlements and warehouses, maintained inland order, recruited from agricultural villages.

technology.zh: 以铁矛、短剑、弓箭和盾牌为主要武器。战船为大型双体船和外撑架船，可搭载数十名战士。
technology.en: Main weapons were iron spears, short swords, bows, and shields. Warships were large double-hulled and outrigger vessels capable of carrying dozens of warriors.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 海盗活动、敌对酋邦的海上突袭、内陆部族的反抗，以及马六甲海峡控制权的竞争。
threats.en: Piracy, maritime raids from hostile chiefdoms, inland tribal resistance, and competition for control of the Strait of Malacca.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

description.zh: 复合弓或长弓，远射主力
description.en: Composite or longbow, primary ranged weapon

populationDescription.zh: 分布于苏门答腊东岸和南岸、爪哇北岸和西岸、加里曼丹沿海以及马来半岛南端。人口集中在河口港口聚落，内陆为散居的稻作农民和山地采集者。
populationDescription.en: Distributed along the eastern and southern coasts of Sumatra, northern and western coasts of Java, coastal Borneo, and the southern tip of the Malay Peninsula. Population concentrated in river-mouth port settlements, with scattered inland rice farmers and highland foragers.

name.zh: 占碑河口聚落（苏门答腊）
巨港河口聚落（苏门答腊）

socialClasses.zh: 达图家族（贸易贵族）、自由民（农民、渔民、工匠）、依附者（债务劳动者和战俘）。
socialClasses.en: Datu families (trade nobility), freemen (farmers, fishers, craftsmen), dependents (debt laborers and war captives).

allies.zh: 各酋邦之间通过婚姻和贸易联盟保持合作关系；与印度商人群体和汉末中国商人有贸易往来。
allies.en: Chiefdoms maintained cooperative relationships through marriage and trade alliances; trading links existed with Indian merchant communities and late-Han Chinese traders.

enemies.zh: 敌对酋邦之间存在海上贸易路线控制权的竞争，部分内陆部族反抗沿海酋邦的控制。
enemies.en: Rival chiefdoms competed for control of maritime trade routes; some inland tribes resisted coastal chiefdom authority.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以吸引外国商船、控制海峡通道和扩大贸易网络为核心外交策略。
foreignPolicy.en: Core diplomatic strategy was attracting foreign vessels, controlling strait passages, and expanding trade networks.

era.zh: 早期铁器与海洋贸易时代
era.en: Early Iron Age and maritime trade era

keyInnovations.zh: 大型双体船与外撑架船建造技术、星象导航术、水稻梯田灌溉、铁器锻造（受印度影响）、铜鼓铸造（东山文化传统）。
keyInnovations.en: Large double-hulled and outrigger vessel construction, celestial navigation, terraced rice irrigation, iron forging (Indian-influenced), bronze drum casting (Dong Son tradition).

strengths.zh: 控制马六甲海峡和巽他海峡等国际贸易咽喉，航海技术先进，热带香料和林产品资源丰富，与印度和中国的贸易网络已建立。
strengths.en: Controlled international trade chokepoints like the Strait of Malacca and Sunda Strait; advanced seafaring technology; rich tropical spice and forest resources; trade networks with India and China already established.

weaknesses.zh: 政治极度分散，缺乏统一政权，各酋邦间互相竞争，内陆控制力薄弱，依赖外部贸易易受国际局势波动影响。
weaknesses.en: Extremely fragmented politically, lacking a unified polity, with inter-chiefdom competition, weak inland control, and dependence on external trade vulnerable to international fluctuations.

outlook.zh: 随着印度文化影响的深化和贸易规模的扩大，部分酋邦将在未来两三百年内整合为更大的政治实体，最终催生室利佛逝（公元7世纪）和马打兰等强大的海洋与农业王国。
outlook.en: As Indian cultural influence deepened and trade expanded, some chiefdoms would consolidate into larger polities over the next two to three centuries, eventually giving rise to powerful maritime and agrarian kingdoms like Srivijaya (7th century CE) and Mataram.

description.zh: 南岛海洋酋邦是公元220年分布于苏门答腊、爪哇、加里曼丹和马来半岛沿海的多个独立的南岛语系海洋政治体的总称。这些酋邦控制着马六甲海峡和巽他海峡等国际贸易要道，以先进的航海技术和丰富的香料资源参与连接印度与中国的"海上丝绸之路"贸易网络。印度文化元素——梵文、印度教和佛教——正开始通过贸易渠道渗透精英阶层，预示着未来的"印度化"进程。虽然此时政治上极度分散，但这些酋邦蕴含着巨大的发展潜力，将在数百年后催生室利佛逝、马打兰等改变东南亚格局的强大王国。
description.en: The Austronesian Maritime Chiefdoms was a collective term for multiple independent Austronesian-speaking maritime polities distributed along the coasts of Sumatra, Java, Borneo, and the Malay Peninsula around 220 CE. These chiefdoms controlled key international trade arteries such as the Strait of Malacca and Sunda Strait, participating in the 'Maritime Silk Road' trade network linking India and China through advanced seafaring technology and abundant spice resources. Indian cultural elements — Sanskrit, Hinduism, and Buddhism — were beginning to penetrate elite circles through trade channels, foreshadowing the future 'Indianization' process. Though extremely fragmented politically at this time, these chiefdoms harbored immense developmental potential, giving rise to powerful kingdoms like Srivijaya and Mataram that would reshape Southeast Asia in the centuries to come.

governmentForm.zh: 半汉化的游牧部族联盟，名义上臣属于中原政权，内部仍以部落制度维持运转
governmentForm.en: A semi-sinicized nomadic tribal confederation, nominally vassal to the Chinese central court while internally maintaining tribal governance

socialStructure.zh: 以单于王族为顶层，左右贤王、左右谷蠡王等部族贵族次之，普通牧民与投附汉族人口为基层，另有少量奴隶。内迁后受汉文化影响，部分贵族通晓汉语文化。
socialStructure.en: Headed by the Chanyu's royal lineage, followed by tribal nobles such as the Wise Kings of the Left and Right and the Luli Kings, with common herders and assimilated Han settlers at the base, along with a small number of slaves. After resettlement inland, some nobles adopted Han language and culture.

rulingClass.zh: 挛鞮氏王族、左右贤王及各部落首领
rulingClass.en: The Luandi royal clan, Wise Kings of the Left and Right, and chieftains of the component tribes

succession.zh: 名义上由汉廷册封单于，实际在挛鞮氏王族内部推举或角力继承
succession.en: Nominally appointed by the Han court, but in practice determined by election or competition within the Luandi royal clan

structure.zh: 南匈奴保留传统左右分翼的部落联盟体制，但已受汉朝行政体系深度影响。单于驻于并州境内，受中原朝廷监护；内部仍以五部（左、右、南、北、中）组织部众，各部设帅统领。
structure.en: The Southern Xiongnu retained the traditional left-right wing tribal confederation structure but were deeply influenced by the Han administrative system. The Chanyu resided within Bing Province under Chinese court supervision; internally, the people were organized into five divisions (Left, Right, South, North, Center), each led by a division chief.

function.zh: 统辖南匈奴五部（左、右、南、北、中部）的军政事务，管理各部游牧民众与驻地
function.en: Governed the military and civil affairs of the five Xiongnu divisions (Left, Right, South, North, Center), managing nomadic populations and their settlements

function.zh: 处理部族间纠纷、对外交涉、祭祀仪典与重大决策
function.en: Handled inter-tribal disputes, external negotiations, ritual ceremonies, and major policy decisions

function.zh: 中原朝廷派驻的监护使节机构，监督单于行政、调解匈奴与汉族边民关系
function.en: A supervisory body dispatched by the central Chinese court to oversee the Chanyu's administration and mediate relations between Xiongnu and Han frontier settlers

laws.zh: 以匈奴传统习惯法为基础，兼受汉律影响。部族内部纠纷由首领裁决，重大事务由单于庭集议处理。盗窃与背叛部落者处以重刑。
laws.en: Based on traditional Xiongnu customary law with Han legal influence. Internal disputes were adjudicated by tribal leaders; major matters were deliberated at the Chanyu's court. Theft and tribal betrayal were punished severely.

diplomacy.zh: 长期作为汉朝的内附藩属，为中原政权提供骑兵助战。220年汉魏禅代之际，南匈奴处于政治依附的过渡期，与曹魏保持臣属关系，同时与鲜卑、乌桓等北方部族存在竞争与交往。
diplomacy.en: Long served as an inner vassal of the Han dynasty, providing cavalry support for the central court. During the Han-Wei transition of 220 CE, the Southern Xiongnu were in a transitional period of political dependence, maintaining vassal ties with Cao Wei while competing and interacting with the Xianbei, Wuhuan, and other northern tribes.

religion.zh: 以萨满信仰和天地崇拜（腾格里）为核心，兼有祖先祭祀传统。内迁后部分贵族接触佛教与中原儒学思想。
religion.en: Centered on shamanistic beliefs and Heaven-Earth worship (Tengri), along with ancestor veneration. After resettlement, some nobles encountered Buddhism and Chinese Confucian thought.

traditions.zh: 保留游牧骑射传统，但内迁后逐渐转向半农半牧的生活方式。仍保持龙城大会等部族集会习俗，祭祀天地与祖先。
traditions.en: Retained nomadic horse-riding and archery traditions, but gradually shifted to a semi-agricultural, semi-pastoral lifestyle after resettlement. Still maintained tribal assembly customs such as the Longcheng Grand Meeting, with sacrifices to Heaven, Earth, and ancestors.

achievements.zh: 精湛的骑射战术与马具制造技术，金银器制作工艺融合草原与中原风格，内迁后在文化融合方面开创先河。
achievements.en: Masterful mounted archery tactics and horse equipment manufacturing, gold and silver craftsmanship blending steppe and Chinese styles, and pioneering cultural synthesis after resettlement in China.

description.zh: 内迁后经济由纯游牧逐渐转向半农半牧。畜牧业仍是基础，但已开始从事农耕。部分部众为中原军事力量服务以换取粮饷供给。与汉人的互市贸易提供了铁器、丝帛等物资。
description.en: After resettlement, the economy shifted from pure nomadism to semi-agricultural pastoralism. Animal husbandry remained foundational, but farming began. Some tribespeople served as military auxiliaries for Chinese forces in exchange for grain and supplies. Border markets with Han settlers provided iron tools, silk, and other goods.

name.en: Bing Province border market trade route

description.zh: 兼具草原游牧技术与部分中原农业及冶铁技术。马具制造、弓箭复合弓制作技术先进；内迁后接触到中原的铁器锻造、灌溉农业与筑城技术。
description.en: Combined steppe nomadic technologies with some Chinese agricultural and iron-smelting techniques. Advanced in horse equipment and composite bow production; after resettlement, gained exposure to Chinese iron forging, irrigation farming, and fortification techniques.

description.zh: 以骑兵为核心战力，仍保有草原骑射传统的强悍战斗力。南匈奴骑兵曾多次被汉朝征调参与讨伐叛乱与对外作战。然内迁日久，兵源与马匹数量较全盛期大幅下降。
description.en: Cavalry remained the core fighting force, retaining the formidable combat capability of steppe mounted archery traditions. Southern Xiongnu cavalry were frequently conscripted by the Han court for suppressing rebellions and frontier campaigns. However, after prolonged resettlement, troop and horse numbers declined significantly from their peak.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 重骑兵标志性武器，冲击力极强
description.en: Signature heavy cavalry weapon with tremendous charge impact

description.zh: 覆盖全身的重装骑兵铁甲
description.en: Full-body heavy cavalry iron barding

description.zh: 单兵弩与床弩，中国军队核心远程武器
description.en: Infantry crossbow and mounted crossbow, core ranged weapon

description.zh: 可连发十矢的机械弩，守城利器
description.en: Mechanical crossbow firing ten bolts in succession, excellent for defense

name.en: Other steppe peoples (Qiang, Di, Xianbei, etc.)

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以谷物仓储、金属储备和织物库存为主，储备水平约可维持0个月开支。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury was primarily grain granaries, metal reserves, and textile stocks, with reserves sufficient for about 0 months of spending.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政体系以实物税收和贡纳为基础，军事支出占据主导地位，国库储备以谷物、金属和织物为主。
fiscalPolicy.en: The fiscal system was based on in-kind taxation and tribute, military spending dominated, with treasury reserves held in grain, metals, and textiles.

ruler.zh: 地方王公（具体世系不详）

governmentForm.zh: 区域性君主制王国，延续印度传统的王权-婆罗门双元治理模式
governmentForm.en: A regional monarchy continuing the traditional Indian dual governance model of royal authority and Brahmanical counsel

socialStructure.zh: 严格的种姓制度主导社会结构：婆罗门掌控宗教与教育，刹帝利掌握政权与军事，吠舍经营商业与农业，首陀罗从事服务与体力劳动，另有大量不可接触者处于社会边缘。佛教僧团仍有重要社会影响力。
socialStructure.en: Rigid caste hierarchy dominated social structure: Brahmins controlled religion and education, Kshatriyas held political and military power, Vaishyas engaged in commerce and agriculture, Shudras performed service and manual labor, with large populations of untouchables at the margins. Buddhist monastic communities retained significant social influence.

rulingClass.zh: 国王、刹帝利贵族、婆罗门祭司阶层
rulingClass.en: The king, Kshatriya aristocracy, and Brahmin priestly class

succession.zh: 王位父子传承为主，但宫廷政变与地方割据势力干预频繁
succession.en: Royal succession was primarily patrilineal, but frequent court coups and interference by regional strongmen disrupted orderly transitions

structure.zh: 摩揭陀在后贵霜时期沦为地方性王国，中央权威有限。国王在婆罗门祭司与大臣辅佐下治国，地方由封建领主与部族首领实际控制。
structure.en: In the post-Kushan period, Magadha was reduced to a regional kingdom with limited central authority. The king governed with the counsel of Brahmin priests and ministers, while local feudal lords and tribal chiefs held effective control over outlying areas.

function.zh: 辅佐国王决策，议定税收、司法与外交事务
function.en: Advised the king on decisions regarding taxation, justice, and diplomatic affairs

function.zh: 管理王室税收、田赋征收与国库支出
function.en: Managed royal revenue, land tax collection, and treasury expenditure

function.zh: 依据达摩经典与国王敕令裁决民事与刑事案件
function.en: Adjudicated civil and criminal cases based on Dharma texts and royal edicts

laws.zh: 以《摩奴法典》等达摩经典为法律基础，结合国王敕令。种姓规范具有准法律效力，婆罗门在司法解释中享有特权。
laws.en: Based on Dharma texts such as the Manusmriti, combined with royal decrees. Caste norms carried quasi-legal force, and Brahmins held privileges in judicial interpretation.

diplomacy.zh: 与周边区域王国保持松散的联盟或竞争关系，与北方残余贵霜势力及南方萨塔瓦哈纳后继政权有间歇性交涉。恒河流域的贸易网络维持着外交联系。
diplomacy.en: Maintained loose alliances or rivalries with neighboring regional kingdoms, with intermittent engagement with residual Kushan powers to the north and post-Satavahana successor states to the south. Trade networks along the Ganges sustained diplomatic connections.

religion.zh: 佛教与婆罗门教并盛。那烂陀地区已成为佛教学术重镇，大乘佛教迅速发展。同时婆罗门教（早期印度教）在王室支持下复兴。耆那教也有一定影响。
religion.en: Buddhism and Brahmanism flourished side by side. The Nalanda area was emerging as a major center of Buddhist scholarship, with Mahayana Buddhism expanding rapidly. Simultaneously, Brahmanism (early Hinduism) was reviving under royal patronage. Jainism also retained some influence.

traditions.zh: 恒河流域古老的农耕节庆、佛教法会与婆罗门祭祀仪式并行。种姓制度规范着日常生活的方方面面，从饮食到婚姻。
traditions.en: Ancient agricultural festivals of the Ganges plain coexisted with Buddhist dharma assemblies and Brahmanical sacrificial rituals. The caste system regulated all aspects of daily life, from diet to marriage.

achievements.zh: 延续了孔雀与巽伽时代的学术传统，佛教文献编纂与哲学辩论活跃。梵文文学开始繁荣，数学与天文学知识有所积累。
achievements.en: Continued the scholarly traditions of the Maurya and Shunga periods, with active Buddhist textual compilation and philosophical debate. Sanskrit literature began to flourish, and knowledge in mathematics and astronomy accumulated.

description.zh: 以恒河平原丰沃的稻作农业为经济基础，辅以棉花种植与手工纺织。华氏城仍是重要的商业枢纽，但规模不及孔雀王朝鼎盛时期。行会制度组织着工匠与商人的经济活动。
description.en: Founded on the fertile rice agriculture of the Ganges plain, supplemented by cotton cultivation and handicraft weaving. Pataliputra remained an important commercial hub, though reduced from its Mauryan peak. Guild systems organized the economic activities of artisans and merchants.

description.zh: 继承了印度次大陆悠久的冶铁、纺织与灌溉技术传统。铁器加工精湛，水利灌溉系统发达。数学与天文学知识居于世界前列，十进制数字系统已在使用中。
description.en: Inherited the Indian subcontinent's long traditions of iron smelting, textile production, and irrigation. Iron working was refined, and hydraulic irrigation systems well developed. Mathematics and astronomy were among the world's most advanced, with the decimal number system already in use.

name.zh: 高碳钢（乌兹钢前身）冶炼技术

description.zh: 军事力量以步兵与战象为核心，辅以骑兵与战车。但作为缩小的区域王国，军队规模远不及孔雀王朝时期，且装备与训练参差不齐。
description.en: Military forces centered on infantry and war elephants, supplemented by cavalry and chariots. However, as a diminished regional kingdom, the army was far smaller than during the Maurya period, with uneven equipment and training.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 复合弓或长弓，远射主力
description.en: Composite or longbow, primary ranged weapon

description.zh: 覆盖全身的重装骑兵铁甲
description.en: Full-body heavy cavalry iron barding

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以谷物仓储、金属储备和织物库存为主，储备水平约可维持0个月开支。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury was primarily grain granaries, metal reserves, and textile stocks, with reserves sufficient for about 0 months of spending.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政体系以实物税收和贡纳为基础，军事支出占据主导地位，国库储备以谷物、金属和织物为主。
fiscalPolicy.en: The fiscal system was based on in-kind taxation and tribute, military spending dominated, with treasury reserves held in grain, metals, and textiles.

ruler.zh: 地方国王（具体王名不详）

dynasty.zh: 后萨塔瓦哈纳时期地方王朝

capital.zh: 陀塔普里（今布巴内斯瓦尔附近）

governmentForm.zh: 沿海区域性君主国，依托海洋贸易维持王权
governmentForm.en: A coastal regional monarchy sustaining royal power through maritime trade

socialStructure.zh: 种姓制度为社会基础，但海洋贸易赋予商人阶层较高地位。婆罗门与佛教僧团共享宗教权威，沿海渔民与造船匠人构成重要社会阶层。
socialStructure.en: Caste system formed the social foundation, but maritime trade granted the merchant class elevated status. Brahmins and Buddhist monasteries shared religious authority, while coastal fishermen and shipbuilders constituted important social strata.

rulingClass.zh: 国王、刹帝利武士贵族、大商人家族
rulingClass.en: The king, Kshatriya warrior aristocracy, and major merchant families

succession.zh: 父子继承为主，商人家族与地方豪强的支持对王位传承有重要影响
succession.en: Primarily patrilineal succession, with the support of merchant families and local magnates significantly influencing royal transitions

structure.zh: 国王统领中央政务，下设官僚体系管理税收、司法与港口事务。沿海港口城市享有一定自治权，由商人行会参与管理。
structure.en: The king oversaw central administration, with a bureaucratic system managing taxation, justice, and port affairs. Coastal port cities enjoyed some autonomy, with merchant guilds participating in governance.

function.zh: 管理沿海港口、征收商税、监督海外贸易与船舶出入
function.en: Managed coastal ports, collected trade taxes, and supervised foreign commerce and ship traffic

function.zh: 辅佐国王处理政务、军事与外交决策
function.en: Assisted the king in administrative, military, and diplomatic decision-making

function.zh: 管理农业赋税征收与土地分配
function.en: Managed agricultural tax collection and land allocation

laws.zh: 以达摩经典为基础，兼有关于海事贸易的专门法规。商业纠纷由行会仲裁，重大案件由王廷裁决。
laws.en: Based on Dharma texts, supplemented by specialized regulations governing maritime trade. Commercial disputes were arbitrated by guilds, with major cases adjudicated by the royal court.

diplomacy.zh: 与东南亚诸港口城市保持密切的海洋贸易联系，与南印度的萨塔瓦哈纳后继政权及斯里兰卡有频繁交涉。远洋商人远达罗马帝国东部港口。
diplomacy.en: Maintained close maritime trade links with Southeast Asian port cities, with frequent contacts with post-Satavahana successor states in South India and Sri Lanka. Ocean-going merchants reached eastern ports of the Roman Empire.

religion.zh: 佛教极为兴盛，大量佛教寺院与僧侣。婆罗门教与耆那教也有广泛信仰。羯陵伽是向东南亚传播佛教与印度文化的重要起点。
religion.en: Buddhism was extremely prosperous, with numerous monasteries and monks. Brahmanism and Jainism were also widely practiced. Kalinga was a major launching point for the spread of Buddhism and Indian culture to Southeast Asia.

traditions.zh: 深厚的航海传统与造船文化，巴利迦特拉节（航海节）为重要节庆。精美的石刻艺术与寺院建筑。
traditions.en: Deep seafaring traditions and shipbuilding culture, with the Bali Jatra (maritime festival) as an important celebration. Exquisite stone carving art and temple architecture.

achievements.zh: 卓越的航海技术与远洋造船能力，向东南亚输出了印度文明的重要元素，石刻艺术与寺院建筑成就显著。
achievements.en: Outstanding navigational technology and ocean-going shipbuilding capability, exporting key elements of Indian civilization to Southeast Asia, with notable achievements in stone carving and temple architecture.

description.zh: 海洋贸易是经济命脉，港口城市繁荣兴旺。内陆以稻作农业为基础，象牙、宝石、香料与纺织品为主要出口商品。与东南亚、斯里兰卡及罗马帝国的海上贸易带来大量财富。
description.en: Maritime trade was the economic lifeblood, with prosperous port cities. The hinterland relied on rice agriculture, while ivory, gems, spices, and textiles were major exports. Sea trade with Southeast Asia, Sri Lanka, and the Roman Empire generated substantial wealth.

name.zh: 孟加拉湾-东南亚海上商路
name.en: Bay of Bengal–Southeast Asia maritime route

description.zh: 航海技术在印度次大陆首屈一指，掌握季风航行规律。造船工艺精湛，能建造远洋大型商船。铁器锻造、纺织与宝石加工技术发达。
description.en: Navigational technology was among the finest on the Indian subcontinent, with mastery of monsoon sailing patterns. Shipbuilding craftsmanship was refined, capable of constructing large ocean-going merchant vessels. Iron forging, textile production, and gem processing were well developed.

description.zh: 以步兵与战象为陆上主力，拥有一定规模的海上力量保护贸易航线。军事实力在区域内属中等偏上，但不以军事扩张为主要目标。
description.en: Land forces centered on infantry and war elephants, with a significant naval force protecting trade routes. Military strength was above average regionally, though territorial expansion was not the primary objective.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 重骑兵标志性武器，冲击力极强
description.en: Signature heavy cavalry weapon with tremendous charge impact

description.zh: 可连发十矢的机械弩，守城利器
description.en: Mechanical crossbow firing ten bolts in succession, excellent for defense

description.zh: 覆盖全身的重装骑兵铁甲
description.en: Full-body heavy cavalry iron barding

name.zh: 印度-雅利安人（奥里亚人先祖）
部族民与沿海渔民群体

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以谷物仓储、金属储备和织物库存为主，储备水平约可维持0个月开支。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury was primarily grain granaries, metal reserves, and textile stocks, with reserves sufficient for about 0 months of spending.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政体系以实物税收和贡纳为基础，军事支出占据主导地位，国库储备以谷物、金属和织物为主。
fiscalPolicy.en: The fiscal system was based on in-kind taxation and tribute, military spending dominated, with treasury reserves held in grain, metals, and textiles.

name.zh: 印度-塞迦王国（西萨特拉普）

governmentForm.zh: 半印度化的中亚军事贵族王国，以萨特拉普（总督）制度管理地方
governmentForm.en: A semi-Indianized Central Asian military aristocratic kingdom, governing provinces through the Satrap (governor) system

socialStructure.zh: 塞迦军事贵族居于统治顶层，但已深度融入印度种姓社会。婆罗门祭司为文化权威，印度本土商人阶层控制着经济活动，普通农民与工匠构成社会基础。
socialStructure.en: Saka military aristocrats sat at the ruling apex but were deeply integrated into Indian caste society. Brahmin priests served as cultural authorities, indigenous Indian merchant classes controlled economic activities, and common farmers and artisans formed the social base.

rulingClass.zh: 塞迦军事贵族（刹特拉帕家族）、婆罗门祭司
rulingClass.en: Saka military aristocracy (Kshatrapa families) and Brahmin priests

succession.zh: 王位在卡尔达马卡家族内传承，摩诃刹特拉帕与刹特拉帕间存在权力共享与竞争
succession.en: The throne was transmitted within the Kardamaka family, with power sharing and competition between the Mahakshatrapa and subordinate Kshatrapas

structure.zh: 中央由摩诃刹特拉帕统治，下辖数个刹特拉帕（总督）管辖省份。行政体系融合了中亚军事传统与印度官僚制度。
structure.en: Centrally ruled by the Mahakshatrapa, with several subordinate Kshatrapas (satraps) governing provinces. The administrative system blended Central Asian military traditions with Indian bureaucratic institutions.

function.zh: 各省刹特拉帕管理地方行政、税收与军事事务
function.en: Provincial Kshatrapas managed local administration, taxation, and military affairs

function.zh: 处理中央政务、外交与重大决策
function.en: Handled central administration, diplomacy, and major policy decisions

function.zh: 管理银币铸造与货币流通，维护贸易秩序
function.en: Managed silver coinage and monetary circulation, maintaining trade order

laws.zh: 融合印度达摩法与中亚习惯法传统。著名的朱那格特铭文记录了鲁德拉达曼一世时期的法律与行政成就。
laws.en: Blended Indian Dharma law with Central Asian customary law traditions. The famous Junagadh inscription records legal and administrative achievements from the time of Rudradaman I.

diplomacy.zh: 与北方贵霜残余势力保持复杂关系，与南部萨塔瓦哈纳后继政权持续竞争德干地区控制权。通过港口与罗马帝国保持贸易往来。
diplomacy.en: Maintained complex relations with residual Kushan power to the north, with ongoing competition with post-Satavahana successor states for control of the Deccan. Sustained trade with the Roman Empire through coastal ports.

religion.zh: 佛教、婆罗门教与耆那教并存。塞迦统治者虽出身中亚，但积极资助印度各教派，建造了大量佛教石窟与印度教神庙。
religion.en: Buddhism, Brahmanism, and Jainism coexisted. Though of Central Asian origin, Saka rulers actively patronized Indian religious traditions, constructing numerous Buddhist cave temples and Hindu shrines.

traditions.zh: 中亚草原文化与印度文明深度融合。统治者使用梵文与普拉克里特文，采用印度历法与节庆，同时保留部分中亚军事习俗。
traditions.en: Deep fusion of Central Asian steppe culture and Indian civilization. Rulers used Sanskrit and Prakrit, adopted Indian calendars and festivals, while retaining some Central Asian military customs.

achievements.zh: 精美的银币铸造工艺，梵文铭文学发展（朱那格特岩刻铭文是早期梵文散文杰作），资助建造了西印度著名石窟寺院群。
achievements.en: Exquisite silver coinage craftsmanship, advancement of Sanskrit epigraphy (the Junagadh rock inscription is a masterpiece of early Sanskrit prose), and patronage of famous rock-cut cave temple complexes in western India.

description.zh: 控制着古吉拉特与马尔瓦的富饶农业区及重要贸易港口。棉纺织品、宝石与香料出口繁荣，银币铸造精美广泛流通。但到220年，领土不断收缩，经济实力有所下降。
description.en: Controlled the fertile agricultural regions and key trading ports of Gujarat and Malwa. Cotton textile, gem, and spice exports flourished, with refined silver coinage in wide circulation. However, by 220 CE, shrinking territory had diminished economic strength.

name.zh: 巴里伽扎（布罗奇）港口贸易航线

description.zh: 继承了印度成熟的冶金、纺织与灌溉技术。银币铸造工艺精湛，水利工程如苏达尔善湖修复工程展现了高水平的工程能力。
description.en: Inherited India's mature metallurgical, textile, and irrigation technologies. Silver coinage was finely crafted, and hydraulic projects like the restoration of Sudarshana Lake demonstrated high-level engineering capability.

description.zh: 融合了中亚骑兵传统与印度步兵战象体系。骑兵仍是精锐力量，但整体军事实力因领土缩减而下降。
description.en: Combined Central Asian cavalry traditions with the Indian infantry and war elephant system. Cavalry remained the elite force, but overall military strength declined due to territorial contraction.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 复合弓或长弓，远射主力
description.en: Composite or longbow, primary ranged weapon

description.zh: 覆盖全身的重装骑兵铁甲
description.en: Full-body heavy cavalry iron barding

description.zh: 水战拍竿，击毁敌船上层结构
description.en: Ship-mounted swinging beam to smash enemy superstructures

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以谷物仓储、金属储备和织物库存为主，储备水平约可维持0个月开支。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury was primarily grain granaries, metal reserves, and textile stocks, with reserves sufficient for about 0 months of spending.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政体系以实物税收和贡纳为基础，军事支出占据主导地位，国库储备以谷物、金属和织物为主。
fiscalPolicy.en: The fiscal system was based on in-kind taxation and tribute, military spending dominated, with treasury reserves held in grain, metals, and textiles.

capital.zh: 扎兰季（今阿富汗扎兰季）

governmentForm.zh: 帕提亚贵族封建制下的家族自治领地，锡斯坦地区的世袭统治家族
governmentForm.en: A hereditary family domain under Parthian feudal nobility, ruling the Sistan region as hereditary lords

socialStructure.zh: 以苏伦家族为最高统治者，帕提亚军事贵族次之，波斯化的定居农民与城市居民为中层，游牧部落民与农奴为基层。
socialStructure.en: Headed by the Suren family as supreme rulers, followed by Parthian military nobility, Persianized settled farmers and urban residents in the middle, and nomadic tribespeople and serfs at the base.

rulingClass.zh: 苏伦家族成员与帕提亚贵族
rulingClass.en: Members of the Suren family and Parthian nobility

succession.zh: 苏伦家族内部传承，由家族长老与安息宗主共同认可
succession.en: Transmitted within the Suren family, with recognition from family elders and the Parthian suzerain

structure.zh: 作为安息帝国七大贵族家族之一，苏伦家族以半独立身份统治锡斯坦。220年前后，安息帝国正被萨珊势力取代，苏伦王国面临政治转型压力。
structure.en: As one of the seven great noble houses of the Parthian Empire, the Suren family ruled Sistan in semi-independence. Around 220 CE, the Parthian Empire was being supplanted by Sasanian power, placing the Suren kingdom under pressure to adapt politically.

function.zh: 处理领地内政务、税收征收与司法裁决
function.en: Handled domain administration, tax collection, and judicial decisions

function.zh: 统领领地武装、边境防御与对游牧部族的军事行动
function.en: Commanded domain armed forces, border defense, and military operations against nomadic tribes

function.zh: 管理锡斯坦的坎儿井灌溉系统与赫尔曼德河水利设施
function.en: Managed Sistan's qanat irrigation systems and Helmand River hydraulic infrastructure

laws.zh: 以帕提亚贵族传统法与波斯习惯法为基础，兼受琐罗亚斯德教律法影响。封建领主对领地内居民有广泛的司法裁决权。
laws.en: Based on Parthian aristocratic customary law and Persian traditions, with influence from Zoroastrian jurisprudence. Feudal lords held broad judicial authority over domain residents.

diplomacy.zh: 220年前后处于安息帝国瓦解与萨珊帝国崛起的夹缝之中。与东方贵霜势力及北方中亚游牧部族均有交涉。面临是否归顺新兴萨珊政权的关键抉择。
diplomacy.en: Around 220 CE, caught between the collapse of the Parthian Empire and the rise of the Sasanians. Engaged with Kushan power to the east and Central Asian nomadic tribes to the north. Facing a critical decision on whether to submit to the emerging Sasanian regime.

religion.zh: 琐罗亚斯德教为主要信仰，并受佛教东传与帕提亚多元宗教传统影响。锡斯坦地区与早期伊朗英雄史诗传说（如鲁斯塔姆故事）关系密切。
religion.en: Zoroastrianism was the primary faith, with influences from eastward-spreading Buddhism and Parthian religious pluralism. The Sistan region was closely associated with early Iranian heroic epic traditions (such as the Rostam legends).

traditions.zh: 帕提亚骑射贵族文化与波斯定居农业传统并存。宫廷宴饮、狩猎与骑术比赛为贵族生活重要内容。诺鲁孜节等波斯传统节日广泛庆祝。
traditions.en: Parthian mounted archery aristocratic culture coexisted with Persian settled agricultural traditions. Court feasts, hunting, and equestrian competitions were important aspects of noble life. Persian traditional festivals like Nowruz were widely celebrated.

achievements.zh: 精湛的灌溉水利工程，尤其是坎儿井地下水渠系统。帕提亚风格的宫殿建筑与金属工艺品。对伊朗英雄史诗传统的保存和传承。
achievements.en: Refined irrigation engineering, especially the qanat underground water channel system. Parthian-style palace architecture and metalwork. Preservation and transmission of Iranian heroic epic traditions.

description.zh: 依赖赫尔曼德河谷灌溉农业与坎儿井系统。锡斯坦地处东西方贸易通道上，丝绸之路南线经过此地，但政治动荡影响了经济稳定。
description.en: Relied on irrigated agriculture in the Helmand River valley and qanat systems. Sistan sat on east-west trade corridors, with the southern branch of the Silk Road passing through, but political turmoil affected economic stability.

name.zh: 丝绸之路南线（锡斯坦段）
赫尔曼德河谷贸易通道

description.zh: 继承了帕提亚与波斯的成熟技术传统。坎儿井灌溉系统工程技术先进，冶金与武器制造水平较高，建筑技术融合了伊朗与中亚风格。
description.en: Inherited mature technological traditions from Parthia and Persia. Qanat irrigation engineering was advanced, metallurgy and weapons manufacturing were of high quality, and architecture blended Iranian and Central Asian styles.

description.zh: 以帕提亚传统的重装骑兵（铁甲骑兵）与轻骑弓射手为核心。苏伦家族曾以击败罗马克拉苏闻名。但到220年，因政治动荡与领土压力，军事实力有所下降。
description.en: Centered on the Parthian tradition of heavy armored cavalry (cataphracts) and light horse archers. The Suren family was famed for the defeat of Roman Crassus. However, by 220 CE, political turmoil and territorial pressure had reduced military strength.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

description.zh: 覆盖全身的重装骑兵铁甲
description.en: Full-body heavy cavalry iron barding

name.zh: 游牧及部族民（巴路支人先祖等）

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以谷物仓储、金属储备和织物库存为主，储备水平约可维持0个月开支。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury was primarily grain granaries, metal reserves, and textile stocks, with reserves sufficient for about 0 months of spending.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政体系以实物税收和贡纳为基础，军事支出占据主导地位，国库储备以谷物、金属和织物为主。
fiscalPolicy.en: The fiscal system was based on in-kind taxation and tribute, military spending dominated, with treasury reserves held in grain, metals, and textiles.

capital.zh: 无固定都城（游牧于巴尔喀什湖至伊犁河流域）
capital.en: No fixed capital (nomadic between Lake Balkhash and Ili River valley)

governmentForm.zh: 以血缘与军事联盟为纽带的松散游牧部族联盟
governmentForm.en: A loose nomadic tribal confederation bound by kinship and military alliance

socialStructure.zh: 以联盟首领家族为核心，各部落首领组成贵族阶层，牧民战士为主体人口，附属部落与被征服部族为底层。
socialStructure.en: Centered on the confederation chief's family, with component tribal leaders forming a noble class, herder-warriors as the main population, and subordinate tribes and conquered peoples at the bottom.

rulingClass.zh: 联盟大首领及各部族酋长
rulingClass.en: The confederation's Great Chief and tribal chieftains

succession.zh: 由各部族首领推举或武力夺取，无固定继承制度
succession.en: Determined by election among tribal chiefs or seizure by force, with no fixed succession system

structure.zh: 松散的部族联盟体制，联盟首领在战时拥有较大指挥权，和平时期各部族高度自治。重大决策在部族首领会议上集体商议。
structure.en: A loose tribal confederation system. The confederation chief held greater command authority in wartime, while individual tribes maintained high autonomy in peacetime. Major decisions were collectively deliberated at tribal leaders' councils.

function.zh: 商议战争、迁徙、联盟外交等重大事务
function.en: Deliberated on war, migration, alliance diplomacy, and other major affairs

function.zh: 战时协调各部族骑兵作战行动
function.en: Coordinated the cavalry operations of various tribes during wartime

laws.zh: 以游牧习惯法为基础，强调骑士荣誉、部族忠诚与畜产权。纠纷由长老裁决，严重罪行由首领会议审判。
laws.en: Based on nomadic customary law, emphasizing warrior honor, tribal loyalty, and livestock property rights. Disputes were settled by elders; serious offenses were tried by the council of chiefs.

diplomacy.zh: 与周边游牧部族（康居、乌孙残部）及丝绸之路绿洲城市有贸易与争夺关系。与北方匈奴残余势力及东方鲜卑联盟存在竞争。
diplomacy.en: Engaged in trade and competition with neighboring nomadic tribes (Kangju, Wusun remnants) and Silk Road oasis cities. Competed with northern Xiongnu remnants and the eastern Xianbei confederation.

religion.zh: 以腾格里（天神）崇拜与萨满信仰为核心，兼有自然崇拜与祖先祭祀。部分接触到沿丝路传入的佛教与琐罗亚斯德教。
religion.en: Centered on Tengri (sky god) worship and shamanistic beliefs, with nature veneration and ancestor worship. Some contact with Buddhism and Zoroastrianism transmitted along the Silk Road.

traditions.zh: 典型的欧亚草原游牧文化：骑射为基本技能，帐篷（毡帐）为居所，随季节迁徙牧场。宰牲祭天、赛马与摔跤为重要庆典活动。
traditions.en: Typical Eurasian steppe nomadic culture: horse riding and archery as fundamental skills, tents (yurts) as dwellings, seasonal migration between pastures. Animal sacrifice to Heaven, horse racing, and wrestling were important ceremonial activities.

achievements.zh: 精湛的骑术与轻骑兵战术，草原风格的动物纹金属工艺品，适应极端环境的游牧生活技术。
achievements.en: Masterful horsemanship and light cavalry tactics, steppe-style animal motif metalwork, and survival techniques adapted to extreme environments.

description.zh: 以游牧畜牧业为绝对主导，牲畜（马、羊、牛）是最重要的财富形式。通过与丝路商队的交换获取铁器、纺织品与粮食。偶有劫掠商队与绿洲城市的行为。
description.en: Dominated by nomadic pastoralism, with livestock (horses, sheep, cattle) as the most important form of wealth. Obtained iron goods, textiles, and grain through trade with Silk Road caravans. Occasionally raided caravans and oasis cities.

description.zh: 掌握适应草原环境的实用技术：马匹繁育与驯化、弓箭制作、毡帐建造、皮革加工。冶金技术有限，依赖贸易获取铁器。
description.en: Mastered practical technologies adapted to the steppe environment: horse breeding and training, bow making, yurt construction, and leather working. Metallurgical technology was limited, relying on trade for iron goods.

name.zh: 便携式毡帐（移动营地技术）

description.zh: 全民皆兵的游牧骑兵体制，成年男子均为骑射战士。轻骑兵机动灵活，擅长长途奔袭与诱敌战术。缺乏攻城能力但野战凶猛。
description.en: A nomadic cavalry system where all adult males were mounted warrior-archers. Light cavalry was mobile and flexible, excelling at long-range raids and feigned retreat tactics. Lacked siege capability but was ferocious in open battle.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 复合弓或长弓，远射主力
description.en: Composite or longbow, primary ranged weapon

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以谷物仓储、金属储备和织物库存为主，储备水平约可维持0个月开支。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury was primarily grain granaries, metal reserves, and textile stocks, with reserves sufficient for about 0 months of spending.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政体系以实物税收和贡纳为基础，军事支出占据主导地位，国库储备以谷物、金属和织物为主。
fiscalPolicy.en: The fiscal system was based on in-kind taxation and tribute, military spending dominated, with treasury reserves held in grain, metals, and textiles.

name.zh: 塔里木盆地绿洲城邦联合

ruler.zh: 各城邦国王（龟兹王、于阗王、疏勒王等）
ruler.en: Kings of each city-state (Kings of Kucha, Khotan, Kashgar, etc.)

governmentForm.zh: 各绿洲独立为城邦王国，以国王为中心的小型官僚体制，受中原、印度与中亚多元政治文化影响
governmentForm.en: Each oasis was an independent city-state kingdom with a small bureaucratic system centered on the king, influenced by Chinese, Indian, and Central Asian political cultures

socialStructure.zh: 国王与贵族为统治层，佛教僧团享有崇高社会地位，商人阶层因丝路贸易而富裕强势，农民与手工匠人为基础人口。各族群混居，文化多元。
socialStructure.en: Kings and nobles formed the ruling class; Buddhist monasteries held exalted social status; the merchant class was wealthy and powerful from Silk Road trade; farmers and artisans comprised the base population. Multiple ethnic groups cohabited in a multicultural environment.

rulingClass.zh: 城邦国王、贵族与佛教高僧
rulingClass.en: City-state kings, nobles, and senior Buddhist monks

succession.zh: 王位在各城邦王族内传承，有时受中原朝廷册封或邻近强权干预影响
succession.en: Thrones passed within each city-state's royal family, sometimes influenced by investiture from Chinese courts or interference by neighboring powers

structure.zh: 各绿洲城邦独立治理，设有小型官僚机构管理农业灌溉、商税征收与城防。城邦间无统一政府，但通过商业网络与佛教文化纽带维持联系。220年前后，中原三国鼎立削弱了对西域的控制。
structure.en: Each oasis city-state governed independently with small bureaucracies managing irrigation, trade taxation, and city defense. No unified government existed between city-states, but connections were maintained through commercial networks and Buddhist cultural ties. Around 220 CE, the Three Kingdoms division of China weakened control over the Western Regions.

function.zh: 管理绿洲灌溉系统、水源分配与农田维护
function.en: Managed oasis irrigation systems, water allocation, and farmland maintenance

function.zh: 对过境丝路商队征收关税与通行费
function.en: Levied tariffs and transit fees on passing Silk Road caravans

function.zh: 组织城市防御、维护城墙与烽燧系统
function.en: Organized city defense, maintained walls and beacon signal systems

function.zh: 协调寺院事务、管理僧团与佛教文化活动
function.en: Coordinated monastery affairs, managed monastic communities, and organized Buddhist cultural activities

laws.zh: 各城邦有独立法律体系，融合印度佛教律法、中原法制传统与中亚习惯法。商业法规较为完善以保障丝路贸易秩序。
laws.en: Each city-state had an independent legal system blending Indian Buddhist law, Chinese legal traditions, and Central Asian customary law. Commercial regulations were well developed to safeguard Silk Road trade order.

diplomacy.zh: 夹在中原政权、游牧帝国（匈奴残部、鲜卑、悦般）与贵霜势力之间，各城邦灵活周旋。220年后中原控制力减弱，各城邦获得更大自主权但也面临更多外部威胁。
diplomacy.en: Caught between Chinese regimes, nomadic empires (Xiongnu remnants, Xianbei, Yueban), and Kushan power, each city-state navigated flexibly. After 220 CE, reduced Chinese control gave city-states greater autonomy but also exposed them to more external threats.

religion.zh: 佛教为主导信仰，龟兹、于阗等地寺院林立，石窟艺术蓬勃发展。同时存在琐罗亚斯德教、摩尼教早期传播与本土萨满信仰。
religion.en: Buddhism was the dominant faith, with numerous monasteries in Kucha, Khotan, and elsewhere, and flourishing cave temple art. Zoroastrianism, early Manichaeism, and native shamanistic beliefs also existed.

traditions.zh: 多元文化交融的典范：印度、中国、波斯与中亚草原文化在此汇聚。龟兹音乐与舞蹈享誉四方，于阗玉石加工传统源远流长。
traditions.en: A paradigm of multicultural synthesis: Indian, Chinese, Persian, and Central Asian steppe cultures converged here. Kuchean music and dance were renowned far and wide, and Khotan's jade crafting tradition had ancient roots.

achievements.zh: 克孜尔等石窟壁画艺术代表了佛教艺术的高峰，龟兹乐舞深刻影响了中原音乐发展，多语言文献的翻译与保存为文化交流做出巨大贡献。
achievements.en: The Kizil cave murals represented a pinnacle of Buddhist art, Kuchean music and dance profoundly influenced Chinese musical development, and the translation and preservation of multilingual texts made enormous contributions to cultural exchange.

description.zh: 丝绸之路贸易是经济命脉，各绿洲城市是东西方商品中转站。本地经济以灌溉农业（粮食、棉花、葡萄）与手工业（玉器、纺织、金属加工）为基础。商税收入丰厚。
description.en: Silk Road trade was the economic lifeblood, with each oasis city serving as a transit station for east-west goods. The local economy was based on irrigated agriculture (grain, cotton, grapes) and handicrafts (jade, textiles, metalwork). Trade tax revenue was substantial.

name.zh: 丝绸之路天山南道（南线）
name.en: Silk Road southern route (south of Tianshan)

name.zh: 丝绸之路天山北道（北线）
name.en: Silk Road northern route (north of Tianshan)

description.zh: 先进的绿洲灌溉技术（坎儿井与明渠系统）是文明存续的关键。吸收了中原的造纸、冶铁技术与印度的佛教建筑工程知识。玉石加工与音乐器械制作技术精湛。
description.en: Advanced oasis irrigation technology (qanat and open channel systems) was key to civilization's survival. Absorbed Chinese papermaking and iron-smelting techniques, and Indian Buddhist architectural engineering knowledge. Jade processing and musical instrument craftsmanship were refined.

name.en: Cave temple excavation and mural techniques

description.zh: 各城邦拥有小规模城防部队与少量骑兵。军事力量有限，主要依靠城墙防御与外交手段抵御外敌。大型军事威胁通常依赖向中原政权或周边强权求援。
description.en: Each city-state maintained small garrison forces and limited cavalry. Military strength was modest, relying primarily on city walls and diplomatic maneuvering to resist enemies. Major military threats typically required seeking aid from Chinese regimes or neighboring powers.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 铁札甲或锁子甲，防护全身要害
description.en: Lamellar or chainmail armor protecting vital areas

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 复合弓或长弓，远射主力
description.en: Composite or longbow, primary ranged weapon

name.zh: 塞种-伊朗系人口（于阗、疏勒等）
汉人、粟特人及其他商旅族群
name.en: Han Chinese, Sogdians, and other trader communities

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以谷物仓储、金属储备和织物库存为主，储备水平约可维持0个月开支。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury was primarily grain granaries, metal reserves, and textile stocks, with reserves sufficient for about 0 months of spending.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政体系以实物税收和贡纳为基础，军事支出占据主导地位，国库储备以谷物、金属和织物为主。
fiscalPolicy.en: The fiscal system was based on in-kind taxation and tribute, military spending dominated, with treasury reserves held in grain, metals, and textiles.

dynasty.zh: 无固定王朝（选举制王权）

capital.zh: 波希米亚境内（具体城址不详）

governmentForm.zh: 日耳曼部族王国，以选举制国王为核心、长老与战士大会为决策基础
governmentForm.en: A Germanic tribal kingdom with an elective king at its center, with councils of elders and warrior assemblies as the decision-making foundation

socialStructure.zh: 国王与战士贵族（从士团首领）为统治阶层，自由民战士构成社会主体，半自由民与奴隶从事农耕与劳役。国王权力受战士大会制约。
socialStructure.en: The king and warrior nobles (comitatus leaders) formed the ruling class; free warrior-farmers comprised the social majority; semi-free people and slaves performed farming and labor. Royal power was checked by the warrior assembly.

rulingClass.zh: 国王、从士团贵族与部族长老
rulingClass.en: The king, comitatus nobles, and tribal elders

succession.zh: 由战士大会从王族或杰出战士中推举产生，非严格世袭
succession.en: Selected by the warrior assembly from the royal family or distinguished warriors; not strictly hereditary

structure.zh: 以日耳曼传统的部族大会（thing）为最高决策机构，国王在战时享有较大权力，和平时期受长老与自由民大会约束。从士团（comitatus）制度将精锐战士与首领紧密联结。
structure.en: The Germanic traditional tribal assembly (thing) served as the supreme decision-making body. The king held greater power in wartime but was constrained by elders and free men's assemblies in peacetime. The comitatus system closely bound elite warriors to their leaders.

function.zh: 决定战争与和平、推举国王、审判重大案件
function.en: Decided matters of war and peace, elected kings, and judged major cases

function.zh: 日常政务咨询、调解部族内部纠纷
function.en: Consulted on daily governance and mediated intra-tribal disputes

function.zh: 国王的精锐近卫武装，执行军事行动与护卫职责
function.en: The king's elite guard force, executing military operations and protection duties

laws.zh: 以日耳曼习惯法为基础，强调血仇（feud）与赔偿金（wergild）制度。重大案件由部族大会审判，普通纠纷由长老仲裁。
laws.en: Based on Germanic customary law, emphasizing blood feud and wergild (compensation payment) systems. Major cases were tried by the tribal assembly; ordinary disputes were arbitrated by elders.

diplomacy.zh: 与罗马帝国边境关系复杂，在贸易、结盟与冲突之间摇摆。马科曼尼战争（166-180年）的记忆仍鲜明。与周边日耳曼部族（夸迪人、阿勒曼尼人）既有联盟也有竞争。
diplomacy.en: Complex frontier relations with the Roman Empire, oscillating between trade, alliance, and conflict. Memories of the Marcomannic Wars (166–180 CE) remained vivid. Both allied and competed with neighboring Germanic tribes (Quadi, Alemanni).

religion.zh: 日耳曼多神信仰：崇拜沃丹（奥丁）、多纳尔（索尔）、弗雷亚等神灵。森林与自然圣地为祭祀场所，有专职祭司阶层。
religion.en: Germanic polytheism: worshipped Wodan (Odin), Donar (Thor), Freyja, and other deities. Forests and natural sacred sites served as places of worship, with a dedicated priestly class.

traditions.zh: 重视战士荣誉与从士忠诚文化，口传英雄史诗传统。冬至大祭（尤尔节前身）为重要节庆。蜂蜜酒宴饮与战士誓言仪式是社交核心。
traditions.en: Valued warrior honor and comitatus loyalty culture, with oral heroic epic traditions. The winter solstice festival (proto-Yule) was an important celebration. Mead feasting and warrior oath ceremonies were central to social life.

achievements.zh: 精良的铁器锻造（日耳曼长剑），独特的动物纹装饰艺术风格，卢恩文字用于宗教与铭刻用途。
achievements.en: Fine iron forging (Germanic longswords), distinctive animal-motif decorative art style, and runic script used for religious and inscriptional purposes.

description.zh: 以定居农业与畜牧为基础经济。波希米亚地区土地肥沃，适于谷物种植。与罗马边境的贸易带来金属器具、葡萄酒与奢侈品。铁器锻造是重要手工业。
description.en: Based on settled agriculture and animal husbandry. Bohemia's fertile land was suitable for cereal cultivation. Trade with the Roman frontier brought metal tools, wine, and luxury goods. Iron forging was an important craft industry.

name.zh: 多瑙河-波希米亚贸易通道

name.en: Northern segment of the Amber Road

description.zh: 铁器锻造技术较为先进，能制作高质量的武器与农具。从罗马帝国吸收了部分建筑、冶金与军事技术。农业技术以粗放耕作为主，但已使用铁犁。
description.en: Iron forging technology was relatively advanced, producing high-quality weapons and farm tools. Absorbed some construction, metallurgical, and military techniques from the Roman Empire. Agriculture was mostly extensive but already used iron plows.

description.zh: 以步兵为核心的强悍部族军队，全体自由民男子皆有从军义务。从士团为精锐核心力量。曾在马科曼尼战争中给罗马帝国造成严重威胁。
description.en: A formidable tribal army centered on infantry, with all free adult males obligated to serve. The comitatus formed the elite core. Had posed a serious threat to the Roman Empire during the Marcomannic Wars.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 步兵标准长兵器
description.en: Standard infantry polearm

description.zh: 单兵弩与床弩，中国军队核心远程武器
description.en: Infantry crossbow and mounted crossbow, core ranged weapon

description.zh: 复合弓或长弓，远射主力
description.en: Composite or longbow, primary ranged weapon

name.zh: 夸迪人及其他日耳曼部族

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以谷物仓储、金属储备和织物库存为主，储备水平约可维持0个月开支。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury was primarily grain granaries, metal reserves, and textile stocks, with reserves sufficient for about 0 months of spending.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政体系以实物税收和贡纳为基础，军事支出占据主导地位，国库储备以谷物、金属和织物为主。
fiscalPolicy.en: The fiscal system was based on in-kind taxation and tribute, military spending dominated, with treasury reserves held in grain, metals, and textiles.

capital.zh: 普里米斯（卡斯尔·伊布里姆附近）

governmentForm.zh: 以部族联盟为基础的游牧-半定居政治组织
governmentForm.en: A tribal federation-based nomadic-semi-sedentary political organization

socialStructure.zh: 以部族长老和战士贵族为统治阶层，牧民与商旅护卫为主体人口，底层为被征服的定居农业社群。
socialStructure.en: Governed by tribal elders and warrior nobles, with herders and caravan guards as the main population, and conquered settled agricultural communities at the bottom.

rulingClass.zh: 部族长老、战士贵族
rulingClass.en: Tribal elders and warrior nobles

succession.zh: 部族长老推举或武力夺权
succession.en: Selected by tribal elders or through military prowess

structure.zh: 松散的部族联盟，各部族在共同首领名义下保持高度自治。首领权力主要体现在战时指挥与对外交涉中。各部族在自己领地内自行管理。
structure.en: A loose tribal confederation where each tribe maintained high autonomy under a common chief's nominal authority. The chief's power was primarily exercised in wartime command and external negotiations. Each tribe self-governed within its own territory.

function.zh: 协调各部族间事务、商议战争与迁徙、调解纠纷
function.en: Coordinated inter-tribal affairs, deliberated on war and migration, and mediated disputes

function.zh: 组织劫掠与防御行动，协调各部族战士
function.en: Organized raiding and defensive operations, coordinating warriors from various tribes

laws.zh: 以习惯法和部族传统为主，强调血仇复仇与部族荣誉。长老裁决纠纷，盗窃牲畜与背叛部族是最严重的罪行。
laws.en: Based on customary law and tribal traditions, emphasizing blood vendetta and tribal honor. Elders adjudicated disputes; livestock theft and tribal betrayal were the gravest offenses.

diplomacy.zh: 与库施王国（麦罗埃）和罗马帝国控制的埃及交替冲突与贸易。控制着尼罗河与红海之间的沙漠通道，利用地理优势从事商队护送与劫掠。
diplomacy.en: Alternated between conflict and trade with the Kingdom of Kush (Meroe) and Roman-controlled Egypt. Controlled desert corridors between the Nile and Red Sea, leveraging geographic advantages for caravan escort and raiding.

religion.zh: 多神信仰，崇拜太阳神曼杜利斯和伊西斯等努比亚-埃及混合神灵。在菲莱岛神庙等地参与宗教仪式。保留萨满与自然崇拜传统。
religion.en: Polytheistic beliefs, worshipping Mandulis the sun god and Isis among Nubian-Egyptian syncretic deities. Participated in religious ceremonies at temples like Philae. Retained shamanistic and nature worship traditions.

traditions.zh: 游牧传统与沙漠生存文化。骆驼骑术与沙漠导航为核心生存技能。独特的身体装饰与纹身文化。口传历史与部族歌谣传统。
traditions.en: Nomadic traditions and desert survival culture. Camel riding and desert navigation were core survival skills. Distinctive body decoration and tattooing culture. Oral history and tribal song traditions.

achievements.zh: 独特的岩画艺术，精湛的沙漠导航与生存技术，连接尼罗河谷与红海的商路控制能力。
achievements.en: Distinctive rock art, masterful desert navigation and survival techniques, and the ability to control trade routes connecting the Nile Valley and Red Sea.

description.zh: 以畜牧和劫掠贸易为主，兼有沙漠商路的中介贸易（护送商队收取费用或直接劫掠）。控制着金矿与绿宝石矿区的通道，部分开采矿产用于交换。
description.en: Based on pastoralism and raiding-trade, supplemented by intermediary desert route trade (escorting caravans for fees or outright raiding). Controlled access to gold and emerald mining areas, with some mineral extraction for exchange.

description.zh: 掌握冶金和骑术，尤其是骆驼骑兵技术在沙漠战争中极具优势。从埃及和麦罗埃吸收了部分冶铁与建筑技术。沙漠水源寻找与贮存技术精湛。
description.en: Mastered metallurgy and horsemanship, with camel cavalry technology providing great advantages in desert warfare. Absorbed some iron-smelting and construction techniques from Egypt and Meroe. Desert water-finding and storage skills were refined.

name.zh: 沙漠导航与水源定位技术

description.zh: 以骆驼骑兵和弓骑兵为主力，在沙漠环境中具有显著优势。擅长突袭、劫掠与游击战术，曾多次袭击罗马帝国在上埃及的边境据点。
description.en: Primarily camel cavalry and horse archers, with significant advantages in desert environments. Excelled at raids, plundering, and guerrilla tactics, having repeatedly attacked Roman frontier outposts in Upper Egypt.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 水战拍竿，击毁敌船上层结构
description.en: Ship-mounted swinging beam to smash enemy superstructures

description.zh: 可连发十矢的机械弩，守城利器
description.en: Mechanical crossbow firing ten bolts in succession, excellent for defense

description.zh: 单刃直刀，三国/南北朝步骑通用
description.en: Single-edged straight saber, used by infantry and cavalry

description.zh: 重骑兵标志性武器，冲击力极强
description.en: Signature heavy cavalry weapon with tremendous charge impact

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以谷物仓储、金属储备和织物库存为主，储备水平约可维持0个月开支。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury was primarily grain granaries, metal reserves, and textile stocks, with reserves sufficient for about 0 months of spending.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政体系以实物税收和贡纳为基础，军事支出占据主导地位，国库储备以谷物、金属和织物为主。
fiscalPolicy.en: The fiscal system was based on in-kind taxation and tribute, military spending dominated, with treasury reserves held in grain, metals, and textiles.

capital.zh: 无固定都城（分布于加那利群岛各岛）
capital.en: No fixed capital (spread across the Canary Islands)

governmentForm.zh: 各岛独立的部族酋长制，以长老议事会辅助决策
governmentForm.en: Independent tribal chieftainships on each island, with councils of elders assisting in decision-making

socialStructure.zh: 各岛由门塞（酋长）统治，贵族阶层与祭司次之，自由民从事畜牧与农耕，底层为依附劳动者。各岛相对独立，社会组织较为原始但有序。
